`centroids.py`, `barycenters.py`, `moran.py`\n- dataset versionado: `trade_s2_v001`, `trade_s2_v002`\n- corrida: `YYYY-MM-DD_NNN_alias`\n- archivos de configuración: `snake_case.yaml`\n- outputs: nombres deterministas, sin timestamp incrustado dentro del archivo; el contexto de corrida vive en la carpeta `run`\n\n**3. Estrategia de Modularización**\n\nLa modularización debe seguir el flujo real del notebook, pero separando “motor” y “consumo”.\n\nLo que debe pasar a código productivo en `src/geoanalisis/`:\n\n- lectura y registro de datasets\n- validación de esquema S2\n- carga de diccionarios\n- descarga/control de Natural Earth\n- cálculo y resolución de centroides\n- construcción de OD matrix\n- cálculo de totales país-año\n- cálculo de barycentros globales y por país\n- cálculo de features de trayectorias\n- clustering gravitacional y métricas de estabilidad\n- Moran’s I global y por SITC\n- drift y speed\n- distancia media del comercio\n- escritura estandarizada de outputs y manifests\n\nLo que debe convertirse en funciones o servicios reusables:\n\n- `haversine_km`\n- `weighted_mean`\n- construcción de pesos espaciales\n- normalización de códigos\n- validaciones de cobertura\n- utilidades de logging\n- resolutores de códigos históricos y territoriales\n- naming y exportación de artefactos\n- carga de parquets con DuckDB\n\nLo que debe quedarse en notebooks:\n\n- exploración visual\n- comparaciones de países\n- gráficos de publicación\n- análisis ad hoc\n- análisis metodológico o sensibilidad\n- QA visual de mapas y resultados\n\nRegla fuerte:\n\n- un notebook no debe contener lógica de negocio no testeada\n- un notebook solo debe invocar funciones del paquete y documentar resultados\n- ningún output crítico debe depender de ejecutar celdas manualmente\n\n**4. Estrategia de Versionamiento**\n\nEsta es la parte más importante del rediseño.\n\n**Versionamiento de inputs**\nNo recomiendo seguir trabajando contra `trade/raw` directamente. En `Geoanalisis` debe existir una copia versionada por dataset.\n\nEjemplo:\n\n- `data/incoming/trade_s2_v001/raw/...`\n- `data/incoming/trade_s2_v002/raw/...`\n\nCada dataset debe tener un `manifest.json` con:\n\n- `dataset_id`\n- fecha de incorporación\n- origen\n- rango temporal\n- cantidad de archivos\n- hashes o al menos tamaño/mtime por archivo\n- diccionarios incluidos\n- notas de compatibilidad\n\nSi cambia la base, se crea una nueva versión de dataset, no se pisa la anterior.\n\n**Versionamiento de outputs**\nCada corrida debe ir en una carpeta propia dentro de `runs/<dataset_id>/`.\n\nEjemplo:\n\n- `runs/trade_s2_v001/2026-03-10_001_baseline/`\n- `runs/trade_s2_v001/2026-03-12_002_gc-tuning/`\n- `runs/trade_s2_v002/2026-04-01_001_baseline/`\n\nEso preserva historia sin sobrescribir nada.\n\n**Identificación de corridas**\nCada corrida debe tener:\n\n- `run_id`\n- `dataset_id`\n- `pipeline_version`\n- `git_commit` cuando ya esté en GitHub\n- configuración usada\n- stages ejecutados\n- fecha/hora\n- estado\n- duración\n- rutas de outputs\n\nEso debe quedar en `run_manifest.json` y opcionalmente en `data/registry/runs.csv`.\n\n**Convención de nombres**\nLa carpeta de corrida es el contenedor de contexto. Dentro de ella, los nombres de archivos deben ser estables.\n\nEjemplo correcto:\n\n- `artifacts/03_barycenters/barycenter_imports_exports.csv`\n- `artifacts/05_moran/moran_global.csv`\n\nNo hace falta meter la fecha o versión en cada archivo si ya vive dentro de una corrida versionada.\n\n**Versionamiento del código**\nEl paquete en sí debe tener una versión semántica interna, por ejemplo:\n\n- `0.1.0` para baseline modular inicial\n- `0.2.0` cuando agregues nuevos análisis\n- `1.0.0` cuando se estabilice\n\n**5. Recomendación Técnica**\n\nMi recomendación es un enfoque híbrido.\n\n- `scripts .py` o CLI para ejecución de pipeline\n- `funciones reusables` dentro de `src/geoanalisis`\n- `notebooks` solo para exploración, reporting y validación visual\n\nNo recomiendo una arquitectura basada principalmente en notebooks. Tampoco recomiendo solo scripts planos sin paquete, porque vas a perder reutilización y orden rápidamente.\n\nLa mejor combinación aquí es:\n\n- un paquete Python modular\n- configuración externa en YAML\n- outputs de corrida versionados\n- notebooks livianos que consuman artefactos ya producidos\n\nEsto te da:\n\n- reproducibilidad\n- trazabilidad\n- facilidad para GitHub\n- facilidad para agregar etapas nuevas\n- facilidad para reusar funciones y testearlas\n\n**6. La corrida registrada del 27 de febrero de 2026 confirma que ejecutó correctamente geografía, validación, barycentros, drift y distancia; por estructura y outputs, también es la base funcional más madura.\n\nTambién confirmé que [C:\\Python\\Geoanalisis](C:/Python/Geoanalisis) ya existe y está vacía, lo cual es conveniente para empezar sin tocar el legado.\n\n**1. Diagnóstico Corto**\n\nEl problema del notebook actual es arquitectónico, no solo de tamaño:\n\n- concentra pipeline de producción, validaciones, análisis, figuras y exploración en un solo `.ipynb`\n- mezcla lógica reusable con lógica ad hoc\n- tiene dependencias implícitas entre celdas\n- repite imports, helpers y configuración\n- hace difícil reruns reproducibles y comparación entre corridas\n- no separa claramente input versionado, artefactos intermedios y productos finales\n- no está preparado para acumulación ordenada de nuevas versiones de datos\n\nRiesgos de seguir así:\n\n- resultados no reproducibles por ejecución parcial o fuera de orden\n- rotura silenciosa cuando cambien inputs o paths\n- dificultad para auditar qué código generó qué output\n- imposibilidad práctica de versionar corridas y publicar en GitHub con orden\n- mayor costo de mantenimiento a medida que se agreguen nuevos análisis\n\n**2. Arquitectura Propuesta para `C:\\Python\\Geoanalisis`**\n\nRecomiendo una arquitectura híbrida: `src/` para producción y notebooks solo para consumo analítico.\n\nÁrbol propuesto:\n\n```text\nC:\\Python\\Geoanalisis\n├── README.md\n├── pyproject.toml\n├── .gitignore\n├── .env.example\n├── configs\n│   ├── project.yaml\n│   ├── datasets\n│   │   ├── trade_s2_v001.yaml\n│   │   └── trade_s2_v002.yaml\n│   └── runs\n│       ├── baseline_1976_2023.yaml\n│       └── gc_periods_default.yaml\n├── docs\n│   ├── architecture.md\n│   ├── project_map.md\n│   ├── pipeline_stages.md\n│   ├── data_versioning.md\n│   └── migration_plan.md\n├── data\n│   ├── incoming\n│   │   └── trade_s2_v001\n│   │       ├── raw\n│   │       │   ├── dataverse_files\n│   │       │   └── reference\n│   │       └── manifest.json\n│   ├── external\n│   │   └── natural_earth\n│   ├── working\n│   │   └── trade_s2_v001\n│   └── registry\n│       ├── datasets.csv\n│       └── runs.csv\n├── src\n│   └── geoanalisis\n│       ├── __init__.py\n│       ├── config.py\n│       ├── logging_utils.py\n│       ├── io\n│       │   ├── manifests.py\n│       │   ├── readers.py\n│       │   └── writers.py\n│       ├── utils\n│       │   ├── paths.py\n│       │   ├── geo.py\n│       │   ├── stats.py\n│       │   └── validation.py\n│       ├── pipelines\n│       │   ├── stage_01_geo.py\n│       │   ├── stage_02_validation.py\n│       │   ├── stage_03_barycenters.py\n│       │   ├── stage_04_clustering.py\n│       │   ├── stage_05_moran.py\n│       │   ├── stage_06_drift.py\n│       │   ├── stage_07_distance.py\n│       │   └── run_pipeline.py\n│       ├── services\n│       │   ├── centroids.py\n│       │   ├── od_matrix.py\n│       │   ├── trade_panel.py\n│       │   ├── barycenters.py\n│       │   ├── clustering.py\n│       │   ├── moran.py\n│       │   ├── drift.py\n│       │   └── distance.py\n│       └── cli\n│           └── main.py\n├── notebooks\n│   ├── exploratory\n│   │   ├── 01_geo_qc.ipynb\n│   │   ├── 02_barycenter_exploration.ipynb\n│   │   └── 03_clustering_exploration.ipynb\n│   └── reporting\n│       ├── 10_baseline_results.ipynb\n│       └── 11_country_case_studies.ipynb\n├── runs\n│   ├── trade_s2_v001\n│   │   ├── 2026-03-10_001_baseline\n│   │   │   ├── run_manifest.json\n│   │   │   ├── logs\n│   │   │   ├── artifacts\n│   │   │   │   ├── 01_geo\n│   │   │   │   ├── 02_validation\n│   │   │   │   ├── 03_barycenters\n│   │   │   │   ├── 04_clustering\n│   │   │   │   ├── 05_moran\n│   │   │   │   ├── 06_drift\n│   │   │   │   └── 07_distance\n│   │   │   └── reports\n│   │   │       ├── figures\n│   │   │       └── tables\n│   └── trade_s2_v002\n└── tests\n    ├── test_geo.py\n    ├── test_barycenters.py\n    ├── test_distance.py\n    └── test_manifests.py\n```\n\nConvenciones de nombres:\n\n- carpetas de pipeline: `stage_01_geo`, `stage_02_validation`, etc.\n- módulos de dominio: `centroids.py`, `barycenters.py`, `moran.py`\n- dataset versionado: `trade_s2_v001`, `trade_s2_v002`\n- corrida: `YYYY-MM-DD_NNN_alias`\n- archivos de configuración: `snake_case.yaml`\n- outputs: nombres deterministas, sin timestamp incrustado dentro del archivo; el contexto de corrida vive en la carpeta `run`\n\n**3. Estrategia de Modularización**\n\nLa modularización debe seguir el flujo real del notebook, pero separando “motor” y “consumo”.\n\nLo que debe pasar a código productivo en `src/geoanalisis/`:\n\n- lectura y registro de datasets\n- validación de esquema S2\n- carga de diccionarios\n- descarga/control de Natural Earth\n- cálculo y resolución de centroides\n- construcción de OD matrix\n- cálculo de totales país-año\n- cálculo de barycentros globales y por país\n- cálculo de features de trayectorias\n- clustering gravitacional y métricas de estabilidad\n- Moran’s I global y por SITC\n- drift y speed\n- distancia media del comercio\n- escritura estandarizada de outputs y manifests\n\nLo que debe convertirse en funciones o servicios reusables:\n\n- `haversine_km`\n- `weighted_mean`\n- construcción de pesos espaciales\n- normalización de códigos\n- validaciones de cobertura\n- utilidades de logging\n- resolutores de códigos históricos y territoriales\n- naming y exportación de artefactos\n- carga de parquets con DuckDB\n\nLo que debe quedarse en notebooks:\n\n- exploración visual\n- comparaciones de países\n- gráficos de publicación\n- análisis ad hoc\n- análisis metodológico o sensibilidad\n- QA visual de mapas y resultados\n\nRegla fuerte:\n\n- un notebook no debe contener lógica de negocio no testeada\n- un notebook solo debe invocar funciones del paquete y documentar resultados\n- ningún output crítico debe depender de ejecutar celdas manualmente\n\n**4. Estrategia de Versionamiento**\n\nEsta es la parte más importante del rediseño.\n\n**Versionamiento de inputs**\nNo recomiendo seguir trabajando contra `trade/raw` directamente. En `Geoanalisis` debe existir una copia versionada por dataset.\n\nEjemplo:\n\n- `data/incoming/trade_s2_v001/raw/...`\n- `data/incoming/trade_s2_v002/raw/...`\n\nCada dataset debe tener un `manifest.json` con:\n\n- `dataset_id`\n- fecha de incorporación\n- origen\n- rango temporal\n- cantidad de archivos\n- hashes o al menos tamaño/mtime por archivo\n- diccionarios incluidos\n- notas de compatibilidad\n\nSi cambia la base, se crea una nueva versión de dataset, no se pisa la anterior.\n\n**Versionamiento de outputs**\nCada corrida debe ir en una carpeta propia dentro de `runs/<dataset_id>/`.\n\nEjemplo:\n\n- `runs/trade_s2_v001/2026-03-10_001_baseline/`\n- `runs/trade_s2_v001/2026-03-12_002_gc-tuning/`\n- `runs/trade_s2_v002/2026-04-01_001_baseline/`\n\nEso preserva historia sin sobrescribir nada.\n\n**Identificación de corridas**\nCada corrida debe tener:\n\n- `run_id`\n- `dataset_id`\n- `pipeline_version`\n- `git_commit` cuando ya esté en GitHub\n- configuración usada\n- stages ejecutados\n- fecha/hora\n- estado\n- duración\n- rutas de outputs\n\nEso debe quedar en `run_manifest.json` y opcionalmente en `data/registry/runs.csv`.\n\n**Convención de nombres**\nLa carpeta de corrida es el contenedor de contexto. Dentro de ella, los nombres de archivos deben ser estables.\n\nEjemplo correcto:\n\n- `artifacts/03_barycenters/barycenter_imports_exports.csv`\n- `artifacts/05_moran/moran_global.csv`\n\nNo hace falta meter la fecha o versión en cada archivo si ya vive dentro de una corrida versionada.\n\n**Versionamiento del código**\nEl paquete en sí debe tener una versión semántica interna, por ejemplo:\n\n- `0.1.0` para baseline modular inicial\n- `0.2.0` cuando agregues nuevos análisis\n- `1.0.0` cuando se estabilice\n\n**5. Recomendación Técnica**\n\nMi recomendación es un enfoque híbrido.\n\n- `scripts .py` o CLI para ejecución de pipeline\n- `funciones reusables` dentro de `src/geoanalisis`\n- `notebooks` solo para exploración, reporting y validación visual\n\nNo recomiendo una arquitectura basada principalmente en notebooks. Tampoco recomiendo solo scripts planos sin paquete, porque vas a perder reutilización y orden rápidamente.\n\nLa mejor combinación aquí es:\n\n- un paquete Python modular\n- configuración externa en YAML\n- outputs de corrida versionados\n- notebooks livianos que consuman artefactos ya producidos\n\nEsto te da:\n\n- reproducibilidad\n- trazabilidad\n- facilidad para GitHub\n- facilidad para agregar etapas nuevas\n- facilidad para reusar funciones y testearlas\n\n**6. Plan de Migración por Fases**\n\n**Fase 0. Congelación lógica del legado**\nNo tocar nada en [C:\\Python\\trade](C:/Python/trade). Se usa solo como referencia y fuente de copia.\n\n**Fase 1. Inicialización del nuevo proyecto**\nCrear estructura base en [C:\\Python\\Geoanalisis](C:/Python/Geoanalisis):\n\n- `src`\n- `data`\n- `configs`\n- `docs`\n- `runs`\n- `notebooks`\n- `tests`\n\n**Fase 2. Copia controlada de insumos**\nCopiar desde `trade/raw` hacia una primera versión:\n\n- `data/incoming/trade_s2_v001/raw/dataverse_files`\n- `data/incoming/trade_s2_v001/raw/reference`\n\nY registrar manifest del dataset.\n\n**Fase 3. Extracción de configuración**\nSacar del notebook maestro la configuración estable:\n\n- base paths\n- años\n- nombre de columna de valor\n- períodos del clustering\n- listas de países foco\n- parámetros de umbrales\n\nEso debe vivir en YAML y `config.py`.\n\n**Fase 4. Modularización por capas**\nExtraer primero lo más estable y transversal:\n\n1. io y paths\n2. geografía\n3. validación\n4. barycenters\n5. distance\n6. moran\n7. clustering\n8. drift\n\nOrden sugerido de migración:\n- `Stage 01`\n- `Stage 02`\n- `Stage 03`\n- `Stage 07`\n- `Stage 05`\n- `Stage 06`\n- `Stage 04`\n\nEse orden reduce riesgo porque `Stage 04` es el más complejo y menos conveniente para migrar primero.\n\n**Fase 5. Comparación contra legado**\nPara cada stage migrado:\n\n- correr sobre `trade_s2_v001`\n- comparar outputs con los de `trade/output`\n- definir tolerancias\n- documentar diferencias esperadas\n\n**Fase 6. Separación analítica**\nCrear notebooks nuevos que lean solo `runs/.../artifacts/...` y no raw directo.\n\n**Fase 7. Endurecimiento**\nAgregar:\n\n- tests unitarios\n- tests de integración por stage\n- manifests de corrida\n- registro de datasets\n- README y documentación de arquitectura\n\n**Fase 8. Preparación para GitHub**\nUna vez estabilizado:\n\n- limpiar secretos y rutas absolutas\n- usar paths relativos\n- fijar dependencias\n- agregar `.gitignore`\n- agregar licencia si corresponde\n\n**7. Documentación Inicial Necesaria**\n\nArchivos mínimos recomendados al arrancar:\n\n- `README.md`\n  - qué es el proyecto\n  - cómo está organizado\n  - cómo correr el pipeline\n  - cómo registrar una nueva versión de datos\n\n- `docs/architecture.md`\n  - visión general\n  - separación pipeline vs notebooks\n  - flujo de artefactos\n\n- `docs/project_map.md`\n  - árbol de carpetas\n  - propósito de cada directorio\n\n- `docs/pipeline_stages.md`\n  - descripción de `01_geo` a `07_distance`\n  - inputs, outputs y dependencias por etapa\n\n- `docs/data_versioning.md`\n  - qué es `dataset_id`\n  - qué es `run_id`\n  - reglas para no sobrescribir resultados\n  - convención de nombres\n\n- `docs/migration_plan.md`\n  - mapa entre notebook legado y módulos nuevos\n\n- `pyproject.toml`\n  - dependencias\n  - paquete\n  - herramientas de test/lint si las agregas\n\n- `configs/project.yaml`\n  - parámetros globales por defecto\n\n**Separación Pipeline vs Analítico**\n\nPropongo esta frontera operativa:\n\nPipeline / producción:\n- `src/geoanalisis/pipelines/*`\n- `src/geoanalisis/services/*`\n- `configs/*`\n- `runs/*`\n- `tests/*`\n\nAnalítico / exploratorio:\n- `notebooks/exploratory/*`\n- `notebooks/reporting/*`\n\nRegla práctica:\n- si algo genera artefactos oficiales o reusables, va al pipeline\n- si algo solo inspecciona, compara o presenta resultados, va a notebooks\n\n**Propuesta de Descomposición del Notebook Maestro**\n\nCorrespondencia recomendada:\n\n- configuración global -> `config.py` + YAML\n- Stage 01 geo -> `services/centroids.py`, `services/od_matrix.py`, `pipelines/stage_01_geo.py`\n- Stage 02 validación -> `services/trade_panel.py`, `pipelines/stage_02_validation.py`\n- Stage 03 barycenters -> `services/barycenters.py`, `pipelines/stage_03_barycenters.py`\n- Stage 04 clustering -> `services/clustering.py`, `pipelines/stage_04_clustering.py`\n- Stage 05 Moran -> `services/moran.py`, `pipelines/stage_05_moran.py`\n- Stage 06 drift -> `services/drift.py`, `pipelines/stage_06_drift.py`\n- Stage 07 distance -> `services/distance.py`, `pipelines/stage_07_distance.py`\n\nVisualizaciones:\n- moverlas a notebooks de reporting o a funciones específicas de `reports/` si decides que algunas deben ser oficiales\n\n**Recomendación Final**\n\nLa decisión correcta aquí es no “partir el notebook en siete notebooks” sin más. Eso solo desplaza el problema. Lo correcto es:\n\n- construir un paquete Python modular en `Geoanalisis`\n- tratar inputs y runs como entidades versionadas\n- usar notebooks solo como capa analítica\n- preservar totalmente el legado en `trade`\n- hacer validación de equivalencia stage por stage\n\nCon esa base, el proyecto quedará listo para crecer, auditarse y eventualmente publicarse.\n\nSi estás de acuerdo con esta propuesta, el siguiente paso sería convertirla en implementación mínima inicial dentro de [C:\\Python\\Geoanalisis](C:/Python/Geoanalisis): estructura de carpetas, archivos base de documentación, configuración, y un esqueleto del paquete sin todavía migrar la lógica pesada."}}

these are INDEPENDENT of TRADE_YEAR_START / TRADE_YEAR_END above.\r\n#   GC_PERIOD_A = (first_year, last_year)  — first clustering window  (inclusive)\r\n#   GC_PERIOD_B = (first_year, last_year)  — second clustering window (inclusive)\r\n# First year should be >= TRADE_YEAR_START + 1 (barycenter steps need ≥ 2 consecutive years).\r\nGC_PERIOD_A: tuple = (1977, 2000)   # first  clustering window\r\nGC_PERIOD_B: tuple = (2001, 2022)   # second clustering window\r\n\r\n# ─────────────────────────────────────────────\r\n# Derived paths  — do not edit below\r\n# ─────────────────────────────────────────────\r\n\r\nRAW_DIR = BASE_DIR / \"raw\"\r\nDOWNLOAD_DIR = BASE_DIR / \"download\"\r\nOUTPUT_DIR = BASE_DIR / OUTPUT_FOLDER_NAME\r\nLOGS_DIR = BASE_DIR / \"logs\"\r\n\r\nDATAVERSE_DIR = RAW_DIR / \"dataverse_files\"  # S2_YYYY.parquet files\r\nREF_DIR = RAW_DIR / \"reference\"              # lookup tables\r\n\r\n# Step output folders (top-level)\r\nOUT_01_GEO = OUTPUT_DIR / \"01_geo\"\r\nOUT_02_AUDIT = OUTPUT_DIR / \"02_symmetry_audit\"\r\nOUT_03_BARY = OUTPUT_DIR / \"03_barycenters\"\r\nOUT_04_MAPS = OUTPUT_DIR / \"04_maps\"\r\nOUT_05_GC = OUTPUT_DIR / \"05_gravitational_clustering\"\r\n\r\n# Canonical registry of step roots\r\n# V6.0 — new stage roots\r\nOUT_06_DRIFT = OUTPUT_DIR / \"06_drift\"\r\nOUT_07_DIST  = OUTPUT_DIR / \"07_distance\"\r\n\r\nSTEP_DIRS = {\r\n    \"01_geo\":                      OUT_01_GEO,\r\n    \"02_symmetry_audit\":           OUT_02_AUDIT,\r\n    \"03_barycenters\":              OUT_03_BARY,\r\n    \"04_maps\":                     OUT_04_MAPS,\r\n    \"05_gravitational_clustering\": OUT_05_GC,\r\n    \"06_drift\":                    OUT_06_DRIFT,\r\n    \"07_distance\":                 OUT_07_DIST,\r\n}\r\n\r\n\r\n# Step-specific data and figure directories (canonical references)\r\nGEO_DATA     = OUT_01_GEO   / \"data\"\r\nGEO_FIG      = OUT_01_GEO   / \"fig\"\r\nAUDIT_DATA   = OUT_02_AUDIT / \"data\"\r\nAUDIT_FIG    = OUT_02_AUDIT / \"fig\"\r\nBARY_DATA    = OUT_03_BARY  / \"data\"\r\nBARY_FIG     = OUT_03_BARY  / \"fig\"\r\nMAPS_DATA    = OUT_04_MAPS  / \"data\"\r\nMAPS_FIG     = OUT_04_MAPS  / \"fig\"\r\nGC_DATA      = OUT_05_GC    / \"data\"\r\nGC_FIG       = OUT_05_GC    / \"fig\"\r\nDRIFT_DATA   = OUT_06_DRIFT / \"data\"\r\nDRIFT_FIG    = OUT_06_DRIFT / \"fig\"\r\nDIST_DATA    = OUT_07_DIST  / \"data\"\r\nDIST_FIG     = OUT_07_DIST  / \"fig\"\r\n\r\n# Canonical intermediate-file references used by downstream stages\r\nCENTROIDS_OUT = GEO_DATA / \"country_centroids_augmented.csv\"\r\nOD_OUT        = GEO_DATA / \"OD_Matrix.csv\"\r\n\r\n# ─────────────────────────────────────────────\r\n# Create ALL directories + required subfolders\r\n# ─────────────────────────────────────────────\r\n\r\ndef ensure_dir(p: Path) -> Path:\r\n    p.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)\r\n    return p\r\n\r\n# Core tree\r\nfor p in [\r\n    BASE_DIR,\r\n    RAW_DIR,\r\n    DOWNLOAD_DIR,\r\n    OUTPUT_DIR,\r\n    LOGS_DIR,\r\n    DATAVERSE_DIR,\r\n    REF_DIR,\r\n]:\r\n    ensure_dir(p)\r\n\r\n# Step tree + required subfolders (data/fig)\r\nfor _, step_root in STEP_DIRS.items():\r\n    ensure_dir(step_root)\r\n    ensure_dir(step_root / \"data\")\r\n    ensure_dir(step_root / \"fig\")\r\n\r\n# Backward-compatible aliases (to be phased out)\r\nGEO_DIR = OUT_01_GEO\r\nOUTPUTS_DIR = OUTPUT_DIR\r\n\r\n\r\nprint(\"[INFO] Directory structure verified/created successfully.\")\r\nprint(f\"       BASE_DIR      : {BASE_DIR}\")\r\nprint(f\"       RAW_DIR       : {RAW_DIR}\")\r\nprint(f\"       DATAVERSE_DIR : {DATAVERSE_DIR}\")\r\nprint(f\"       REF_DIR       : {REF_DIR}\")\r\nprint(f\"       OUTPUT_DIR    : {OUTPUT_DIR}\")\r\nprint(f\"       LOGS_DIR      : {LOGS_DIR}\")\r\nprint(f\"       DRIFT_DATA    : {DRIFT_DATA}\")\r\nprint(f\"       DIST_DATA     : {DIST_DATA}\")\r\n\r\n# Reference dictionary paths\r\nCOUNTRY_DICT_PATH = REF_DIR / \"country_code_iso.txt\"\r\nSITC2_DICT_PATH = REF_DIR / \"sitc2-2digit.txt\"\r\nSITC3_DICT_PATH = REF_DIR / \"sitc2-3digit.txt\"\r\n\r\n# ─────────────────────────────────────────────\r\n# Run logger  — one timestamped log file per run\r\n# ─────────────────────────────────────────────\r\n_RUN_TS = datetime.datetime.now().strftime(\"%Y%m%d_%H%M%S\")\r\n_LOG_FILE = LOGS_DIR / f\"pipeline_run_{_RUN_TS}.log\"\r\n\r\nlogging.basicConfig(\r\n    level=logging.INFO,\r\n    format=\"%(asctime)s  %(levelname)-8s  %(message)s\",\r\n    datefmt=\"%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S\",\r\n    handlers=[\r\n        logging.FileHandler(_LOG_FILE, encoding=\"utf-8\"),\r\n        logging.StreamHandler(sys.stdout),\r\n    ],\r\n    force=True,\r\n)\r\nlogger = logging.getLogger(\"trade_pipeline\")\r\n\r\nlogger.info(\"=\" * 60)\r\nlogger.info(\"Pipeline v6.0 run started\")\r\nlogger.info(\"  BASE_DIR         : %s\", BASE_DIR)\r\nlogger.info(\"  TRADE_YEAR_START : %d\", TRADE_YEAR_START)\r\nlogger.info(\"  TRADE_YEAR_END   : %d\", TRADE_YEAR_END)\r\nlogger.info(\"  TRADE_VALUE_COL  : %s\", TRADE_VALUE_COL)\r\nlogger.info(\"  OUTPUT_DIR       : %s\", OUTPUT_DIR)\r\nlogger.info(\"  Log file         : %s\", _LOG_FILE)\r\nlogger.info(\"=\" * 60)\r\n\r\n# ─────────────────────────────────────────────\r\n# Load reference dictionaries\r\n# ─────────────────────────────────────────────\r\ndef _read_tsv(path: Path) -> pd.DataFrame:\r\n    if not path.exists():\r\n        raise FileNotFoundError(\r\n            f\"Missing required reference file: {path}\\n\"\r\n            f\"Place the file under {REF_DIR} and re-run this cell.\"\r\n        )\r\n    return pd.read_csv(path, sep=\"\\t\", dtype=str)\r\n\r\ncountry_dict_df = _read_tsv(COUNTRY_DICT_PATH)\r\nsitc2_dict_df = _read_tsv(SITC2_DICT_PATH)\r\nsitc3_dict_df = _read_tsv(SITC3_DICT_PATH)\r\n\r\n# Defensive: validate expected columns early (fail fast with a clear message)\r\n_required_country_cols = {\"country_code\", \"country\"}\r\n_required_sitc2_cols = {\"sitc2\", \"sitc2_name\", \"nickname2\"}\r\n_required_sitc3_cols = {\"sitc3\", \"sitc3_name\", \"nickname3\"}\r\n\r\nif not _required_country_cols.issubset(set(country_dict_df.columns)):\r\n    raise ValueError(f\"{COUNTRY_DICT_PATH} missing columns: {_required_country_cols - set(country_dict_df.columns)}\")\r\nif not _required_sitc2_cols.issubset(set(sitc2_dict_df.columns)):\r\n    raise ValueError(f\"{SITC2_DICT_PATH} missing columns: {_required_sitc2_cols - set(sitc2_dict_df.columns)}\")\r\nif not _required_sitc3_cols.issubset(set(sitc3_dict_df.columns)):\r\n    raise ValueError(f\"{SITC3_DICT_PATH} missing columns: {_required_sitc3_cols - set(sitc3_dict_df.columns)}\")\r\n\r\nCOUNTRY_NAME_BY_CODE = dict(zip(country_dict_df[\"country_code\"], country_dict_df[\"country\"]))\r\nSITC2_NAME_BY_CODE = dict(zip(sitc2_dict_df[\"sitc2\"], sitc2_dict_df[\"sitc2_name\"]))\r\nSITC2_NICK_BY_CODE = dict(zip(sitc2_dict_df[\"sitc2\"], sitc2_dict_df[\"nickname2\"]))\r\nSITC3_NAME_BY_CODE = dict(zip(sitc3_dict_df[\"sitc3\"], sitc3_dict_df[\"sitc3_name\"]))\r\nSITC3_NICK_BY_CODE = dict(zip(sitc3_dict_df[\"sitc3\"], sitc3_dict_df[\"nickname3\"]))\r\n\r\nlogger.info(\r\n    \"Reference dictionaries loaded — %d countries, %d SITC2, %d SITC3\",\r\n    len(COUNTRY_NAME_BY_CODE),\r\n    len(SITC2_NAME_BY_CODE),\r\n    len(SITC3_NAME_BY_CODE),\r\n)\r\n\r\n# ─────────────────────────────────────────────\r\n# Summary\r\n# ─────────────────────────────────────────────\r\nprint(f\"\\n{'='*55}\")\r\nprint(f\"  BASE_DIR        : {BASE_DIR}\")\r\nprint(f\"  DATAVERSE_DIR   : {DATAVERSE_DIR}\")\r\nprint(f\"  REF_DIR         : {REF_DIR}\")\r\nprint(f\"  OUTPUT_DIR      : {OUTPUT_DIR}\")\r\nprint(f\"  Year range      : {TRADE_YEAR_START} to {TRADE_YEAR_END}\")\r\nprint(f\"  Trade value col : {TRADE_VALUE_COL}\")\r\nprint(f\"  Log file        : {_LOG_FILE.name}\")\r\nprint(f\"{'='*55}\\n\")\r\n\r\n===== CELL 10 =====\r\nfrom pathlib import Path\r\nimport zipfile\r\nimport numpy as np\r\nimport pandas as pd\r\nimport geopandas as gpd\r\nimport requests\r\n\r\n\r\n===== CELL 11 =====\r\n# -------------------------------------------------\r\n# 01. Geo inputs/outputs — derived paths\r\n# All base paths come from the config cell (Cell 2).\r\n# -------------------------------------------------\r\nfrom pathlib import Path\r\n\r\n# Country code list for centroid resolution\r\nCODES_CSV = REF_DIR / \"codes.csv\"\r\n\r\n# Authoritative outputs consumed by downstream steps\r\nCENTROIDS_OUT = OUT_01_GEO / \"data\" / \"country_centroids_augmented.csv\"\r\nOD_OUT        = OUT_01_GEO / \"data\" / \"OD_Matrix.csv\"\r\n\r\n# Historic-to-modern ISO3 mapping\r\nHISTORIC_TO_MODERN = {\r\n    \"ANS\": \"CUW\", \"ANT\": \"CUW\", \"CSK\": \"CZE\", \"DDR\": \"DEU\",\r\n    \"NTZ\": \"SAU\", \"PCI\": \"FSM\", \"PCZ\": \"PAN\", \"PUS\": \"UMI\",\r\n    \"SCG\": \"SRB\", \"SUN\": \"RUS\", \"YMD\": \"YEM\", \"YUG\": \"SRB\",\r\n}\r\n\r\n# Natural Earth shapefile (auto-downloaded; never written to raw/)\r\nNE_URL = \"https://naturalearth.s3.amazonaws.com/110m_cultural/ne_110m_admin_0_countries.zip\"\r\nNE_DIR = DOWNLOAD_DIR / \"natural_earth\" / \"ne_110m_admin_0_countries\"\r\nNE_ZIP = DOWNLOAD_DIR / \"natural_earth\" / \"ne_110m_admin_0_countries.zip\"\r\nNE_SHP = NE_DIR / \"ne_110m_admin_0_countries.shp\"\r\n\r\nNE_ZIP.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)\r\nNE_DIR.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)\r\n\r\nlogger.info(\"Stage 01 geo config loaded.\")\r\nprint(\"Geo config loaded.\")\r\nprint(\"  CODES_CSV    :\", CODES_CSV)\r\nprint(\"  CENTROIDS_OUT:\", CENTROIDS_OUT)\r\nprint(\"  OD_OUT       :\", OD_OUT)\r\nprint(\"  NE_SHP       :\", NE_SHP)\r\n\r\n\r\n===== CELL 13 =====\r\ndef log(msg: str, level: str = 'info'):\r\n    \"\"\"Thin wrapper so legacy log('...') calls route through the run logger.\"\"\"\r\n    getattr(logger, level.lower(), logger.info)(msg)\r\n\r\n\r\n===== CELL 14 =====\r\ndef download_file(url, dest):\r\n    if dest.exists() and dest.stat().st_size > 0:\r\n        log(\"Natural Earth zip already exists.\")\r\n        return\r\n    log(\"Downloading Natural Earth boundaries …\")\r\n    r = requests.get(url, stream=True, timeout=60)\r\n    r.raise_for_status()\r\n    with open(dest, \"wb\") as f:\r\n        for chunk in r.iter_content(chunk_size=1024 * 1024):\r\n            if chunk:\r\n                f.write(chunk)\r\n    log(\"Download complete.\")\r\n\r\n\r\n===== CELL 15 =====\r\ndef unzip(zip_path, out_dir):\r\n    if any(out_dir.glob(\"*.shp\")):\r\n        log(\"Natural Earth shapefile already extracted.\")\r\n        return\r\n    log(\"Extracting Natural Earth shapefile …\")\r\n    with zipfile.ZipFile(zip_path, \"r\") as z:\r\n        z.extractall(out_dir)\r\n    log(\"Extraction complete.\")\r\n\r\n\r\n===== CELL 16 =====\r\ndef haversine_km(lat1, lon1, lat2, lon2):\r\n    R = 6371.0088\r\n    lat1 = np.radians(lat1)\r\n    lon1 = np.radians(lon1)\r\n    lat2 = np.radians(lat2)\r\n    lon2 = np.radians(lon2)\r\n    dlat = lat2 - lat1\r\n    dlon = lon2 - lon1\r\n    a = np.sin(dlat / 2)**2 + np.cos(lat1)*np.cos(lat2)*np.sin(dlon / 2)**2\r\n    c = 2 * np.arcsin(np.sqrt(a))\r\n    return R * c\r\n\r\n\r\n===== CELL 18 =====\r\nlog(\"Reading codes.csv …\")\r\n\r\nif not CODES_CSV.exists():\r\n    raise FileNotFoundError(f\"Missing {CODES_CSV}\")\r\n\r\ncodes_df = pd.read_csv(CODES_CSV)\r\nif \"code\" not in codes_df.columns:\r\n    raise ValueError(\"codes.csv must have a column named 'code'\")\r\n\r\ncodes = (\r\n    codes_df[\"code\"]\r\n    .dropna()\r\n    .astype(str)\r\n    .str.strip()\r\n    .loc[lambda s: s != \"\"]\r\n    .unique()\r\n    .tolist()\r\n)\r\n\r\ncodes = sorted(codes)\r\n\r\nlog(f\"Loaded {len(codes)} country codes.\")\r\ncodes[:10]\r\n\r\n\r\n===== CELL 20 =====\r\nlog(\"Preparing Natural Earth geometry …\")\r\n\r\ndownload_file(NE_URL, NE_ZIP)\r\nunzip(NE_ZIP, NE_DIR)\r\n\r\nif not NE_SHP.exists():\r\n    raise FileNotFoundError(\"Natural Earth shapefile not found.\")\r\n\r\nworld = gpd.read_file(NE_SHP).to_crs(epsg=4326)\r\n\r\nlog(f\"Loaded {len(world)} polygons from Natural Earth.\")\r\nworld.head()\r\n\r\n\r\n===== CELL 22 =====\r\nlog(\"Computing modern centroids …\")\r\n\r\niso = world[\"ISO_A3\"].astype(str).values\r\nadm = world[\"ADM0_A3\"].astype(str).values\r\ncode = np.where(iso != \"-99\", iso, adm)\r\n\r\nworld = world.assign(code=code)\r\n\r\npts = world.geometry.representative_point()\r\n\r\ncentroids_modern = pd.DataFrame({\r\n    \"code\": world[\"code\"].astype(str).values,\r\n    \"lat\": pts.y.values,\r\n    \"lon\": pts.x.values,\r\n    \"name\": world.get(\"ADMIN\", \"\"),\r\n    \"assigned_from\": \"\"\r\n})\r\n\r\ncentroids_modern = (\r\n    centroids_modern\r\n    .dropna(subset=[\"code\", \"lat\", \"lon\"])\r\n    .drop_duplicates(subset=[\"code\"])\r\n    .reset_index(drop=True)\r\n)\r\n\r\nlog(f\"Computed centroids for {len(centroids_modern)} modern jurisdictions.\")\r\ncentroids_modern.head()\r\n\r\n\r\n===== CELL 24 =====\r\n# Diagnostic — coverage of Natural Earth vs your codes.csv\r\n\r\nne_codes = set(centroids_modern[\"code\"].astype(str))\r\nyour_codes = set(pd.Series(codes).astype(str))\r\n\r\nmissing_ne = sorted(your_codes - ne_codes)\r\n\r\nprint(f\"[INFO] Natural Earth codes available: {len(ne_codes)}\")\r\nprint(f\"[INFO] Your code universe (codes.csv): {len(your_codes)}\")\r\nprint(f\"[INFO] Missing from Natural Earth: {len(missing_ne)}\")\r\n\r\n# Classify missing codes\r\nmissing_historic = sorted([c for c in missing_ne if c in HISTORIC_TO_MODERN])\r\nmissing_manual = sorted([c for c in missing_ne if c not in HISTORIC_TO_MODERN])\r\n\r\nprint(f\"[INFO] Missing but covered by HISTORIC_TO_MODERN mapping: {len(missing_historic)}\")\r\nprint(f\"[INFO] Missing and NOT covered (needs manual centroid or alternative geometry source): {len(missing_manual)}\")\r\n\r\nprint(\"\\n--- Missing (historic-mapped) ---\")\r\nprint(missing_historic)\r\n\r\nprint(\"\\n--- Missing (needs manual / alternative) ---\")\r\nprint(missing_manual)\r\n\r\n\r\n===== CELL 25 =====\r\n# Diagnostic — coverage of Natural Earth vs codes.csv + historic mapping viability\r\n# Produces reports in GEO_DATA:\r\n#   1) audit_missing_codes.csv\r\n#   2) audit_historic_mapping_report.csv\r\n\r\nimport pandas as pd\r\n\r\n# Sets\r\nne_codes = set(centroids_modern[\"code\"].astype(str))\r\nyour_codes = set(pd.Series(codes).astype(str))\r\n\r\nmissing_ne = sorted(your_codes - ne_codes)\r\n\r\nprint(f\"[INFO] Natural Earth codes available: {len(ne_codes)}\")\r\nprint(f\"[INFO] Your code universe (codes.csv): {len(your_codes)}\")\r\nprint(f\"[INFO] Missing from Natural Earth: {len(missing_ne)}\")\r\n\r\n# Identify historic jurisdictions (by your mapping dict keys)\r\nmissing_historic = sorted([c for c in missing_ne if c in HISTORIC_TO_MODERN])\r\nmissing_nonhistoric = sorted([c for c in missing_ne if c not in HISTORIC_TO_MODERN])\r\n\r\nprint(f\"[INFO] Missing historic jurisdictions (in HISTORIC_TO_MODERN keys): {len(missing_historic)}\")\r\nprint(f\"[INFO] Missing non-historic codes: {len(missing_nonhistoric)}\")\r\n\r\n# --- Report 1: all missing codes with a basic classification ---\r\nmissing_report = pd.DataFrame({\r\n    \"code\": missing_ne,\r\n    \"is_historic\": [c in HISTORIC_TO_MODERN for c in missing_ne],\r\n    \"historic_maps_to\": [HISTORIC_TO_MODERN.get(c, \"\") for c in missing_ne],\r\n    \"mapped_target_in_natural_earth\": [\r\n        (HISTORIC_TO_MODERN.get(c, \"\") in ne_codes) if c in HISTORIC_TO_MODERN else \"\"\r\n        for c in missing_ne\r\n    ],\r\n})\r\nmissing_report_path = GEO_DATA / \"audit_missing_codes.csv\"\r\nmissing_report.to_csv(missing_report_path, index=False)\r\nprint(f\"[INFO] Wrote: {missing_report_path}\")\r\n\r\n# --- Report 2: deep dive only for historic jurisdictions ---\r\nhist_rows = []\r\nfor h in HISTORIC_TO_MODERN.keys():\r\n    target = HISTORIC_TO_MODERN[h]\r\n    hist_rows.append({\r\n        \"historic_code\": h,\r\n        \"historic_in_your_codes\": (h in your_codes),\r\n        \"historic_in_natural_earth\": (h in ne_codes),\r\n        \"maps_to\": target,\r\n        \"target_in_your_codes\": (target in your_codes),\r\n        \"target_in_natural_earth\": (target in ne_codes),\r\n        \"status\": (\r\n            \"OK_direct\" if (h in your_codes and h not in ne_codes and target in ne_codes) else\r\n            \"NE_has_historic\" if (h in ne_codes) else\r\n            \"needs_extra_rule\" if (h in your_codes and h not in ne_codes and target not in ne_codes) else\r\n            \"not_used\"  # historic mapping exists but code not in your codes.csv\r\n        )\r\n    })\r\n\r\nhistoric_report = pd.DataFrame(hist_rows).sort_values([\"status\", \"historic_code\"]).reset_index(drop=True)\r\nhistoric_report_path = GEO_DATA / \"audit_historic_mapping_report.csv\"\r\nhistoric_report.to_csv(historic_report_path, index=False)\r\nprint(f\"[INFO] Wrote: {historic_report_path}\")\r\n\r\n# --- Console summary for the key risk you highlighted ---\r\nneeds_extra = historic_report[historic_report[\"status\"] == \"needs_extra_rule\"]\r\nok_direct = historic_report[historic_report[\"status\"] == \"OK_direct\"]\r\nnot_used = historic_report[historic_report[\"status\"] == \"not_used\"]\r\n\r\nprint(\"\\n[INFO] Historic mapping summary\")\r\nprint(f\"  OK_direct (historic missing, target present in NE): {len(ok_direct)}\")\r\nprint(f\"  needs_extra_rule (historic missing, target also missing in NE): {len(needs_extra)}\")\r\nprint(f\"  not_used (historic code not in your codes.csv): {len(not_used)}\")\r\n\r\nif len(needs_extra) > 0:\r\n    print(\"\\n--- Historic mappings that NEED extra rule (target not in NE) ---\")\r\n    print(needs_extra[[\"historic_code\", \"maps_to\", \"target_in_natural_earth\"]].to_string(index=False))\r\n\r\nprint(\"\\n--- Missing historic codes (present in codes.csv but absent in NE) ---\")\r\nprint(missing_historic)\r\n\r\nprint(\"\\n--- Missing non-historic codes (present in codes.csv but absent in NE) ---\")\r\nprint(missing_nonhistoric)\r\n\r\n\r\n===== CELL 27 =====\r\n# Cell 6 — Resolve historic / missing codes, prefer explicit coords, chaining-safe, + audit reports\r\n# POLICY:\r\n#   1) Use Natural Earth centroid if available\r\n#   2) Else use MANUAL_CENTROIDS if provided (preferred over parent-sovereign fallback)\r\n#   3) Else resolve via HISTORIC_TO_MODERN (and repeat)\r\n#   4) Else resolve via TERRITORY_TO_SOVEREIGN (and repeat)\r\n#   5) Else unresolved\r\n#\r\n# Audit outputs in GEO_DATA:\r\n#   - audit_natural_earth_codes_present.csv\r\n#   - audit_missing_codes_from_natural_earth.csv\r\n#   - audit_historic_mapping_report.csv\r\n#   - audit_resolution_methods.csv        <-- key: shows which method used per code\r\n#   - audit_unresolved_codes.csv          (only if unresolved remain)\r\n\r\nlog(\"Resolving centroids for full country universe + writing audit outputs …\")\r\n\r\n# --- Territory → sovereign fallback mapping (use only if no explicit coords available) ---\r\nTERRITORY_TO_SOVEREIGN = {\r\n    # Netherlands\r\n    \"ABW\": \"NLD\", \"BES\": \"NLD\", \"CUW\": \"NLD\", \"SXM\": \"NLD\",\r\n\r\n    # United Kingdom\r\n    \"AIA\": \"GBR\", \"BMU\": \"GBR\", \"CYM\": \"GBR\", \"GIB\": \"GBR\",\r\n    \"MSR\": \"GBR\", \"SHN\": \"GBR\", \"TCA\": \"GBR\", \"VGB\": \"GBR\",\r\n    \"SGS\": \"GBR\", \"IOT\": \"GBR\",\r\n\r\n    # France\r\n    \"BLM\": \"FRA\", \"GLP\": \"FRA\", \"GUF\": \"FRA\", \"MTQ\": \"FRA\",\r\n    \"MYT\": \"FRA\", \"REU\": \"FRA\", \"SPM\": \"FRA\", \"WLF\": \"FRA\",\r\n    \"PYF\": \"FRA\",\r\n\r\n    # United States\r\n    \"ASM\": \"USA\", \"GUM\": \"USA\", \"MNP\": \"USA\", \"VIR\": \"USA\", \"UMI\": \"USA\",\r\n\r\n    # China (customs areas)\r\n    \"HKG\": \"CHN\", \"MAC\": \"CHN\",\r\n\r\n    # Australia / NZ\r\n    \"CCK\": \"AUS\", \"CXR\": \"AUS\", \"NFK\": \"AUS\", \"HMD\": \"AUS\",\r\n    \"COK\": \"NZL\", \"TKL\": \"NZL\",\r\n\r\n    # Denmark\r\n    \"FRO\": \"DNK\",\r\n\r\n    # Norway\r\n    \"BVT\": \"NOR\",\r\n}\r\n\r\n# --- Manual centroids (you can expand this aggressively over time) ---\r\n# Strong recommendation: add territories here if you can. This will override parent-sovereign fallback.\r\n# Format: \"CODE\": (lat, lon)\r\nMANUAL_CENTROIDS = {\r\n    # Sovereign micro-states (examples)\r\n    \"AND\": (42.5462, 1.6016),\r\n    \"ATG\": (17.0608, -61.7964),\r\n    \"BHR\": (26.0667, 50.5577),\r\n    \"BRB\": (13.1939, -59.5432),\r\n    \"COM\": (-11.6455, 43.3333),\r\n    \"CPV\": (15.1111, -23.6167),\r\n    \"DMA\": (15.4150, -61.3710),\r\n    \"FSM\": (6.9248, 158.1620),\r\n    \"GRD\": (12.1165, -61.6790),\r\n    \"KIR\": (1.4518, 173.0329),\r\n    \"KNA\": (17.3578, -62.7830),\r\n    \"LCA\": (13.9094, -60.9789),\r\n    \"MDV\": (3.2028, 73.2207),\r\n    \"MHL\": (7.1315, 171.1845),\r\n    \"MLT\": (35.9375, 14.3754),\r\n    \"MUS\": (-20.3484, 57.5522),\r\n    \"NIU\": (-19.0544, -169.8672),\r\n    \"NRU\": (-0.5228, 166.9315),\r\n    \"PCN\": (-24.3768, -128.3242),\r\n    \"PLW\": (7.5150, 134.5825),\r\n    \"SGP\": (1.3521, 103.8198),\r\n    \"SMR\": (43.9424, 12.4578),\r\n    \"STP\": (0.1864, 6.6131),\r\n    \"SYC\": (-4.6796, 55.4920),\r\n    \"TON\": (-21.1790, -175.1982),\r\n    \"TUV\": (-7.1095, 177.6493),\r\n    \"VAT\": (41.9029, 12.4534),\r\n    \"VCT\": (13.2528, -61.1971),\r\n    \"WSM\": (-13.7590, -172.1046),\r\n    \r\n\"ABW\": (12.5211, -69.9683),   # Aruba (Oranjestad)\r\n\"BES\": (12.1784, -68.2385),   # Caribbean Netherlands (Bonaire)\r\n\"CUW\": (12.1696, -68.9900),   # Curaçao (Willemstad)\r\n\"SXM\": (18.0425, -63.0548),   # Sint Maarten (Philipsburg)\r\n\r\n\"AIA\": (18.2206, -63.0686),   # Anguilla (The Valley)\r\n\"BMU\": (32.2949, -64.7814),   # Bermuda (Hamilton)\r\n\"CYM\": (19.3133, -81.2546),   # Cayman Islands (George Town)\r\n\"GIB\": (36.1408, -5.3536),    # Gibraltar\r\n\"MSR\": (16.7425, -62.1874),   # Montserrat (Brades)\r\n\"SHN\": (-15.9650, -5.7089),   # Saint Helena (Jamestown)\r\n\"TCA\": (21.6940, -71.7979),   # Turks & Caicos (Cockburn Town)\r\n\"VGB\": (18.4286, -64.6185),   # British Virgin Islands (Road Town)\r\n\"SGS\": (-54.4296, -36.5879),  # South Georgia (King Edward Point)\r\n\"IOT\": (-7.3340, 72.4240),    # British Indian Ocean Territory (Diego Garcia)\r\n\r\n\"BLM\": (17.8962, -62.8498),   # Saint Barthélemy (Gustavia)\r\n\"GLP\": (16.2650, -61.5510),   # Guadeloupe (Basse-Terre)\r\n\"GUF\": (4.9372, -52.3260),    # French Guiana (Cayenne)\r\n\"MTQ\": (14.6161, -61.0588),   # Martinique (Fort-de-France)\r\n\"MYT\": (-12.8275, 45.1662),   # Mayotte (Mamoudzou)\r\n\"REU\": (-20.8789, 55.4481),   # Réunion (Saint-Denis)\r\n\"SPM\": (46.7770, -56.1773),   # Saint Pierre & Miquelon\r\n\"WLF\": (-13.2825, -176.1760), # Wallis & Futuna (Mata-Utu)\r\n\"PYF\": (-17.5516, -149.5585), # French Polynesia (Papeete)\r\n\r\n\"ASM\": (-14.2710, -170.1322), # American Samoa (Pago Pago)\r\n\"GUM\": (13.4443, 144.7937),   # Guam (Hagåtña)\r\n\"MNP\": (15.2123, 145.7545),   # Northern Mariana Islands (Saipan)\r\n\"VIR\": (18.3358, -64.8963),   # U.S. Virgin Islands (Charlotte Amalie)\r\n\"UMI\": (19.2823, 166.6470),   # U.S. Minor Outlying Islands (Wake Island ref.)\r\n\r\n\"HKG\": (22.3193, 114.1694),   # Hong Kong\r\n\"MAC\": (22.1987, 113.5439),   # Macau\r\n\r\n\"CCK\": (-12.1642, 96.8710),   # Cocos (Keeling) Islands\r\n\"CXR\": (-10.4475, 105.6904),  # Christmas Island\r\n\"NFK\": (-29.0333, 167.9500),  # Norfolk Island\r\n\"HMD\": (-53.0818, 73.5042),   # Heard & McDonald Islands (uninhabited ref.)\r\n\"COK\": (-21.2367, -159.7777), # Cook Islands (Avarua)\r\n\"TKL\": (-9.2002, -171.8484),  # Tokelau (Atafu ref.)\r\n\r\n\"FRO\": (62.0079, -6.7900),    # Faroe Islands (Tórshavn)\r\n\r\n\"BVT\": (-54.4208, 3.3464),    # Bouvet Island (uninhabited ref.)\r\n \r\n\r\n}\r\n\r\n# -------------------------\r\n# Coverage diagnostics (NE vs your codes.csv)\r\n# -------------------------\r\nne_codes = set(centroids_modern[\"code\"].astype(str))\r\nyour_codes = set(pd.Series(codes).astype(str))\r\nmissing_ne = sorted(your_codes - ne_codes)\r\n\r\nmissing_historic = sorted([c for c in missing_ne if c in HISTORIC_TO_MODERN])\r\nmissing_nonhistoric = sorted([c for c in missing_ne if c not in HISTORIC_TO_MODERN])\r\n\r\nlog(f\"Natural Earth codes available: {len(ne_codes)}\")\r\nlog(f\"Your code universe (codes.csv): {len(your_codes)}\")\r\nlog(f\"Missing from Natural Earth: {len(missing_ne)}\")\r\nlog(f\"Missing historic jurisdictions: {len(missing_historic)}\")\r\nlog(f\"Missing non-historic codes: {len(missing_nonhistoric)}\")\r\n\r\n# audit: NE present codes\r\nne_present_path = GEO_DATA / \"audit_natural_earth_codes_present.csv\"\r\ncentroids_modern[[\"code\", \"name\"]].drop_duplicates().sort_values(\"code\").to_csv(ne_present_path, index=False)\r\nlog(f\"Wrote: {ne_present_path}\")\r\n\r\n# audit: missing codes classification\r\nmissing_audit_path = GEO_DATA / \"audit_missing_codes_from_natural_earth.csv\"\r\npd.DataFrame({\r\n    \"code\": missing_ne,\r\n    \"is_historic\": [c in HISTORIC_TO_MODERN for c in missing_ne],\r\n    \"historic_maps_to\": [HISTORIC_TO_MODERN.get(c, \"\") for c in missing_ne],\r\n    \"historic_target_in_ne\": [(HISTORIC_TO_MODERN.get(c, \"\") in ne_codes) if c in HISTORIC_TO_MODERN else \"\" for c in missing_ne],\r\n    \"is_territory_fallback\": [c in TERRITORY_TO_SOVEREIGN for c in missing_ne],\r\n    \"territory_maps_to\": [TERRITORY_TO_SOVEREIGN.get(c, \"\") for c in missing_ne],\r\n    \"territory_target_in_ne\": [(TERRITORY_TO_SOVEREIGN.get(c, \"\") in ne_codes) if c in TERRITORY_TO_SOVEREIGN else \"\" for c in missing_ne],\r\n    \"has_manual_centroid\": [c in MANUAL_CENTROIDS for c in missing_ne],\r\n}).to_csv(missing_audit_path, index=False)\r\nlog(f\"Wrote: {missing_audit_path}\")\r\n\r\n# audit: historic viability\r\nhist_rows = []\r\nfor h, target in HISTORIC_TO_MODERN.items():\r\n    hist_rows.append({\r\n        \"historic_code\": h,\r\n        \"historic_in_your_codes\": (h in your_codes),\r\n        \"historic_in_ne\": (h in ne_codes),\r\n        \"maps_to\": target,\r\n        \"target_in_ne\": (target in ne_codes),\r\n        \"target_has_manual\": (target in MANUAL_CENTROIDS),\r\n        \"target_is_territory_fallback\": (target in TERRITORY_TO_SOVEREIGN),\r\n    })\r\nhistoric_report = pd.DataFrame(hist_rows)\r\nhistoric_report_path = GEO_DATA / \"audit_historic_mapping_report.csv\"\r\nhistoric_report.to_csv(historic_report_path, index=False)\r\nlog(f\"Wrote: {historic_report_path}\")\r\n\r\n# -------------------------\r\n# Resolver: prefer MANUAL over territory fallback, supports chaining\r\n# -------------------------\r\nc_index = centroids_modern.set_index(\"code\")[[\"lat\", \"lon\", \"name\"]]\r\n\r\ndef resolve_to_centroid(code: str, max_hops: int = 8):\r\n    \"\"\"\r\n    Returns dict: lat, lon, source_code, method, path\r\n    method in {NE, MANUAL, HIST_CHAIN, TERR_CHAIN}\r\n    \"\"\"\r\n    seen = []\r\n    cur = code\r\n    used_hist = False\r\n    used_terr = False\r\n\r\n    for _ in range(max_hops):\r\n        seen.append(cur)\r\n\r\n        if cur in c_index.index:\r\n            method = \"NE\"\r\n            if used_hist and not used_terr:\r\n                method = \"HIST_CHAIN\"\r\n            elif used_terr and not used_hist:\r\n                method = \"TERR_CHAIN\"\r\n            elif used_hist and used_terr:\r\n                method = \"HIST+TERR_CHAIN\"\r\n            return {\r\n                \"lat\": float(c_index.loc[cur, \"lat\"]),\r\n                \"lon\": float(c_index.loc[cur, \"lon\"]),\r\n                \"source_code\": cur,\r\n                \"method\": method,\r\n                \"path\": \"->\".join(seen),\r\n            }\r\n\r\n        # prefer explicit manual coordinates before any further mapping\r\n        if cur in MANUAL_CENTROIDS:\r\n            lat, lon = MANUAL_CENTROIDS[cur]\r\n            method = \"MANUAL\"\r\n            if used_hist and not used_terr:\r\n                method = \"HIST+MANUAL\"\r\n            elif used_terr and not used_hist:\r\n                method = \"TERR+MANUAL\"\r\n            elif used_hist and used_terr:\r\n                method = \"HIST+TERR+MANUAL\"\r\n            return {\r\n                \"lat\": float(lat),\r\n                \"lon\": float(lon),\r\n                \"source_code\": \"MANUAL\",\r\n                \"method\": method,\r\n                \"path\": \"->\".join(seen),\r\n            }\r\n\r\n        if cur in HISTORIC_TO_MODERN:\r\n            used_hist = True\r\n            cur = HISTORIC_TO_MODERN[cur]\r\n            continue\r\n\r\n        if cur in TERRITORY_TO_SOVEREIGN:\r\n            used_terr = True\r\n            cur = TERRITORY_TO_SOVEREIGN[cur]\r\n            continue\r\n\r\n        break\r\n\r\n    return None\r\n\r\n\r\n# -------------------------\r\n# Build rows for codes missing in NE + resolution report\r\n# -------------------------\r\nrows = []\r\nresolution_rows = []\r\nunresolved = []\r\n\r\nfor code in missing_ne:\r\n    resolved = resolve_to_centroid(code)\r\n    if resolved is None:\r\n        unresolved.append(code)\r\n        resolution_rows.append({\r\n            \"code\": code,\r\n            \"resolved\": False,\r\n            \"method\": \"\",\r\n            \"path\": \"\",\r\n            \"source_code\": \"\",\r\n        })\r\n        continue\r\n\r\n    log(f\"Resolved {code} via {resolved['path']} ({resolved['method']})\")\r\n\r\n    rows.append({\r\n        \"code\": code,\r\n        \"lat\": resolved[\"lat\"],\r\n        \"lon\": resolved[\"lon\"],\r\n        \"name\": f\"{resolved['method']}:{resolved['path']}\",\r\n        \"assigned_from\": resolved[\"source_code\"],\r\n        \"resolution_path\": resolved[\"path\"],\r\n        \"resolution_method\": resolved[\"method\"],\r\n    })\r\n\r\n    resolution_rows.append({\r\n        \"code\": code,\r\n        \"resolved\": True,\r\n        \"method\": resolved[\"method\"],\r\n        \"path\": resolved[\"path\"],\r\n        \"source_code\": resolved[\"source_code\"],\r\n    })\r\n\r\n# Write resolution methods report (key to improving manual coords later)\r\nresolution_report_path = GEO_DATA / \"audit_resolution_methods.csv\"\r\npd.DataFrame(resolution_rows).sort_values([\"resolved\", \"code\"]).to_csv(resolution_report_path, index=False)\r\nlog(f\"Wrote: {resolution_report_path}\")\r\n\r\n# If unresolved remain, write and fail\r\nif unresolved:\r\n    unresolved_path = GEO_DATA / \"audit_unresolved_codes.csv\"\r\n    pd.DataFrame({\"code\": unresolved}).to_csv(unresolved_path, index=False)\r\n    log(f\"Wrote: {unresolved_path}\")\r\n    raise ValueError(\"Unresolved codes remain after resolver: \" + \", \".join(unresolved))\r\n\r\n# -------------------------\r\n# Final centroids table (must match codes.csv)\r\n# -------------------------\r\ncentroids = pd.concat([centroids_modern, pd.DataFrame(rows)], ignore_index=True)\r\n\r\ncentroids = (\r\n    centroids[centroids[\"code\"].isin(codes)]\r\n    .drop_duplicates(subset=[\"code\"])\r\n    .sort_values(\"code\")\r\n    .reset_index(drop=True)\r\n)\r\n\r\nlog(f\"Final centroid table: {len(centroids)} entries (expected {len(your_codes)}).\")\r\n\r\nif len(centroids) != len(your_codes):\r\n    have = set(centroids[\"code\"].astype(str))\r\n    missing_final = sorted(your_codes - have)\r\n    missing_final_path = GEO_DATA / \"audit_missing_from_final_centroids.csv\"\r\n    pd.DataFrame({\"code\": missing_final}).to_csv(missing_final_path, index=False)\r\n    log(f\"Wrote: {missing_final_path}\")\r\n    raise ValueError(f\"Final centroid table incomplete. Missing {len(missing_final)} codes.\")\r\n\r\ncentroids.tail()\r\n\r\n\r\n===== CELL 29 =====\r\nlog(\"Saving centroid file …\")\r\ncentroids.to_csv(CENTROIDS_OUT, index=False)\r\nCENTROIDS_OUT\r\n\r\n\r\n===== CELL 31 =====\r\nlog(\"Computing OD distance matrix …\")\r\n\r\nids = centroids[\"code\"].values\r\nlat = centroids[\"lat\"].values\r\nlon = centroids[\"lon\"].values\r\n\r\ndist = haversine_km(lat[:, None], lon[:, None], lat[None, :], lon[None, :])\r\n\r\nod = pd.DataFrame(dist, index=ids, columns=ids)\r\nod.index.name = \"origin\"\r\n\r\nod_long = (\r\n    od.stack()\r\n      .rename(\"distance_km\")\r\n      .reset_index()\r\n      .rename(columns={\"level_0\": \"origin\", \"level_1\": \"destination\"})\r\n)\r\n\r\nod_long = od_long[od_long[\"origin\"] != od_long[\"destination\"]].reset_index(drop=True)\r\n\r\nlog(f\"OD rows: {len(od_long)} (expected {len(ids)*(len(ids)-1)})\")\r\nod_long.head()\r\n\r\n\r\n===== CELL 33 =====\r\nlog(\"Saving OD matrix …\")\r\nod_long.to_csv(OD_OUT, index=False)\r\nOD_OUT\r\n\r\n\r\n===== CELL 35 =====\r\nimport pandas as pd\r\nimport geopandas as gpd\r\nimport matplotlib.pyplot as plt\r\nfrom pathlib import Path\r\n\r\n# -------------------------------------------------\r\n# Paths\r\n# -------------------------------------------------\r\n# Data and figure directories for Stage 01\r\n\r\nCENTROID_CANDIDATES = [\r\n    GEO_DATA / \"country_centroids_augmented.csv\",\r\n    GEO_DATA / \"country_centroids.csv\",\r\n    GEO_DATA / \"centroids.csv\",\r\n]\r\n\r\ncentroids_path = next((p for p in CENTROID_CANDIDATES if p.exists()), None)\r\nif centroids_path is None:\r\n    raise FileNotFoundError(\"Centroids CSV not found in working directory.\")\r\n\r\nprint(\"[INFO] Using centroids file:\", centroids_path.name)\r\ncentroids = pd.read_csv(centroids_path)\r\n\r\n# NE_SHP is already set by the geo config cell (Cell 11) — use it directly\r\n# NE_SHP = NE_SHP  (already available from config)\r\nif not NE_SHP.exists():\r\n    raise FileNotFoundError(\"Natural Earth shapefile not found.\")\r\n\r\n# -------------------------------------------------\r\n# Load basemap and drop Antarctica\r\n# -------------------------------------------------\r\nworld = gpd.read_file(NE_SHP)\r\nworld = world[world[\"ISO_A3\"] != \"ATA\"].copy()\r\n\r\nif world.crs is None:\r\n    world = world.set_crs(\"EPSG:4326\")\r\nelse:\r\n    world = world.to_crs(\"EPSG:4326\")\r\n\r\n# -------------------------------------------------\r\n# Build centroid GeoDataFrame\r\n# -------------------------------------------------\r\ng_cent = gpd.GeoDataFrame(\r\n    centroids,\r\n    geometry=gpd.points_from_xy(centroids[\"lon\"], centroids[\"lat\"]),\r\n    crs=\"EPSG:4326\",\r\n)\r\n\r\n# -------------------------------------------------\r\n# Project to Web Mercator\r\n# -------------------------------------------------\r\nworld_m = world.to_crs(epsg=3857)\r\ng_cent_m = g_cent.to_crs(epsg=3857)\r\n\r\n# -------------------------------------------------\r\n# Identify \"problematic\" centroids\r\n# -------------------------------------------------\r\niso = world[\"ISO_A3\"].astype(str)\r\nadm = world[\"ADM0_A3\"].astype(str)\r\nne_code = iso.where(iso != \"-99\", adm)\r\nne_codes = set(ne_code.unique())\r\n\r\nhas_resolution_method = \"resolution_method\" in g_cent.columns\r\nhas_assigned_from = \"assigned_from\" in g_cent.columns\r\n\r\ndef is_problematic(row):\r\n    code = str(row[\"code\"])\r\n    if code not in ne_codes:\r\n        return True\r\n    if has_resolution_method and pd.notna(row.get(\"resolution_method\", None)):\r\n        return True\r\n    if has_assigned_from and str(row.get(\"assigned_from\", \"\")).upper() == \"MANUAL\":\r\n        return True\r\n    return False\r\n\r\ng_cent_m[\"problematic\"] = g_cent_m.apply(is_problematic, axis=1)\r\ng_ok = g_cent_m[~g_cent_m[\"problematic\"]].copy()\r\ng_bad = g_cent_m[g_cent_m[\"problematic\"]].copy()\r\n\r\nprint(f\"[INFO] Total centroids: {len(g_cent_m)}\")\r\nprint(f\"[INFO] No-problem centroids: {len(g_ok)}\")\r\nprint(f\"[INFO] Problematic centroids: {len(g_bad)}\")\r\n\r\n# -------------------------------------------------\r\n# Plot + save helper\r\n# -------------------------------------------------\r\ndef plot_and_save(title, points, filename):\r\n    fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(16, 9))\r\n    world_m.plot(ax=ax, linewidth=0.4)\r\n    points.plot(\r\n        ax=ax,\r\n        color=\"red\",\r\n        markersize=12,\r\n        alpha=0.8\r\n    )\r\n    ax.set_title(title)\r\n    ax.set_axis_off()\r\n\r\n    out_path = GEO_FIG / filename\r\n    plt.savefig(out_path, dpi=300, bbox_inches=\"tight\")\r\n    plt.show()\r\n    plt.close(fig)\r\n\r\n    print(f\"[SAVED] {out_path}\")\r\n\r\n# -------------------------------------------------\r\n# 1) All centroids\r\n# -------------------------------------------------\r\nplot_and_save(\r\n    \"All centroids (Mercator projection)\",\r\n    g_cent_m,\r\n    \"map_all_centroids.png\"\r\n)\r\n\r\n# -------------------------------------------------\r\n# 2) No-problem centroids\r\n# -------------------------------------------------\r\nplot_and_save(\r\n    \"Centroids found directly in Natural Earth (no problem)\",\r\n    g_ok,\r\n    \"map_no_problem_centroids.png\"\r\n)\r\n\r\n# -------------------------------------------------\r\n# 3) Problematic centroids\r\n# -------------------------------------------------\r\nplot_and_save(\r\n    \"Problematic centroids (manual / chained / not in NE)\",\r\n    g_bad,\r\n    \"map_problematic_centroids.png\"\r\n)\r\n\r\n\r\n"}}

3.3464),\n+}\n+\n+\n+@dataclass(frozen=True)\n+class GeoArtifacts:\n+    stage_dir: Path\n+    data_dir: Path\n+    fig_dir: Path\n+    centroids_path: Path\n+    od_matrix_path: Path\n+    natural_earth_present_path: Path\n+    natural_earth_missing_path: Path\n+    historic_mapping_report_path: Path\n+    resolution_methods_path: Path\n+    missing_codes_path: Path\n+\n+\n+def build_geo_artifact_paths(stage_dir: Path) -> GeoArtifacts:\n+    data_dir = stage_dir / \"data\"\n+    fig_dir = stage_dir / \"fig\"\n+    return GeoArtifacts(\n+        stage_dir=stage_dir,\n+        data_dir=data_dir,\n+        fig_dir=fig_dir,\n+        centroids_path=data_dir / \"country_centroids_augmented.csv\",\n+        od_matrix_path=data_dir / \"OD_Matrix.csv\",\n+        natural_earth_present_path=data_dir / \"audit_natural_earth_codes_present.csv\",\n+        natural_earth_missing_path=data_dir / \"audit_missing_codes_from_natural_earth.csv\",\n+        historic_mapping_report_path=data_dir / \"audit_historic_mapping_report.csv\",\n+        resolution_methods_path=data_dir / \"audit_resolution_methods.csv\",\n+        missing_codes_path=data_dir / \"audit_missing_codes.csv\",\n+    )\n+\n+\n+def load_country_codes(reference_dir: Path) -> list[str]:\n+    codes_path = reference_dir / \"codes.csv\"\n+    codes_df = pd.read_csv(codes_path)\n+    if \"code\" not in codes_df.columns:\n+        raise ValueError(\"codes.csv must have a column named 'code'\")\n+    return sorted(\n+        codes_df[\"code\"].dropna().astype(str).str.strip().loc[lambda s: s != \"\"].unique().tolist()\n+    )\n+\n+\n+def ensure_natural_earth(config: ProjectConfig, logger) -> Path:\n+    zip_path = config.natural_earth_zip_path\n+    shp_path = config.natural_earth_shapefile_path\n+    if shp_path.exists():\n+        return shp_path\n+\n+    zip_path.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)\n+    config.natural_earth_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)\n+\n+    if not zip_path.exists():\n+        logger.info(\"Downloading Natural Earth boundaries to %s\", zip_path)\n+        response = requests.get(config.natural_earth_url, stream=True, timeout=60)\n+        response.raise_for_status()\n+        with open(zip_path, \"wb\") as handle:\n+            for chunk in response.iter_content(chunk_size=1024 * 1024):\n+                if chunk:\n+                    handle.write(chunk)\n+\n+    with zipfile.ZipFile(zip_path, \"r\") as zf:\n+        zf.extractall(config.natural_earth_dir)\n+\n+    if not shp_path.exists():\n+        raise FileNotFoundError(f\"Natural Earth shapefile not found at {shp_path}\")\n+    return shp_path\n+\n+\n+def load_modern_centroids(shapefile_path: Path) -> tuple[gpd.GeoDataFrame, pd.DataFrame]:\n+    world = gpd.read_file(shapefile_path).to_crs(epsg=4326)\n+    iso = world[\"ISO_A3\"].astype(str).values\n+    adm = world[\"ADM0_A3\"].astype(str).values\n+    code = np.where(iso != \"-99\", iso, adm)\n+    world = world.assign(code=code)\n+    pts = world.geometry.representative_point()\n+    centroids_modern = pd.DataFrame(\n+        {\n+            \"code\": world[\"code\"].astype(str).values,\n+            \"lat\": pts.y.values,\n+            \"lon\": pts.x.values,\n+            \"name\": world.get(\"ADMIN\", \"\"),\n+            \"assigned_from\": \"\",\n+        }\n+    )\n+    centroids_modern = (\n+        centroids_modern.dropna(subset=[\"code\", \"lat\", \"lon\"])\n+        .drop_duplicates(subset=[\"code\"])\n+        .reset_index(drop=True)\n+    )\n+    return world, centroids_modern\n+\n+\n+def write_geo_audits(\n+    codes: list[str], centroids_modern: pd.DataFrame, artifacts: GeoArtifacts, logger\n+) -> list[str]:\n+    ne_codes = set(centroids_modern[\"code\"].astype(str))\n+    your_codes = set(codes)\n+    missing_ne = sorted(your_codes - ne_codes)\n+\n+    centroids_modern[[\"code\", \"name\"]].drop_duplicates().sort_values(\"code\").to_csv(\n+        artifacts.natural_earth_present_path, index=False\n+    )\n+    pd.DataFrame(\n+        {\n+            \"code\": missing_ne,\n+            \"is_historic\": [c in HISTORIC_TO_MODERN for c in missing_ne],\n+            \"historic_maps_to\": [HISTORIC_TO_MODERN.get(c, \"\") for c in missing_ne],\n+            \"historic_target_in_ne\": [\n+                (HISTORIC_TO_MODERN.get(c, \"\") in ne_codes) if c in HISTORIC_TO_MODERN else \"\"\n+                for c in missing_ne\n+            ],\n+            \"is_territory_fallback\": [c in TERRITORY_TO_SOVEREIGN for c in missing_ne],\n+            \"territory_maps_to\": [TERRITORY_TO_SOVEREIGN.get(c, \"\") for c in missing_ne],\n+            \"territory_target_in_ne\": [\n+                (TERRITORY_TO_SOVEREIGN.get(c, \"\") in ne_codes)\n+                if c in TERRITORY_TO_SOVEREIGN\n+                else \"\"\n+                for c in missing_ne\n+            ],\n+            \"has_manual_centroid\": [c in MANUAL_CENTROIDS for c in missing_ne],\n+        }\n+    ).to_csv(artifacts.natural_earth_missing_path, index=False)\n+    pd.DataFrame(\n+        {\n+            \"code\": missing_ne,\n+            \"is_historic\": [c in HISTORIC_TO_MODERN for c in missing_ne],\n+            \"historic_maps_to\": [HISTORIC_TO_MODERN.get(c, \"\") for c in missing_ne],\n+            \"mapped_target_in_natural_earth\": [\n+                (HISTORIC_TO_MODERN.get(c, \"\") in ne_codes) if c in HISTORIC_TO_MODERN else \"\"\n+                for c in missing_ne\n+            ],\n+        }\n+    ).to_csv(artifacts.missing_codes_path, index=False)\n+    hist_rows = []\n+    for historic_code, target in HISTORIC_TO_MODERN.items():\n+        hist_rows.append(\n+            {\n+                \"historic_code\": historic_code,\n+                \"historic_in_your_codes\": historic_code in your_codes,\n+                \"historic_in_ne\": historic_code in ne_codes,\n+                \"maps_to\": target,\n+                \"target_in_ne\": target in ne_codes,\n+                \"target_has_manual\": target in MANUAL_CENTROIDS,\n+                \"target_is_territory_fallback\": target in TERRITORY_TO_SOVEREIGN,\n+            }\n+        )\n+    pd.DataFrame(hist_rows).to_csv(artifacts.historic_mapping_report_path, index=False)\n+    logger.info(\"Stage 01 audits written to %s\", artifacts.data_dir)\n+    return missing_ne\n+\n+\n+def resolve_missing_centroids(\n+    codes: list[str], centroids_modern: pd.DataFrame, artifacts: GeoArtifacts, logger\n+) -> pd.DataFrame:\n+    ne_codes = set(centroids_modern[\"code\"].astype(str))\n+    missing_ne = sorted(set(codes) - ne_codes)\n+    c_index = centroids_modern.set_index(\"code\")[[\"lat\", \"lon\", \"name\"]]\n+\n+    def resolve_to_centroid(code: str, max_hops: int = 8):\n+        seen = []\n+        cur = code\n+        used_hist = False\n+        used_terr = False\n+        for _ in range(max_hops):\n+            seen.append(cur)\n+            if cur in c_index.index:\n+                method = \"NE\"\n+                if used_hist and not used_terr:\n+                    method = \"HIST_CHAIN\"\n+                elif used_terr and not used_hist:\n+                    method = \"TERR_CHAIN\"\n+                elif used_hist and used_terr:\n+                    method = \"HIST+TERR_CHAIN\"\n+                return {\n+                    \"lat\": float(c_index.loc[cur, \"lat\"]),\n+                    \"lon\": float(c_index.loc[cur, \"lon\"]),\n+                    \"source_code\": cur,\n+                    \"method\": method,\n+                    \"path\": \"->\".join(seen),\n+                }\n+            if cur in MANUAL_CENTROIDS:\n+                lat, lon = MANUAL_CENTROIDS[cur]\n+                method = \"MANUAL\"\n+                if used_hist and not used_terr:\n+                    method = \"HIST+MANUAL\"\n+                elif used_terr and not used_hist:\n+                    method = \"TERR+MANUAL\"\n+                elif used_hist and used_terr:\n+                    method = \"HIST+TERR+MANUAL\"\n+                return {\n+                    \"lat\": float(lat),\n+                    \"lon\": float(lon),\n+                    \"source_code\": \"MANUAL\",\n+                    \"method\": method,\n+                    \"path\": \"->\".join(seen),\n+                }\n+            if cur in HISTORIC_TO_MODERN:\n+                used_hist = True\n+                cur = HISTORIC_TO_MODERN[cur]\n+                continue\n+            if cur in TERRITORY_TO_SOVEREIGN:\n+                used_terr = True\n+                cur = TERRITORY_TO_SOVEREIGN[cur]\n+                continue\n+            break\n+        return None\n+\n+    rows = []\n+    resolution_rows = []\n+    unresolved = []\n+    for code in missing_ne:\n+        resolved = resolve_to_centroid(code)\n+        if resolved is None:\n+            unresolved.append(code)\n+            resolution_rows.append(\n+                {\"code\": code, \"resolved\": False, \"method\": \"\", \"path\": \"\", \"source_code\": \"\"}\n+            )\n+            continue\n+        rows.append(\n+            {\n+                \"code\": code,\n+                \"lat\": resolved[\"lat\"],\n+                \"lon\": resolved[\"lon\"],\n+                \"name\": f\"{resolved['method']}:{resolved['path']}\",\n+                \"assigned_from\": resolved[\"source_code\"],\n+                \"resolution_path\": resolved[\"path\"],\n+                \"resolution_method\": resolved[\"method\"],\n+            }\n+        )\n+        resolution_rows.append(\n+            {\n+                \"code\": code,\n+                \"resolved\": True,\n+                \"method\": resolved[\"method\"],\n+                \"path\": resolved[\"path\"],\n+                \"source_code\": resolved[\"source_code\"],\n+            }\n+        )\n+    pd.DataFrame(resolution_rows).sort_values([\"resolved\", \"code\"]).to_csv(\n+        artifacts.resolution_methods_path, index=False\n+    )\n+    if unresolved:\n+        unresolved_path = artifacts.data_dir / \"audit_unresolved_codes.csv\"\n+        pd.DataFrame({\"code\": unresolved}).to_csv(unresolved_path, index=False)\n+        raise ValueError(f\"Unresolved codes remain after resolver: {', '.join(unresolved)}\")\n+\n+    centroids = pd.concat([centroids_modern, pd.DataFrame(rows)], ignore_index=True)\n+    centroids = (\n+        centroids[centroids[\"code\"].isin(codes)]\n+        .drop_duplicates(subset=[\"code\"])\n+        .sort_values(\"code\")\n+        .reset_index(drop=True)\n+    )\n+    if len(centroids) != len(set(codes)):\n+        have = set(centroids[\"code\"].astype(str))\n+        missing_final = sorted(set(codes) - have)\n+        missing_final_path = artifacts.data_dir / \"audit_missing_from_final_centroids.csv\"\n+        pd.DataFrame({\"code\": missing_final}).to_csv(missing_final_path, index=False)\n+        raise ValueError(f\"Final centroid table incomplete. Missing {len(missing_final)} codes.\")\n+    return centroids\n+\n+\n+def haversine_km(lat1, lon1, lat2, lon2):\n+    radius = 6371.0088\n+    lat1 = np.radians(lat1)\n+    lon1 = np.radians(lon1)\n+    lat2 = np.radians(lat2)\n+    lon2 = np.radians(lon2)\n+    dlat = lat2 - lat1\n+    dlon = lon2 - lon1\n+    a = np.sin(dlat / 2) ** 2 + np.cos(lat1) * np.cos(lat2) * np.sin(dlon / 2) ** 2\n+    return radius * (2 * np.arcsin(np.sqrt(a)))\n+\n+\n+def build_od_matrix(centroids: pd.DataFrame) -> pd.DataFrame:\n+    ids = centroids[\"code\"].values\n+    lat = centroids[\"lat\"].values\n+    lon = centroids[\"lon\"].values\n+    dist = haversine_km(lat[:, None], lon[:, None], lat[None, :], lon[None, :])\n+    od = pd.DataFrame(dist, index=ids, columns=ids)\n+    od.index.name = \"origin\"\n+    od_long = (\n+        od.stack()\n+        .rename(\"distance_km\")\n+        .reset_index()\n+        .rename(columns={\"level_0\": \"origin\", \"level_1\": \"destination\"})\n+    )\n+    return od_long[od_long[\"origin\"] != od_long[\"destination\"]].reset_index(drop=True)\n+\n+\n+def plot_centroid_diagnostics(\n+    centroids: pd.DataFrame, shapefile_path: Path, artifacts: GeoArtifacts\n+) -> None:\n+    import matplotlib.pyplot as plt\n+\n+    world = gpd.read_file(shapefile_path)\n+    world = world[world[\"ISO_A3\"] != \"ATA\"].copy()\n+    if world.crs is None:\n+        world = world.set_crs(\"EPSG:4326\")\n+    else:\n+        world = world.to_crs(\"EPSG:4326\")\n+    g_cent = gpd.GeoDataFrame(\n+        centroids, geometry=gpd.points_from_xy(centroids[\"lon\"], centroids[\"lat\"]), crs=\"EPSG:4326\"\n+    )\n+    world_m = world.to_crs(epsg=3857)\n+    g_cent_m = g_cent.to_crs(epsg=3857)\n+    iso = world[\"ISO_A3\"].astype(str)\n+    adm = world[\"ADM0_A3\"].astype(str)\n+    ne_code = iso.where(iso != \"-99\", adm)\n+    ne_codes = set(ne_code.unique())\n+    has_resolution_method = \"resolution_method\" in g_cent.columns\n+    has_assigned_from = \"assigned_from\" in g_cent.columns\n+\n+    def is_problematic(row):\n+        code = str(row[\"code\"])\n+        if code not in ne_codes:\n+            return True\n+        if has_resolution_method and pd.notna(row.get(\"resolution_method\", None)):\n+            return True\n+        if has_assigned_from and str(row.get(\"assigned_from\", \"\")).upper() == \"MANUAL\":\n+            return True\n+        return False\n+\n+    g_cent_m[\"problematic\"] = g_cent_m.apply(is_problematic, axis=1)\n+    groups = {\n+        \"map_all_centroids.png\": g_cent_m,\n+        \"map_no_problem_centroids.png\": g_cent_m[~g_cent_m[\"problematic\"]].copy(),\n+        \"map_problematic_centroids.png\": g_cent_m[g_cent_m[\"problematic\"]].copy(),\n+    }\n+    titles = {\n+        \"map_all_centroids.png\": \"All centroids (Mercator projection)\",\n+        \"map_no_problem_centroids.png\": \"Centroids found directly in Natural Earth (no problem)\",\n+        \"map_problematic_centroids.png\": \"Problematic centroids (manual / chained / not in NE)\",\n+    }\n+    for filename, points in groups.items():\n+        fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(16, 9))\n+        world_m.plot(ax=ax, linewidth=0.4)\n+        points.plot(ax=ax, color=\"red\", markersize=12, alpha=0.8)\n+        ax.set_title(titles[filename])\n+        ax.set_axis_off()\n+        fig.savefig(artifacts.fig_dir / filename, dpi=300, bbox_inches=\"tight\")\n+        plt.close(fig)\n*** Update File: C:\\Python\\Geoanalisis\\src\\geoanalisis\\pipelines\\stage_01_geo.py\n@@\n-\"\"\"Stage 01: geografia, centroides y matriz OD.\"\"\"\n-\n-from geoanalisis.config import ProjectConfig\n-\n-\n-def annotations\n+\n+import logging\n+\n+from geoanalisis.config import ProjectConfig\n+from geoanalisis.services.centroids import (\n+    build_geo_artifact_paths,\n+    build_od_matrix,\n+    ensure_natural_earth,\n+    load_country_codes,\n+    load_modern_centroids,\n+    plot_centroid_diagnostics,\n+    resolve_missing_centroids,\n+    write_geo_audits,\n+)\n+from geoanalisis.utils.run_structure import initialize_project_tree, initialize_run_tree\n+\n+\n+def run(config: ProjectConfig, run_id: str, make_figures: bool = True) -> dict[str, str]:\n+    logger = logging.getLogger(\"geoanalisis.stage_01_geo\")\n+    initialize_project_tree(config)\n+    run_dir = initialize_run_tree(config, run_id)\n+    artifacts = build_geo_artifact_paths(run_dir / \"artifacts\" / \"01_geo\")\n+\n+    logger.info(\"Stage 01 started for dataset %s and run %s\", config.dataset_version, run_id)\n+    codes = load_country_codes(config.dataset_reference_dir)\n+    shapefile_path = ensure_natural_earth(config, logger)\n+    _, centroids_modern = load_modern_centroids(shapefile_path)\n+    write_geo_audits(codes, centroids_modern, artifacts, logger)\n+    centroids = resolve_missing_centroids(codes, centroids_modern, artifacts, logger)\n+    centroids.to_csv(artifacts.centroids_path, index=False)\n+\n+    od_matrix = build_od_matrix(centroids)\n+    od_matrix.to_csv(artifacts.od_matrix_path, index=False)\n+\n+    if make_figures:\n+        plot_centroid_diagnostics(centroids, shapefile_path, artifacts)\n+\n+    logger.info(\n+        \"Stage 01 complete. centroids=%d od_rows=%d output=%s\",\n+        len(centroids),\n+        len(od_matrix),\n+        artifacts.stage_dir,\n+    )\n+    return {\n+        \"run_dir\": str(run_dir),\n+
