{"timestamp":"2026-03-10T23:37:52.884Z","type":"response_item","payload":{"type":"message","role":"user","content":[{"type":"input_text","text":"# AGENTS.md instructions for c:\\Python\\trade\n\n<INSTRUCTIONS>\n## Skills\nA skill is a set of local instructions to follow that is stored in a `SKILL.md` file. Below is the list of skills that can be used. Each entry includes a name, description, and file path so you can open the source for full instructions when using a specific skill.\n### Available skills\n- skill-creator: Guide for creating effective skills. This skill should be used when users want to create a new skill (or update an existing skill) that extends Codex's capabilities with specialized knowledge, workflows, or tool integrations. (file: C:/Users/ROBERTODU/.codex/skills/.system/skill-creator/SKILL.md)\n- skill-installer: Install Codex skills into $CODEX_HOME/skills from a curated list or a GitHub repo path. Use when a user asks to list installable skills, install a curated skill, or install a skill from another repo (including private repos). (file: C:/Users/ROBERTODU/.codex/skills/.system/skill-installer/SKILL.md)\n### How to use skills\n- Discovery: The list above is the skills available in this session (name + description + file path). Skill bodies live on disk at the listed paths.\n- Trigger rules: If the user names a skill (with `$SkillName` or plain text) OR the task clearly matches a skill's description shown above, you must use that skill for that turn. Multiple mentions mean use them all. Do not carry skills across turns unless re-mentioned.\n- Missing/blocked: If a named skill isn't in the list or the path can't be read, say so briefly and continue with the best fallback.\n- How to use a skill (progressive disclosure):\n  1) After deciding to use a skill, open its `SKILL.md`. Read only enough to follow the workflow.\n  2) When `SKILL.md` references relative paths (e.g., `scripts/foo.py`), resolve them relative to the skill directory listed above first, and only consider other paths if needed.\n  3) If `SKILL.md` points to extra folders such as `references/`, load only the specific files needed for the request; don't bulk-load everything.\n  4) If `scripts/` exist, prefer running or patching them instead of retyping large code blocks.\n  5) If `assets/` or templates exist, reuse them instead of recreating from scratch.\n- Coordination and sequencing:\n  - If multiple skills apply, choose the minimal set that covers the request and state the order you'll use them.\n  - Announce which skill(s) you're using and why (one short line). If you skip an obvious skill, say why.\n- Context hygiene:\n  - Keep context small: summarize long sections instead of pasting them; only load extra files when needed.\n  - Avoid deep reference-chasing: prefer opening only files directly linked from `SKILL.md` unless you're blocked.\n  - When variants exist (frameworks, providers, domains), pick only the relevant reference file(s) and note that choice.\n- Safety and fallback: If a skill can't be applied cleanly (missing files, unclear instructions), state the issue, pick the next-best approach, and continue.\n</INSTRUCTIONS>"},{"type":"input_text","text":"<environment_context>\n  <cwd>c:\\Python\\trade</cwd>\n  <shell>powershell</shell>\n  <current_date>2026-03-10</current_date>\n  <timezone>America/New_York</timezone>\n</environment_context>"}]}}
{"timestamp":"2026-03-10T23:37:52.885Z","type":"response_item","payload":{"type":"message","role":"user","content":[{"type":"input_text","text":"Convert the revised preservation analysis into a closed compatibility ticket list.\r\n\r\nFor each ticket, provide:\r\n1. Ticket ID\r\n2. Stage\r\n3. Issue title\r\n4. Type:\r\n   - bug\r\n   - schema compatibility\r\n   - visual compatibility\r\n   - methodological parity\r\n   - explicit scope decision\r\n5. Why it matters\r\n6. Required action\r\n7. Acceptance criteria\r\n8. Priority:\r\n   - P0 must fix before baseline freeze\r\n   - P1 should fix soon after\r\n   - P2 can defer\r\n\r\nImportant constraints:\r\n- Separate legacy-compatible outputs from optional extended outputs.\r\n- For Stage 06, define a parameterized decision between legacy-focus mode and expanded-coverage mode.\r\n- For Stage 04, explicitly determine whether the current implementation is methodologically equivalent to legacy or not.\r\n- For Stage 03, treat the global/USA/China special map design as canonical and non-negotiable.\r\n\r\nDo not implement code yet.\r\nFirst produce the ticket list only.\n"}]}}
{"timestamp":"2026-03-10T23:38:47.419Z","type":"event_msg","payload":{"type":"agent_message","message":"**Closed Compatibility Ticket List**\n\n**Legacy-Compatible Outputs**\nThese are the outputs that define baseline parity and should govern the ticket list:\n\n- `Stage 01`: centroids, OD matrix, core geo audits\n- `Stage 02`: schema validation outputs, dictionaries, country totals\n- `Stage 03`: global barycenter CSV, required country barycenter CSVs, special USA/China trade CSVs, canonical global/USA/China maps\n- `Stage 04`: core clustering state/features/matrices/clusters/attractors/stability outputs\n- `Stage 05`: Moran global/SITC CSVs and labeled heatmaps\n- `Stage 06`: drift steps/indices and principal maps, under an explicitly defined mode\n- `Stage 07`: distance core CSVs and labeled heatmaps/panels\n\n**Optional Extended Outputs**\nThese are not baseline parity targets and can be dropped or deferred:\n\n- exploratory per-country Stage 03 figures outside global/USA/China\n- `barycenter_panel_exploratory_mercator.*`\n- duplicate PDF exports where PNG is sufficient\n- redundant clustering helper artifacts and `_demeaned` variants not consumed downstream\n- optional symmetry audit outputs\n- extra publication-only clustering figures and period extras\n\n**Tickets**\n\n**GA-COMP-001**\n1. Ticket ID: `GA-COMP-001`\n2. Stage: `Stage 05`\n3. Issue title: `Moran heatmaps do not join SITC product labels`\n4. Type: `bug`\n5. Why it matters: The legacy output uses interpretable product labels; numeric-only SITC heatmaps break analytical readability and fail compatibility expectations.\n6. Required action: Load product dictionaries from `raw/reference`, join labels to SITC2 and SITC3 heatmap inputs, and render labels instead of bare codes.\n7. Acceptance criteria:\n- SITC2 and SITC3 Moran heatmaps display product labels from reference files\n- labels are traceable to the correct SITC code\n- no heatmap falls back to numeric-only labels when dictionary entries exist\n8. Priority: `P0`\n\n**GA-COMP-002**\n1. Ticket ID: `GA-COMP-002`\n2. Stage: `Stage 07`\n3. Issue title: `Distance heatmaps must render SITC labels legibly`\n4. Type: `visual compatibility`\n5. Why it matters: Even if labels are technically joined, the output is not legacy-compatible unless the labels are visibly usable in the rendered figure.\n6. Required action: Review Stage 07 heatmap rendering and adjust label formatting/layout so SITC labels are readable and not effectively reduced to code-only visuals.\n7. Acceptance criteria:\n- SITC2 and SITC3 distance heatmaps visibly show product labels, not just compressed numeric codes\n- label text is readable in exported figures without manual editing\n- output remains stable across the full 1976–2023 run\n8. Priority: `P0`\n\n**GA-COMP-003**\n1. Ticket ID: `GA-COMP-003`\n2. Stage: `Stage 03`\n3. Issue title: `Restore canonical global USA China barycenter map design`\n4. Type: `visual compatibility`\n5. Why it matters: The global/USA/China map design in legacy is deliberate and canonical, not exploratory. It is part of the preserved analytical presentation.\n6. Required action: Port the legacy design behavior into modular Stage 03, including the Mediterranean AEQD projection, legacy extent logic, and the specific global/USA/China rendering rules.\n7. Acceptance criteria:\n- global imports/exports maps use the legacy-style AEQD Mediterranean projection\n- USA and China canonical maps preserve the same projection/extent behavior as legacy\n- rendered composition, trajectory framing, and labeling behavior match legacy intent closely enough for side-by-side recognition\n8. Priority: `P0`\n\n**GA-COMP-004**\n1. Ticket ID: `GA-COMP-004`\n2. Stage: `Stage 03`\n3. Issue title: `Preserve schema of special USA barycenter trade file`\n4. Type: `schema compatibility`\n5. Why it matters: Downstream compatibility and legacy parity require the special USA file to retain its historical schema, not just equivalent values.\n6. Required action: Make `barycenter_usa_trade_1976_2023.csv` emit the legacy-compatible column names and structure.\n7. Acceptance criteria:\n- output file exists with the legacy filename\n- columns match legacy expectations, including USA-specific naming\n- values remain numerically consistent with the current implementation\n8. Priority: `P0`\n\n**GA-COMP-005**\n1. Ticket ID: `GA-COMP-005`\n2. Stage: `Stage 05`\n3. Issue title: `Remove schema drift from global Moran output`\n4. Type: `schema compatibility`\n5. Why it matters: The extra `zero_share` column breaks exact compatibility and makes the new artifact structurally different from the canonical legacy output.\n6. Required action: Align the exported schema of the global Moran CSV with the legacy output or explicitly split extra diagnostics into a non-canonical file.\n7. Acceptance criteria:\n- `moran_global_*` canonical CSV matches legacy schema exactly\n- any extra derived diagnostics are written to a separate non-canonical output if retained\n8. Priority: `P0`\n\n**GA-COMP-006**\n1. Ticket ID: `GA-COMP-006`\n2. Stage: `Stage 06`\n3. Issue title: `Define parameterized drift mode legacy-focus vs expanded-coverage`\n4. Type: `explicit scope decision`\n5. Why it matters: Stage 06 currently changes the analytical universe from the legacy 12-country focus to broad coverage. That is a scope decision, not an incidental difference.\n6. Required action: Introduce an explicit configuration parameter for drift mode with at least:\n- `legacy_focus`\n- `expanded_coverage`\nDocument which mode is canonical for baseline parity.\n7. Acceptance criteria:\n- Stage 06 can be run in `legacy_focus` mode and `expanded_coverage` mode\n- default baseline parity mode is explicitly declared\n- run metadata clearly records which mode was used\n8. Priority: `P0`\n\n**GA-COMP-007**\n1. Ticket ID: `GA-COMP-007`\n2. Stage: `Stage 06`\n3. Issue title: `Reproduce legacy drift outputs in legacy-focus mode`\n4. Type: `methodological parity`\n5. Why it matters: Baseline freeze requires a legacy-compatible mode, not only an expanded modernized implementation.\n6. Required action: Constrain Stage 06 legacy mode to the same country universe and output semantics used by the legacy pipeline, then validate against legacy CSV row counts and content.\n7. Acceptance criteria:\n- `legacy_focus` mode reproduces the legacy output universe\n- row counts and entity coverage align with the 12-country legacy expectation\n- drift CSVs and principal maps are materially comparable to legacy\n8. Priority: `P0`\n\n**GA-COMP-008**\n1. Ticket ID: `GA-COMP-008`\n2. Stage: `Stage 04`\n3. Issue title: `Formally classify current clustering implementation as non-equivalent to legacy`\n4. Type: `explicit scope decision`\n5. Why it matters: The current implementation is not just a formatting variant; it differs methodologically and should not be treated as baseline parity by accident.\n6. Required action: Mark the current Stage 04 implementation as non-equivalent to legacy until proven otherwise, and separate baseline parity targets from interim outputs.\n7. Acceptance criteria:\n- project documentation or compatibility notes explicitly state that current Stage 04 is not yet legacy-equivalent\n- no baseline freeze claims include Stage 04 parity until validated\n8. Priority: `P0`\n\n**GA-COMP-009**\n1. Ticket ID: `GA-COMP-009`\n2. Stage: `Stage 04`\n3. Issue title: `Define canonical clustering outputs required for baseline parity`\n4. Type: `explicit scope decision`\n5. Why it matters: Stage 04 has many legacy artifacts, but only a subset should control parity. Without defining that subset, parity work will drift.\n6. Required action: Lock the canonical Stage 04 parity target set: state long table, feature table, main distance matrices, clusters, attractors, and stability summary.\n7. Acceptance criteria:\n- canonical Stage 04 output list is explicitly documented\n- exploratory and redundant clustering artifacts are excluded from baseline parity scope\n8. Priority: `P0`\n\n**GA-COMP-010**\n1. Ticket ID: `GA-COMP-010`\n2. Stage: `Stage 04`\n3. Issue title: `Bring clustering methodology closer to legacy canonical behavior`\n4. Type: `methodological parity`\n5. Why it matters: Stage 04 is currently the largest parity gap in the pipeline. Baseline freeze is incomplete if canonical clustering outputs are methodologically different.\n6. Required action: Reconcile feature engineering, trajectory distance definitions, cluster assignment behavior, attractors, and stability calculations with the legacy implementation.\n7. Acceptance criteria:\n- canonical Stage 04 outputs are regenerated with materially aligned methodology\n- dimensions, coverage, and key summary behavior are comparable to legacy\n- remaining differences are documented as intentional if any persist\n8. Priority: `P1`\n\n**GA-COMP-011**\n1. Ticket ID: `GA-COMP-011`\n2. Stage: `Stage 03`\n3. Issue title: `Separate canonical Stage 03 outputs from exploratory per-country figures`\n4. Type: `explicit scope decision`\n5. Why it matters: Many missing legacy figures are exploratory and should not distort the parity target.\n6. Required action: Declare the canonical Stage 03 set and explicitly exclude exploratory per-country map exports from baseline compatibility scope.\n7. Acceptance criteria:\n- Stage 03 parity scope is documented\n- global/USA/China maps are marked canonical\n- exploratory per-country figures are marked optional or dropped\n8. Priority: `P1`\n\n**GA-COMP-012**\n1. Ticket ID: `GA-COMP-012`\n2. Stage: `Stage 05`\n3. Issue title: `Separate canonical Moran outputs from optional diagnostics`\n4. Type: `explicit scope decision`\n5. Why it matters: Extra diagnostics are useful, but they should not modify canonical output schemas or parity expectations.\n6. Required action: Define which Moran outputs are canonical and move any extra diagnostics to clearly optional artifacts.\n7. Acceptance criteria:\n- canonical Moran files are fixed and schema-stable\n- optional diagnostics are written separately and documented as non-parity outputs\n8. Priority: `P1`\n\n**GA-COMP-013**\n1. Ticket ID: `GA-COMP-013`\n2. Stage: `Stage 07`\n3. Issue title: `Separate canonical distance outputs from optional presentation variants`\n4. Type: `explicit scope decision`\n5. Why it matters: Distance already matches well; the remaining issue is preserving core labeled outputs while avoiding scope creep on presentation variants.\n6. Required action: Lock the canonical Distance CSV and figure set, and classify any additional variants as optional extensions.\n7. Acceptance criteria:\n- canonical Stage 07 outputs are documented and stable\n- extra presentation exports are clearly optional\n8. Priority: `P1`\n\n**GA-COMP-014**\n1. Ticket ID: `GA-COMP-014`\n2. Stage: `Cross-stage`\n3. Issue title: `Document baseline parity boundary vs optional extended outputs`\n4. Type: `explicit scope decision`\n5. Why it matters: The project now contains both parity goals and modernization opportunities. A closed boundary is needed before baseline freeze.\n6. Required action: Publish a compatibility boundary that lists what must match legacy, what can differ intentionally, and what is out of scope.\n7. Acceptance criteria:\n- a single baseline parity list exists for Stages 01–07\n- optional extended outputs are explicitly listed separately\n- future work cannot silently expand the parity target\n8. Priority: `P0`\n\n**Tickets Explicitly Out of Baseline Scope**\nThese do not need standalone compatibility tickets for baseline freeze:\n\n- exploratory Stage 03 country maps outside canonical global/USA/China set\n- `barycenter_panel_exploratory_mercator.*`\n- duplicate PDF exports where PNG is already sufficient\n- redundant clustering helper artifacts and `_demeaned` variants not consumed downstream\n- optional symmetry audit outputs\n- publication-only visual variants not required for the baseline run\n\nIf you want, the next step can be to reorder these tickets into an execution sequence for baseline freeze, but still without implementing code yet.","phase":"final_answer"}}
{"timestamp":"2026-03-10T23:38:48.775Z","type":"response_item","payload":{"type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"output_text","text":"**Closed Compatibility Ticket List**\n\n**Legacy-Compatible Outputs**\nThese are the outputs that define baseline parity and should govern the ticket list:\n\n- `Stage 01`: centroids, OD matrix, core geo audits\n- `Stage 02`: schema validation outputs, dictionaries, country totals\n- `Stage 03`: global barycenter CSV, required country barycenter CSVs, special USA/China trade CSVs, canonical global/USA/China maps\n- `Stage 04`: core clustering state/features/matrices/clusters/attractors/stability outputs\n- `Stage 05`: Moran global/SITC CSVs and labeled heatmaps\n- `Stage 06`: drift steps/indices and principal maps, under an explicitly defined mode\n- `Stage 07`: distance core CSVs and labeled heatmaps/panels\n\n**Optional Extended Outputs**\nThese are not baseline parity targets and can be dropped or deferred:\n\n- exploratory per-country Stage 03 figures outside global/USA/China\n- `barycenter_panel_exploratory_mercator.*`\n- duplicate PDF exports where PNG is sufficient\n- redundant clustering helper artifacts and `_demeaned` variants not consumed downstream\n- optional symmetry audit outputs\n- extra publication-only clustering figures and period extras\n\n**Tickets**\n\n**GA-COMP-001**\n1. Ticket ID: `GA-COMP-001`\n2. Stage: `Stage 05`\n3. Issue title: `Moran heatmaps do not join SITC product labels`\n4. Type: `bug`\n5. Why it matters: The legacy output uses interpretable product labels; numeric-only SITC heatmaps break analytical readability and fail compatibility expectations.\n6. Required action: Load product dictionaries from `raw/reference`, join labels to SITC2 and SITC3 heatmap inputs, and render labels instead of bare codes.\n7. Acceptance criteria:\n- SITC2 and SITC3 Moran heatmaps display product labels from reference files\n- labels are traceable to the correct SITC code\n- no heatmap falls back to numeric-only labels when dictionary entries exist\n8. Priority: `P0`\n\n**GA-COMP-002**\n1. Ticket ID: `GA-COMP-002`\n2. Stage: `Stage 07`\n3. Issue title: `Distance heatmaps must render SITC labels legibly`\n4. Type: `visual compatibility`\n5. Why it matters: Even if labels are technically joined, the output is not legacy-compatible unless the labels are visibly usable in the rendered figure.\n6. Required action: Review Stage 07 heatmap rendering and adjust label formatting/layout so SITC labels are readable and not effectively reduced to code-only visuals.\n7. Acceptance criteria:\n- SITC2 and SITC3 distance heatmaps visibly show product labels, not just compressed numeric codes\n- label text is readable in exported figures without manual editing\n- output remains stable across the full 1976–2023 run\n8. Priority: `P0`\n\n**GA-COMP-003**\n1. Ticket ID: `GA-COMP-003`\n2. Stage: `Stage 03`\n3. Issue title: `Restore canonical global USA China barycenter map design`\n4. Type: `visual compatibility`\n5. Why it matters: The global/USA/China map design in legacy is deliberate and canonical, not exploratory. It is part of the preserved analytical presentation.\n6. Required action: Port the legacy design behavior into modular Stage 03, including the Mediterranean AEQD projection, legacy extent logic, and the specific global/USA/China rendering rules.\n7. Acceptance criteria:\n- global imports/exports maps use the legacy-style AEQD Mediterranean projection\n- USA and China canonical maps preserve the same projection/extent behavior as legacy\n- rendered composition, trajectory framing, and labeling behavior match legacy intent closely enough for side-by-side recognition\n8. Priority: `P0`\n\n**GA-COMP-004**\n1. Ticket ID: `GA-COMP-004`\n2. Stage: `Stage 03`\n3. Issue title: `Preserve schema of special USA barycenter trade file`\n4. Type: `schema compatibility`\n5. Why it matters: Downstream compatibility and legacy parity require the special USA file to retain its historical schema, not just equivalent values.\n6. Required action: Make `barycenter_usa_trade_1976_2023.csv` emit the legacy-compatible column names and structure.\n7. Acceptance criteria:\n- output file exists with the legacy filename\n- columns match legacy expectations, including USA-specific naming\n- values remain numerically consistent with the current implementation\n8. Priority: `P0`\n\n**GA-COMP-005**\n1. Ticket ID: `GA-COMP-005`\n2. Stage: `Stage 05`\n3. Issue title: `Remove schema drift from global Moran output`\n4. Type: `schema compatibility`\n5. Why it matters: The extra `zero_share` column breaks exact compatibility and makes the new artifact structurally different from the canonical legacy output.\n6. Required action: Align the exported schema of the global Moran CSV with the legacy output or explicitly split extra diagnostics into a non-canonical file.\n7. Acceptance criteria:\n- `moran_global_*` canonical CSV matches legacy schema exactly\n- any extra derived diagnostics are written to a separate non-canonical output if retained\n8. Priority: `P0`\n\n**GA-COMP-006**\n1. Ticket ID: `GA-COMP-006`\n2. Stage: `Stage 06`\n3. Issue title: `Define parameterized drift mode legacy-focus vs expanded-coverage`\n4. Why it matters: The project now contains both parity goals and modernization opportunities. A closed boundary is needed before baseline freeze.\n6. Required action: Publish a compatibility boundary that lists what must match legacy, what can differ intentionally, and what is out of scope.\n7. Acceptance criteria:\n- a single baseline parity list exists for Stages 01–07\n- optional extended outputs are explicitly listed separately\n- future work cannot silently expand the parity target\n8. Priority: `P0`\n\n**Tickets Explicitly Out of Baseline Scope**\nThese do not need standalone compatibility tickets for baseline freeze:\n\n- exploratory Stage 03 country maps outside canonical global/USA/China set\n- `barycenter_panel_exploratory_mercator.*`\n- duplicate PDF exports where PNG is already sufficient\n- redundant clustering helper artifacts and `_demeaned` variants not consumed downstream\n- optional symmetry audit outputs\n- publication-only visual variants not required for the baseline run\n\nIf you want, the next step can be to reorder these tickets into an execution sequence for baseline freeze, but still without implementing code yet."}}

initialize_project_tree, initialize_run_tree\r\n\r\n\r\ndef run(config: ProjectConfig, run_id: str) -> dict[str, str]:\r\n    logger = logging.getLogger(\"geoanalisis.stage_05_moran\")\r\n    initialize_project_tree(config)\r\n    run_dir = initialize_run_tree(config, run_id)\r\n    tag = f\"{config.trade_year_start}_{config.trade_year_end}\"\r\n    artifacts = build_moran_artifact_paths(run_dir / \"artifacts\" / \"05_moran\", tag)\r\n    od_path = build_stage_dir(config, run_id, \"01_geo\") / \"data\" / \"OD_Matrix.csv\"\r\n    if not od_path.exists():\r\n        raise FileNotFoundError(f\"Stage 05 requires Stage 01 OD matrix at {od_path}\")\r\n\r\n    w, codes = build_weights_from_od(od_path)\r\n    global_rows = []\r\n    sitc2_rows = []\r\n    sitc3_rows = []\r\n\r\n    for year in range(config.trade_year_start, config.trade_year_end + 1):\r\n        trade_path = config.dataset_trade_dir / f\"S2_{year}.parquet\"\r\n        if not trade_path.exists():\r\n            continue\r\n\r\n        exp, imp = aggregate_year_global(trade_path, config.trade_value_column)\r\n        row_exp = moran_row(exp, codes, w)\r\n        row_imp = moran_row(imp, codes, w)\r\n        global_rows.append({\"year\": year, \"flow\": \"exports\", **row_exp, \"exp_zero_share\": row_exp[\"zero_share\"], \"imp_zero_share\": row_imp[\"zero_share\"], \"exp_not_locatable_codes\": 0, \"imp_not_locatable_codes\": 0})\r\n        global_rows.append({\"year\": year, \"flow\": \"imports\", **row_imp, \"exp_zero_share\": row_exp[\"zero_share\"], \"imp_zero_share\": row_imp[\"zero_share\"], \"exp_not_locatable_codes\": 0, \"imp_not_locatable_codes\": 0})\r\n\r\n        exp2, imp2 = aggregate_year_product(trade_path, config.trade_value_column, 2)\r\n        for product in sorted(set(exp2[\"product\"]) | set(imp2[\"product\"])):\r\n            r2e = moran_row(exp2[exp2[\"product\"] == product][[\"code\", \"value\"]], codes, w)\r\n            r2i = moran_row(imp2[imp2[\"product\"] == product][[\"code\", \"value\"]], codes, w)\r\n            sitc2_rows.append({\"year\": year, \"sitc2\": product, \"flow\": \"exports\", **r2e, \"exp_not_locatable_codes\": 0, \"imp_not_locatable_codes\": 0})\r\n            sitc2_rows.append({\"year\": year, \"sitc2\": product, \"flow\": \"imports\", **r2i, \"exp_not_locatable_codes\": 0, \"imp_not_locatable_codes\": 0})\r\n\r\n        exp3, imp3 = aggregate_year_product(trade_path, config.trade_value_column, 3)\r\n        for product in sorted(set(exp3[\"product\"]) | set(imp3[\"product\"])):\r\n            r3e = moran_row(exp3[exp3[\"product\"] == product][[\"code\", \"value\"]], codes, w)\r\n            r3i = moran_row(imp3[imp3[\"product\"] == product][[\"code\", \"value\"]], codes, w)\r\n            sitc3_rows.append({\"year\": year, \"sitc3\": product, \"flow\": \"exports\", **r3e, \"exp_not_locatable_codes\": 0, \"imp_not_locatable_codes\": 0})\r\n            sitc3_rows.append({\"year\": year, \"sitc3\": product, \"flow\": \"imports\", **r3i, \"exp_not_locatable_codes\": 0, \"imp_not_locatable_codes\": 0})\r\n\r\n    global_df = pd.DataFrame(global_rows)\r\n    sitc2_df = pd.DataFrame(sitc2_rows)\r\n    sitc3_df = pd.DataFrame(sitc3_rows)\r\n    global_df.to_csv(artifacts.global_path, index=False)\r\n    sitc2_df.to_csv(artifacts.sitc2_path, index=False)\r\n    sitc3_df.to_csv(artifacts.sitc3_path, index=False)\r\n\r\n    # figures\r\n    for flow, zero_col, outfile in [\r\n        (\"exports\", \"moran_global_exports.png\", \"moran_i\"),\r\n        (\"imports\", \"moran_global_imports.png\", \"moran_i\"),\r\n    ]:\r\n        sub = global_df[global_df[\"flow\"] == flow].copy()\r\n        fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(10, 5))\r\n        ax.plot(sub[\"year\"], sub[\"moran_i\"], linewidth=2)\r\n        ax.axhline(0.0, color=\"black\", linewidth=0.8, alpha=0.5)\r\n        ax.set_xlabel(\"Year\")\r\n        ax.set_ylabel(\"Moran's I\")\r\n        fig.tight_layout()\r\n        fig.savefig(artifacts.fig_dir / zero_col, dpi=300)\r\n        plt.close(fig)\r\n\r\n    def heatmap(df, code_col, outname):\r\n        pivot = df.pivot(index=code_col, columns=\"year\", values=\"moran_i\").sort_index()\r\n        fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(20, 14 if code_col == \"sitc2\" else 24), dpi=200)\r\n        im = ax.imshow(pivot.values.astype(float), aspect=\"auto\", interpolation=\"nearest\", cmap=\"RdBu_r\", vmin=-0.20, vmax=0.20)\r\n        ax.set_xticks(range(len(pivot.columns)))\r\n        ax.set_xticklabels([str(int(y)) if i % 2 == 0 else \"\" for i, y in enumerate(pivot.columns)], rotation=90)\r\n        ax.set_yticks(range(len(pivot.index)))\r\n        ax.set_yticklabels(list(pivot.index), fontsize=4 if code_col == \"sitc3\" else 5)\r\n        fig.colorbar(im, ax=ax).set_label(\"Moran's I\")\r\n        fig.tight_layout()\r\n        fig.savefig(artifacts.fig_dir / outname, bbox_inches=\"tight\")\r\n        plt.close(fig)\r\n\r\n    heatmap(sitc2_df[sitc2_df[\"flow\"] == \"exports\"], \"sitc2\", \"heatmap_moran_sitc2_level_exports.png\")\r\n    heatmap(sitc2_df[sitc2_df[\"flow\"] == \"imports\"], \"sitc2\", \"heatmap_moran_sitc2_level_imports.png\")\r\n    heatmap(sitc3_df[sitc3_df[\"flow\"] == \"exports\"], \"sitc3\", \"heatmap_moran_sitc3_level_exports.png\")\r\n    heatmap(sitc3_df[sitc3_df[\"flow\"] == \"imports\"], \"sitc3\", \"heatmap_moran_sitc3_level_imports.png\")\r\n\r\n    logger.info(\r\n        \"Stage 05 complete. global_rows=%d sitc2_rows=%d sitc3_rows=%d output=%s\",\r\n        len(global_df),\r\n        len(sitc2_df),\r\n        len(sitc3_df),\r\n        artifacts.stage_dir,\r\n    )\r\n    return {\r\n        \"run_dir\": str(run_dir),\r\n        \"stage_dir\": str(artifacts.stage_dir),\r\n        \"global_path\": str(artifacts.global_path),\r\n        \"sitc2_path\": str(artifacts.sitc2_path),\r\n        \"sitc3_path\": str(artifacts.sitc3_path),\r\n    }\r\n"}}
{"timestamp":"2026-03-10T23:42:33.699Z","type":"response_item","payload":{"type":"function_call_output","call_id":"call_U2fZP0zGwzbDBaGOLmeIwH3c","output":"Exit code: 0\nWall time: 0.9 seconds\nOutput:\nfrom __future__ import annotations\r\n\r\nimport math\r\nfrom dataclasses import dataclass\r\nfrom pathlib import Path\r\n\r\nimport duckdb\r\nimport numpy as np\r\nimport pandas as pd\r\nfrom esda.moran import Moran\r\nfrom libpysal.weights import W\r\n\r\n\r\n@dataclass(frozen=True)\r\nclass MoranArtifacts:\r\n    stage_dir: Path\r\n    data_dir: Path\r\n    fig_dir: Path\r\n    global_path: Path\r\n    sitc2_path: Path\r\n    sitc3_path: Path\r\n\r\n\r\ndef build_moran_artifact_paths(stage_dir: Path, tag: str) -> MoranArtifacts:\r\n    data_dir = stage_dir / \"data\"\r\n    fig_dir = stage_dir / \"fig\"\r\n    return MoranArtifacts(\r\n        stage_dir=stage_dir,\r\n        data_dir=data_dir,\r\n        fig_dir=fig_dir,\r\n        global_path=data_dir / f\"moran_global_S2_{tag}_normal_inference.csv\",\r\n        sitc2_path=data_dir / f\"moran_sitc2_S2_{tag}_normal_inference.csv\",\r\n        sitc3_path=data_dir / f\"moran_sitc3_S2_{tag}_normal_inference.csv\",\r\n    )\r\n\r\n\r\ndef log10_1p(x: np.ndarray) -> np.ndarray:\r\n    return np.log10(1.0 + np.maximum(x, 0.0))\r\n\r\n\r\ndef norm_cdf(z: float) -> float:\r\n    return 0.5 * (1.0 + math.erf(z / math.sqrt(2.0)))\r\n\r\n\r\ndef two_sided_p_from_z(z: float) -> float:\r\n    return 2.0 * (1.0 - norm_cdf(abs(z)))\r\n\r\n\r\ndef build_weights_from_od(od_path: Path, epsilon_factor: float = 1e-3) -> tuple[W, list[str]]:\r\n    od = pd.read_csv(od_path)\r\n    od[\"origin\"] = od[\"origin\"].astype(str)\r\n    od[\"destination\"] = od[\"destination\"].astype(str)\r\n    od[\"distance_km\"] = pd.to_numeric(od[\"distance_km\"], errors=\"coerce\")\r\n    od = od.dropna(subset=[\"origin\", \"destination\", \"distance_km\"])\r\n    min_positive = od.loc[od[\"distance_km\"] > 0, \"distance_km\"].min()\r\n    epsilon = float(min_positive * epsilon_factor) if pd.notna(min_positive) else 1e-6\r\n    od[\"distance_km\"] = od[\"distance_km\"].replace(0, epsilon)\r\n    od[\"weight\"] = 1.0 / od[\"distance_km\"]\r\n    codes = sorted(set(od[\"origin\"]) | set(od[\"destination\"]))\r\n    neighbors = {code: [] for code in codes}\r\n    weights = {code: [] for code in codes}\r\n    for _, row in od.iterrows():\r\n        origin = row[\"origin\"]\r\n        dest = row[\"destination\"]\r\n        if origin == dest:\r\n            continue\r\n        neighbors[origin].append(dest)\r\n        weights[origin].append(float(row[\"weight\"]))\r\n    w = W(neighbors, weights, silence_warnings=True)\r\n    w.transform = \"R\"\r\n    return w, codes\r\n\r\n\r\ndef aggregate_year_global(trade_path: Path, value_col: str) -> tuple[pd.DataFrame, pd.DataFrame]:\r\n    con = duckdb.connect()\r\n    exports = con.execute(\r\n        f\"\"\"\r\n        SELECT exporter AS code, SUM({value_col}) AS value\r\n        FROM read_parquet('{trade_path}')\r\n        GROUP BY exporter\r\n        \"\"\"\r\n    ).df()\r\n    imports = con.execute(\r\n        f\"\"\"\r\n        SELECT importer AS code, SUM({value_col}) AS value\r\n        FROM read_parquet('{trade_path}')\r\n        GROUP BY importer\r\n        \"\"\"\r\n    ).df()\r\n    con.close()\r\n    exports[\"code\"] = exports[\"code\"].astype(str)\r\n    imports[\"code\"] = imports[\"code\"].astype(str)\r\n    return exports, imports\r\n\r\n\r\ndef aggregate_year_product(trade_path: Path, value_col: str, product_digits: int) -> tuple[pd.DataFrame, pd.DataFrame]:\r\n    con = duckdb.connect()\r\n    exports = con.execute(\r\n        f\"\"\"\r\n        SELECT exporter AS code,\r\n               SUBSTR(LPAD(CAST(commoditycode AS VARCHAR), 4, '0'), 1, {product_digits}) AS product,\r\n               SUM({value_col}) AS value\r\n        FROM read_parquet('{trade_path}')\r\n        GROUP BY exporter, product\r\n        \"\"\"\r\n    ).df()\r\n    imports = con.execute(\r\n        f\"\"\"\r\n        SELECT importer AS code,\r\n               SUBSTR(LPAD(CAST(commoditycode AS VARCHAR), 4, '0'), 1, {product_digits}) AS product,\r\n               SUM({value_col}) AS value\r\n        FROM read_parquet('{trade_path}')\r\n        GROUP BY importer, product\r\n        \"\"\"\r\n    ).df()\r\n    con.close()\r\n    exports[\"code\"] = exports[\"code\"].astype(str)\r\n    imports[\"code\"] = imports[\"code\"].astype(str)\r\n    exports[\"product\"] = exports[\"product\"].astype(str).str.zfill(product_digits)\r\n    imports[\"product\"] = imports[\"product\"].astype(str).str.zfill(product_digits)\r\n    return exports, imports\r\n\r\n\r\ndef moran_row(values_df: pd.DataFrame, codes: list[str], w: W) -> dict[str, float]:\r\n    s = pd.Series(0.0, index=codes, dtype=float)\r\n    if not values_df.empty:\r\n        vals = values_df.groupby(\"code\")[\"value\"].sum()\r\n        s.loc[vals.index.intersection(s.index)] = vals.loc[vals.index.intersection(s.index)].astype(float)\r\n    x = log10_1p(s.to_numpy(dtype=float))\r\n    mor = Moran(x, w, two_tailed=True)\r\n    z = float((mor.I - mor.EI) / math.sqrt(mor.VI_norm)) if mor.VI_norm > 0 else float(\"nan\")\r\n    p = two_sided_p_from_z(z) if not math.isnan(z) else float(\"nan\")\r\n    return {\r\n        \"n\": len(codes),\r\n        \"moran_i\": float(mor.I),\r\n        \"mean_H0\": float(mor.EI),\r\n        \"variance_H0\": float(mor.VI_norm),\r\n        \"z\": z,\r\n        \"p_value\": p,\r\n        \"ci_5\": float(mor.I - 1.96 * math.sqrt(mor.VI_norm)) if mor.VI_norm > 0 else float(\"nan\"),\r\n        \"ci_95\": float(mor.I + 1.96 * math.sqrt(mor.VI_norm)) if mor.VI_norm > 0 else float(\"nan\"),\r\n        \"zero_share\": float((s == 0).mean()),\r\n    }\r\n"}}

Path(r\"C:\\Python\\Geoanalisis\")\r\n    data_dir: Path = Path(r\"C:\\Python\\Geoanalisis\\data\")\r\n    incoming_dir: Path = Path(r\"C:\\Python\\Geoanalisis\\data\\incoming\")\r\n    external_dir:  \"BRA\",\r\n        \"ARG\",\r\n        \"MEX\",\r\n        \"COL\",\r\n        \"CHL\",\r\n        \"CAN\",\r\n        \"USA\",\r\n        \"CHN\",\r\n        \"JPN\",\r\n        \"GBR\",\r\n )\r\n    run_alias_default: str = \"baseline\"\r\n    run_subdirs: tuple[str, ...] = (\"logs\", \"artifacts\", \"reports\")\r\n    stage_output_dirs: tuple[str, ...] = (\r\n        \"01_geo\",\r\n def dataset_dir(self) -> Path:\r\n        return self.incoming_dir / self.dataset_version\r\n\r\n    @property\r\n    def dataset_raw_dir(self) -> Path:\r\n        return self.dataset_dir / \"raw\"\r\n\r\n    @property\r\n    def dataset_trade_dir(self) -> Path:\r\n        return self.dataset_raw_dir / \"dataverse_files\"\r\n\r\n    @property\r\n    def dataset_reference_dir(self) -> Path:\r\n        return self.dataset_raw_dir / \"reference\"\r\n\r\n    @property\r\n    def natural_earth_dir(self) -> Path:\r\n        return self.external_dir / self.natural_earth_dirname / df[label_col].astype(str).str.strip()\n+    labels = {}\n+    for row in df[[code_col, label_col]].itertuples(index=False):\n+        code, label = row\n+        labels[code] = f\"{code}-{label}\" if sitc3_labels = _load_sitc_labels(\n+        config.dataset_reference_dir / \"sitc2-3digit.txt\",\n+        \"sitc3\",\n+        \"nickname3\",\n+    )\n+\n+    for flow, outfile in [\n+        (\"exports\", \"moran_global_exports.png\"),\n+        (\"imports\", \"moran_global_imports.png\"),\n+    ]:\n+        sub = global_df[global_df[\"flow\"] == flow].copy()\n+        fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(10, 5))\n+        ax.plot(sub[\"year\"], sub[\"moran_i\"], linewidth=2)\n+        ax.axhline(0.0, color=\"black\", linewidth=0.8, alpha=0.5)\n+        ax.set_xlabel(\"Year\")\n+        ax.set_ylabel(\"Moran's I\")\n+        fig.tight_layout()\n+        fig.savefig(fig_dir / outfile, dpi=300)\n+        plt.close(fig)\n+\n+    def heatmap(df: pd.DataFrame, code_col: str, labels: dict[str, str], outname: str) -> None:\n+        pivot = df.pivot(index=code_col, columns=\"year\", values=\"moran_i\").sort_index()\n+        ylabels = [labels.get(str(code), str(code)) for code in pivot.index]\n+        fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(20, 14 if code_col == \"sitc2\" else 24), dpi=200)\n+        im = ax.imshow(\n+            pivot.values.astype(float),\n+            aspect=\"auto\",\n+            interpolation=\"nearest\",\n+            cmap=\"RdBu_r\",\n+            vmin=-0.20,\n+            vmax=0.20,\n+        )\n+        ax.set_xticks(range(len(pivot.columns)))\n+        ax.set_xticklabels([str(int(y)) if i % 2 == 0 else \"\" for i, y in enumerate(pivot.columns)], rotation=90)\n+        ax.set_yticks(range(len(pivot.index)))\n+        ax.set_yticklabels(ylabels, fontsize=4 if code_col == \"sitc3\" else 5)\n+        fig.colorbar(im, ax=ax).set_label(\"Moran's I\")\n+        fig.tight_layout()\n+        fig.savefig(fig_dir / outname, bbox_inches=\"tight\")\n+        plt.close(fig)\n+\n+    heatmap(sitc2_df[sitc2_df[\"flow\"] == \"exports\"], \"sitc2\", sitc2_labels, \"heatmap_moran_sitc2_level_exports.png\")\n+    heatmap(sitc2_df[sitc2_df[\"flow\"] == \"imports\"], \"sitc2\", sitc2_labels, \"heatmap_moran_sitc2_level_imports.png\")\n+ pd.read_csv(artifacts.global_path)\n+    sitc2_df = pd.read_csv(artifacts.sitc2_path, dtype={\"sitc2\": str})\n+    sitc3_df = pd.read_csv(artifacts.sitc3_path, dtype={\"sitc3\": str})\n+ {"timestamp":"2026-03-10T23:44:29.049Z","type":"event_msg","payload":{"type":"agent_message","message":"**GA-COMP-001**\n\n**Code changes**\nEdited [stage_05_moran.py](C:/Python/Geoanalisis/src/geoanalisis/pipelines/stage_05_moran.py#L23) only.\n\nKey changes:\n- added `_load_sitc_labels()` to read product labels from:\n  - [sitc2-2digit.txt](C:/Python/Geoanalisis/data/incoming/trade_s2_v001/raw/reference/sitc2-2digit.txt)\n  - [sitc2-3digit.txt](C:/Python/Geoanalisis/data/incoming/trade_s2_v001/raw/reference/sitc2-3digit.txt)\n- extracted figure generation into `_render_figures(...)`\n- changed heatmap y-axis labels from raw codes to `code-nickname`\n- added `rerender_figures(config, run_id)` so Stage 05 visuals can be regenerated from existing CSV artifacts without recomputing the stage data\n\nRelevant lines:\n- label loader: [stage_05_moran.py#L23](C:/Python/Geoanalisis/src/geoanalisis/pipelines/stage_05_moran.py#L23)\n- figure renderer: [stage_05_moran.py#L34](C:/Python/Geoanalisis/src/geoanalisis/pipelines/stage_05_moran.py#L34)\n- rerender entrypoint: [stage_05_moran.py#L93](C:/Python/Geoanalisis/src/geoanalisis/pipelines/stage_05_moran.py#L93)\n\n**What was modified**\nThe bug was that Stage 05 heatmaps used `pivot.index` directly, so they rendered only numeric SITC codes. The fix now loads the legacy reference dictionaries and maps each SITC row to a readable label before drawing the figure.\n\n**Validation run**\nI regenerated only the Stage 05 figures for the existing run:\n- run: [2026-03-10_005_full-pipeline](C:/Python/Geoanalisis/runs/trade_s2_v001/2026-03-10_005_full-pipeline)\n- target folder: [05_moran/fig](C:/Python/Geoanalisis/runs/trade_s2_v001/2026-03-10_005_full-pipeline/artifacts/05_moran/fig)\n\nNo upstream stages were recomputed, and no Stage 05 CSVs were rewritten.\n\n**Acceptance criteria**\nConfirmed:\n- SITC2 and SITC3 Moran heatmaps now use product labels from `raw/reference`\n- labels resolve correctly, e.g. `00-Livestock`, `01-Meat`, `02-Dairy & Eggs`\n- the fix is confined to Stage 05\n- regeneration reused existing Stage 05 artifacts only\n\n`GA-COMP-001` is complete.\n\nNext in order is `GA-COMP-002`.","phase":"final_answer"}}

seconds\nOutput:\n\"\"\"Stage 07: distancia promedio del comercio.\"\"\"\r\n\r\nfrom __future__ import annotations\r\n\r\nimport logging\r\nfrom pathlib import Path\r\n\r\nimport matplotlib.pyplot as plt\r\nimport numpy as np\r\nimport pandas as pd\r\n\r\nfrom geoanalisis.config import ProjectConfig\r\nfrom geoanalisis.services.distance import (\r\n    build_con,\r\n    build_distance_artifact_paths,\r\n    compute_year,\r\n    load_od,\r\n    make_matrix,\r\n    plot_heatmap,\r\n    read_code_dict,\r\n)\r\nfrom geoanalisis.utils.paths import build_stage_dir\r\nfrom geoanalisis.utils.run_structure import initialize_project_tree, initialize_run_tree\r\n\r\n\r\ndef run(config: ProjectConfig, run_id: str) -> dict[str, str]:\r\n    logger = logging.getLogger(\"geoanalisis.stage_07_distance\")\r\n    initialize_project_tree(config)\r\n    run_dir = initialize_run_tree(config, run_id)\r\n    tag = f\"{config.trade_year_start}_{config.trade_year_end}\"\r\n    artifacts = build_distance_artifact_paths(run_dir / \"artifacts\" / \"07_distance\", tag)\r\n    od_path = build_stage_dir(config, run_id, \"01_geo\") / \"data\" / \"OD_Matrix.csv\"\r\n    if not od_path.exists():\r\n        raise FileNotFoundError(f\"Stage 07 requires Stage 01 OD matrix at {od_path}\")\r\n\r\n    con = build_con()\r\n    load_od(con, od_path)\r\n    cy_parts, gl_parts, s2_parts, s3_parts, dg_parts = [], [], [], [], []\r\n    done = 0\r\n    for year in range(config.trade_year_start, config.trade_year_end + 1):\r\n        res = compute_year(con, year, config.dataset_trade_dir, config.trade_value_column)\r\n        if res is None:\r\n            continue\r\n        cy, gl, s2, s3, dg = res\r\n        cy_parts.append(cy)\r\n        gl_parts.append(gl)\r\n        s2_parts.append(s2)\r\n        s3_parts.append(s3)\r\n        dg_parts.append(dg)\r\n        done += 1\r\n    con.close()\r\n    if done == 0:\r\n        raise RuntimeError(\"Stage 07 processed no yearly files.\")\r\n\r\n    cy_df = pd.concat(cy_parts, ignore_index=True).sort_values([\"year\", \"country\"]).reset_index(drop=True)\r\n    gl_df = pd.concat(gl_parts, ignore_index=True).sort_values(\"year\").reset_index(drop=True)\r\n    s2_df = pd.concat(s2_parts, ignore_index=True).sort_values([\"year\", \"sitc2\"]).reset_index(drop=True)\r\n    s3_df = pd.concat(s3_parts, ignore_index=True).sort_values([\"year\", \"sitc3\"]).reset_index(drop=True)\r\n    dg_df = pd.concat(dg_parts, ignore_index=True).sort_values(\"year\").reset_index(drop=True)\r\n\r\n    cy_df.to_csv(artifacts.country_year_path, index=False)\r\n    gl_df.to_csv(artifacts.global_path, index=False)\r\n    s2_df.to_csv(artifacts.sitc2_path, index=False)\r\n    s3_df.to_csv(artifacts.sitc3_path, index=False)\r\n    dg_df.to_csv(artifacts.diagnostics_path, index=False)\r\n\r\n    fig_dir = artifacts.fig_dir\r\n    fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(12, 5))\r\n    ax.plot(gl_df[\"year\"], gl_df[\"global_export_distance_km\"], label=\"Exports\", color=\"steelblue\", linewidth=2)\r\n    ax.plot(\r\n        gl_df[\"year\"],\r\n        gl_df[\"global_import_distance_km\"],\r\n        label=\"Imports\",\r\n        color=\"tomato\",\r\n        linewidth=2,\r\n        linestyle=\"--\",\r\n    )\r\n    ax.set_xlabel(\"Year\")\r\n    ax.set_ylabel(\"Average distance (km)\")\r\n    ax.legend()\r\n    ax.grid(True, linewidth=0.5, alpha=0.4)\r\n    fig.tight_layout()\r\n    fig.savefig(fig_dir / f\"distance_global_line_{tag}.png\", dpi=300)\r\n    plt.close(fig)\r\n\r\n    cy = cy_df.copy()\r\n    cy[\"year\"] = cy[\"year\"].astype(int)\r\n    cy[\"country\"] = cy[\"country\"].astype(str)\r\n    for flow, col, cmap in [\r\n        (\"exports\", \"avg_export_distance_km\", \"Blues\"),\r\n        (\"imports\", \"avg_import_distance_km\", \"Reds\"),\r\n    ]:\r\n        cy[col] = pd.to_numeric(cy[col], errors=\"coerce\")\r\n        mat = make_matrix(cy, \"country\", \"year\", col)\r\n        mat = mat.loc[mat.median(axis=1).sort_values(ascending=False).index]\r\n        vmin = float(np.nanpercentile(mat.values, 1))\r\n        vmax = float(np.nanpercentile(mat.values, 99))\r\n        plot_heatmap(\r\n            mat,\r\n            cmap,\r\n            fig_dir / f\"distance_country_heatmap_{flow}_{tag}.png\",\r\n            vmin,\r\n            vmax,\r\n            4,\r\n            (14, 18),\r\n        )\r\n\r\n    s2 = s2_df.copy()\r\n    s2[\"year\"] = s2[\"year\"].astype(int)\r\n    s2[\"sitc2\"] = s2[\"sitc2\"].astype(str).str.zfill(2)\r\n    s2d = read_code_dict(config.dataset_reference_dir / \"sitc2-2digit.txt\", \"sitc2\")\r\n    if not s2d.empty:\r\n        s2d[\"sitc2\"] = s2d[\"sitc2\"].astype(str).str.zfill(2)\r\n    for flow, col, cmap in [\r\n        (\"exports\", \"global_export_distance_km\", \"Blues\"),\r\n        (\"imports\", \"global_import_distance_km\", \"Reds\"),\r\n    ]:\r\n        s2[col] = pd.to_numeric(s2[col], errors=\"coerce\")\r\n        mat = make_matrix(s2, \"sitc2\", \"year\", col).sort_index()\r\n        vmin = float(np.nanpercentile(mat.values, 1))\r\n        vmax = float(np.nanpercentile(mat.values, 99))\r\n        labels = []\r\n        for code in mat.index:\r\n            if s2d.empty:\r\n                labels.append(code)\r\n            else:\r\n                nick = s2d.loc[s2d[\"sitc2\"] == code, \"nickname\"].values\r\n                labels.append(f\"{code}-{nick[0]}\" if len(nick) and str(nick[0]).strip() else code)\r\n        plot_heatmap(\r\n            mat,\r\n            cmap,\r\n            fig_dir / f\"distance_sitc2_heatmap_{flow}_{tag}.png\",\r\n            vmin,\r\n            vmax,\r\n            5,\r\n            (20, 14),\r\n            labels,\r\n        )\r\n\r\n    s3 = s3_df.copy()\r\n    s3[\"year\"] = s3[\"year\"].astype(int)\r\n    s3[\"sitc3\"] = s3[\"sitc3\"].astype(str).str.zfill(3)\r\n    s3d = read_code_dict(config.dataset_reference_dir / \"sitc2-3digit.txt\", \"sitc3\")\r\n    if not s3d.empty:\r\n        s3d[\"sitc3\"] = s3d[\"sitc3\"].astype(str).str.zfill(3)\r\n    for flow, col, cmap in [\r\n        (\"exports\", \"global_export_distance_km\", \"Blues\"),\r\n        (\"imports\", \"global_import_distance_km\", \"Reds\"),\r\n    ]:\r\n        s3[col] = pd.to_numeric(s3[col], errors=\"coerce\")\r\n        mat = make_matrix(s3, \"sitc3\", \"year\", col).sort_index()\r\n        vmin = float(np.nanpercentile(mat.values, 1))\r\n        vmax = float(np.nanpercentile(mat.values, 99))\r\n        labels = []\r\n        for code in mat.index:\r\n            if s3d.empty:\r\n                labels.append(code)\r\n            else:\r\n                nick = s3d.loc[s3d[\"sitc3\"] == code, \"nickname\"].values\r\n                labels.append(f\"{code}-{nick[0]}\" if len(nick) and str(nick[0]).strip() else code)\r\n        plot_heatmap(\r\n            mat,\r\n            cmap,\r\n            fig_dir / f\"distance_sitc3_heatmap_{flow}_{tag}.png\",\r\n            vmin,\r\n            vmax,\r\n            4,\r\n            (14, 40),\r\n            labels,\r\n        )\r\n\r\n    def country_panels(country_list, outfile):\r\n        sub = cy_df[cy_df[\"country\"].isin(country_list)].copy()\r\n        sub[\"year\"] = pd.to_numeric(sub[\"year\"], errors=\"coerce\").astype(int)\r\n        sub[\"avg_export_distance_km\"] = pd.to_numeric(sub[\"avg_export_distance_km\"], errors=\"coerce\")\r\n        sub = sub.dropna(subset=[\"avg_export_distance_km\"])\r\n        if sub.empty:\r\n            return\r\n        ymin = sub[\"avg_export_distance_km\"].min()\r\n        ymax = sub[\"avg_export_distance_km\"].max()\r\n        n = len(country_list)\r\n        ncol = min(4, n)\r\n        nrow = (n + ncol - 1) // ncol\r\n        fig, axes = plt.subplots(nrow, ncol, figsize=(4 * ncol, 3 * nrow), sharex=True, sharey=True)\r\n        axes = np.array(axes).flatten()\r\n        for ax, country in zip(axes, country_list):\r\n            d = sub[sub[\"country\"] == country].sort_values(\"year\")\r\n            ax.plot(d[\"year\"], d[\"avg_export_distance_km\"], linewidth=1.8)\r\n            ax.set_ylim(ymin, ymax)\r\n            ax.text(0.05, 0.90, country, transform=ax.transAxes, fontsize=10, fontweight=\"bold\")\r\n            ax.tick_params(axis=\"both\", labelsize=8)\r\n        for ax in axes[n:]:\r\n            ax.axis(\"off\")\r\n        fig.tight_layout()\r\n        fig.savefig(outfile, dpi=300)\r\n        plt.close(fig)\r\n\r\n    country_panels(\r\n        [\"USA\", \"CHN\", \"DEU\", \"JPN\", \"FRA\", \"GBR\", \"CAN\", \"KOR\"],\r\n        fig_dir / f\"distance_panels_exports_major_economies_{tag}.png\",\r\n    )\r\n    country_panels(\r\n        [\"ARG\", \"BRA\", \"CHL\", \"COL\", \"DOM\", \"MEX\", \"PER\", \"URY\"],\r\n        fig_dir / f\"distance_panels_exports_latin_america_{tag}.png\",\r\n    )\r\n    country_panels(\r\n        [\"VNM\", \"THA\", \"IND\", \"IDN\", \"SAU\", \"EGY\", \"NGA\", \"ZAF\"],\r\n        fig_dir / f\"distance_panels_exports_emerging_{tag}.png\",\r\n    )\r\n\r\n    logger.info(\"Stage 07 complete. years=%d output=%s\", done, artifacts.stage_dir)\r\n    return {\r\n        \"run_dir\": str(run_dir),\r\n        \"stage_dir\": str(artifacts.stage_dir),\r\n        \"country_year_path\": str(artifacts.country_year_path),\r\n        \"global_path\": str(artifacts.global_path),\r\n        \"sitc2_path\": str(artifacts.sitc2_path),\r\n        \"sitc3_path\": str(artifacts.sitc3_path),\r\n    }\r\n"}}
{"timestamp":"2026-03-10T23:45:03.113Z","type":"response_item","payload":{"type":"function_call_output","call_id":"call_yXWQtoaAYGaZp1VJwb5yKvjZ","output":"Exit code: 0\nWall time: 0.5 seconds\nOutput:\nfrom __future__ import annotations\r\n\r\nimport re\r\nfrom dataclasses import dataclass\r\nfrom pathlib import Path\r\n\r\nimport duckdb\r\nimport matplotlib.pyplot as plt\r\nimport numpy as np\r\nimport pandas as pd\r\n\r\n\r\n@dataclass(frozen=True)\r\nclass DistanceArtifacts:\r\n    stage_dir: Path\r\n    data_dir: Path\r\n    fig_dir: Path\r\n    country_year_path: Path\r\n    global_path: Path\r\n    sitc2_path: Path\r\n    sitc3_path: Path\r\n    diagnostics_path: Path\r\n\r\n\r\ndef build_distance_artifact_paths(stage_dir: Path, tag: str) -> DistanceArtifacts:\r\n    data_dir = stage_dir / \"data\"\r\n    fig_dir = stage_dir / \"fig\"\r\n    return DistanceArtifacts(\r\n        stage_dir=stage_dir,\r\n        data_dir=data_dir,\r\n        fig_dir=fig_dir,\r\n        country_year_path=data_dir / f\"distance_country_year_{tag}.csv\",\r\n        global_path=data_dir / f\"distance_global_{tag}.csv\",\r\n        sitc2_path=data_dir / f\"distance_global_sitc2_{tag}.csv\",\r\n        sitc3_path=data_dir / f\"distance_global_sitc3_{tag}.csv\",\r\n        diagnostics_path=data_dir / f\"distance_diagnostics_{tag}.csv\",\r\n    )\r\n\r\n\r\ndef build_con():\r\n    con = duckdb.connect(database=\":memory:\")\r\n    con.execute(\"PRAGMA threads=8;\")\r\n    return con\r\n\r\n\r\ndef load_od(con, od_path: Path) -> None:\r\n    con.execute(\"DROP TABLE IF EXISTS od;\")\r\n    con.execute(\r\n        \"\"\"\r\n        CREATE TABLE od AS\r\n        SELECT\r\n            CAST(origin AS VARCHAR) AS origin,\r\n            CAST(destination AS VARCHAR) AS destination,\r\n            CAST(distance_km AS DOUBLE) AS distance_km\r\n        FROM read_csv(\r\n            ?,\r\n            columns = {'origin':'VARCHAR','destination':'VARCHAR','distance_km':'DOUBLE'},\r\n            header  = TRUE,\r\n            delim   = ','\r\n        )\r\n        \"\"\",\r\n        [str(od_path)],\r\n    )\r\n\r\n\r\ndef compute_year(con, year: int, trade_dir: Path, value_col: str, exclude_self: bool = True):\r\n    fpath = trade_dir / f\"S2_{year}.parquet\"\r\n    if not fpath.exists():\r\n        return None\r\n    con.execute(\"DROP TABLE IF EXISTS flows;\")\r\n    con.execute(\r\n        f\"\"\"\r\n        CREATE TABLE flows AS\r\n        SELECT\r\n            CAST(exporter AS VARCHAR) AS exporter,\r\n            CAST(importer AS VARCHAR) AS importer,\r\n            LPAD(CAST(commoditycode AS VARCHAR), 4, '0') AS commoditycode,\r\n            CAST({value_col} AS DOUBLE) AS value_final\r\n        FROM read_parquet(?)\r\n        WHERE {value_col} IS NOT NULL AND {value_col} >= 0\r\n        \"\"\",\r\n        [str(fpath)],\r\n    )\r\n    if exclude_self:\r\n        con.execute(\"DELETE FROM flows WHERE exporter = importer;\")\r\n    con.execute(\"DROP TABLE IF EXISTS flows_coded;\")\r\n    con.execute(\r\n        \"\"\"\r\n        CREATE TABLE flows_coded AS\r\n        SELECT exporter, importer, commoditycode,\r\n               SUBSTR(commoditycode, 1, 2) AS sitc2,\r\n               SUBSTR(commoditycode, 1, 3) AS sitc3,\r\n               value_final\r\n        FROM flows\r\n        \"\"\"\r\n    )\r\n    con.execute(\"DROP TABLE IF EXISTS joined;\")\r\n    con.execute(\r\n        \"\"\"\r\n        CREATE TABLE joined AS\r\n        SELECT f.exporter, f.importer, f.sitc2, f.sitc3, f.value_final, o.distance_km\r\n        FROM flows_coded f\r\n        LEFT JOIN od o ON f.exporter = o.origin AND f.importer = o.destination\r\n        \"\"\"\r\n    )\r\n    diag = con.execute(\r\n        \"\"\"\r\n        SELECT ?::INT AS year,\r\n               COUNT(*) AS n_flows,\r\n               SUM(value_final) AS total_trade_value,\r\n               SUM(CASE WHEN distance_km IS NULL THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS n_missing_distance,\r\n               SUM(CASE WHEN distance_km IS NULL THEN value_final ELSE 0 END) AS value_missing_distance\r\n        FROM joined\r\n        \"\"\",\r\n        [year],\r\n    ).fetchdf()\r\n    con.execute(\"DELETE FROM joined WHERE distance_km IS NULL;\")\r\n    exp_cy = con.execute(\r\n        \"\"\"\r\n        SELECT ?::INT AS year, exporter AS country,\r\n               SUM(value_final) AS total_exports,\r\n               SUM(value_final * distance_km) / NULLIF(SUM(value_final), 0) AS avg_export_distance_km\r\n        FROM joined GROUP BY exporter\r\n        \"\"\",\r\n        [year],\r\n    ).fetchdf()\r\n    imp_cy = con.execute(\r\n        \"\"\"\r\n        SELECT ?::INT AS year, importer AS country,\r\n               SUM(value_final) AS total_imports,\r\n               SUM(value_final * distance_km) / NULLIF(SUM(value_final), 0) AS avg_import_distance_km\r\n        FROM joined GROUP BY importer\r\n        \"\"\",\r\n        [year],\r\n    ).fetchdf()\r\n    cy = pd.merge(exp_cy, imp_cy, on=[\"year\", \"country\"], how=\"outer\")\r\n    tot_x = cy[\"total_exports\"].sum(skipna=True)\r\n    tot_m = cy[\"total_imports\"].sum(skipna=True)\r\n    gx = (\r\n        (cy[\"total_exports\"] * cy[\"avg_export_distance_km\"]).sum(skipna=True) / tot_x\r\n        if tot_x\r\n        else float(\"nan\")\r\n    )\r\n    gm = (\r\n        (cy[\"total_imports\"] * cy[\"avg_import_distance_km\"]).sum(skipna=True) / tot_m\r\n        if tot_m\r\n        else float(\"nan\")\r\n    )\r\n    global_df = pd.DataFrame(\r\n        [\r\n            {\r\n                \"year\": year,\r\n                \"global_export_distance_km\": gx,\r\n                \"global_import_distance_km\": gm,\r\n                \"global_total_exports\": tot_x,\r\n                \"global_total_imports\": tot_m,\r\n                \"global_exports_minus_imports\": tot_x - tot_m,\r\n                \"global_export_minus_import_distance_km\": gx - gm,\r\n            }\r\n        ]\r\n    )\r\n    s2x = con.execute(\r\n        \"\"\"\r\n        SELECT ?::INT AS year, sitc2,\r\n               SUM(value_final) AS total_exports,\r\n               SUM(value_final * distance_km) / NULLIF(SUM(value_final), 0) AS global_export_distance_km\r\n        FROM joined GROUP BY sitc2\r\n        \"\"\",\r\n        [year],\r\n    ).fetchdf()\r\n    s2m = con.execute(\r\n        \"\"\"\r\n        SELECT ?::INT AS year, sitc2,\r\n               SUM(value_final) AS total_imports,\r\n               SUM(value_final * distance_km) / NULLIF(SUM(value_final), 0) AS global_import_distance_km\r\n        FROM joined GROUP BY sitc2\r\n        \"\"\",\r\n        [year],\r\n    ).fetchdf()\r\n    sitc2_df = pd.merge(s2x, s2m, on=[\"year\", \"sitc2\"], how=\"outer\")\r\n    s3x = con.execute(\r\n        \"\"\"\r\n        SELECT ?::INT AS year, sitc3,\r\n               SUM(value_final) AS total_exports,\r\n               SUM(value_final * distance_km) / NULLIF(SUM(value_final), 0) AS global_export_distance_km\r\n        FROM joined GROUP BY sitc3\r\n        \"\"\",\r\n        [year],\r\n    ).fetchdf()\r\n    s3m = con.execute(\r\n        \"\"\"\r\n        SELECT ?::INT AS year, sitc3,\r\n               SUM(value_final) AS total_imports,\r\n               SUM(value_final * distance_km) / NULLIF(SUM(value_final), 0) AS global_import_distance_km\r\n        FROM joined GROUP BY sitc3\r\n        \"\"\",\r\n        [year],\r\n    ).fetchdf()\r\n    sitc3_df = pd.merge(s3x, s3m, on=[\"year\", \"sitc3\"], how=\"outer\")\r\n    return cy, global_df, sitc2_df, sitc3_df, diag\r\n\r\n\r\ndef read_code_dict(path: Path, code_col: str) -> pd.DataFrame:\r\n    if not path.exists():\r\n        return pd.DataFrame(columns=[code_col, \"name\", \"nickname\"])\r\n    with open(path, \"r\", encoding=\"utf-8\", errors=\"replace\") as fh:\r\n        lines = [ln.strip() for ln in fh if ln.strip() and not ln.strip().startswith(\"#\")]\r\n    for delim in [\"\\t\", \"|\", \";\", \",\"]:\r\n        rows = [ln.split(delim) for ln in lines]\r\n        k = max(len(r) for r in rows) if rows else 0\r\n        if k >= 3:\r\n            rows = [r + [\"\"] * max(0, k - len(r)) for r in rows]\r\n            df = pd.DataFrame(rows).iloc[:, :3]\r\n            df.columns = [code_col, \"name\", \"nickname\"]\r\n            for c in df.columns:\r\n                df[c] = df[c].astype(str).str.strip()\r\n            if len(df) and not re.fullmatch(r\"\\d{1,4}\", str(df.iloc[0][code_col])):\r\n                df = df.iloc[1:].reset_index(drop=True)\r\n            return df\r\n    return pd.DataFrame(columns=[code_col, \"name\", \"nickname\"])\r\n\r\n\r\ndef make_matrix(df, row_col, col_col, val_col):\r\n    df2 = df[[row_col, col_col, val_col]].groupby([row_col, col_col], as_index=False)[val_col].mean()\r\n    return df2.pivot(index=row_col, columns=col_col, values=val_col).sort_index(axis=1)\r\n\r\n\r\ndef plot_heatmap(mat, cmap, outpath, vmin, vmax, ytick_fs, figsize, yticklabels=None):\r\n    fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=figsize)\r\n    im = ax.imshow(\r\n        mat.to_numpy(dtype=float),\r\n        aspect=\"auto\",\r\n        interpolation=\"nearest\",\r\n        cmap=cmap,\r\n        vmin=vmin,\r\n        vmax=vmax,\r\n    )\r\n    years = mat.columns.tolist()\r\n    ax.set_xticks(range(len(years)))\r\n    ax.set_xticklabels([str(y) for y in years], rotation=90, fontsize=8)\r\n    rows = mat.index.tolist()\r\n    ax.set_yticks(range(len(rows)))\r\n    ax.set_yticklabels(yticklabels if yticklabels is not None else rows, fontsize=ytick_fs)\r\n    cbar = fig.colorbar(im, ax=ax)\r\n    cbar.set_label(\"Average distance (km)\")\r\n    fig.tight_layout()\r\n    fig.savefig(outpath, dpi=300)\r\n    plt.close(fig)\r\n"}}

{"timestamp":"2026-03-10T23:45:52.425Z","type":"response_item","payload":{"type":"function_call_output","call_id":"call_jc7kEGKVrMdC4E8CrR1oIxsG","output":"Exit code: 0\nWall time: 0.9 seconds\nOutput:\n# ============================================================\n# Stage 07 — Visualizations\n# ============================================================\nimport re\nfrom pathlib import Path\n\nimport numpy as np\nimport pandas as pd\nimport matplotlib.pyplot as plt\n\n_DPI            = 300\n_EXP_COLOR      = \"steelblue\"\n_IMP_COLOR      = \"tomato\"\n_CMAP_EXP       = \"Blues\"\n_CMAP_IMP       = \"Reds\"\n_FONT_COUNTRY   = 4\n_FONT_SITC2     = 5\n_FONT_SITC3     = 4\n_FSIZ_GLOBAL    = (12, 5)\n_FSIZ_CY        = (14, 18)\n_FSIZ_SITC2     = (20, 14)\n_FSIZ_SITC3     = (14, 40)\n_s07_tag        = f\"{TRADE_YEAR_START}_{TRADE_YEAR_END}\"\n\n\ndef _s07_read_code_dict(path, code_col):\n    \"\"\"\n    Load a SITC reference dict. Auto-detects delimiter.\n    Returns DataFrame with columns [code_col, 'name', 'nickname'].\n    Gracefully returns an empty frame if file is missing.\n    Accepts str or Path.\n    \"\"\"\n    path = Path(path)  # Option A: coerce str -> Path\n\n    if not path.exists():\n        logger.warning(\"Stage 07: ref dict not found: %s — nicknaming unavailable.\", path)\n        return pd.DataFrame(columns=[code_col, \"name\", \"nickname\"])\n\n    with open(path, \"r\", encoding=\"utf-8\", errors=\"replace\") as fh:\n        lines = [ln.strip() for ln in fh if ln.strip() and not ln.strip().startswith(\"#\")]\n\n    for _delim in [\"\\t\", \"|\", \";\", \",\"]:\n        rows = [ln.split(_delim) for ln in lines]\n        k = max(len(r) for r in rows) if rows else 0\n        if k >= 3:\n            rows = [r + [\"\"] * max(0, k - len(r)) for r in rows]\n            df = pd.DataFrame(rows).iloc[:, :3]\n            df.columns = [code_col, \"name\", \"nickname\"]\n\n            for c in df.columns:\n                df[c] = df[c].astype(str).str.strip()\n\n            # If first row looks like a header (code is not 1-4 digits), drop it\n            if len(df) and not re.fullmatch(r\"\\d{1,4}\", str(df.iloc[0][code_col])):\n                df = df.iloc[1:].reset_index(drop=True)\n\n            return df\n\n    return pd.DataFrame(columns=[code_col, \"name\", \"nickname\"])\n\n\ndef _s07_make_matrix(df, row_col, col_col, val_col):\n    df2 = df[[row_col, col_col, val_col]].groupby([row_col, col_col], as_index=False)[val_col].mean()\n    return df2.pivot(index=row_col, columns=col_col, values=val_col).sort_index(axis=1)\n\n\ndef _s07_plot_heatmap(mat, cmap, outpath, vmin, vmax, ytick_fs, figsize, yticklabels=None):\n    fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=figsize)\n    im = ax.imshow(\n        mat.to_numpy(dtype=float),\n        aspect=\"auto\",\n        interpolation=\"nearest\",\n        cmap=cmap,\n        vmin=vmin,\n        vmax=vmax,\n    )\n\n    years = mat.columns.tolist()\n    ax.set_xticks(range(len(years)))\n    ax.set_xticklabels([str(y) for y in years], rotation=90, fontsize=8)\n\n    rows = mat.index.tolist()\n    ax.set_yticks(range(len(rows)))\n    ax.set_yticklabels(yticklabels if yticklabels is not None else rows, fontsize=ytick_fs)\n\n    cbar = fig.colorbar(im, ax=ax)\n    cbar.set_label(\"Average distance (km)\")\n\n    fig.tight_layout()\n    outpath = Path(outpath)  # safe if passed as str\n    fig.savefig(outpath, dpi=_DPI)\n    plt.close(fig)\n\n\n# ── 1) Global line plot (exports + imports) ──────────────────\n_g = _s07_gl_df.copy()\n_g[\"year\"] = _g[\"year\"].astype(int)\n\nfig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=_FSIZ_GLOBAL)\nax.plot(\n    _g[\"year\"],\n    _g[\"global_export_distance_km\"],\n    label=\"Exports\",\n    color=_EXP_COLOR,\n    linewidth=2,\n)\nax.plot(\n    _g[\"year\"],\n    _g[\"global_import_distance_km\"],\n    label=\"Imports\",\n    color=_IMP_COLOR,\n    linewidth=2,\n    linestyle=\"--\",\n)\n\nax.set_xlabel(\"Year\")\nax.set_ylabel(\"Average distance (km)\")\nax.legend()\nax.grid(True, linewidth=0.5, alpha=0.4)\n\n_out = Path(DIST_FIG) / f\"distance_global_line_{_s07_tag}.png\"\nfig.tight_layout()\nfig.savefig(_out, dpi=_DPI)\nplt.close(fig)\nlogger.info(\"Stage 07: saved %s\", _out.name)\n\n\n# ── 2) Country heatmaps ──────────────────────────────────────\n_cy = _s07_cy_df.copy()\n_cy[\"year\"] = _cy[\"year\"].astype(int)\n_cy[\"country\"] = _cy[\"country\"].astype(str)\n\nfor _flow, _col, _cmap in [\n    (\"exports\", \"avg_export_distance_km\", _CMAP_EXP),\n    (\"imports\", \"avg_import_distance_km\", _CMAP_IMP),\n]:\n    _cy[_col] = pd.to_numeric(_cy[_col], errors=\"coerce\")\n    _mat = _s07_make_matrix(_cy, \"country\", \"year\", _col)\n    if _mat.empty:\n        logger.warning(\"Stage 07: country heatmap skipped (empty) for %s\", _flow)\n        continue\n\n    _vmin = float(np.nanpercentile(_mat.values, 1))\n    _vmax = float(np.nanpercentile(_mat.values, 99))\n\n    # Sort countries by median distance (desc)\n    _mat = _mat.loc[_mat.median(axis=1).sort_values(ascending=False).index]\n\n    _out = Path(DIST_FIG) / f\"distance_country_heatmap_{_flow}_{_s07_tag}.png\"\n    _s07_plot_heatmap(_mat, _cmap, _out, _vmin, _vmax, _FONT_COUNTRY, _FSIZ_CY)\n    logger.info(\"Stage 07: saved %s\", _out.name)\n\n\n# ── 3) SITC-2 heatmaps ───────────────────────────────────────\n_s2 = _s07_s2_df.copy()\n_s2[\"year\"]  = _s2[\"year\"].astype(int)\n_s2[\"sitc2\"] = _s2[\"sitc2\"].astype(str).str.zfill(2)\n\n_s2d = _s07_read_code_dict(SITC2_DICT_PATH, \"sitc2\")\nif not _s2d.empty:\n    _s2d[\"sitc2\"] = _s2d[\"sitc2\"].astype(str).str.zfill(2)\n\nfor _flow, _col, _cmap in [\n    (\"exports\", \"global_export_distance_km\", _CMAP_EXP),\n    (\"imports\", \"global_import_distance_km\", _CMAP_IMP),\n]:\n    _s2[_col] = pd.to_numeric(_s2[_col], errors=\"coerce\")\n    _mat = _s07_make_matrix(_s2, \"sitc2\", \"year\", _col).sort_index()\n    if _mat.empty:\n        logger.warning(\"Stage 07: SITC2 heatmap skipped (empty) for %s\", _flow)\n        continue\n\n    _vmin = float(np.nanpercentile(_mat.values, 1))\n    _vmax = float(np.nanpercentile(_mat.values, 99))\n\n    _lbls = []\n    for _c in _mat.index:\n        if _s2d.empty:\n            _lbls.append(_c)\n        else:\n            _n = _s2d.loc[_s2d[\"sitc2\"] == _c, \"nickname\"].values\n            _lbls.append(f\"{_c}-{_n[0]}\" if len(_n) and str(_n[0]).strip() else _c)\n\n    _out = Path(DIST_FIG) / f\"distance_sitc2_heatmap_{_flow}_{_s07_tag}.png\"\n    _s07_plot_heatmap(_mat, _cmap, _out, _vmin, _vmax, _FONT_SITC2, _FSIZ_SITC2, _lbls)\n    logger.info(\"Stage 07: saved %s\", _out.name)\n\n\n# ── 4) SITC-3 heatmaps ───────────────────────────────────────\n_s3 = _s07_s3_df.copy()\n_s3[\"year\"]  = _s3[\"year\"].astype(int)\n_s3[\"sitc3\"] = _s3[\"sitc3\"].astype(str).str.zfill(3)\n\n_s3d = _s07_read_code_dict(SITC3_DICT_PATH, \"sitc3\")\nif not _s3d.empty:\n    _s3d[\"sitc3\"] = _s3d[\"sitc3\"].astype(str).str.zfill(3)\n\nfor _flow, _col, _cmap in [\n    (\"exports\", \"global_export_distance_km\", _CMAP_EXP),\n    (\"imports\", \"global_import_distance_km\", _CMAP_IMP),\n]:\n    _s3[_col] = pd.to_numeric(_s3[_col], errors=\"coerce\")\n    _mat = _s07_make_matrix(_s3, \"sitc3\", \"year\", _col).sort_index()\n    if _mat.empty:\n        logger.warning(\"Stage 07: SITC3 heatmap skipped (empty) for %s\", _flow)\n        continue\n\n    _vmin = float(np.nanpercentile(_mat.values, 1))\n    _vmax = float(np.nanpercentile(_mat.values, 99))\n\n    _lbls = []\n    for _c in _mat.index:\n        if _s3d.empty:\n            _lbls.append(_c)\n        else:\n            _n = _s3d.loc[_s3d[\"sitc3\"] == _c, \"nickname\"].values\n            _lbls.append(f\"{_c}-{_n[0]}\" if len(_n) and str(_n[0]).strip() else _c)\n\n    _out = Path(DIST_FIG) / f\"distance_sitc3_heatmap_{_flow}_{_s07_tag}.png\"\n    _s07_plot_heatmap(_mat, _cmap, _out, _vmin, _vmax, _FONT_SITC3, _FSIZ_SITC3, _lbls)\n    logger.info(\"Stage 07: saved %s\", _out.name)\n\nprint(f\"[Stage 07] All visualizations saved -> {DIST_FIG}\")"}}

_mat.empty:\n        logger.warning(\"Stage 07: SITC3 heatmap skipped (empty) for %s\", _flow)\n        continue\n\n    _vmin = float(np.nanpercentile(_mat.values, 1))\n    _vmax = float(np.nanpercentile(_mat.values, 99))\n\n    _lbls = []\n    for _c in _mat.index:\n        if _s3d.empty:\n            _lbls.append(_c)\n        else:\n            _n = _s3d.loc[_s3d[\"sitc3\"] == _c, \"nickname\"].values\n            _lbls.append(f\"{_c}-{_n[0]}\" if len(_n) and str(_n[0]).strip() else _c)\n\n    _out = Path(DIST_FIG) / f\"distance_sitc3_heatmap_{_flow}_{_s07_tag}.png\"\n    _s07_plot_heatmap(_mat, _cmap, _out, _vmin, _vmax, _FONT_SITC3, _FSIZ_SITC3, _lbls)\n    logger.info(\"Stage 07: saved %s\", _out.name)\n\nprint(f\"[Stage 07] All visualizations saved -> {DIST_FIG}\")"}}

wrap_width is None:\n+        return labels\n+    formatted = []\n+    for label in labels:\n+        text = str(label)\n+        if len(text) <= wrap_width:\n+            formatted.append(text)\n+        else:\n+            formatted.append(\"\\n\".join(textwrap.wrap(text, width=wrap_width)))\n+    return formatted\n+\n+\n+def plot_heatmap(mat, cmap, outpath, vmin, vmax, ytick_fs, figsize, yticklabels=None):\n     fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=figsize)\n     im = ax.imshow(\n         mat.to_numpy(dtype=float),\n         aspect=\"auto\",\n         interpolation=\"nearest\",\n@@\n     )\n     years = mat.columns.tolist()\n     ax.set_xticks(range(len(years)))\n     ax.set_xticklabels([str(y) for y in years], rotation=90, fontsize=8)\n     rows = mat.index.tolist()\n+    labels = yticklabels if yticklabels is not None else rows\n+    labels = [str(label) for label in labels]\n+    wrap_width = 22 if yticklabels is not None else None\n+    labels = _format_yticklabels(labels, plt.close(fig)\n*** End Patch\n"}}

pd.to_numeric(sub[\"avg_export_distance_km\"], errors=\"coerce\")\n+        sub = sub.dropna(subset=[\"avg_export_distance_km\"])\n+        if sub.empty:\n+            return\n+        ymin = sub[\"avg_export_distance_km\"].min()\n+        ymax = sub[\"avg_export_distance_km\"].max()\n+        n = len(country_list)\n+        ncol = min(4, n)\n+        nrow = (n + ncol - 1) // ncol\n+ ax.axis(\"off\")\n+        fig.tight_layout()\n+        fig.savefig(outfile, dpi=300)\n+        plt.close(fig)\n+\n+    country_panels(\n+        [\"USA\", \"CHN\", \"DEU\", \"JPN\", \"FRA\", \"GBR\",
