Editing files in other directories requires approval. Network access is restricted.\n# Escalation Requests\r\n\r\nCommands are run outside the sandbox if they are approved by the user, or match an existing rule that allows it to run unrestricted. The command string is split into independent command segments at shell control operators, including but not limited to:\r\n\r\n- Pipes: |\r\n- Logical operators: &&, ||\r\n- Command separators: ;\r\n- Subshell boundaries: (...), $(...)\r\n\r\nEach resulting segment is evaluated independently for sandbox restrictions and approval requirements.\r\n\r\nExample:\r\n\r\ngit pull | tee output.txt\r\n\r\nThis is treated as two command segments:\r\n\r\n[\"git\", \"pull\"]\r\n\r\n[\"tee\", \"output.txt\"]\r\n\r\n## How to request escalation\r\n\r\nIMPORTANT: To request approval to execute a command that will require escalated privileges:\r\n\r\n- Provide the `sandbox_permissions` parameter with the value `\"require_escalated\"`\r\n- Include a short question asking the user if they want to allow the action in `justification` parameter. e.g. \"Do you want to download and install dependencies for this project?\"\r\n- Optionally suggest a `prefix_rule` - this will be shown to the user with an option to persist the rule approval for future sessions.\r\n\r\nIf you run a command that is important to solving the user's query, but it fails because of sandboxing or with a likely sandbox-related network error (for example DNS/host resolution, registry/index access, or dependency download failure), rerun the command with \"require_escalated\". ALWAYS proceed to use the `justification` parameter - do not message the user before requesting approval for the command.\r\n\r\n## When to request escalation\r\n\r\nWhile commands are running inside the sandbox, here are some scenarios that will require escalation outside the sandbox:\r\n\r\n- You need to run a command that writes to a directory that requires it (e.g. running tests that write to /var)\r\n- You need to run a GUI app (e.g., open/xdg-open/osascript) to open browsers or files.\r\n- If you run a command that is important to solving the user's query, but it fails because of sandboxing or with a likely sandbox-related network error (for example DNS/host resolution, registry/index access, or dependency download failure), rerun the command with `require_escalated`. ALWAYS proceed to use the `sandbox_permissions` and `justification` parameters. do not message the user before requesting approval for the command.\r\n- You are about to take a potentially destructive action such as an `rm` or `git reset` that the user did not explicitly ask for.\r\n- Be judicious with escalating, but if completing the user's request requires it, you should do so - don't try and circumvent approvals by using other tools.\r\n\r\n## prefix_rule guidance\r\n\r\nWhen choosing a `prefix_rule`, request one that will allow you to fulfill similar requests from the user in the future without re-requesting escalation. It should be categorical and reasonably scoped to similar capabilities. You should rarely pass the entire command into `prefix_rule`.\r\n\r\n### Banned prefix_rules \r\nAvoid requesting overly broad prefixes that the user would be ill-advised to approve. For example, do not request [\"python3\"], [\"python\", \"-\"], or other similar prefixes.\r\nNEVER provide a prefix_rule argument for destructive commands like rm.\r\nNEVER provide a prefix_rule if your command uses a heredoc or herestring. \r\n\r\n### Examples\r\nGood examples of prefixes:\r\n- [\"npm\", \"run\", \"dev\"]\r\n- [\"gh\", \"pr\", \"check\"]\r\n- [\"pytest\"]\r\n- [\"cargo\", \"test\"]\r\n\n## Approved command prefixes\nThe following prefix rules have already been approved: \n The writable roots are `c:\\Python\\trade`, `c:\\Python\\trade`.\n</permissions instructions>"},{"type":"input_text","text":"<collaboration_mode># Collaboration Mode: Default\r\n\r\nYou are now in Default mode. Any previous instructions for other modes (e.g. Plan mode) are no longer active.\r\n\r\nYour active mode changes only when new developer instructions with a different `<collaboration_mode>...</collaboration_mode>` change it; user requests or tool descriptions do not change mode by themselves. Known mode names are Default and Plan.\r\n\r\n## request_user_input availability\r\n\r\nThe `request_user_input` tool is unavailable in Default mode. If you call it while in Default mode, it will return an error.\r\n\r\nIn Default mode, strongly prefer making reasonable assumptions and executing the user's request rather than stopping to ask questions. Never write a multiple choice question as a textual assistant message.\r\n</collaboration_mode>"}]},{"type":"message","role":"user","content":[{"type":"input_text","text":"# AGENTS.md instructions for c:\\Python\\trade\n\n<INSTRUCTIONS>\n## Skills\nA skill is a set of local instructions to follow that is stored in a `SKILL.md` file. Below is the list of skills that can be used. Each entry includes a name, description, and file path so you can open the source for full instructions when using a specific skill.\n### Available skills\n- skill-creator: Guide for creating effective skills. This skill should be used when users want to create a new skill (or update an existing skill) that extends Codex's capabilities with specialized knowledge, workflows, or tool integrations. (file: C:/Users/ROBERTODU/.codex/skills/.system/skill-creator/SKILL.md)\n- skill-installer: Install Codex skills into $CODEX_HOME/skills from a curated list or a GitHub repo path. Use when a user asks to list installable skills, install a curated skill, or install a skill from another repo (including private repos). (file: C:/Users/ROBERTODU/.codex/skills/.system/skill-installer/SKILL.md)\n### How to use skills\n- Discovery: The list above is the skills available in this session (name + description + file path). Skill bodies live on disk at the listed paths.\n- Trigger rules: If the user names a skill (with `$SkillName` or plain text) OR the task clearly matches a skill's description shown above, you must use that skill for that turn. Multiple mentions mean use them all. Do not carry skills across turns unless re-mentioned.\n- Missing/blocked: If a named skill isn't in the list or the path can't be read, say so briefly and continue with the best fallback.\n- How to use a skill (progressive disclosure):\n  1) After deciding to use a skill, open its `SKILL.md`. Read only enough to follow the workflow.\n  2) When `SKILL.md` references relative paths (e.g., `scripts/foo.py`), resolve them relative to the skill directory listed above first, and only consider other paths if needed.\n  3) If `SKILL.md` points to extra folders such as `references/`, load only the specific files needed for the request; don't bulk-load everything.\n  4) If `scripts/` exist, prefer running or patching them instead of retyping large code blocks.\n  5) If `assets/` or templates exist, reuse them instead of recreating from scratch.\n- Coordination and sequencing:\n  - If multiple skills apply, choose the minimal set that covers the request and state the order you'll use them.\n  - Announce which skill(s) you're using and why (one short line). If you skip an obvious skill, say why.\n- Context hygiene:\n  - Keep context small: summarize long sections instead of pasting them; only load extra files when needed.\n  - Avoid deep reference-chasing: prefer opening only files directly linked from `SKILL.md` unless you're blocked.\n  - When variants exist (frameworks, providers, domains), pick only the relevant reference file(s) and note that choice.\n- Safety and fallback: If a skill can't be applied cleanly (missing files, unclear instructions), state the issue, pick the next-best approach, and continue.\n</INSTRUCTIONS>"},{"type":"input_text","text":"<environment_context>\n  <cwd>c:\\Python\\trade</cwd>\n  <shell>powershell</shell>\n  <current_date>2026-03-11</current_date>\n  <timezone>America/New_York</timezone>\n</environment_context>"}]},{"type":"message","role":"user","content":[{"type":"input_text","text":"# Context from my IDE setup:\n\n## Active file: Geoanalisis/src/geoanalisis/config.py\n\n## Open tabs:\n- config.py: Geoanalisis/src/geoanalisis/config.py\n- run_structure.py: Geoanalisis/src/geoanalisis/utils/run_structure.py\n- paths.py: Geoanalisis/src/geoanalisis/utils/paths.py\n- __init__.py: Geoanalisis/src/geoanalisis/utils/__init__.py\n- trade_panel.py: Geoanalisis/src/geoanalisis/services/trade_panel.py\n\n## My request for Codex:\nI want a focused performance audit of the current pipeline implementation, especially Stage 03 barycenters and Stage 05 Moran.\r\n\r\nContext:\r\n- The original legacy notebook was faster.\r\n- The current modular pipeline is functionally much better, but some stages are taking too long.\r\n- I do NOT want blind refactoring.\r\n- I want a diagnosis first, then only high-impact safe optimizations.\r\n\r\nTasks:\r\n\r\n1. Inspect the code paths for:\r\n- stage_03_barycenters\r\n- stage_05_moran\r\nand the related service modules they call.\r\n\r\n2. Identify where time is most likely being spent, separating:\r\n- input loading / file I/O\r\n- repeated dataframe transformations\r\n- repeated merges / groupbys\r\n- distance calculations\r\n- spatial weights construction\r\n- Moran statistics loops\r\n- plotting / figure generation\r\n- writing outputs to disk\r\n\r\n3. Determine whether the current modular implementation is doing extra work that the legacy notebook did not do.\r\n\r\n4. Identify repeated computations that could be cached or reused safely.\r\n\r\n5. Propose the highest-impact optimizations, ranked by:\r\n- expected speedup\r\n- implementation risk\r\n- risk of changing analytical results\r\n\r\n6. Clearly distinguish:\r\n- safe performance optimizations that should preserve identical outputs\r\n- risky methodological changes that could alter results\r\n\r\n7. Do not implement changes yet.\r\nReturn only:\r\n- a concise performance diagnosis\r\n- the top recommended optimizations for Stage 03 and Stage 05\r\n- and which ones are safe to apply before the final baseline freeze.\n"}]},{"type":"compaction","encrypted_content":"gAAAAABpsPipgvHPYHVC8-tIIHpluBClga6vqdK69QldEe8xutgbzc7vBGoEr0P8bemrJAZ26l07FeMj778w-touMn2xVWYyxPXP2tV_Y-jmN7FcJIA-wn07Svusrxi0DX-nWWH1gB-pLU-0dsSoh3MZIxjEKggDmMjlY5G0DYbl23iqQXkpvZ1THykM1lbYSVMo6TIFrKYGfVhL6c2LiCQDQ9dvmF4Sn_RMFU6pWkaFVOthfjjlEeywNCAto6Yuhu31f0fAIGXa_W1BAPyQndq7ue57tehLKfVCYGNFlaf_V0_s_SXSfSiLQ5hyD-pZJkd9o6sq15icasal-MnXzeUN4hDPYWdRT2r14YDXPxYv6CDxVJCK2BfH5qBrwXdnvhxIofLT31J5KyP03grRNdYnTZwdTtAkf_PBtLhGnVKj9wwOXtYHqUECkoJlRz-ePye-H0K71MlQuoEmFOtvmy7AdsOtNcaelDd8mIpER5zncQPWSO02gkLEQRHA9ju92OwqO5GaMciWR-Hc-JmymZY5XKoBlehSS5vlI96bPXLnjkqxSoFFYMinWinhh_5cLWZQC7jr8gT5xWjrsMWB7St1mMjW6YuAhQJRWQLW4jFw-0ZH8JwHbwQSUGtwRepo4Lha8b7WFaiRRV9tEunk797D4A3V3Or5jao3e-ZeFs9i9l7dAgCLXQ_Aw2detfT8a6o-ESrIjyNiTMC-rR1EYsOhce_mzbLS3mdXuU2M_9dHRXp2tXEMZJFNhQwG_TN5fdmKWf6jZxP_CdwcczmqnCpe-fNLXhvWrAaHi-sLnxHDUCrOgPtwk0jSlLm30pCtAoE6NqE62Xy8yo6VQYEVqDIEHSuV7npk0YtRi77XFahHUZfdx-k-37C4zV8gAJK6b0Tr98xfl3q3KqymgRkmRjx38F83Swy8GitfBZF2T7PciydTVH87bcP2SGrJjO4O7QFPzYpVUw87QKnjowJG7dEKiLNoG_BKcYzmMKb32z6ipv4fk-_lm0d2QrkZo6ienFGTf2PD257IvzRU3Q7_C4CFBFVAkhc6Kt6UIYOy8NXo6TCYeFb_GzZdKcaCGcY09qugK4FQFo_g2hNB6QewkzjGt3y0EILdm3x0yJYBevGsbNjF5kMnpL4qi8mLpZ5kuQG7Tf6g8rEY-J6A19S7epjMEPt8orMFohBtyySLaPBG6Q5KddG_YWk41X-O_9-nbm68SbioJvACO1cZO5_o608vahzatU4bhECs5RSBb2RBkPkLvt8F1AgPiAQ4M92c4plxlEIKtfcPpUYXlSQpaGXowp8RYsyOVxShC0HcDEeWx_3L-qJJ2IfdeoyOKQ29ctPjJZe53R4Xclt7Jw14eTXgDOhEq5xNI7CD4kT6ih-ghmHoxtHYGM6N9Z4QFf_NZjlI7UOelJ9B7S6SQiKbRz3bD9eclpa7majqgc82Gx0X6N5LAHxrZ-BJBDwunX313mHxRmj2sRWf7LobZ8exoC25z9SAes7DQwcVmaGnRePtPerpM5jwuZYju_CDXj9sIZM6Qom7TR3URu_Uo9ubV116uh5ps7GaaP9yZYm95FGKsV7Up2R8f3rIEiQ4oBEwn_lAXFfRh7yGLrvKOZssXp21uYFX7q9Pnql6Vpm_lO6fiOYq9aJhwCzBx71jO0P2N_tagX9YpDDOOLfeAOjFgDLIBR62kVd0SCvSgC3xzwmcVbfSzbO8qu95jZMGHYgZ941NzvCASFaMmcI9p08qZDyHrFM9wjCWKeY1txqHOgkoBK6fDDxZlB_ADZH57ZZGjR6sdUVrhNfwXMblJy_xZltb-avDJV_hL4stt8km01gSFl2aU-q65Bjdce6ic-oGl3p2yqlZ0oYvoajhZrqd614HLrOKz4WtiA4_fr_wsSy3VilX2Vw-RRksG7EYjwZUSB8-qtmS1FKYEVuZWSaPX7QS {"timestamp":"2026-03-11T05:08:20.499Z","type":"event_msg","payload":{"type":"agent_message","message":"**Performance Diagnosis**\n\n**Stage 03 barycenters**\nMost time is likely going into repeated parquet scans and repeated per-country DataFrame work, not plotting.\n\nMain cost centers:\n- `input loading / file I/O`\n  - yearly `S2_YYYY.parquet` files are scanned multiple times\n  - `detect_all_country_codes()` performs an extra full scan\n  - global barycenters and country barycenters are computed in separate passes\n- `repeated dataframe transformations`\n  - exports/imports are aggregated year by year, then filtered, then merged with centroids repeatedly\n- `repeated merges / groupbys`\n  - partner-centroid joins happen every year for both exports and imports\n  - country-level weighted means are then recomputed in Python loops\n- `plotting / figure generation`\n  - present, but secondary versus the data scans\n- `writing outputs`\n  - low relative cost\n\nMost likely extra work versus the legacy notebook:\n- focus-country barycenters are computed once, then all-country barycenters are computed again, duplicating work for focus countries\n- `detect_all_country_codes()` rescans the dataset even though Stage 03 already traverses the same data\n- global barycenters are computed in a separate full pass\n- special map rendering reloads CSVs that were just produced\n\n**Stage 05 Moran**\nMost time is likely going into repeated yearly parquet scans, repeated product-level loops, and repeated Moran vector setup.\n\nMain cost centers:\n- `input loading / file I/O`\n  - each yearly parquet is scanned multiple times:\n    - once for global totals\n    - once for SITC2\n    - once for SITC3\n- `repeated dataframe transformations`\n  - grouped yearly results are rebuilt repeatedly\n- `spatial weights construction`\n  - OD matrix is rebuilt into libpysal weights each run\n  - current implementation uses slow row-wise Python iteration\n- `Moran statistics loops`\n  - per year, per flow, per product, the code repeatedly filters DataFrames and rebuilds zero-filled vectors\n  - this is likely the main compute bottleneck inside Stage 05\n- `plotting / figure generation`\n  - moderate, but probably smaller than aggregation + Moran loops\n- `writing outputs`\n  - low relative cost\n\nMost likely extra work versus the legacy notebook:\n- multiple scans per year instead of consolidated yearly aggregation\n- repeated DuckDB connection setup inside helper calls\n- rebuilding weights from OD every run\n- repeated per-product DataFrame filtering and repeated Series creation inside `moran_row()`\n\n**Top Recommended Optimizations**\n\n**Stage 03**\n1. Compute country barycenters once for all countries, then derive focus-country outputs from that result.\n- Expected speedup: high\n- Risk: low\n- Result-change risk: low\n- Safe before baseline freeze: yes\n\n2. Eliminate the separate `detect_all_country_codes()` scan by deriving the universe from the same pass used for country barycenters.\n- Expected speedup: medium to high\n- Risk: low\n- Result-change risk: low\n- Safe before baseline freeze: yes\n\n3. Replace the inner per-country Python subsetting loop with grouped weighted aggregations.\n- Expected speedup: high\n- Risk: medium\n- Result-change risk: low if formulas stay identical\n- Safe before baseline freeze: yes, but requires careful validation\n\n4. Reuse in-memory DataFrames for special-map rendering instead of rereading freshly written CSVs.\n- Expected speedup: medium\n- Risk: low\n- Result-change risk: none\n- Safe before baseline freeze: yes\n\n**Stage 05**\n1. Consolidate yearly aggregation so each parquet year is scanned once, not three times.\n- Expected speedup: very high\n- Risk: medium\n- Result-change risk: low if SQL semantics are preserved\n- Safe before baseline freeze: yes, with output parity check\n\n2. Reuse one DuckDB connection for the whole stage.\n- Expected speedup: medium\n- Risk: low\n- Result-change risk: none\n- Safe before baseline freeze: yes\n\n3. Cache/reuse spatial weights built from `OD_Matrix.csv`.\n- Expected speedup: medium\n- Risk: low\n- Result-change risk: none if OD matrix unchanged\n- Safe before baseline freeze: yes\n\n4. Replace repeated per-product DataFrame filtering with grouped iteration or prebuilt lookup structures.\n- Expected speedup: high\n- Risk: low to medium\n- Result-change risk: low\n- Safe before baseline freeze: yes\n\n5. Optimize `moran_row()` to use pre-aligned arrays/code-index mapping instead of rebuilding a pandas Series each call.\n- Expected speedup: high\n- Risk: medium\n- Result-change risk: low if alignment is preserved exactly\n- Safe before baseline freeze: yes, but should be validated carefully\n\n6. Replace `iterrows()` in weights construction with vectorized/grouped construction.\n- Expected speedup: medium\n- Risk: low\n- Result-change risk: none\n- Safe before baseline freeze: yes\n\n**Safe vs Risky**\n\nSafe optimizations that should preserve identical outputs:\n- removing duplicate Stage 03 passes\n- eliminating redundant code-detection scans\n- reusing in-memory Stage 03 outputs for plotting\n- consolidating Stage 05 yearly scans\n- keeping one DuckDB connection open\n- caching OD-based spatial weights\n- replacing repeated filtering with grouped iteration\n- optimizing `moran_row()` without changing formulas\n- vectorizing weights construction\n\nRisky methodological changes that could alter results:\n- changing inclusion rules for countries/products\n- changing weighting formulas, centroids logic, or Moran definitions\n- changing zero-fill behavior or neighborhood logic\n- changing figure set or suppressing canonical outputs\n- approximations to Moran or barycenter calculations\n\n**What I would apply before final baseline freeze**\nSafe, high-impact candidates:\n- Stage 03: single all-country barycenter pass + remove duplicate focus pass\n- Stage 03: remove separate country-code scan\n- Stage 05: single yearly scan per parquet\n- Stage 05: single DuckDB connection\n- Stage 05: cache spatial weights\n- Stage 05: grouped iteration instead of repeated product filtering\n\nThose are the best speed wins with low analytical risk.","phase":"final_answer"}}

Reuse one DuckDB connection for the whole stage.\n- Expected speedup: medium\n- Risk: low\n- Result-change risk: none\n- Safe before baseline freeze: yes\n\n3. Cache/reuse spatial weights built from `OD_Matrix.csv`.\n- Expected speedup: medium\n- Risk: low\n- Result-change risk: none if OD matrix unchanged\n- Safe before baseline freeze: yes\n\n4. Optimize `moran_row()` to use pre-aligned arrays/code-index mapping instead of rebuilding a pandas Series each call.\n- Expected speedup: high\n- Risk: medium\n- Result-change risk: low if alignment is preserved exactly\n- Safe before baseline freeze: yes, but should be validated carefully\n\n6. Replace `iterrows()` in weights construction with vectorized/grouped construction.\n- Expected speedup: medium\n- Risk: low\n- Result-change risk: none\n- Safe before baseline freeze: yes\n\n**Safe vs Risky**\n\nSafe optimizations that should preserve identical outputs:\n- removing duplicate Stage 03 passes\n- eliminating redundant code-detection scans\n- reusing in-memory Stage 03 outputs for plotting\n- consolidating Stage 05 yearly scans\n- keeping one DuckDB connection open\n- caching OD-based spatial weights\n- replacing repeated filtering with grouped iteration\n- optimizing `moran_row()` without changing formulas\n- vectorizing weights construction\n\nRisky methodological changes that could alter results:\n- changing inclusion rules for countries/products\n- changing weighting formulas, centroids logic, or Moran definitions\n- changing zero-fill behavior or neighborhood logic\n- changing figure set or suppressing canonical outputs\n- approximations to Moran or barycenter calculations\n\n**What I would apply before final baseline freeze**\nSafe, high-impact candidates:\n- Stage 03: single all-country barycenter pass + remove duplicate focus pass\n- Stage 03: remove separate country-code scan\n- Stage 05: single yearly scan per parquet\n- Stage 05: single DuckDB connection\n- Stage 05: cache spatial weights\n- Stage 05: grouped iteration instead of repeated product filtering\n\nThose are the best speed wins with low analytical risk."}],"phase":"final_answer"}}

Cache/reuse spatial weights built from `OD_Matrix.csv`.\n- Expected speedup: medium\n- Risk: low\n- Result-change risk: none if OD matrix unchanged\n- Safe before baseline freeze: yes\n\n4. Replace repeated per-product DataFrame filtering with grouped iteration or prebuilt lookup structures.\n- Expected speedup: high\n- Risk: low to medium\n- Result-change risk: low\n- Safe before baseline freeze: yes\n\n5. Optimize `moran_row()` to use pre-aligned arrays/code-index mapping instead of rebuilding a pandas Series each call.\n- Expected speedup: high\n- Risk: medium\n- Result-change risk: low if alignment is preserved exactly\n- Safe before baseline freeze: yes, but should be validated carefully\n\n6. Replace `iterrows()` in weights construction with vectorized/grouped construction.\n- Expected speedup: medium\n- Risk: low\n- Result-change risk: none\n- Safe before baseline freeze: yes\n\n**Safe vs Risky**\n\nSafe optimizations that should preserve identical outputs:\n- removing duplicate Stage 03 passes\n- eliminating redundant code-detection scans\n- reusing in-memory Stage 03 outputs for plotting\n- consolidating Stage 05 yearly scans\n- keeping one DuckDB connection open\n- caching OD-based spatial weights\n- replacing repeated filtering with grouped iteration\n- optimizing `moran_row()` without changing formulas\n- vectorizing weights construction\n\nRisky methodological changes that could alter results:\n- changing inclusion rules for countries/products\n- changing weighting formulas, centroids logic, or Moran definitions\n- changing zero-fill behavior or neighborhood logic\n- changing figure set or suppressing canonical outputs\n- approximations to Moran or barycenter calculations\n\n**What I would apply before final baseline freeze**\nSafe, high-impact candidates:\n- Stage 03: single all-country barycenter pass + remove duplicate focus pass\n- Stage 03: remove separate country-code scan\n- Stage 05: single yearly scan per parquet\n- Stage 05: single DuckDB connection\n- Stage 05: cache spatial weights\n- Stage 05: grouped iteration instead of repeated product filtering\n\nThose are the best speed wins with low analytical risk."}}

{"timestamp":"2026-03-11T12:24:19.527Z","type":"response_item","payload":{"type":"function_call_output","call_id":"call_QkKp9xKoveBKRhRBo6vJneFM","output":"Exit code: 1\nWall time: 1.3 seconds\nTotal output lines: 1107\nOutput:\n===== CELL 54 =====\r\nimport os\r\nimport duckdb\r\nimport pandas as pd\r\nimport numpy as np\r\n\r\n# -------------------------\r\n# Authoritative paths\r\n# -------------------------\r\nGEO_DIR_STR = str(GEO_DIR)  # keep GEO_DIR as Path (from config)\r\nCENTROIDS_CSV = str(CENTROIDS_OUT)  # authoritative centroids output\r\n\r\n# Trade files directory (adjust if your parquet files are elsewhere)\r\nTRADE_DIR = str(DATAVERSE_DIR)\r\n\r\nOUT_DIR = str(OUT_03_BARY / \"data\")\r\nOUT_FILE = os.path.join(OUT_DIR, f\"barycenter_imports_exports_{TRADE_YEAR_START}_{TRADE_YEAR_END}.csv\")\r\n\r\nSTART_YEAR, END_YEAR = TRADE_YEAR_START, TRADE_YEAR_END\r\n\r\n# -------------------------\r\n# Load centroids (single source of truth)\r\n# -------------------------\r\ncentroids = pd.read_csv(CENTROIDS_CSV, usecols=[\"code\", \"lat\", \"lon\"])\r\ncentroids[\"code\"] = centroids[\"code\"].astype(str)\r\n\r\n# Basic sanity: drop rows with missing coords (do not impute)\r\ncentroids = centroids.dropna(subset=[\"lat\", \"lon\"]).copy()\r\n\r\n# -------------------------\r\n# Helper: weighted mean (safe)\r\n# -------------------------\r\ndef weighted_mean(values: pd.Series, weights: pd.Series) -> float:\r\n    w = weights.to_numpy(dtype=float)\r\n    v = values.to_numpy(dtype=float)\r\n    s = np.nansum(w)\r\n    if s == 0 or np.isnan(s):\r\n        return np.nan\r\n    return float(np.nansum(v * w) / s)\r\n\r\n# -------------------------\r\n# Main loop\r\n# -------------------------\r\nos.makedirs(OUT_DIR, exist_ok=True)\r\ncon = duckdb.connect()\r\n\r\nrows = []\r\n\r\nfor year in range(START_YEAR, END_YEAR + 1):\r\n    fpath = os.path.join(TRADE_DIR, f\"S2_{year}.parquet\")\r\n    if not os.path.exists(fpath):\r\n        # Keep a record if you want; otherwise skip silently\r\n        continue\r\n\r\n    # Aggregate with DuckDB for speed\r\n    # Exports: sum by exporter\r\n    exports_df = con.execute(f\"\"\"\r\n        SELECT exporter AS code, SUM({TRADE_VALUE_COL}) AS exports_value\r\n        FROM read_parquet('{fpath}')\r\n        GROUP BY exporter\r\n    \"\"\").df()\r\n\r\n    # Imports: sum by importer\r\n    imports_df = con.execute(f\"\"\"\r\n        SELECT importer AS code, SUM({TRADE_VALUE_COL}) AS imports_value\r\n        FROM read_parquet('{fpath}')\r\n        GROUP BY importer\r\n    \"\"\").df()\r\n\r\n    # Ensure code is str\r\n    exports_df[\"code\"] = exports_df[\"code\"].astype(str)\r\n    imports_df[\"code\"] = imports_df[\"code\"].astype(str)\r\n\r\n    # Merge with fixed centroids (no geographic modifications)\r\n    ex = exports_df.merge(centroids, on=\"code\", how=\"left\")\r\n    im = imports_df.merge(centroids, on=\"code\", how=\"left\")\r\n\r\n    # Unmatched codes (trade present but no centroid)\r\n    ex_unmatched = ex[\"lat\"].isna() | ex[\"lon\"].isna()\r\n    im_unmatched = im[\"lat\"].isna() | im[\"lon\"].isna()\r\n\r\n    n_ex_total = len(ex)\r\n    n_im_total = len(im)\r\n    n_ex_matched = int((~ex_unmatched).sum())\r\n    n_im_matched = int((~im_unmatched).sum())\r\n    n_ex_unmatched = int(ex_unmatched.sum())\r\n    n_im_unmatched = int(im_unmatched.sum())\r\n\r\n    # Drop unmatched for barycenter calculation\r\n    exm = ex.loc[~ex_unmatched].copy()\r\n    imm = im.loc[~im_unmatched].copy()\r\n\r\n    # Totals (over matched set used for barycenter)\r\n    total_exports_matched = float(exm[\"exports_value\"].sum()) if len(exm) else 0.0\r\n    total_imports_matched = float(imm[\"imports_value\"].sum()) if len(imm) else 0.0\r\n\r\n    # Barycenters (simple weighted mean in lat/lon)\r\n    lat_exports = weighted_mean(exm[\"lat\"], exm[\"exports_value\"]) if len(exm) else np.nan\r\n    lon_exports = weighted_mean(exm[\"lon\"], exm[\"exports_value\"]) if len(exm) else np.nan\r\n\r\n    lat_imports = weighted_mean(imm[\"lat\"], imm[\"imports_value\"]) if len(imm) else np.nan\r\n    lon_imports = weighted_mean(imm[\"lon\"], imm[\"imports_value\"]) if len(imm) else np.nan\r\n\r\n    # Optional: list unmatched codes (truncated) for auditing\r\n    # Keep them as a semicolon-delimited string; cap length to avoid huge CSV cells\r\n    ex_unmatched_codes = ex.loc[ex_unmatched, \"code\"].unique().tolist()\r\n    im_unmatched_codes = im.loc[im_unmatched, \"code\"].unique().tolist()\r\n\r\n    def truncate_codes(codes, max_codes=50):\r\n        if not codes:\r\n            return \"\"\r\n        if len(codes) <= max_codes:\r\n            return \";\".join(codes)\r\n        return \";\".join(codes[:max_codes]) + f\";...(plus {len(codes)-max_codes} more)\"\r\n\r\n    rows.append({\r\n        \"year\": year,\r\n\r\n        \"lat_exports\": lat_exports,\r\n        \"lon_exports\": lon_exports,\r\n        \"lat_imports\": lat_imports,\r\n        \"lon_imports\": lon_imports,\r\n\r\n        \"total_exports_value_matched\": total_exports_matched,\r\n        \"total_imports_value_matched\": total_imports_matched,\r\n\r\n        \"n_exporter_nodes_total\": n_ex_total,\r\n        \"n_importer_nodes_total\": n_im_total,\r\n        \"n_exporter_nodes_matched\": n_ex_matched,\r\n        \"n_importer_nodes_matched\": n_im_matched,\r\n        \"n_exporter_nodes_unmatched\": n_ex_unmatched,\r\n        \"n_importer_nodes_unmatched\": n_im_unmatched,\r\n\r\n        \"unmatched_exporter_codes_sample\": truncate_codes(ex_unmatched_codes),\r\n        \"unmatched_importer_codes_sample\": truncate_codes(im_unmatched_codes),\r\n    })\r\n\r\n# -------------------------\r\n# Write output (overwrite)\r\n# -------------------------\r\nout = pd.DataFrame(rows).sort_value\r\n\r\n===== CELL 58 =====\r\nimport os\r\nimport duckdb\r\nimport pandas as pd\r\nimport numpy as np\r\nimport geopandas as gpd\r\nimport matplotlib.pyplot as plt\r\nfrom shapely.geometry import Point, LineString\r\nfrom pyproj import CRS\r\nfrom matplotlib.lines import Line2D\r\n\r\n# ------------------------------------------------------------\r\n# Authoritative geo paths + trade dir (set correctly)\r\n# ------------------------------------------------------------\r\nGEO_DIR_STR = str(GEO_DIR)  # keep GEO_DIR as Path (from config)\r\nCENTROIDS_CSV = str(CENTROIDS_OUT)  # authoritative centroids output\r\nSHAPEFILE = str(NE_SHP)\r\n\r\nTRADE_DIR = str(DATAVERSE_DIR)\r\nSTART_YEAR, END_YEAR = TRADE_YEAR_START, TRADE_YEAR_END\r\n\r\nOUT_DIR = str(OUT_03_BARY / \"data\")\r\nos.makedirs(OUT_DIR, exist_ok=True)\r\n\r\nOUT_CSV = os.path.join(OUT_DIR, f\"barycenter_usa_trade_{TRADE_YEAR_START}_{TRADE_YEAR_END}.csv\")\r\nOUT_PNG = str(BARY_FIG / \"barycenter_usa_trade_mediterranean_zoom.png\")\r\nOUT_PDF = str(BARY_FIG / \"barycenter_usa_trade_mediterranean_zoom.pdf\")\r\n\r\nUSA_CODE = \"USA\"  # must match your parquet exporter/importer codes\r\n\r\n# ------------------------------------------------------------\r\n# Helpers\r\n# ------------------------------------------------------------\r\ndef weighted_mean(values: pd.Series, weights: pd.Series) -> float:\r\n    w = weights.to_numpy(dtype=float)\r\n    v = values.to_numpy(dtype=float)\r\n    s = np.nansum(w)\r\n    if s == 0 or np.isnan(s):\r\n        return np.nan\r\n    return float(np.nansum(v * w) / s)\r\n\r\ndef trunc_codes(codes, max_codes=50) -> str:\r\n    codes = list(codes) if codes is not None else []\r\n    if not codes:\r\n        return \"\"\r\n    if len(codes) <= max_codes:\r\n        return \";\".join(codes)\r\n    return \";\".join(codes[:max_codes]) + f\";...(plus {len(codes)-max_codes} more)\"\r\n\r\n# ------------------------------------------------------------\r\n# Load centroids (single source of truth)\r\n# ------------------------------------------------------------\r\ncentroids = pd.read_csv(CENTROIDS_CSV, usecols=[\"code\", \"lat\", \"lon\"])\r\ncentroids[\"code\"] = centroids[\"code\"].astype(str)\r\ncentroids = centroids.dropna(subset=[\"lat\", \"lon\"]).copy()\r\n\r\n# ------------------------------------------------------------\r\n# Compute USA partner-weighted barycenters per year\r\n# ------------------------------------------------------------\r\ncon = duckdb.connect()\r\nrows = []\r\n\r\nfor year in range(START_YEAR, END_YEAR + 1):\r\n    fpath = os.path.join(TRADE_DIR, f\"S2_{year}.parquet\")\r\n    if not os.path.exists(fpath):\r\n        continue\r\n\r\n    # USA exports: exporter=USA, partner is importer\r\n    usx = con.execute(f\"\"\"\r\n        SELECT importer AS partner, SUM({TRADE_VALUE_COL}) AS w\r\n        FROM read_parquet('{fpath}')\r\n        WHERE exporter = '{USA_CODE}'\r\n        GROUP BY importer\r\n    \"\"\").df()\r\n\r\n    # USA imports: importer=USA, partner is exporter\r\n    usm = con.execute(f\"\"\"\r\n        SELECT exporter AS partner, SUM({TRADE_VALUE_COL}) AS w\r\n        FROM read_parquet('{fpath}')\r\n        WHERE importer = '{USA_CODE}'\r\n        GROUP BY exporter\r\n    \"\"\").df()\r\n\r\n    usx[\"partner\"] = usx[\"partner\"].astype(str)\r\n    usm[\"partner\"] = usm[\"partner\"].astype(str)\r\n\r\n    usxg = usx.merge(centroids, left_on=\"partner\", right_on=\"code\", how=\"left\")\r\n    usmg = usm.merge(centroids, left_on=\"partner\", right_on=\"code\", how=\"left\")\r\n\r\n    usx_unmatched = usxg[\"lat\"].isna() | usxg[\"lon\"].isna()\r\n    usm_unmatched = usmg[\"lat\"].isna() | usmg[\"lon\"].isna()\r\n\r\n    usx_mat = usxg.loc[~usx_unmatched].copy()\r\n    usm_mat = usmg.loc[~usm_unmatched].copy()\r\n\r\n    total_us_exports = float(usx_mat[\"w\"].sum()) if len(usx_mat) else 0.0\r\n    total_us_imports = float(usm_mat[\"w\"].sum()) if len(usm_mat) else 0.0\r\n\r\n    lat_us_exports = weighted_mean(usx_mat[\"lat\"], usx_mat[\"w\"]) if len(usx_mat) else np.nan\r\n    lon_us_exports = weighted_mean(usx_mat[\"lon\"], usx_mat[\"w\"]) if len(usx_mat) else np.nan\r\n\r\n    lat_us_imports = weighted_mean(usm_mat[\"lat\"], usm_mat[\"w\"]) if len(usm_mat) else np.nan\r\n    lon_us_imports = weighted_mean(usm_mat[\"lon\"], usm_mat[\"w\"]) if len(usm_mat) else np.nan\r\n\r\n    rows.append({\r\n        \"year\": year,\r\n        \"lat_us_exports\": lat_us_exports,\r\n        \"lon_us_exports\": lon_us_exports,\r\n        \"lat_us_imports\": lat_us_imports,\r\n        \"lon_us_imports\": lon_us_imports,\r\n        \"total_us_exports_value_final_matched\": total_us_exports,\r\n        \"total_us_imports_value_final_matched\": total_us_imports,\r\n        \"n_export_partners_total\": int(len(usxg)),\r\n        \"n_import_partners_total\": int(len(usmg)),\r\n        \"n_export_partners_unmatched\": int(usx_unmatched.sum()),\r\n        \"n_import_partners_unmatched\": int(usm_unmatched.sum()),\r\n        \"unmatched_export_partners_sample\": trunc_codes(usxg.loc[usx_unmatched, \"partner\"].unique()),\r\n        \"unmatched_import_partners_sample\": trunc_codes(usmg.loc[usm_unmatched, \"partner\"].unique()),\r\n    })\r\n\r\nout = pd.DataFrame(rows).sort_values(\"year\")\r\nout.to_csv(OUT_CSV, index=False)\r\nprint(f\"Wrote: {OUT_CSV} | Years: {out['year'].nunique()}\")\r\n\r\n# ------------------------------------------------------------\r\n# Paths (USA barycenters already computed)\r\n# ------------------------------------------------------------\r\nGEO_DIR_STR = str(GEO_DIR)  # keep GEO_DIR as Path (from config)\r\nSHAPEFILE = str(NE_SHP)\r\n\r\nBARY_DIR = str(OUT_03_BARY / \"data\")\r\nUSA_CSV = os.path.join(BARY_DIR, f\"barycenter_usa_trade_{TRADE_YEAR_START}_{TRADE_YEAR_END}.csv\")\r\n\r\nOUT_IMPORTS_PNG = str(BARY_FIG / \"barycenter_usa_imports_mediterranean.png\")\r\nOUT_IMPORTS_PDF = str(BARY_FIG / \"barycenter_usa_exports_mediterranean.pdf\")\r\n\r\n# ------------------------------------------------------------\r\n# Load basemap and remove Antarctica\r\n# ------------------------------------------------------------\r\nworld = gpd.read_file(SHAPEFILE)\r\nif \"ISO_A3\" in world.columns:\r\n    world = world[world[\"ISO_A3\"] != \"ATA\"].copy()\r\nif world.crs is None:\r\n    world = world.set_crs(epsg=4326)\r\n\r\n# ------------------------------------------------------------\r\n# Load USA barycenters\r\n# ------------------------------------------------------------\r\ndf = pd.read_csv(USA_CSV).sort_values(\"year\")\r\ndf = df.dropna(\r\n    subset=[\"lat_us_exports\", \"lon_us_exports\", \"lat_us_imports\", \"lon_us_imports\"]\r\n).copy()\r\n\r\nexports = gpd.GeoDataFrame(\r\n    df[[\"year\", \"lat_us_exports\", \"lon_us_exports\"]],\r\n    geometry=[Point(xy) for xy in zip(df[\"lon_us_exports\"], df[\"lat_us_exports\"])],\r\n    crs=\"EPSG:4326\",\r\n).sort_values(\"year\")\r\n\r\nimports = gpd.GeoDataFrame(\r\n    df[[\"year\", \"lat_us_imports\", \"lon_us_imports\"]],\r\n    geometry=[Point(xy) for xy in zip(df[\"lon_us_imports\"], df[\"lat_us_imports\"])],\r\n    crs=\"EPSG:4326\",\r\n).sort_values(\"year\")\r\n\r\nexports_path = gpd.GeoDataFrame(\r\n    geometry=[LineString(exports.geometry.tolist())],\r\n    crs=\"EPSG:4326\"\r\n)\r\n\r\nimports_path = gpd.GeoDataFrame(\r\n    geometry=[LineString(imports.geometry.tolist())],\r\n    crs=\"EPSG:4326\"\r\n)\r\n\r\n# ------------------------------------------------------------\r\n# SAME Mediterranean projection\r\n# ------------------------------------------------------------\r\ncrs_med = CRS.from_proj4(\"+proj=aeqd +lat_0=35 +lon_0=18 +datum=WGS84 +units=m +no_defs\")\r\n\r\nworld_p = world.to_crs(crs_med)\r\nexports_p = exports.to_crs(crs_med)\r\nimports_p = imports.to_crs(crs_med)\r\nexports_path_p = exports_path.to_crs(crs_med)\r\nimports_path_p = imports_path.to_crs(crs_med)\r\n\r\n# ------------------------------------------------------------\r\n# SAME zoom window (derived from global barycenter map)\r\n# ------------------------------------------------------------\r\nGLOBAL_BARY_FILE = os.path.join(BARY_DIR, f\"barycenter_imports_exports_{TRADE_YEAR_START}_{TRADE_YEAR_END}.csv\")\r\n\r\ngdf_global = pd.read_csv(GLOBAL_BARY_FILE).dropna(\r\n    subset=[\"lat_exports\", \"lon_exports\", \"lat_imports\", \"lon_imports\"]\r\n)\r\n\r\nglobal_exports = gpd.GeoDataFrame(\r\n    geometry=[Point(xy) for xy in zip(gdf_global[\"lon_exports\"], gdf_global[\"lat_exports\"])],\r\n    crs=\"EPSG:4326\",\r\n).to_crs(crs_med)\r\n\r\nglobal_imports = gpd.GeoDataFrame(\r\n    geometry=[Point(xy) for xy in global_imports.geometry]),\r\n    crs=crs_med\r\n).total_bounds\r\n\r\nbuffer = 1.2e6\r\nX_LIM = (minx - buffer, maxx + buffer)\r\nY_LIM = (miny - buffer, maxy + buffer)\r\n\r\n# {1977, 1980, 1982, 1983, 1985, 1987, 1990, 1994, 1999, 2001, 2009, 2012, 2015, 2018, 2022}\r\n\r\ndef add_year_labels(ax, gdf_sorted, dx=20000, dy=20000, fontsize=5):\r\n    for _, r in gdf_sorted.iterrows():\r\n        y = int(r[\"year\"])\r\n        if y in LABEL_YEARS:\r\n            x0, y0 = r.geometry.x, r.geometry.y\r\n            ax.text(\r\n                x0 + dx, y0 + dy,\r\n                str(y),\r\n                fontsize=fontsize,\r\n                color=\"black\",\r\n                alpha=0.8,\r\n                zorder=10\r\n            )\r\n\r\n#    fontsize=9,\r\n        \r\n\r\n===== CELL 61 =====\r\nimport os\r\nimport duckdb\r\nimport pandas as pd\r\nimport numpy as np\r\nimport geopandas as gpd\r\nimport matplotlib.pyplot as plt\r\nfrom authoritative centroids output\r\nSHAPEFILE = str(NE_SHP)\r\n\r\nTRADE_DIR = str(DATAVERSE_DIR)  # <-- set to where S2_YYYY.parquet lives\r\nSTART_YEAR, END_YEAR = TRADE_YEAR_START, file:\r\nGLOBAL_BARY_FILE = os.path.join(BARY_DIR, f\"barycenter_imports_exports_{TRADE_YEAR_START}_{TRADE_YEAR_END}.csv\")\r\n\r\n# Output for China\r\nOUT_CSV = os.path.join(BARY_DIR, f\"barycenter_china_trade_{TRADE_YEAR_START}_{TRADE_YEAR_END}.csv\")\r\nOUT_PNG = str(BARY_FIG / \"barycenter_china_trade_mediterranean_zoom.png\")\r\nOUT_PDF = str(BARY_FIG / values.to_numpy(dtype=float)\r\n    s = np.nansum(w)\r\n    if s == 0 or np.isnan(s):\r\n        return np.nan\r\n    return float(np.nansum(v * w) / s)\r\n\r\ndef trunc_codes(codes, max_codes=50) -> str:\r\n    codes = list(codes) if codes is not None else []\r\n    if not codes:\r\n        return \"\"\r\n    if len(codes) <= max_codes:\r\n        return \";\".join(codes)\r\n    return \";\".join(codes[:max_codes]) + f\";...(plus {len(codes)-max_codes} more)\"\r\n\r\ndef first_existing_parquet(trade_dir: str, start: int, end: int):\r\n    for y in range(start, end + 1):\r\n        fp = os.path.join(trade_dir, f\"S2_{y}.parquet\")\r\n        if os.path.exists(fp):\r\n            return y, fp\r\n    return None, None\r\n\r\n# ------------------------------------------------------------\r\n# Load centroids (single source of truth)\r\n# ------------------------------------------------------------\r\ncentroids = pd.read_csv(CENTROIDS_CSV, usecols=[\"code\", \"lat\", \"lon\"])\r\ncentroids[\"code\"] = centroids[\"code\"].astype(str)\r\ncentroids = centroids.dropna(subset=[\"lat\", \"lon\"]).copy()\r\n\r\n# ------------------------------------------------------------\r\n# Connect DuckDB + validate that FOCUS_CODE exists (less guess)\r\n# ------------------------------------------------------------\r\ncon = duckdb.connect()\r\n\r\ny0, fp0 = first_existing_parquet(TRADE_DIR, START_YEAR, END_YEAR)\r\nif fp0 is None:\r\n    raise FileNotFoundError(f\"No S2_YYYY.parquet files found in {TRADE_DIR} for {TRADE_YEAR_START}-{TRADE_YEAR_END}.\")\r\n\r\nexists = con.execute(f\"\"\"\r\n    SELECT\r\n      SUM(CASE WHEN exporter = '{FOCUS_CODE}' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS as_exporter,\r\n      SUM(CASE WHEN importer = '{FOCUS_CODE}' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS as_importer\r\n    FROM read_parquet('{fp0}')\r\n\"\"\").fetchone()\r\n\r\nif (exists[0] == 0) and (exists[1] == 0):\r\n    # Show likely candidates by volume (top 20) so you can pick the correct code if different\r\n    top_exporters = con.execute(f\"\"\"\r\n        SELECT exporter AS code, SUM({TRADE_VALUE_COL}) AS v\r\n        FROM read_parquet('{fp0}')\r\n        GROUP BY exporter\r\n        ORDER BY v DESC\r\n        LIMIT 20\r\n    \"\"\").df()\r\n\r\n    top_importers = con.execute(f\"\"\"\r\n        SELECT importer AS code, SUM({TRADE_VALUE_COL}) AS v\r\n        FROM read_parquet('{fp0}')\r\n        GROUP BY importer\r\n        ORDER BY v DESC\r\n        LIMIT 20\r\n    \"\"\").df()\r\n\r\n    msg = (\r\n        f\"FOCUS_CODE='{FOCUS_CODE}' not found as exporter or importer in the first available file \"\r\n        f\"(S2_{y0}.parquet). Your dataset may use a different code.\\n\\n\"\r\n        f\"Top exporters in {y0}:\\n{top_exporters.to_string(index=False)}\\n\\n\"\r\n        f\"Top importers in {y0}:\\n{top_importers.to_string(index=False)}\\n\"\r\n    )\r\n    raise ValueError(msg)\r\n\r\nprint(f\"Validated: {FOCUS_CODE} appears in S2_{y0}.parquet (exporter count={exists[0]}, importer gpd.GeoDataFrame(\r\n    {\"name\": [\"exports_path\"]},\r\n    geometry=[LineString(list(exports.geometry.values))],\r\n    crs=\"EPSG:4326\",\r\n)\r\n\r\nimports_path = gpd.GeoDataFrame(\r\n    {\"name\": [\"imports_path\"]},\r\n    geometry=[LineString(list(imports.geometry.values))],\r\n    crs=\"EPSG:4326\",\r\n)\r\n\r\n# ------------------------------------------------------------\r\n# Projection + fixed window (Central Asia; include south)\r\n# ------------------------------------------------------------\r\ncrs_ca = CRS.from_proj4(\"+proj=aeqd +lat_0=40 +lon_0=80 +datum=WGS84 +units=m +no_defs\")\r\n\r\n# Window: Eastern Mediterranean -> West China; include more south to avoid chopping\r\nlon_min, lon_max = 15, 115\r\nlat_min, lat_max = 5, 60\r\n\r\nbbox_ll = gpd.GeoSeries([box(lon_min, lat_min, lon_max, lat_max)], crs=\"EPSG:4326\")\r\nbbox_p = bbox_ll.to_crs(crs_ca).iloc[0]\r\nxmin, ymin, xmax, ymax = bbox_p.bounds\r\n\r\n# Project layers\r\nworld_p = world.to_crs(crs_ca)\r\nexports_p = exports.to_crs(crs_ca)\r\nimports_p = imports.to_crs(crs_ca)\r\nexports_path_p = exports_path.to_crs(crs_ca)\r\nimports_path_p = imports_path.to_crs(crs_ca)\r\n\r\n# ------------------------------------------------------------\r\n# Labels: small, along trajectory\r\n# ------------------------------------------------------------\r\nLABEL_EVERY = 5  # label every 5 years; set to 10 if too dense\r\n\r\nyears = df[\"year\"].astype(int).tolist()\r\n#ymin_year, ymax_year = min(years), max(years)\r\n\r\nlabel_years = set(range(TRADE_YEAR_START, TRADE_YEAR_END + 1, LABEL_EVERY))\r\nlabel_years.update([TRADE_YEAR_START, TRADE_YEAR_END])  # force endpoints\r\n\r\ndef add_year_labels(ax, gdf_sorted, dx=25000, dy=25000, fontsize=6):\r\n    for _, r in gdf_sorted.iterrows():\r\n        y = int(r[\"year\"])\r\n        if y in label_years:\r\n            x0, y0 = r.geometry.x, r.geometry.y\r\n            ax.text(\r\n                x0 + dx, y0 + dy, str(y),\r\n                fontsize=fontsize, color=\"black\", alpha=0.85, zorder=10\r\n            )\r\n\r\n# ------------------------------------------------------------\r\n# Render function: single-map output with bottom caption-style label\r\n# ------------------------------------------------------------\r\ndef render_single_map(points_p, path_p, color, footer_label, out_png, out_pdf):\r\n    fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(10, 6))  # taller aspect\r\n\r\n    # Basemap\r\n    world_p.plot(ax=ax, linewidth=0.4, edgecolor=\"white\", color=\"lightgray\", zorder=1)\r\n\r\n    # Path + points\r\n    path_p.plot(ax=ax, linewidth=1.1,   0.02, 0.02, footer_label,\r\n        transform=ax.transAxes,\r\n        fontsize=9,\r\n        color=\"black\",\r\n        ha=\"left\",\r\n        va=\"bottom\"\r\n    )\r\n\r\n    ax.set_axis_off()\r\n    plt.tight_layout()\r\n\r\n    # Save (overwrite)\r\n    plt.savefig(out_png, dpi=300, bbox_inches=\"tight\")\r\n    plt.savefig(out_pdf, bbox_inches=\"tight\")\r\n    plt.close(fig)\r\n\r\n    print(f\"Saved: {o\r\n\r\n===== CELL 64 =====\r\nimport os\r\nimport duckdb\r\nimport pandas as pd\r\nimport numpy as np\r\n\r\n# ------------------------------------------------------------\r\n# CONFIG (edit TRADE_DIR if needed; add ISO3 codes in FOCUS_CODES)\r\n# ------------------------------------------------------------\r\nGEO_DIR_STR = str(GEO_DIR)  # keep GEO_DIR as Path (from config)\r\nCENTROIDS_CSV = str(CENTROIDS_OUT)  # authoritative centroids output\r\n\r\nTRADE_DIR = str(DATAVERSE_DIR)\r\nSTART_YEAR, END_YEAR = TRADE_YEAR_START, TRADE_YEAR_END\r\n\r\nOUT_DIR = str(OUT_03_BARY / \"data\")\r\nos.makedirs(OUT_DIR, exist_ok=True)\r\n\r\n# Add / remove ISO3 codes here (manual control)\r\nFOCUS_CODES = [\"BRA\", \"ARG\", \"MEX\", \"COL\", \"CHL\", \"CAN\", \"USA\", \"CHN\", \"JPN\", \"GBR\",\"DEU\", \"FRA\"]\r\n\r\n# ------------------------------------------------------------\r\n# Helpers\r\n# ------------------------------------------------------------\r\ndef weighted_mean(values: pd.Series, weights: pd.Series) -> float:\r\n    w = weights.to_numpy(dtype=float)\r\n    v = values.to_numpy(dtype=float)\r\n    s = np.nansum(w)\r\n    if s == 0 or np.isnan(s):\r\n        return np.nan\r\n    return float(np.nansum(v * w) / s)\r\n\r\ndef trunc_codes(codes, max_codes=50) -> str:\r\n    codes = list(codes) if codes is not None else []\r\n    if not codes:\r\n        return \"\"\r\n    if len(codes) <= max_codes:\r\n        return \";\".join(codes)\r\n    return \";\".join(codes[:max_codes]) + f\";...(plus {len(codes)-max_codes} more)\"\r\n\r\n# ------------------------------------------------------------\r\n# Load centroids (single source of truth)\r\n# ------------------------------------------------------------\r\ncentroids = pd.read_csv(CENTROIDS_CSV, usecols=[\"code\", \"lat\", \"lon\"])\r\ncentroids[\"code\"] = centroids[\"code\"].astype(str)\r\ncentroids = centroids.dropna(subset=[\"lat\", \"lon\"]).copy()\r\n\r\n# ------------------------------------------------------------\r\n# DuckDB connection\r\n# ------------------------------------------------------------\r\ncon = duckdb.connect()\r\n\r\n# Collect rows per country, then write one file per ISO3\r\nrows_by_country = {c: [] for c in FOCUS_CODES}\r\n\r\nfor year in range(START_YEAR, END_YEAR + 1):\r\n    fpath = os.path.join(TRADE_DIR, f\"S2_{year}.parquet\")\r\n    if not os.path.exists(fpath):\r\n        continue\r\n\r\n    for c in FOCUS_CODES:\r\n        # Exports: exporter=c, partner is importer\r\n        ex = con.execute(f\"\"\"\r\n            SELECT importer AS partner, SUM({TRADE_VALUE_COL}) AS w\r\n            FROM read_parquet('{fpath}')\r\n            WHERE exporter = '{c}'\r\n            GROUP BY importer\r\n        \"\"\").df()\r\n\r\n        # Imports: importer=c, partner is exporter\r\n        im = con.execute(f\"\"\"\r\n            SELECT exporter AS partner, SUM({TRADE_VALUE_COL}) AS w\r\n            FROM read_parquet('{fpath}')\r\n            WHERE importer = '{c}'\r\n            GROUP BY exporter\r\n        \"\"\").df()\r\n\r\n        ex[\"partner\"] = ex[\"partner\"].astype(str)\r\n        im[\"partner\"] = im[\"partner\"].astype(str)\r\n\r\n        exg = ex.merge(centroids, left_on=\"partner\", right_on=\"code\", how=\"left\")\r\n        img = im.merge(centroids, left_on=\"partner\", right_on=\"code\", how=\"left\")\r\n\r\n        ex_unmatched = exg[\"lat\"].isna() | exg[\"lon\"].isna()\r\n        im_unmatched = img[\"lat\"].isna() | img[\"lon\"].isna()\r\n\r\n        exm = exg.loc[~ex_unmatched].copy()\r\n        imm = img.loc[~im_unmatched].copy()\r\n\r\n        total_exports = float(exm[\"w\"].sum()) if len(exm) else 0.0\r\n        total_imports = float(imm[\"w\"].sum()) if len(imm) else 0.0\r\n\r\n        lat_exports = weighted_mean(exm[\"lat\"], exm[\"w\"]) if len(exm) else np.nan\r\n        lon_exports = weighted_mean(exm[\"lon\"], exm[\"w\"]) if len(exm) else np.nan\r\n\r\n        lat_imports = weighted_mean(imm[\"lat\"], imm[\"w\"]) if len(imm) else np.nan\r\n        lon_imports = weighted_mean(imm[\"lon\"], imm[\"w\"]) if len(imm) else np.nan\r\n\r\n        rows_by_country[c].append({\r\n            \"year\": year,\r\n\r\n            \"lat_exports\": lat_exports,\r\n            \"lon_exports\": lon_exports,\r\n            \"lat_imports\": lat_imports,\r\n            \"lon_imports\": lon_imports,\r\n\r\n            \"total_exports_value_final_matched\": total_exports,\r\n            \"total_imports_value_final_matched\": total_imports,\r\n\r\n            \"n_export_partners_total\": int(len(exg)),\r\n            \"n_import_partners_total\": int(len(img)),\r\n            \"n_export_partners_unmatched\": int(ex_unmatched.sum()),\r\n            \"n_import_partners_unmatched\": int(im_unmatched.sum()),\r\n\r\n            \"unmatched_export_partners_sample\": trunc_codes(exg.loc[ex_unmatched, \"partner\"].unique()),\r\n            \"unmatched_import_partners_sample\": trunc_codes(img.loc[im_unmatched, \"partner\"].unique()),\r\n        })\r\n\r\n# Write one CSV per ISO3 (overwrite)\r\nfor c, rows in rows_by_country.items():\r\n    out = pd.DataFrame(rows).sort_values(\"year\")\r\n\r\n    out_file = os.path.join(OUT_DIR, f\"barycenter_{c}_{TRADE_YEAR_START}_{TRADE_YEAR_END}.csv\")\r\n    out.to_csv(out_file, index=False)\r\n\r\n    if len(out):\r\n        print(f\"Wrote: {out_file} | years countries\r\n\r\n===== CELL 66 =====\r\nimport os\r\nimport pandas as pd\r\nimport geopandas as gpd\r\nimport matplotlib.pyplot as plt\r\nfrom shapely.geometry import Point, LineString, box\r\nfrom GLOBAL_LAT_MIN, GLOBAL_LON_MAX, GLOBAL_LAT_MAX)], crs=\"EPSG:4326\")\r\nbbox_p = bbox_ll.to_crs(CRS_MERC).iloc[0]\r\nXMIN, YMIN, XMAX, YMAX = bbox_p.bounds\r\n\r\n# pandas as pd\r\nimport geopandas as gpd\r\nimport matplotlib.pyplot as plt\r\nfrom shapely.geometry import Point, LineString, box\r\nfrom pyproj import CRS\r\n\r\n# {\r\n    \"BRA\": \"Brazil\",\r\n    \"ARG\": \"Argentina\",\r\n    \"CHL\": \"Chile\",\r\n    \"COL\": \"Colombia\",\r\n    \"MEX\": \"Mexico\",\r\n    \"CAN\": \"Canada\",\r\n    \"JPN\": \"Japan\",\r\n    \"GBR\": \"United Kingdom\",\r\n    \"DEU\": \"Germany\",\r\n    \"FRA\": \"France\",\r\n}\r\n\r\n# Labels (updated): use 2022 (not 2023)\r\nLABEL_YEARS = {1977, 1981, 1985, 1988, 1994, 1997, 2001, 2009, 2018, 2022}\r\nEMPH_YEARS = {1977, 2022}  # bold + italic\r\n\r\n# Visuals\r\nBASEMAP_FACE = \"lightgray\"\r\nBASEMAP_EDGE = \"white\"\r\nBASEMAP_LW = 0.35\r\nPATH_LW = 1.1\r\nPATH_ALPHA = 0.28\r\nPOINT_SIZE = 8\r\n\r\n# Smaller year labels\r\nLABEL_FONTSIZE = 4\r\nLABEL_ALPHA = 0.8\r\n\r\n# Output filename format: barycenter_<ISO3>_exports/imports\r\n# Output \"barycenter_{iso3}_{flow}.pdf\")\r\n\r\nos.makedirs(BARY_DIR, exist_ok=True)\r\n\r\n# ------------------------------------------------------------\r\n# CRS helpers\r\n# ------------------------------------------------------------\r\ndef aeqd(lat0, lon0) -> CRS:\r\n    return CRS.from_proj4(f\"+proj=aeqd +lat_0={lat0} +lon_0={lon0} +datum=WGS84 +units=m +no_defs\")\r\n\r\n# ------------------------------------------------------------\r\n# Projection + extent rules (lon/lat)\r\n# ------------------------------------------------------------\r\n# 1) Argentina, Brazil, Chile:\r\n#    Center North Africa; N=southern France (~43N), S=equatorial Africa (0),\r\n#    W=eastern tip of Brazil (~-35), E=Iraq (~44E)\r\nCRS_N_AFRICA = aeqd(20, 10)\r\nBBOX_N_AFRICA = (-35, 0, 44, 43)\r\n\r\n# 2) Colombia:\r\n#    N=UK (~58N), S=Ecuador (~-5), W=Yucatan (~-92), E=France (~2)\r\nCRS_COL = aeqd(15, -40)\r\nBBOX_COL = (-92, -5, 2, 58)\r\n\r\n# 3) Mexico:\r\n#    Projection centered around NY (ok)\r\n#    Extent: N=southern Canada (~49N), S=southern Mexico (~14N),\r\n#            W=California (~-125), E=Spain (~3E)\r\nCRS_MEX = aeqd(40.7, -74.0)\r\nBBOX_MEX = (-125, 14, 3, 49)\r\n\r\n# 4) Canada: use same projection and extent as Mexico\r\nCRS_CAN = CRS_MEX\r\nBBOX_CAN = BBOX_MEX\r\n\r\n# 5) Japan:\r\n#    Center Egypt; N=southern Europe (~45N), S=Yemen (~12N),\r\n#    W=Canary Islands (~-16), E=Pakistan (~75E)\r\nCRS_JPN = aeqd(26, 30)\r\nBBOX_JPN = (-16, 12, 75, 45)\r\n\r\n# 6) European countries: use Mediterranean projection + extent from USA logic\r\nCRS_MED = aeqd(35, 18)\r\nGLOBAL_BARY_FILE = os.path.join(BARY_DIR, f\"barycenter_imports_exports_{TRADE_YEAR_START}_{TRADE_YEAR_END}.csv\")\r\nEU_ISOS = {\"GBR\", \"DEU\", \"FRA\"}\r\n\r\nCOUNTRY_SETTINGS = {\r\n    \"ARG\": {\"crs\": CRS_N_AFRICA, \"bbox\": BBOX_N_AFRICA},\r\n    \"BRA\": {\"crs\": CRS_N_AFRICA, \"bbox\": BBOX_N_AFRICA},\r\n    \"CHL\": {\"crs\": artifacts.data_dir / \"_tmp_barycenter_usa_trade_legacy_check.csv\"\r\n    if temp_legacy_check.exists():\r\n        try:\r\n            temp_legacy_check.chmod(stat.S_IWRITE)\r\n            temp_legacy_check.unlink()\r\n        except PermissionError:\r\n            logger.warning(\"Stage 03 could not remove temporary file: %s\", temp_legacy_check)\r\n\r\n    logger.info(\r\n        \"Stage 03 complete. focus=%d all=%d global_rows=%d output=%s\",\r\n        len(focus_written),\r\n        len(all_written),\r\n        len(global_df),\r\n        artifacts.stage_dir,\r\n    )\r\n    return {\r\n        \"run_dir\": str(run_dir),\r\n        \"stage_dir\": str(artifacts.stage_dir),\r\n        \"global_barycenter_path\": str(artifacts.global_barycenter_path),\r\n        \"usa_trade_path\": str(artifacts.usa_trade_path),\r\n        \"china_trade_path\": str(artifacts.china_trade_path),\r\n    }\r\n"}}

seconds\nOutput:\nfrom __future__ import annotations\r\n\r\nfrom dataclasses import dataclass\r\nfrom pathlib import Path\r\n\r\nimport duckdb\r\nimport geopandas as gpd\r\nimport matplotlib.pyplot as plt\r\nimport numpy as np\r\nimport pandas as pd\r\nfrom pyproj import CRS\r\nfrom shapely.geometry import LineString, Point, box\r\nfrom matplotlib.lines import Line2D\r\n\r\nfrom geoanalisis.config import ProjectConfig\r\n\r\n\r\n@dataclass(frozen=True)\r\nclass BarycenterArtifacts:\r\n    stage_dir: Path\r\n    data_dir: Path\r\n    fig_dir: Path\r\n    global_barycenter_path: Path\r\n    usa_trade_path: Path\r\n    china_trade_path: Path\r\n\r\n\r\ndef build_barycenter_artifact_paths(stage_dir: Path) -> BarycenterArtifacts:\r\n    data_dir = stage_dir / \"data\"\r\n    fig_dir = stage_dir / \"fig\"\r\n    return BarycenterArtifacts(\r\n        stage_dir=stage_dir,\r\n        data_dir=data_dir,\r\n        fig_dir=fig_dir,\r\n        global_barycenter_path=data_dir / \"barycenter_imports_exports_1976_2023.csv\",\r\n        usa_trade_path=data_dir / \"barycenter_usa_trade_1976_2023.csv\",\r\n        china_trade_path=data_dir / \"barycenter_china_trade_1976_2023.csv\",\r\n    )\r\n\r\n\r\ndef _connect_duckdb():\r\n    con = duckdb.connect()\r\n    con.execute(\"PRAGMA threads=8\")\r\n    return con\r\n\r\n\r\ndef weighted_mean(values: pd.Series, weights: pd.Series) -> float:\r\n    w = weights.to_numpy(dtype=float)\r\n    v = values.to_numpy(dtype=float)\r\n    s = np.nansum(w)\r\n    if s == 0 or np.isnan(s):\r\n        return np.nan\r\n    return float(np.nansum(v * w) / s)\r\n\r\n\r\ndef truncate_codes(codes, max_codes: int = 50) -> str:\r\n    codes = list(codes) if codes is not None else []\r\n    if not codes:\r\n        return \"\"\r\n    if len(codes) <= max_codes:\r\n        return \";\".join(codes)\r\n    return \";\".join(codes[:max_codes]) + f\";...(plus {len(codes) - max_codes} more)\"\r\n\r\n\r\ndef load_centroids(centroids_path: Path) -> pd.DataFrame:\r\n    centroids = pd.read_csv(centroids_path, usecols=[\"code\", \"lat\", \"lon\"])\r\n    centroids[\"code\"] = centroids[\"code\"].astype(str)\r\n    return centroids.dropna(subset=[\"lat\", \"lon\"]).copy()\r\n\r\n\r\ndef compute_global_barycenters(\r\n    config: ProjectConfig, centroids: pd.DataFrame, output_path: Path\r\n) -> pd.DataFrame:\r\n    con = _connect_duckdb()\r\n    rows = []\r\n    for year in range(config.trade_year_start, config.trade_year_end + 1):\r\n        fpath = config.dataset_trade_dir / f\"S2_{year}.parquet\"\r\n        if not fpath.exists():\r\n            continue\r\n        exports_df = con.execute(\r\n            f\"\"\"\r\n            SELECT exporter AS code, SUM({config.trade_value_column}) AS exports_value\r\n            FROM read_parquet('{fpath}')\r\n            GROUP BY exporter\r\n            \"\"\"\r\n        ).df()\r\n        imports_df = con.execute(\r\n            f\"\"\"\r\n            SELECT importer AS code, SUM({config.trade_value_column}) AS imports_value\r\n            FROM read_parquet('{fpath}')\r\n            GROUP BY importer\r\n            \"\"\"\r\n        ).df()\r\n        exports_df[\"code\"] = exports_df[\"code\"].astype(str)\r\n        imports_df[\"code\"] = imports_df[\"code\"].astype(str)\r\n        ex = exports_df.merge(centroids, on=\"code\", how=\"left\")\r\n        im = imports_df.merge(centroids, on=\"code\", how=\"left\")\r\n        ex_unmatched = ex[\"lat\"].isna() | ex[\"lon\"].isna()\r\n        im_unmatched = im[\"lat\"].isna() | im[\"lon\"].isna()\r\n        exm = ex.loc[~ex_unmatched].copy()\r\n        imm = im.loc[~im_unmatched].copy()\r\n        rows.append(\r\n            {\r\n                \"year\": year,\r\n                \"lat_exports\": weighted_mean(exm[\"lat\"], exm[\"exports_value\"]) if len(exm) else np.nan,\r\n                \"lon_exports\": weighted_mean(exm[\"lon\"], exm[\"exports_value\"]) if len(exm) else np.nan,\r\n                \"lat_imports\": weighted_mean(imm[\"lat\"], imm[\"imports_value\"]) if len(imm) else np.nan,\r\n                \"lon_imports\": weighted_mean(imm[\"lon\"], imm[\"imports_value\"]) if len(imm) else np.nan,\r\n                \"total_exports_value_matched\": float(exm[\"exports_value\"].sum()) if len(exm) else 0.0,\r\n                \"total_imports_value_matched\": float(imm[\"imports_value\"].sum()) if len(imm) else 0.0,\r\n                \"n_exporter_nodes_total\": len(ex),\r\n                \"n_importer_nodes_total\": len(im),\r\n                \"n_exporter_nodes_matched\": int((~ex_unmatched).sum()),\r\n                \"n_importer_nodes_matched\": int((~im_unmatched).sum()),\r\n                \"n_exporter_nodes_unmatched\": int(ex_unmatched.sum()),\r\n                \"n_importer_nodes_unmatched\": int(im_unmatched.sum()),\r\n                \"unmatched_exporter_codes_sample\": truncate_codes(\r\n                    ex.loc[ex_unmatched, \"code\"].unique().tolist()\r\n                ),\r\n                \"unmatched_importer_codes_sample\": truncate_codes(\r\n                    im.loc[im_unmatched, \"code\"].unique().tolist()\r\n                ),\r\n            }\r\n        )\r\n    con.close()\r\n    out = pd.DataFrame(rows).sort_values(\"year\").reset_index(drop=True)\r\n    out.to_csv(output_path, index=False)\r\n    return out\r\n\r\n\r\ndef compute_country_barycenters(\r\n    config: ProjectConfig,\r\n    centroids: pd.DataFrame,\r\n    focus_codes: list[str],\r\n    output_dir: Path,\r\n) -> dict[str, Path]:\r\n    con = _connect_duckdb()\r\n    rows_by_country = {code: [] for code in focus_codes}\r\n    for year in range(config.trade_year_start, config.trade_year_end + 1):\r\n        fpath = config.dataset_trade_dir / f\"S2_{year}.parquet\"\r\n        if not fpath.exists():\r\n            continue\r\n        ex_all = con.execute(\r\n            f\"\"\"\r\n            SELECT exporter AS country, importer AS partner, SUM({config.trade_value_column}) AS w\r\n            FROM read_parquet('{fpath}')\r\n            GROUP BY exporter, importer\r\n            \"\"\"\r\n        ).df()\r\n        im_all = con.execute(\r\n            f\"\"\"\r\n       ).df()\r\n        ex_all[\"country\"] = ex_all[\"country\"].astype(str)\r\n        ex_all[\"partner\"] = ex_all[\"partner\"].astype(str)\r\n        im_all[\"country\"] = im_all[\"country\"].astype(str)\r\n        im_all[\"partner\"] = im_all[\"partner\"].astype(str)\r\n\r\n        ex_focus = ex_all[ex_all[\"country\"].isin(focus_codes)].copy()\r\n        im_focus = im_all[im_all[\"country\"].isin(focus_codes)].copy()\r\n\r\n        ex_focus = ex_focus.merge(centroids, left_on=\"partner\", right_on=\"code\", how=\"left\")\r\n        im_focus = im_focus.merge(centroids, left_on=\"partner\", right_on=\"code\", how=\"left\")\r\n\r\n        for code in focus_codes:\r\n            exg = ex_focus[ex_focus[\"country\"] == code].copy()\r\n            img = im_focus[im_focus[\"country\"] == code].copy()\r\n            ex_unmatched = exg[\"lat\"].isna() | exg[\"lon\"].isna()\r\n            im_unmatched = img[\"lat\"].isna() | img[\"lon\"].isna()\r\n            exm = exg.loc[~ex_unmatched].copy()\r\n            imm = img.loc[~im_unmatched].copy()\r\n            rows_by_country[code].append(\r\n                {\r\n \"total_imports_value_final_matched\": float(imm[\"w\"].sum()) if len(imm) else 0.0,\r\n                    \"n_export_partners_total\": int(len(exg)),\r\n                    \"n_import_partners_total\": int(len(img)),\r\n                    \"n_export_partners_unmatched\": int(ex_unmatched.sum()),\r\n                    \"n_import_partners_unmatched\":     ),\r\n                    \"unmatched_import_partners_sample\": truncate_codes(\r\n                        img.loc[im_unmatched, \"partner\"].unique().tolist()\r\n                    ),\r\n                }\r\n            )\r\n    con.close()\r\n    written = {}\r\n    for code, rows in rows_by_country.items():\r\n        out = pd.DataFrame(rows).sort_values(\"year\").reset_index(drop=True)\r\n        out_path = output_dir / f\"barycenter_{code}_{config.trade_year_start}_{config.trade_year_end}.csv\"\r\n        out.to_csv(out_path, index=False)\r\n        written[code] = out_path\r\n    return written\r\n\r\n\r\ndef write_legacy_special_country_trade_file(\r\n    source_path: Path,\r\n    output_path: Path,\r\n    country_prefix: str,\r\n) -> Path:\r\n    df = pd.read_csv(source_path)\r\n    prefix = country_prefix.lower()\r\n    rename_map = {\r\n        \"lat_exports\": f\"lat_{prefix}_exports\",\r\n        \"lon_exports\": f\"lon_{prefix}_exports\",\r\n        \"lat_imports\": f\"lat_{prefix}_imports\",\r\n        \"lon_imports\": f\"lon_{prefix}_imports\",\r\n        \"total_exports_value_final_matched\": f\"total_{prefix}_exports_value_final_matched\",\r\n        \"total_imports_value_final_matched\": f\"total_{prefix}_imports_value_final_matched\",\r\n    }\r\n    cols = []\r\n    for col in df.columns:\r\n        cols.append(rename_map.get(col, col))\r\n    df.columns = cols\r\n    df.to_csv(output_path, index=False)\r\n    return output_path\r\n\r\n\r\ndef detect_all_country_codes(config: ProjectConfig) -> list[str]:\r\n    con = _connect_duckdb()\r\n    s2_glob = str(config.dataset_trade_dir / \"S2_*.parquet\")\r\n    df = con.execute(\r\n        f\"\"\"\r\n        WITH all_s2 AS (\r\n            SELECT exporter::VARCHAR AS exporter_code, importer::VARCHAR AS importer_code\r\n            FROM read_parquet('{s2_glob}')\r\n        )\r\n        SELECT DISTINCT code\r\n        FROM (\r\n            SELECT exporter_code AS code FROM all_s2 WHERE exporter_code IS NOT NULL AND exporter_code <> ''\r\n            UNION\r\n            SELECT importer_code AS code FROM all_s2 WHERE importer_code IS NOT NULL AND importer_code <> ''\r\n        )\r\n        ORDER BY code\r\n        \"\"\"\r\n    ).df()\r\n    con.close()\r\n    return df[\"code\"].astype(str).tolist()\r\n\r\n\r\ndef render_global_maps(global_df: pd.DataFrame, shapefile_path: Path, fig_dir: Path) -> None:\r\n    world = gpd.read_file(shapefile_path)\r\n    if \"ISO_A3\" in world.columns:\r\n        world = world[world[\"ISO_A3\"] != \"ATA\"].copy()\r\n    if world.crs is None:\r\n        world = world.set_crs(epsg=4326)\r\n    exports = gpd.GeoDataFrame(\r\n        global_df[[\"year\", \"lat_exports\", \"lon_exports\"]].dropna().copy(),\r\n        geometry=[\r\n            Point(xy)\r\n            for xy in zip(\r\n                global_df.dropna(subset=[\"lat_exports\", \"lon_exports\"])[\"lon_exports\"],\r\n                global_df.dropna(subset=[\"lat_exports\", \"lon_exports\"])[\"lat_exports\"],\r\n            )\r\n        ],\r\n        crs=\"EPSG:4326\",\r\n    ).sort_values(\"year\")\r\n    imports = gpd.GeoDataFrame(\r\n        global_df[[\"year\", \"lat_imports\", \"lon_imports\"]].dropna().copy(),\r\n        geometry=[\r\n            Point(xy)\r\n            for xy in zip(\r\n                global_df.dropna(subset=[\"lat_imports\", \"lon_imports\"])[\"lon_imports\"],\r\n                global_df.dropna(subset=[\"lat_imports\", \"lon_imports\"])[\"lat_imports\"],\r\n            )\r\n        ],\r\n        crs=\"EPSG:4326\",\r\n    ).sort_values(\"year\")\r\n    if exports.empty or imports.empty:\r\n        return\r\n    exports_path = gpd.GeoDataFrame(geometry=[LineString(exports.geometry.tolist())], crs=\"EPSG:4326\")\r\n    imports_path = gpd.GeoDataFrame(geometry=[LineString(imports.geometry.tolist())], crs=\"EPSG:4326\")\r\n    crs_med = CRS.from_proj4(\"+proj=aeqd +lat_0=35 +lon_0=18 +datum=WGS84 +units=m +no_defs\")\r\n    world_p = world.to_crs(crs_med)\r\n    exports_p = exports.to_crs(crs_med)\r\n    imports_p = imports.to_crs(crs_med)\r\n    exports_path_p = exports_path.to_crs(crs_med)\r\n    imports_path_p = imports_path.to_crs(crs_med)\r\n    minx, miny, maxx, maxy = gpd.GeoSeries(\r\n        pd.concat([exports_p.geometry, imports_p.geometry], ignore_index=True),\r\n        crs=crs_med,\r\n    ).total_bounds\r\n    buffer = 1.2e6\r\n    xlim = (minx - buffer, maxx + buffer)\r\n    ylim = (miny - buffer, maxy + buffer)\r\n\r\n    def render(points_p, path_p, color, footer_label, basename):\r\n        fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(10, 6))\r\n        world_p.plot(ax=ax, linewidth=0.4, edgecolor=\"white\", color=\"lightgray\", zorder=1)\r\n        path_p.plot(ax=ax, linewidth=1.1, color=color, alpha=0.28, zorder=3)\r\n        points_p.plot(ax=ax, color=color, markersize=8, zorder=4)\r\n        ax.set_xlim(*xlim)\r\n        ax.set_ylim(*ylim)\r\n        ax.text(0.02, 0.02, footer_label, transform=ax.transAxes, fontsize=9, ha=\"left\", va=\"bottom\")\r\n        ax.set_axis_off()\r\n        plt.tight_layout()\r\n        fig.savefig(fig_dir / f\"{basename}.png\", dpi=300, bbox_inches=\"tight\")\r\n        fig.savefig(fig_dir / f\"{basename}.pdf\", bbox_inches=\"tight\")\r\n        plt.close(fig)\r\n\r\n    render(imports_p, imports_path_p, \"red\", \"Global imports barycenter\", \"barycenter_global_imports_mediterranean\")\r\n    render(\r\n        exports_p,\r\n        exports_path_p,\r\n        \"dodgerblue\",\r\n        \"Global exports barycenter\",\r\n        \"barycenter_global_exports_mediterranean\",\r\n    )\r\n\r\n\r\ndef _load_world_projected(shapefile_path: Path, crs_med: CRS) -> gpd.GeoDataFrame:\r\n    world = gpd.read_file(shapefile_path)\r\n    if \"ISO_A3\" in world.columns:\r\n        world = world[world[\"ISO_A3\"] != \"ATA\"].copy()\r\n    if world.crs is None:\r\n        world = world.set_crs(epsg=4326)\r\n    return world.to_crs(crs_med)\r\n\r\n\r\ndef _load_barycenter_points(\r\n    csv_path: Path,\r\n    lat_exports_col: str,\r\n    lon_exports_col: str,\r\n    lat_imports_col: str,\r\n    lon_imports_col: str,\r\n) -> tuple[gpd.GeoDataFrame, gpd.GeoDataFrame]:\r\n    df = pd.read_csv(csv_path).sort_values(\"year\")\r\n    df = df.dropna(subset=[lat_exports_col, lon_exports_col, lat_imports_col, lon_imports_col]).copy()\r\n    exports = gpd.GeoDataFrame(\r\n        df[[\"year\", lat_exports_col, lon_exports_col]].copy(),\r\n        geometry=[Point(xy) for xy in zip(df[lon_exports_col], df[lat_exports_col])],\r\n        crs=\"EPSG:4326\",\r\n    ).sort_values(\"year\")\r\n    imports = gpd.GeoDataFrame(\r\n        df[[\"year\", lat_imports_col, lon_imports_col]].copy(),\r\n        geometry=[Point(xy) for xy in zip(df[lon_imports_col], df[lat_imports_col])],\r\n        crs=\"EPSG:4326\",\r\n    ).sort_values(\"year\")\r\n    return exports, imports\r\n\r\n\r\ndef _build_projected_paths(\r\n    exports: gpd.GeoDataFrame,\r\n    imports: gpd.GeoDataFrame,\r\n    crs_med: CRS,\r\n) -> tuple[gpd.GeoDataFrame, gpd.GeoDataFrame, gpd.GeoDataFrame, gpd.GeoDataFrame]:\r\n    exports_path = gpd.GeoDataFrame(geometry=[LineString(exports.geometry.tolist())], crs=\"EPSG:4326\")\r\n    imports_path = gpd.GeoDataFrame(geometry=[LineString(imports.geometry.tolist())], crs=\"EPSG:4326\")\r\n    return (\r\n        exports.to_crs(crs_med),\r\n        imports.to_crs(crs_med),\r\n        exports_path.to_crs(crs_med),\r\n        imports_path.to_crs(crs_med),\r\n    )\r\n\r\n\r\ndef _global_extent_from_global_barycenters(global_csv_path: Path, crs_med: CRS, buffer: float = 1.2e6) -> tuple[tuple[float, float], tuple[float, float]]:\r\n    exports, imports = _load_barycenter_points(\r\n        global_csv_path,\r\n        \"lat_exports\",\r\n        \"lon_exports\",\r\n        \"lat_imports\",\r\n        \"lon_imports\",\r\n    )\r\n    exports_p = exports.to_crs(crs_med)\r\n    imports_p = imports.to_crs(crs_med)\r\n    minx, miny, maxx, maxy = gpd.GeoSeries(\r\n        pd.concat([exports_p.geometry, imports_p.geometry], ignore_index=True),\r\n        crs=crs_med,\r\n    ).total_bounds\r\n    return (minx - buffer, maxx + buffer), (miny - buffer, maxy + buffer)\r\n\r\n\r\ndef _selected_year_labels(ax, gdf_sorted: gpd.GeoDataFrame, years: set[int], dx: int, dy: int, fontsize: int) -> None:\r\n    for _, row in gdf_sorted.iterrows():\r\n        year = int(row[\"year\"])\r\n        if year in years:\r\n            ax.text(\r\n                row.geometry.x + dx,\r\n                row.geometry.y + dy,\r\n                str(year),\r\n                fontsize=fontsize,\r\n                color=\"black\",\r\n                alpha=0.8 if fontsize <= 5 else 0.9,\r\n                zorder=10,\r\n            )\r\n\r\n\r\ndef _render_single_footer_map(\r\n    world_p: gpd.GeoDataFrame,\r\n    points_p: gpd.GeoDataFrame,\r\n    path_p: gpd.GeoDataFrame,\r\n    color: str,\r\n    footer_label: str,\r\n    out_png: Path,\r\n    out_pdf: Path,\r\n    xlim: tuple[float, float],\r\n    ylim: tuple[float, float],\r\n    label_years: set[int],\r\n    dx: int,\r\n    dy: int,\r\n    fontsize: int,\r\n) -> None:\r\n    fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(10, 6))\r\n    world_p.plot(ax=ax, linewidth=0.4, edgecolor=\"white\", color=\"lightgray\", zorder=1)\r\n    path_p.plot(ax=ax, linewidth=1.1, color=color, alpha=0.28, zorder=3)\r\n    points_p.plot(ax=ax, color=color, markersize=8, zorder=4)\r\n    _selected_year_labels(ax, points_p.sort_values(\"year\"), label_years, dx, dy, fontsize)\r\n    ax.set_xlim(*xlim)\r\n    ax.set_ylim(*ylim)\r\n    ax.text(0.02, 0.02, footer_label, transform=ax.transAxes, fontsize=9, color=\"black\", ha=\"left\", va=\"bottom\")\r\n    ax.set_axis_off()\r\n    plt.tight_layout()\r\n    plt.savefig(out_png, dpi=300, bbox_inches=\"tight\")\r\n    plt.savefig(out_pdf, bbox_inches=\"tight\")\r\n    plt.close(fig)\r\n\r\n\r\ndef _render_combined_country_map(\r\n    world_p: gpd.GeoDataFrame,\r\n    exports_p: gpd.GeoDataFrame,\r\n    imports_p: gpd.GeoDataFrame,\r\n    exports_path_p: gpd.GeoDataFrame,\r\n    imports_path_p: gpd.GeoDataFrame,\r\n    xlim: tuple[float, float],\r\n    ylim: tuple[float, float],\r\n    title_prefix: str,\r\n    out_png: Path,\r\n    out_pdf: Path,\r\n) -> None:\r\n    fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(10, 8))\r\n    world_p.plot(ax=ax, linewidth=0.4, edgecolor=\"white\", color=\"lightgray\", zorder=1)\r\n    imports_path_p.plot(ax=ax, linewidth=1.0, color=\"red\", alpha=0.25, zorder=3)\r\n    exports_path_p.plot(ax=ax, linewidth=1.0, color=\"dodgerblue\", alpha=0.25, zorder=4)\r\n    imports_p.plot(ax=ax, color=\"red\", markersize=8, zorder=5)\r\n    exports_p.plot(ax=ax, color=\"dodgerblue\", markersize=8, zorder=6)\r\n    legend_elements = [\r\n        Line2D([0], [0], marker=\"o\", color=\"w\", label=f\"{title_prefix} imports barycenter (partners)\", markerfacecolor=\"red\", markersize=6),\r\n        Line2D([0], [0], marker=\"o\", color=\"w\", label=f\"{title_prefix} exports barycenter (partners)\", markerfacecolor=\"dodgerblue\", markersize=6),\r\n    ]\r\n    ax.legend(handles=legend_elements, loc=\"lower left\", frameon=False, fontsize=9)\r\n    years = sorted(set(exports_p[\"year\"].astype(int)).union(set(imports_p[\"year\"].astype(int))))\r\n    ymin, ymax = min(years), max(years)\r\n    label_years = set(range((ymin // 10) * 10, ymax + 1, 10))\r\n    label_years.update([ymin, ymax])\r\n    _selected_year_labels(ax, imports_p.sort_values(\"year\"), label_years, 35000, 35000, 8)\r\n    _selected_year_labels(ax, exports_p.sort_values(\"year\"), label_years, 35000, 35000, 8)\r\n    ax.set_xlim(*xlim)\r\n    ax.set_ylim(*ylim)\r\n    ax.set_axis_off()\r\n    plt.tight_layout()\r\n    plt.savefig(out_png, dpi=300, bbox_inches=\"tight\")\r\n    plt.savefig(out_pdf, bbox_inches=\"tight\")\r\n    plt.close(fig)\r\n\r\n\r\ndef render_legacy_special_maps(\r\n    global_csv_path: Path,\r\n    usa_csv_path: Path,\r\n    china_csv_path: Path,\r\n    shapefile_path: Path,\r\n    fig_dir: Path,\r\n) -> None:\r\n    crs_med = CRS.from_proj4(\"+proj=aeqd +lat_0=35 +lon_0=18 +datum=WGS84 +units=m +no_defs\")\r\n    world_p = _load_world_projected(shapefile_path, crs_med)\r\n    xlim, ylim = _global_extent_from_global_barycenters(global_csv_path, crs_med, buffer=1.2e6)\r\n\r\n    global_exports, global_imports = _load_barycenter_points(\r\n        global_csv_path,\r\n        \"lat_exports\",\r\n        \"lon_exports\",\r\n        \"lat_imports\",\r\n        \"lon_imports\",\r\n    )\r\n    global_exports_p, global_imports_p, global_exports_path_p, global_imports_path_p = _build_projected_paths(\r\n        global_exports,\r\n        global_imports,\r\n        crs_med,\r\n    )\r\n    global_label_years = {1977, 1980, 1985, 1994, 2001, 2009, 2018, 2023}\r\n    _render_single_footer_map(\r\n        world_p,\r\n        global_imports_p,\r\n        global_imports_path_p,\r\n        \"red\",\r\n        \"Global imports barycenter\",\r\n        fig_dir / \"barycenter_global_imports_mediterranean.png\",\r\n        fig_dir / \"barycenter_global_imports_mediterranean.pdf\",\r\n        xlim,\r\n        ylim,\r\n        global_label_years,\r\n        20000,\r\n        20000,\r\n        5,\r\n    )\r\n    _render_single_footer_map(\r\n        world_p,\r\n        global_exports_p,\r\n        global_exports_path_p,\r\n        \"dodgerblue\",\r\n        \"Global exports barycenter\",\r\n        fig_dir / \"barycenter_global_exports_mediterranean.png\",\r\n        fig_dir / \"barycenter_global_exports_mediterranean.pdf\",\r\n        xlim,\r\n        ylim,\r\n        global_label_years,\r\n        20000,\r\n        20000,\r\n        5,\r\n    )\r\n\r\n    usa_exports, usa_imports = _load_barycenter_points(\r\n        usa_csv_path,\r\n        \"lat_us_exports\",\r\n        \"lon_us_exports\",\r\n        \"lat_us_imports\",\r\n        \"lon_us_imports\",\r\n    )\r\n    usa_exports_p, usa_imports_p, usa_exports_path_p, usa_imports_path_p = _build_projected_paths(\r\n        usa_exports,\r\n        usa_imports,\r\n        crs_med,\r\n    )\r\n    _render_combined_country_map(\r\n        world_p,\r\n        usa_exports_p,\r\n        usa_imports_p,\r\n        usa_exports_path_p,\r\n        usa_imports_path_p,\r\n        xlim,\r\n        ylim,\r\n        \"USA\",\r\n        fig_dir / \"barycenter_usa_trade_mediterranean_zoom.png\",\r\n        fig_dir / \"barycenter_usa_trade_mediterranean_zoom.pdf\",\r\n    )\r\n    usa_label_years = {1977, 1980, 1982, 1983, 1985, 1987, 1990, 1994, 1999, 2001, 2009, 2012, 2015, 2018, 2022}\r\n    _render_single_footer_map(\r\n        world_p,\r\n        usa_imports_p,\r\n        usa_imports_path_p,\r\n        \"red\",\r\n        \"USA imports barycenter\",\r\n        fig_dir / \"barycenter_usa_imports_mediterranean.png\",\r\n        fig_dir / \"barycenter_usa_imports_mediterranean.pdf\",\r\n        xlim,\r\n        ylim,\r\n        usa_label_years,\r\n        20000,\r\n        20000,\r\n        5,\r\n"}}
