{"timestamp":"2026-03-11T12:24:29.703Z","type":"response_item","payload":{"type":"function_call_output","call_id":"call_hDLD3OJ6nBbeqMr04UiWHdI9","output":"Exit code: 0\nWall time: 0.6 seconds\nOutput:\n===== CELL 96 =====\r\n**Hurricane Barycenter**\r\n\r\n===== CELL 97 =====\r\n# ============================================================\r\n# Mercator maps of country barycenters (\"stars\") for ALL countries\r\n# - One map for exports, one map for imports\r\n# - No labels\r\n# - Basemap: Natural Earth countries, gray, no Antarctica\r\n# - Points: spherical barycenter per country (across ALL years in the file)\r\n#\r\n# Inputs:\r\n#   1) STATE_FILE: gc_state_long_demeaned.csv (must contain raw x,y,z OR demeaned x_demean,y_demean,z_demean)\r\n#   2) SHAPEFILE:  Natural Earth admin_0 countries shapefile (EPSG:4326)\r\n#\r\n# Output:\r\n#   - gc_country_barycenters_exports.png\r\n#   - gc_country_barycenters_imports.png\r\n# ============================================================\r\n\r\nimport os\r\nimport numpy as np\r\nimport pandas as pd\r\nimport geopandas as gpd\r\nimport matplotlib.pyplot as plt\r\n\r\n# ----------------------------\r\n# CONFIG\r\n# ----------------------------\r\nGC_BASE = str(GC_DATA)\r\nSTATE_FILE = os.path.join(GC_BASE, \"gc_state_long_demeaned.csv\")\r\n\r\nSHAPEFILE = str(NE_SHP)\r\n\r\nFLOWS = [\"exports\", \"imports\"]\r\n\r\n# Use RAW for interpretable geography. If you only have demeaned, set True.\r\nUSE_DEMEANED = False  # <-- set True ONLY if raw x,y,z are not available\r\n\r\n# Basemap styling\r\nBASEMAP_FACE = \"#f2f2f2\"\r\nBASEMAP_EDGE = \"#cfcfcf\"\r\nBASEMAP_EDGE_W = 0.35\r\n\r\n# Point styling (publish-friendly, no labels)\r\nEXPORT_POINT_COLOR = \"#b2182b\"  # deep red\r\nIMPORT_POINT_COLOR = \"#2166ac\"  # deep blue\r\nPOINT_SIZE = 10\r\nPOINT_ALPHA = 0.85\r\nPOINT_EDGE = \"white\"\r\nPOINT_EDGE_W = 0.25\r\n\r\nDPI = 300\r\n\r\n# ----------------------------\r\n# Geometry helpers\r\n# ----------------------------\r\ndef arccos_clip(x):\r\n    return np.arccos(np.clip(x, -1.0, 1.0))\r\n\r\ndef spherical_mean_unitvec(V):\r\n    s = np.sum(V, axis=0)\r\n    n = np.linalg.norm(s)\r\n    if not np.isfinite(n) or n == 0:\r\n        return np.array([np.nan, np.nan, np.nan], dtype=float)\r\n    return s / n\r\n\r\ndef unitvec_to_latlon_deg(v):\r\n    x, y, z = float(v[0]), float(v[1]), float(v[2])\r\n    lat = np.degrees(np.arcsin(np.clip(z, -1.0, 1.0)))\r\n    lon = np.degrees(np.arctan2(y, x))\r\n    if lon <= -180:\r\n        lon += 360\r\n    elif lon > 180:\r\n        lon -= 360\r\n    return float(lat), float(lon)\r\n\r\ndef assert_exists(path):\r\n    if not os.path.exists(path):\r\n        raise FileNotFoundError(f\"Missing file: {path}\")\r\n\r\n# ----------------------------\r\n# Compute barycenters per country\r\n# ----------------------------\r\ndef compute_country_barycenters(state_df, flow):\r\n    df = state_df[state_df[\"flow\"] == flow].copy()\r\n    if df.empty:\r\n        return pd.DataFrame(columns=[\"country\", \"lat\", \"lon\"])\r\n\r\n    # Drop non-finite vectors\r\n    ok = np.isfinite(df[[\"x\", \"y\", \"z\"]]).all(axis=1)\r\n    df = df[ok].copy()\r\n    if df.empty:\r\n        return pd.DataFrame(columns=[\"country\", \"lat\", \"lon\"])\r\n\r\n    out = []\r\n    for c, gc in df.groupby(\"country\", sort=True):\r\n        V = gc[[\"x\", \"y\", \"z\"]].to_numpy(dtype=float)\r\n\r\n        # normalize rows defensively\r\n        norms = np.linalg.norm(V, axis=1)\r\n        keep = np.isfinite(norms) & (norms > 0)\r\n        V = V[keep]\r\n        if V.shape[0] == 0:\r\n            continue\r\n        V = V / norms[keep][:, None]\r\n\r\n        star = spherical_mean_unitvec(V)\r\n        if not np.isfinite(star).all():\r\n            continue\r\n\r\n        lat, lon = unitvec_to_latlon_deg(star)\r\n        out.append({\"country\": c, \"lat\": lat, \"lon\": lon})\r\n\r\n    return pd.DataFrame(out)\r\n\r\n# ----------------------------\r\n# Plot map\r\n# ----------------------------\r\ndef plot_barycenters(world_3857, pts_ll, flow):\r\n    pts = gpd.GeoDataFrame(\r\n        pts_ll,\r\n        geometry=gpd.points_from_xy(pts_ll[\"lon\"], pts_ll[\"lat\"]),\r\n        crs=\"EPSG:4326\",\r\n    ).to_crs(\"EPSG:3857\")\r\n\r\n    fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(14, 8))\r\n    ax.set_axis_off()\r\n\r\n    # Basemap (no Antarctica)\r\n    world_3857.plot(\r\n        ax=ax,\r\n        color=BASEMAP_FACE,\r\n        edgecolor=BASEMAP_EDGE,\r\n        linewidth=BASEMAP_EDGE_W,\r\n        zorder=1\r\n    )\r\n\r\n    color = EXPORT_POINT_COLOR if flow == \"exports\" else IMPORT_POINT_COLOR\r\n\r\n    pts.plot(\r\n        ax=ax,\r\n        color=color,\r\n        markersize=POINT_SIZE,\r\n        alpha=POINT_ALPHA,\r\n        edgecolor=POINT_EDGE,\r\n        linewidth=POINT_EDGE_W,\r\n        zorder=5\r\n    )\r\n\r\n    ax.set_title(f\"{flow.capitalize()} country barycenters (Mercator)\", fontsize=14)\r\n\r\n    out = os.path.join(GC_BASE, f\"gc_country_barycenters_{flow}.png\")\r\n    plt.tight_layout()\r\n    plt.savefig(out, dpi=DPI, bbox_inches=\"tight\")\r\n    plt.close(fig)\r\n    print(f\"✓ Saved: {out}\")\r\n\r\n# ----------------------------\r\n# MAIN\r\n# ----------------------------\r\ndef main():\r\n    assert_exists(STATE_FILE)\r\n    assert_exists(SHAPEFILE)\r\n\r\n    state = pd.read_csv(STATE_FILE)\r\n    state[\"year\"] = state[\"year\"].astype(int)\r\n\r\n    if USE_DEMEANED:\r\n        needed = {\"country\", \"flow\", \"year\", \"x_demean\", \"y_demean\", \"z_demean\"}\r\n        miss = needed - set(state.columns)\r\n        if miss:\r\n            raise ValueError(f\"{STATE_FILE} missing columns: {sorted(miss)}\")\r\n        state = state.rename(columns={\"x_demean\": \"x\", \"y_demean\": \"y\", \"z_demean\": \"z\"})\r\n    else:\r\n        needed = {\"country\", \"flow\", \"year\", \"x\", \"y\", \"z\"}\r\n        miss = needed - set(state.columns)\r\n        if miss:\r\n            raise ValueError(\r\n                f\"{STATE_FILE} missing RAW columns: {sorted(miss)}.\\n\"\r\n                \"Set USE_DEMEANED=True only if you intentionally want demeaned barycenters.\"\r\n            )\r\n\r\n    state = state[[\"country\", \"flow\", \"year\", \"x\", \"y\", \"z\"]].copy()\r\n\r\n    world = gpd.read_file(SHAPEFILE)\r\n    if world.crs is None:\r\n        world = world.set_crs(\"EPSG:4326\")\r\n    else:\r\n        world = world.to_crs(\"EPSG:4326\")\r\n\r\n    # Remove Antarctica and junk codes, then project to Mercator\r\n    world = world[world[\"ISO_A3\"].notna() & (world[\"ISO_A3\"] != \"-99\")].copy()\r\n    world = world[world[\"ISO_A3\"] != \"ATA\"].copy()\r\n    world_3857 = world.to_crs(\"EPSG:3857\")\r\n\r\n    for flow in FLOWS:\r\n        pts = compute_country_barycenters(state, flow)\r\n        if pts.empty:\r\n            print(f\"[WARN] No barycenters computed for flow={flow}.\")\r\n            continue\r\n        plot_barycenters(world_3857, pts, flow)\r\n\r\n    print(\"\\nDone.\")\r\n\r\nif __name__ == \"__mai\r\n\r\n===== CELL 98 =====\r\n# ============================================================\r\n# Country barycenter trajectories (year-to-year great-circle paths)\r\n# - Two maps: exports and imports\r\n# - Mercator EPSG:3857, no Antarctica\r\n# - Trajectories are drawn as great-circle interpolations between yearly barycenters\r\n#\r\n# Inputs:\r\n#   - gc_state_long_demeaned.csv  (must contain raw x,y,z or demeaned)\r\n#   - Natural Earth shapefile (EPSG:4326)\r\n#\r\n# Outputs:\r\n#   - gc_trajectories_exports.png\r\n#   - gc_trajectories_imports.png\r\n# ============================================================\r\n\r\nimport os\r\nimport numpy as np\r\nimport pandas as pd\r\nimport geopandas as gpd\r\nimport matplotlib.pyplot as plt\r\nfrom shapely.geometry import LineString\r\n\r\n# ----------------------------\r\n# CONFIG\r\n# ----------------------------\r\nGC_BASE = str(GC_DATA)\r\nSTATE_FILE = os.path.join(GC_BASE, \"gc_state_long_demeaned.csv\")\r\nSHAPEFILE = str(NE_SHP)\r\n\r\nFLOWS = [\"exports\", \"imports\"]\r\n\r\n# Use raw geography vectors (recommended). Set True only if raw x,y,z missing.\r\nUSE_DEMEANED = False\r\n\r\n# Years to plot (inclusive). Use None to use full available range.\r\nYEAR_MIN = 1980\r\nYEAR_MAX = 2022\r\n\r\n# Trajectory detail: points per segment (year->year). 20 is smooth enough.\r\nPTS_PER_SEGMENT = 20\r\n\r\n# Plot control to avoid spaghetti:\r\n# - \"all\": draw all countries\r\n# - \"topN\": draw only top N countries by number of years present\r\n# - \"subset\": draw only ISO3 in SELECT_COUNTRIES\r\nMODE = \"all\"\r\nTOP_N = 60\r\nSELECT_COUNTRIES = [\"USA\", \"CHN\", \"DEU\", \"JPN\", \"MEX\", \"BRA\", \"IND\"]\r\n\r\n# Basemap styling\r\nBASEMAP_FACE = \"#f2f2f2\"\r\nBASEMAP_EDGE = \"#cfcfcf\"\r\nBASEMAP_EDGE_W = 0.35\r\n\r\n# Line styling (publish-friendly)\r\nEXPORT_LINE = \"#b2182b\"  # deep red\r\nIMPORT_LINE = \"#2166ac\"  # deep blue\r\nLINE_W = 0.55\r\nLINE_ALPHA = 0.20\r\n\r\n# Start/end markers (optional; keep subtle)\r\nDRAW_ENDPOINTS = True\r\nSTART_MARKER = \"o\"\r\nEND_MARKER = \"s\"\r\nMARKER_SIZE = 8\r\nMARKER_ALPHA = 0.35\r\nMARKER_EDGE = \"white\"\r\nMARKER_EDGE_W = 0.25\r\n\r\nDPI = 300\r\n\r\n# ----------------------------\r\n# Helpers (spherical interpolation)\r\n# ----------------------------\r\ndef assert_exists(path):\r\n    if not os.path.exists(path):\r\n        raise FileNotFoundError(f\"Missing file: {path}\")\r\n\r\ndef _normalize_rows(V):\r\n    n = np.linalg.norm(V, axis=1)\r\n    keep = np.isfinite(n) & (n > 0)\r\n    V2 = V[keep] / n[keep][:, None]\r\n    return V2, keep\r\n\r\ndef slerp(u, v, t):\r\n    \"\"\"\r\n    Spherical linear interpolation between unit vectors u and v.\r\n    u, v: (3,) unit vectors\r\n    t: float in [0,1]\r\n    returns: (3,) unit vector\r\n    \"\"\"\r\n    dot = float(np.clip(np.dot(u, v), -1.0, 1.0))\r\n    omega = np.arccos(dot)\r\n    if omega < 1e-12:\r\n        return u.copy()\r\n    so = np.sin(omega)\r\n    return (np.sin((1 - t) * omega) / so) * u + (np.sin(t * omega) / so) * v\r\n\r\ndef unitvec_to_lonlat_deg(p):\r\n    x, y, z = float(p[0]), float(p[1]), float(p[2])\r\n    lat = np.degrees(np.arcsin(np.clip(z, -1.0, 1.0)))\r\n    lon = np.degrees(np.arctan2(y, x))\r\n    # normalize lon to (-180, 180]\r\n    if lon <= -180:\r\n        lon += 360\r\n    elif lon > 180:\r\n        lon -= 360\r\n    return lon, lat\r\n\r\ndef build_country_lines(df_country, pts_per_segment=PTS_PER_SEGMENT):\r\n    \"\"\"\r\n    df_country: rows for a single country+flow with columns year,x,y,z (unit-ish)\r\n    returns: list of shapely LineString in lon/lat\r\n    \"\"\"\r\n    dfc = df_country.sort_values(\"year\")\r\n    V = dfc[[\"x\",\"y\",\"z\"]].to_numpy(float)\r\n    Vn, keep = _normalize_rows(V)\r\n    if Vn.shape[0] < 2:\r\n        return [], None, None\r\n\r\n    years = dfc[\"year\"].to_numpy(int)[keep]\r\n    # Build segments only where consecutive years exist (t -> t+1)\r\n    # If you want to connect across gaps, change this logic.\r\n    lines = []\r\n    start_ll = None\r\n    end_ll = None\r\n\r\n    for i in range(len(years) - 1):\r\n        y0, y1 = years[i], years[i+1]\r\n        if y1 != y0 + 1:\r\n            continue  # skip gaps; avoids long jumps across missing years\r\n        u = Vn[i]\r\n        v = Vn[i+1]\r\n        pts = []\r\n        for k in range(pts_per_segment + 1):\r\n            t = k / pts_per_segment\r\n            p = slerp(u, v, t)\r\n            lon, lat = unitvec_to_lonlat_deg(p)\r\n            pts.append((lon, lat))\r\n        if not start_ll:\r\n            start_ll = pts[0]\r\n        end_ll = pts[-1]\r\n        lines.append(LineString(pts))\r\n\r\n    return lines, start_ll, end_ll\r\n\r\n# ----------------------------\r\n# MAIN\r\n# ----------------------------\r\ndef main():\r\n    assert_exists(STATE_FILE)\r\n    assert_exists(SHAPEFILE)\r\n\r\n    state = pd.read_csv(STATE_FILE)\r\n    state[\"year\"] = state[\"year\"].astype(int)\r\n\r\n    if USE_DEMEANED:\r\n        needed = {\"country\",\"flow\",\"year\",\"x_demean\",\"y_demean\",\"z_demean\"}\r\n        miss = needed - set(state.columns)\r\n        if miss:\r\n            raise ValueError(f\"{STATE_FILE} missing columns: {sorted(miss)}\")\r\n        state = state.rename(columns={\"x_demean\":\"x\",\"y_demean\":\"y\",\"z_demean\":\"z\"})\r\n    else:\r\n        needed = {\"country\",\"flow\",\"year\",\"x\",\"y\",\"z\"}\r\n        miss = needed - set(state.columns)\r\n        if miss:\r\n            raise ValueError(\r\n                f\"{STATE_FILE} missing RAW columns: {sorted(miss)}.\\n\"\r\n                \"Set USE_DEMEANED=True only if you intentionally want demeaned trajectories.\"\r\n            )\r\n\r\n    state = state[[\"country\",\"flow\",\"year\",\"x\",\"y\",\"z\"]].copy()\r\n    state[\"country\"] = state[\"country\"].astype(str).str.upper()\r\n\r\n    # Filter years\r\n    if YEAR_MIN is not None:\r\n        state = state[state[\"year\"] >= YEAR_MIN].copy()\r\n    if YEAR_MAX is not None:\r\n        state = state[state[\"year\"] <= YEAR_MAX].copy()\r\n\r\n    # Basemap -> Mercator\r\n    world = gpd.read_file(SHAPEFILE)\r\n    if world.crs is None:\r\n        world = world.set_crs(\"EPSG:4326\")\r\n    else:\r\n        world = world.to_crs(\"EPSG:4326\")\r\n    world = world[world[\"ISO_A3\"].notna() & (world[\"ISO_A3\"] != \"-99\")].copy()\r\n    world = world[world[\"ISO_A3\"] != \"ATA\"].copy()\r\n    world_3857 = world.to_crs(\"EPSG:3857\")\r\n\r\n    for flow in FLOWS:\r\n        df = state[state[\"flow\"] == flow].copy()\r\n        if df.empty:\r\n            print(f\"[WARN] No data for flow={flow}\")\r\n            continue\r\n\r\n        # choose which countries to draw\r\n        if MODE == \"subset\":\r\n            use\r\n\r\n===== CELL 99 =====\r\nimport os\r\nimport glob\r\nimport numpy as np\r\nimport pandas as pd\r\nimport geopandas as gpd\r\nimport matplotlib.pyplot as plt\r\nfrom shapely.geometry import LineString\r\nfrom matplotlib.lines import Line2D\r\n\r\n# ============================================================\r\n# CONFIG\r\n# ============================================================\r\nGC_BASE = str(GC_DATA)\r\nSHAPEFILE = str(NE_SHP)\r\n\r\n# Your per-country files:\r\n#   barycenter_ABW_1977_2022.csv, barycenter_USA_1977_2022.csv, ...\r\nBARY_GLOB = os.path.join(GC_BASE, f\"barycenter_*_{TRADE_YEAR_START}_{TRADE_YEAR_END}*.csv\")\r\n\r\nYEAR_MIN = TRADE_YEAR_START\r\nYEAR_MAX = TRADE_YEAR_END\r\n\r\n# East/West classification threshold per year (degrees longitude)\r\nDLON_THRESH_DEG = 2.0\r\n\r\n# Optional densification of each yearly segment in lon/lat (0 = none)\r\nPTS_PER_SEGMENT = 0\r\n\r\n# Basemap\r\nBASEMAP_FACE = \"#f2f2f2\"\r\nBASEMAP_EDGE = \"#cfcfcf\"\r\nBASEMAP_EDGE_W = 0.35\r\n\r\n# Trajectories\r\nLINE_W = 0.55\r\nALPHA = 0.22\r\nCOLOR_EAST = \"#b2182b\"   # red\r\nCOLOR_WEST = \"#2166ac\"   # blue\r\nCOLOR_UNCL = \"#7f7f7f\"   # gray\r\n\r\nDPI = 300\r\n\r\n# Projection\r\nPROJ_PRIMARY = \"ESRI:54030\"   # Robinson\r\nPROJ_FALLBACK = \"ESRI:54009\"  # Mollweide\r\n\r\n# Output\r\nOUT_EXPORTS = str(GC_FIG / \"gc_trajectories_exports_publish.png\")\r\nOUT_IMPORTS = str(GC_FIG / \"gc_trajectories_imports_publish.png\")\r\n\r\n\r\n# ============================================================\r\n# HELPERS\r\n# ============================================================\r\ndef assert_exists(path):\r\n    if not os.path.exists(path):\r\n        raise FileNotFoundError(f\"Missing file: {path}\")\r\n\r\ndef pick_projection(world_gdf):\r\n    try:\r\n        world_gdf.to_crs(PROJ_PRIMARY)\r\n        return PROJ_PRIMARY\r\n    except Exception:\r\n        return PROJ_FALLBACK\r\n\r\ndef unwrap_lon(lon_deg_array):\r\n    lon_rad = np.deg2rad(lon_deg_array.astype(float))\r\n    lon_unw = np.unwrap(lon_rad)\r\n    return np.rad2deg(lon_unw)\r\n\r\ndef densify_segment(lon0, lat0, lon1, lat1, n=0):\r\n    if not n or n <= 0:\r\n        return [(lon0, lat0), (lon1, lat1)]\r\n    xs = np.linspace(lon0, lon1, n + 2)\r\n    ys = np.linspace(lat0, lat1, n + 2)\r\n    return list(zip(xs, ys))\r\n\r\ndef read_country_barycenter(path):\r\n    \"\"\"\r\n    Reads your schema:\r\n      country, lat_exports, lon_exports, exports_weight,\r\n              lat_imports, lon_imports, imports_weight, year\r\n    Returns a dataframe with year and the relevant lon/lat columns for both flows.\r\n    \"\"\"\r\n    df = pd.read_csv(path)\r\n\r\n    required = {\r\n        \"country\", \"year\",\r\n        \"lat_exports\", \"lon_exports\",\r\n        \"lat_imports\", \"lon_imports\",\r\n    }\r\n    missing = required - set(df.columns)\r\n    if missing:\r\n        raise ValueError(f\"{os.path.basename(path)} missing columns: {sorted(missing)}\")\r\n\r\n    df = df.copy()\r\n    df[\"year\"] = df[\"year\"].astype(int)\r\n\r\n    for c in [\"lat_exports\",\"lon_exports\",\"lat_imports\",\"lon_imports\"]:\r\n        df[c] = pd.to_numeric(df[c], errors=\"coerce\")\r\n\r\n    df = df[(df[\"year\"] >= YEAR_MIN) & (df[\"year\"] <= YEAR_MAX)].copy()\r\n    return df\r\n\r\ndef build_segments_for_flow(df, flow):\r\n    \"\"\"\r\n    flow in {\"exports\",\"imports\"}\r\n    Returns 3 lists of LineString in lon/lat: east, west, unclear\r\n    \"\"\"\r\n    if flow == \"exports\":\r\n        latcol, loncol = \"lat_exports\", \"lon_exports\"\r\n    else:\r\n        latcol, loncol = \"lat_imports\", \"lon_imports\"\r\n\r\n    d = df[[\"year\", loncol, latcol]].rename(columns={loncol: \"lon\", latcol: \"lat\"}).copy()\r\n    d = d[np.isfinite(d[\"lon\"]) & np.isfinite(d[\"lat\"])].sort_values(\"year\")\r\n\r\n    if d.shape[0] < 2:\r\n        return [], [], []\r\n\r\n    years = d[\"year\"].to_numpy(int)\r\n    lons = d[\"lon\"].to_numpy(float)\r\n    lats = d[\"lat\"].to_numpy(float)\r\n    lons_unw = unwrap_lon(lons)\r\n\r\n    east, west, uncl = [], [], []\r\n\r\n    for i in range(len(years) - 1):\r\n        y0, y1 = years[i], years[i + 1]\r\n        if y1 != y0 + 1:\r\n            continue  # skip gaps\r\n\r\n        dlon = lons_unw[i + 1] - lons_unw[i]\r\n\r\n        pts = densify_segment(lons[i], lats[i], lons[i + 1], lats[i + 1], n=PTS_PER_SEGMENT)\r\n        seg = LineString(pts)\r\n\r\n        if dlon > DLON_THRESH_DEG:\r\n            east.append(seg)\r\n        elif dlon < -DLON_THRESH_DEG:\r\n            west.append(seg)\r\n        else:\r\n            uncl.append(seg)\r\n\r\n    return east, west, uncl\r\n\r\ndef plot_flow(world_4326, flow, out_path):\r\n    files = sorted(glob.glob(BARY_GLOB))\r\n    print(f\"[INFO] {flow}: found {len(files)} barycenter files matching {os.path.basename(BARY_GLOB)}\")\r\n    if not files:\r\n        raise FileNotFoundError(f\"No barycenter files found at: {BARY_GLOB}\")\r\n\r\n    east_lines, west_lines, uncl_lines = [], [], []\r\n\r\n    for fp in files:\r\n        # skip Antarctica barycenter if present\r\n        if os.path.basename(fp).upper().startswith(\"BARYCENTER_ATA_\"):\r\n            continue\r\n\r\n        try:\r\n            df = read_country_barycenter(fp)\r\n        except Exception as e:\r\n            print(f\"[WARN] Skipping {os.path.basename(fp)}: {e}\")\r\n            continue\r\n\r\n        e, w, u = build_segments_for_flow(df, flow)\r\n        east_lines.extend(e)\r\n        west_lines.extend(w)\r\n        uncl_lines.extend(u)\r\n\r\n    if not (east_lines or west_lines or uncl_lines):\r\n        raise ValueError(f\"No valid segments constructed for {flow} in {YEAR_MIN}-{YEAR_MAX}.\")\r\n\r\n    proj = pick_projection(world_4326)\r\n\r\n    world = world_4326.copy()\r\n    world = world[world[\"ISO_A3\"].notna() & (world[\"ISO_A3\"] != \"-99\")].copy()\r\n    world = world[world[\"ISO_A3\"] != \"ATA\"].copy()\r\n    world_p = world.to_crs(proj)\r\n\r\n    def to_proj(lines):\r\n        if not lines:\r\n            return None\r\n        g = gpd.GeoDataFrame(geometry=lines, crs=\"EPSG:4326\")\r\n        return g.to_crs(proj)\r\n\r\n    east_g = to_proj(east_lines)\r\n    west_g = to_proj(west_lines)\r\n    uncl_g = to_proj(uncl_lines)\r\n\r\n    fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(14, 8))\r\n    ax.set_axis_off()\r\n\r\n    world_p.plot(ax=ax, color=BASEMAP_FACE, edgecolor=BASEMAP_EDGE, linewidth=BASEMAP_EDGE_W, zorder=1)\r\n\r\n    # draw unclear first, then directional\r\n    if uncl_g is not None:\r\n        uncl_g.plot(ax=ax, color=COLOR_UNCL, linewidth=LINE_W, alpha=ALPHA, zorder=2)\r\n    if west_g is not None:\r\n        west_g.plot(ax=ax, color=COLOR_WEST, linewidth=LINE_W, al\r\n\r\n===== CELL 100 =====\r\n## Stage 04 — Barycenter Motion Indices (Speed & Drift)\r\n\r\nComputes three motion indices per country and flow (exports / imports), derived from the year-to-year sequence of trade barycenters:\r\n\r\n| Index | Description |\r\n|-------|-------------|\r\n| **Speed** (km/yr) | Mean great-circle distance between consecutive annual barycenters |\r\n| **E-W Drift** (km/yr) | Mean eastward component of each step; positive = drifting toward Asia |\r\n| **Instability** | Fraction of steps where the bearing reverses by more than 90° |\r\n\r\n**Inputs:** per-country barycenter files in `output/05_gravitational_clustering/data/` (written by the *Barycenters for all countries* cell above).\r\n\r\n**Outputs:** `motion_indices_exports_{TRADE_YEAR_START}_{TRADE_YEAR_END}.csv` and `motion_indices_imports_{TRADE_YEAR_START}_{TRADE_YEAR_END}.csv` in the same directory.\r\n\r\n\r\n===== CELL 101 =====\r\nimport os\r\nimport glob\r\nimport numpy as np\r\nimport pandas as pd\r\n\r\n# ============================================================\r\n# CONFIG\r\n# ============================================================\r\nGC_BASE = str(GC_DATA)\r\n\r\n# Input: per-country barycenter files written by the \"Barycenters for all countries\" cell.\r\n# Files follow the pattern: barycenter_<ISO3>_{TRADE_YEAR_START}_{TRADE_YEAR_END}.csv\r\nBARY_GLOB = os.path.join(\r\n    GC_BASE,\r\n    f\"barycenter_*_{TRADE_YEAR_START}_{TRADE_YEAR_END}.csv\"\r\n)\r\n\r\n# Output files\r\nOUT_EXPORTS = os.path.join(\r\n    GC_BASE,\r\n    f\"motion_indices_exports_{TRADE_YEAR_START}_{TRADE_YEAR_END}.csv\"\r\n)\r\nOUT_IMPORTS = os.path.join(\r\n    GC_BASE,\r\n    f\"motion_indices_imports_{TRADE_YEAR_START}_{TRADE_YEAR_END}.csv\"\r\n)\r\n\r\n# Minimum step distance (km) to include a step in bearing calculations.\r\n# Steps shorter than this are considered positional noise and excluded\r\n# from E-W drift and instability to prevent spurious direction changes.\r\nMIN_STEP_KM: float = 75.0\r\n\r\nEARTH_RADIUS_KM: float = 6371.0088\r\n\r\n# ============================================================\r\n# GEOMETRY HELPERS (spherical)\r\n# ============================================================\r\ndef haversine_km(lat1, lon1, lat2, lon2):\r\n    \"\"\"Great-circle distance in km between two lat/lon points (degrees).\"\"\"\r\n    R = EARTH_RADIUS_KM\r\n    lat1, lon1, lat2, lon2 = map(np.radians, [lat1, lon1, lat2, lon2])\r\n    dlat = lat2 - lat1\r\n    dlon = lon2 - lon1\r\n    a = np.sin(dlat / 2)**2 + np.cos(lat1) * np.cos(lat2) * np.sin(dlon / 2)**2\r\n    return 2 * R * np.arcsin(np.sqrt(np.clip(a, 0, 1)))\r\n\r\n\r\ndef initial_bearing_deg(lat1, lon1, lat2, lon2):\r\n    \"\"\"\r\n    Initial bearing from (lat1, lon1) → (lat2, lon2), in degrees [0, 360).\r\n    Uses the spherical-geometry formula; inputs in degrees.\r\n    \"\"\"\r\n    lat1, lon1, lat2, lon2 = map(np.radians, [lat1, lon1, lat2, lon2])\r\n    dlon = lon2 - lon1\r\n    y = np.sin(dlon) * np.cos(lat2)\r\n    x = np.cos(lat1) * np.sin(lat2) - np.sin(lat1) * np.cos(lat2) * np.cos(dlon)\r\n    brng = np.degrees(np.arctan2(y, x))\r\n    return (brng + 360.0) % 360.0\r\n\r\n\r\ndef bearing_change_deg(b1, b2):\r\n    \"\"\"Absolute angular difference between two bearings (0-180°).\"\"\"\r\n    diff = abs(b1 - b2) % 360.0\r\n    return min(diff, 360.0 - diff)\r\n\r\n\r\n# ============================================================\r\n# PER-COUNTRY COMPUTATION\r\n# ============================================================\r\ndef compute_motion_indices(df, flow, min_step_km=MIN_STEP_KM):\r\n    \"\"\"\r\n    df: DataFrame with columns year, lat_{flow}, lon_{flow}\r\n    Returns a dict of motion indices for this country+flow, or None if columns are absent.\r\n    \"\"\"\r\n    if flow == \"exports\":\r\n        lat_col, lon_col = \"lat_exports\", \"lon_exports\"\r\n    else:\r\n        lat_col, lon_col = \"lat_imports\", \"lon_imports\"\r\n\r\n    # Guard: skip files that don't carry the expected per-country schema.\r\n    # (e.g. barycenter_usa_trade_*.csv uses lat_us_exports instead of lat_exports)\r\n    required = {\"year\", lat_col, lon_col}\r\n    if not required.issubset(df.columns):\r\n        return None\r\n\r\n    d = (\r\n        df[[\"year\", lat_col, lon_col]]\r\n        .rename(columns={lat_col: \"lat\", lon_col: \"lon\"})\r\n        .dropna(subset=[\"lat\", \"lon\"])\r\n        .sort_values(\"year\")\r\n        .reset_index(drop=True)\r\n    )\r\n\r\n    if len(d) < 2:\r\n        return None\r\n\r\n    years = d[\"year\"].to_numpy()\r\n    lats  = d[\"lat\"].to_numpy()\r\n    lons  = d[\"lon\"].to_numpy()\r\n\r\n    # Only consecutive year-pairs (no gaps)\r\n    mask = (np.diff(years) == 1)\r\n    if mask.sum() == 0:\r\n        return None\r\n\r\n    # Step distances (all consecutive pairs)\r\n    dists = haversine_km(lats[:-1], lons[:-1], lats[1:], lons[1:])[mask]\r\n    bearings_all = np.array([\r\n        initial_bearing_deg(lats[i], lons[i], lats[i+1], lons[i+1])\r\n        for i in np.where(mask)[0]\r\n    ])\r\n\r\n    # Speed: mean km/yr over all valid consecutive steps\r\n    avg_speed_km_yr = float(np.mean(dists)) if dists.size > 0 else np.nan\r\n\r\n    # Filter short steps before computing bearing-based indices (A.5 fix)\r\n    long_mask = dists >= min_step_km\r\n    dists_long    = dists[long_mask]\r\n    bearings_long = bearings_all[long_mask]\r\n\r\n    if dists_long.size == 0:\r\n        ew_drift_km_yr = np.nan\r\n        instability    = np.nan\r\n    else:\r\n        # E-W drift: mean eastward displacement per step = d * sin(bearing)\r\n        # Positive → net eastward (toward Asia); negative → net westward\r\n        ew_components = dists_long * np.sin(np.radians(bearings_long))\r\n        ew_drift_km_yr = float(np.mean(ew_components))\r\n\r\n        # Instability: fraction of consecutive long steps where bearing reverses > 90°\r\n        if bearings_long.size < 2:\r\n            instability = np.nan\r\n        else:\r\n            changes = np.array([\r\n                bearing_change_deg(bearings_long[k], bearings_long[k+1])\r\n                for k in range(len(bearings_long) - 1)\r\n            ])\r\n            instability = float(np.mean(changes > 90.0))\r\n\r\n    return {\r\n        \"n_steps_total\":     int(dists.size),\r\n        \"n_steps_long\":      int(dists_long.size),\r\n        \"avg_speed_km_yr\":   avg_speed_km_yr,\r\n        \"ew_drift_km_yr\":    ew_drift_km_yr,\r\n        \"instability\":       instability,\r\n        \"min_step_km_used\":  min_step_km,\r\n    }\r\n\r\n\r\n# ============================================================\r\n# MAIN\r\n# ============================================================\r\ndef main():\r\n    files = sorted(glob.glob(BARY_GLOB))\r\n    if not files:\r\n        raise FileNotFoundError(\r\n            f\"No barycenter files found at:\\n  {BARY_GLOB}\\n\"\r\n            \"Make sure the \\'Barycenters for all countries\\' cell has been run first.\"\r\n        )\r\n\r\n    print(f\"Found {len(files)} barycenter files. Computing motion indices …\")\r\n\r\n    results = {\"exports\": [], \"imports\": []}\r\n\r\n    for fp in files:\r\n        try:\r\n            df = pd.read_csv(fp)\r\n        except Exception as e:\r\n            print(f\"  [WARN] Could not read {os.path.basename(fp)}: {e}\")\r\n            continue\r\n\r\n        # Skip Antarctica\r\n        fname = os.path.basename(fp).upper()\r\n        if fname.startswith(\"BARYCENTER_ATA_\"):\r\n        \r\n\r\n"}}
{"timestamp":"2026-03-11T12:24:55.807Z","type":"event_msg","payload":{"type":"agent_message","message":"**Legacy vs Modular: Stage 03 All-Country Barycenter Map Logic**\n\n**What existed in the legacy notebook**\n- In the Stage 03 core cells, the notebook did **not** produce a canonical “all-country barycenter map”.\n- The Stage 03 barycenter logic was split roughly like this:\n  - `Cell 54`: global barycenter CSV\n  - `Cells 58–62`: special global / USA / China maps\n  - `Cell 64`: per-country barycenter CSVs for a **manual focus list** of 12 countries\n  - `Cells 66 and 68`: **subset/exploratory** maps for selected countries only\n- The actual **all-country map behavior** appears later, outside the Stage 03 canonical block:\n  - `Cell 97`: “Mercator maps of country barycenters ('stars') for ALL countries”\n  - This uses `gc_state_long_demeaned.csv` and belongs to the gravitational-clustering workflow, not the core Stage 03 output.\n- So yes, legacy had an all-country barycenter map, but it was a **derived visualization in the clustering section**, not the main Stage 03 deliverable.\n\n**Was all-country data prepared even when only a subset was shown?**\n- Yes, but in two different ways:\n  - In core Stage 03, only the **focus-country** per-country barycenter files were written in `Cell 64`.\n  - Later, a separate “barycenters for all countries” workflow produced all-country per-country files and/or `gc_state_long*` structures for clustering-related maps.\n- The all-country “stars” map in `Cell 97` was not simply plotting Stage 03 focus outputs; it relied on clustering-stage state data and computed one point per country from that state table.\n\n**Which cells/functions handled that legacy behavior**\n- Core Stage 03:\n  - `Cell 54`: global barycenter data\n  - `Cells 58–62`: canonical global / USA / China map logic\n  - `Cell 64`: focus-country per-country barycenters\n  - `Cells 66, 68`: exploratory subset maps\n- All-country derived mapping:\n  - `Cell 97`: all-country barycenter “stars” map\n  - `Cell 99` and neighboring GC cells: all-country trajectory / publish maps based on broad per-country barycenter sets\n\n**What exists now in the modular pipeline**\n- The modular `Stage 03` currently does:\n  - global barycenters\n  - focus-country barycenters\n  - detection of all country codes\n  - all-country barycenters\n  - special global / USA / China maps\n- It does **not** render an all-country map in `Stage 03`.\n- That means the modular version is computing the **underlying all-country data**, but not producing the comparable all-country visualization.\n\n**Is that all-country plotting logic missing now?**\n- As a figure: **yes**, it is missing from the modular pipeline.\n- As underlying data preparation: **no**, the modular pipeline already computes the all-country per-country barycenter CSVs.\n\n**Is the modular version duplicating work that legacy did only once?**\n- Yes, and this is the important performance point.\n- In the modular `stage_03_barycenters.run()`:\n  - it computes focus-country barycenters once\n  - then rescans the country universe and computes all-country barycenters again\n- That duplicates work for the focus countries.\n- Legacy core Stage 03 did not appear to do this exact duplication inside one stage. It separated:\n  - focus-country outputs for Stage 03/reporting\n  - broader all-country preparation later for clustering-style analysis\n\n**Does this change the Stage 03 performance diagnosis?**\n- No, not materially.\n- It reinforces the earlier diagnosis that the real bottleneck is still:\n  - duplicate data scans\n  - duplicate barycenter passes\n  - repeated year-by-year aggregation and merge work\n- It does **not** suggest plotting was a major hidden cost in legacy Stage 03.\n- The missing all-country map would add some plotting overhead, but that would be modest relative to the cost of recomputing barycenters for all countries.\n\n**Classification of the missing all-country map**\n- It should be treated as **optional / exploratory**, not canonical Stage 03 parity.\n- Reason:\n  - the canonical Stage 03 visual outputs are the global / USA / China special maps\n  - the all-country “stars” map in legacy lived in the clustering section and was derived from later-state data\n  - it was not part of the core baseline Stage 03 output contract\n\n**Would restoring it materially affect runtime?**\n- Probably not much.\n- Restoring the map itself would add some figure-generation time, but the heavy runtime remains in the data preparation.\n- The main Stage 03 performance issue is still the duplicated computation path, not the absence of that map.\n\n**Updated Stage 03 performance interpretation**\n- The legacy notebook supports your hypothesis only partially:\n  - there was an all-country barycenter map\n  - but it was not a core Stage 03 canonical map\n  - it was a later derived visualization in the clustering block\n- The modular pipeline already computes the expensive all-country barycenter data needed for such a map.\n- Therefore the strongest performance conclusion remains unchanged:\n  - the waste is in **computing focus-country and all-country barycenters separately**, plus the extra country-detection scan\n  - not in omitted plotting behavior","phase":"final_answer"}}

It separated:\n  - focus-country outputs for Stage 03/reporting\n  - broader all-country preparation later for clustering-style analysis\n\n**Does this change the Stage 03 performance diagnosis?**\n- No, not materially.\n- It reinforces the earlier diagnosis that the real bottleneck is still:\n  - duplicate data scans\n  - duplicate barycenter passes\n  - repeated year-by-year aggregation and merge work\n- It does **not** suggest plotting was a major hidden cost in legacy Stage 03.\n- The missing all-country map would add some plotting overhead, but that would be modest relative to the cost of recomputing barycenters for all countries.\n\n**Classification of the missing all-country map**\n- It should be treated as **optional / exploratory**, not canonical Stage 03 parity.\n- Reason:\n  - the canonical Stage 03 visual outputs are the global / USA / China special maps\n  - the all-country “stars” map in legacy lived in the clustering section and was derived from later-state data\n  - it was not part of the core baseline Stage 03 output contract\n\n**Would restoring it materially affect runtime?**\n- Probably not much.\n- Restoring the map itself would add some figure-generation time, but the heavy runtime remains in the data preparation.\n- The main Stage 03 performance issue is still the duplicated computation path, not the absence of that map.\n\n**Updated Stage 03 performance interpretation**\n- The legacy notebook supports your hypothesis only partially:\n  - there was an all-country barycenter map\n  - but it was not a core Stage 03 canonical map\n  - it was a later derived visualization in the clustering block\n- The modular pipeline already computes the expensive all-country barycenter data needed for such a map.\n- Therefore the strongest performance conclusion remains unchanged:\n  - the waste is in **computing focus-country and all-country barycenters separately**, plus the extra country-detection scan\n  - not in omitted plotting behavior"}],"phase":"final_answer"}}
{"timestamp":"2026-03-11T12:24:56.986Z","type":"event_msg","payload":{"type":"task_complete","turn_id":"019cdcda-8b6b-7ca0-b176-072d50a08d64","last_agent_message":"**Legacy vs Modular: Stage 03 All-Country Barycenter Map Logic**\n\n**What existed in the legacy notebook**\n- In the Stage 03 core cells, the notebook did **not** produce a canonical “all-country barycenter map”.\n- The Stage 03 barycenter logic was split roughly like this:\n  - `Cell 54`: global barycenter CSV\n  - `Cells 58–62`: special global / USA / China maps\n  - `Cell 64`: per-country barycenter CSVs for a **manual focus list** of 12 countries\n  - `Cells 66 and 68`: **subset/exploratory** maps for selected countries only\n- The actual **all-country map behavior** appears later, outside the Stage 03 canonical block:\n  - `Cell 97`: “Mercator maps of country barycenters ('stars') for ALL countries”\n  - This uses `gc_state_long_demeaned.csv` and belongs to the gravitational-clustering workflow, not the core Stage 03 output.\n- So yes, legacy had an all-country barycenter map, but it was a **derived visualization in the clustering section**, not the main Stage 03 deliverable.\n\n**Was all-country data prepared even when only a subset was shown?**\n- Yes, but in two different ways:\n  - In core Stage 03, only the **focus-country** per-country barycenter files were written in `Cell 64`.\n  - Later, a separate “barycenters for all countries” workflow produced all-country per-country files and/or `gc_state_long*` structures for clustering-related maps.\n- The all-country “stars” map in `Cell 97` was not simply plotting Stage 03 focus outputs; it relied on clustering-stage state data and computed one point per country from that state table.\n\n**Which cells/functions handled that legacy behavior**\n- Core Stage 03:\n  - `Cell 54`: global barycenter data\n  - `Cells 58–62`: canonical global / USA / China map logic\n  - `Cell 64`: focus-country per-country barycenters\n  - `Cells 66, 68`: exploratory subset maps\n- All-country derived mapping:\n  - `Cell 97`: all-country barycenter “stars” map\n  - `Cell 99` and neighboring GC cells: all-country trajectory / publish maps based on broad per-country barycenter sets\n\n**What exists now in the modular pipeline**\n- The modular `Stage 03` currently does:\n  - global barycenters\n  - focus-country barycenters\n  - detection of all country codes\n  - all-country barycenters\n  - special global / USA / China maps\n- It does **not** render an all-country map in `Stage 03`.\n- That means the modular version is computing the **underlying all-country data**, but not producing the comparable all-country visualization.\n\n**Is that all-country plotting logic missing now?**\n- As a figure: **yes**, it is missing from the modular pipeline.\n- As underlying data preparation: **no**, the modular pipeline already computes the all-country per-country barycenter CSVs.\n\n**Is the modular version duplicating work that legacy did only once?**\n- Yes, and this is the important performance point.\n- In the modular `stage_03_barycenters.run()`:\n  - it computes focus-country barycenters once\n  - then rescans the country universe and computes all-country barycenters again\n- That duplicates work for the focus countries.\n- Legacy core Stage 03 did not appear to do this exact duplication inside one stage. It separated:\n  - focus-country outputs for Stage 03/reporting\n  - broader all-country preparation later for clustering-style analysis\n\n**Does this change the Stage 03 performance diagnosis?**\n- No, not materially.\n- It reinforces the earlier diagnosis that the real bottleneck is still:\n  - duplicate data scans\n  - duplicate barycenter passes\n  - repeated year-by-year aggregation and merge work\n- It does **not** suggest plotting was a major hidden cost in legacy Stage 03.\n- The missing all-country map would add some plotting overhead, but that would be modest relative to the cost of recomputing barycenters for all countries.\n\n**Classification of the missing all-country map**\n- It should be treated as **optional / exploratory**, not canonical Stage 03 parity.\n- Reason:\n  - the canonical Stage 03 visual outputs are the global / USA / China special maps\n  - the all-country “stars” map in legacy lived in the clustering section and was derived from later-state data\n  - it was not part of the core baseline Stage 03 output contract\n\n**Would restoring it materially affect runtime?**\n- Probably not much.\n- Restoring the map itself would add some figure-generation time, but the heavy runtime remains in the data preparation.\n- The main Stage 03 performance issue is still the duplicated computation path, not the absence of that map.\n\n**Updated Stage 03 performance interpretation**\n- The legacy notebook supports your hypothesis only partially:\n  - there was an all-country barycenter map\n  - but it was not a core Stage 03 canonical map\n  - it was a later derived visualization in the clustering block\n- The modular pipeline already computes the expensive all-country barycenter data needed for such a map.\n- Therefore the strongest performance conclusion remains unchanged:\n  - the waste is in **computing focus-country and all-country barycenters separately**, plus the extra country-detection scan\n  - not in omitted plotting behavior"}}

\"country_centroids_augmented.csv\"\r\n    if not centroids_path.exists():\r\n        raise FileNotFoundError(f\"Stage 03 requires Stage 01 centroids at {centroids_path}\")\r\n    centroids = shutil.copyfile(china_path, artifacts.china_trade_path)\r\n\r\n    if make_figures:\r\n        render_legacy_special_maps(\r\n            artifacts.global_barycenter_path,\r\n            artifacts.usa_trade_path,\r\n            artifacts.china_trade_path,\r\n            config.natural_earth_shapefile_path,\r\n            artifacts.fig_dir,\r\n        )\r\n\r\n    temp_legacy_check = \"Stage 03 complete. focus=%d all=%d global_rows=%d output=%s\",\r\n        len(focus_written),\r\n        len(all_written),\r\n        len(global_df),\r\n        artifacts.stage_dir,\r\n    )\r\n    return {\r\n        \"run_dir\": str(run_dir),\r\n        \"stage_dir\": str(artifacts.stage_dir),\r\n        \"global_barycenter_path\": str(artifacts.global_barycenter_path),\r\n        \"usa_trade_path\": str(artifacts.usa_trade_path),\r\n        \"china_trade_path\": str(artifacts.china_trade_path),\r\n    }\r\n"}}
{"timestamp":"2026-03-11T12:31:20.926Z","type":"response_item","payload":{"type":"function_call_output","call_id":"call_OMGIOZgWBLeu2bzMJ9xSk7Sv","output":"Exit code: 0\nWall time: 0.7 seconds\nOutput:\nfrom __future__ import annotations\r\n\r\nfrom dataclasses import dataclass\r\nfrom pathlib import Path\r\n\r\nimport duckdb\r\nimport geopandas as gpd\r\nimport matplotlib.pyplot as plt\r\nimport numpy as np\r\nimport pandas as pd\r\nfrom pyproj import CRS\r\nfrom shapely.geometry import LineString, Point, box\r\nfrom matplotlib.lines import Line2D\r\n\r\nfrom geoanalisis.config import ProjectConfig\r\n\r\n\r\n@dataclass(frozen=True)\r\nclass BarycenterArtifacts:\r\n    stage_dir: Path\r\n    data_dir: Path\r\n    fig_dir: Path\r\n    global_barycenter_path: Path\r\n    usa_trade_path: Path\r\n    china_trade_path: Path\r\n\r\n\r\ndef build_barycenter_artifact_paths(stage_dir: Path) -> BarycenterArtifacts:\r\n    data_dir = stage_dir / \"data\"\r\n    fig_dir = stage_dir / \"fig\"\r\n    return BarycenterArtifacts(\r\n        stage_dir=stage_dir,\r\n        data_dir=data_dir,\r\n        fig_dir=fig_dir,\r\n        global_barycenter_path=data_dir / \"barycenter_imports_exports_1976_2023.csv\",\r\n        usa_trade_path=data_dir / \"barycenter_usa_trade_1976_2023.csv\",\r\n        china_trade_path=data_dir / \"barycenter_china_trade_1976_2023.csv\",\r\n    )\r\n\r\n\r\ndef _connect_duckdb():\r\n    con = duckdb.connect()\r\n    con.execute(\"PRAGMA threads=8\")\r\n    return con\r\n\r\n\r\ndef weighted_mean(values: pd.Series, weights: pd.Series) -> float:\r\n    w = weights.to_numpy(dtype=float)\r\n    v = values.to_numpy(dtype=float)\r\n    s = np.nansum(w)\r\n    if s == 0 or np.isnan(s):\r\n        return np.nan\r\n    return float(np.nansum(v * w) / s)\r\n\r\n\r\ndef truncate_codes(codes, max_codes: int = 50) -> str:\r\n    codes = list(codes) if codes is not None else []\r\n    if not codes:\r\n        return \"\"\r\n    if len(codes) <= max_codes:\r\n        return \";\".join(codes)\r\n    return \";\".join(codes[:max_codes]) + f\";...(plus {len(codes) - max_codes} more)\"\r\n\r\n\r\ndef load_centroids(centroids_path: Path) -> pd.DataFrame:\r\n    centroids = pd.read_csv(centroids_path, usecols=[\"code\", \"lat\", \"lon\"])\r\n    centroids[\"code\"] = centroids[\"code\"].astype(str)\r\n    return centroids.dropna(subset=[\"lat\", \"lon\"]).copy()\r\n\r\n\r\ndef compute_global_barycenters(\r\n    config: ProjectConfig, centroids: pd.DataFrame, output_path: Path\r\n) -> pd.DataFrame:\r\n    con = _connect_duckdb()\r\n    rows = []\r\n    for year in range(config.trade_year_start, config.trade_year_end + 1):\r\n        fpath = config.dataset_trade_dir / f\"S2_{year}.parquet\"\r\n        if not fpath.exists():\r\n            continue\r\n        exports_df = con.execute(\r\n            f\"\"\"\r\n            SELECT exporter AS code, SUM({config.trade_value_column}) AS exports_value\r\n            FROM read_parquet('{fpath}')\r\n            GROUP BY exporter\r\n            \"\"\"\r\n        ).df()\r\n        imports_df = con.execute(\r\n            f\"\"\"\r\n            SELECT importer AS code, SUM({config.trade_value_column}) AS imports_value\r\n            FROM read_parquet('{fpath}')\r\n            GROUP BY importer\r\n            \"\"\"\r\n        ).df()\r\n        exports_df[\"code\"] = exports_df[\"code\"].astype(str)\r\n        imports_df[\"code\"] = imports_df[\"code\"].astype(str)\r\n        ex = exports_df.merge(centroids, on=\"code\", how=\"left\")\r\n        im = imports_df.merge(centroids, on=\"code\", how=\"left\")\r\n        ex_unmatched = ex[\"lat\"].isna() | ex[\"lon\"].isna()\r\n        im_unmatched = im[\"lat\"].isna() | im[\"lon\"].isna()\r\n        exm = ex.loc[~ex_unmatched].copy()\r\n        imm = im.loc[~im_unmatched].copy()\r\n        rows.append(\r\n            {\r\n                \"year\": year,\r\n                \"lat_exports\": weighted_mean(exm[\"lat\"], exm[\"exports_value\"]) if len(exm) else np.nan,\r\n                \"lon_exports\": weighted_mean(exm[\"lon\"], exm[\"exports_value\"]) if len(exm) else np.nan,\r\n                \"lat_imports\": weighted_mean(imm[\"lat\"], imm[\"imports_value\"]) if len(imm) else np.nan,\r\n                \"lon_imports\": weighted_mean(imm[\"lon\"], imm[\"imports_value\"]) if len(imm) else np.nan,\r\n                \"total_exports_value_matched\": float(exm[\"exports_value\"].sum()) if len(exm) else 0.0,\r\n                \"total_imports_value_matched\": float(imm[\"imports_value\"].sum()) if len(imm) else 0.0,\r\n                \"n_exporter_nodes_total\": len(ex),\r\n                \"n_importer_nodes_total\": len(im),\r\n                \"n_exporter_nodes_matched\": int((~ex_unmatched).sum()),\r\n                \"n_importer_nodes_matched\": int((~im_unmatched).sum()),\r\n                \"n_exporter_nodes_unmatched\": int(ex_unmatched.sum()),\r\n                \"n_importer_nodes_unmatched\": int(im_unmatched.sum()),\r\n                \"unmatched_exporter_codes_sample\": truncate_codes(\r\n                    ex.loc[ex_unmatched, \"code\"].unique().tolist()\r\n                ),\r\n                \"unmatched_importer_codes_sample\": truncate_codes(\r\n                    im.loc[im_unmatched, \"code\"].unique().tolist()\r\n                ),\r\n            }\r\n        )\r\n    con.close()\r\n    out = pd.DataFrame(rows).sort_values(\"year\").reset_index(drop=True)\r\n    out.to_csv(output_path, index=False)\r\n    return out\r\n\r\n\r\ndef compute_country_barycenters(\r\n    config: ProjectConfig,\r\n    centroids: pd.DataFrame,\r\n    focus_codes: list[str],\r\n    output_dir: Path,\r\n) -> dict[str, Path]:\r\n    con = _connect_duckdb()\r\n    rows_by_country = {code: [] for code in focus_codes}\r\n    for year in range(config.trade_year_start, config.trade_year_end + 1):\r\n        fpath = config.dataset_trade_dir / f\"S2_{year}.parquet\"\r\n        if not fpath.exists():\r\n            continue\r\n        ex_all = con.execute(\r\n            f\"\"\"\r\n            SELECT exporter AS country, importer AS partner, SUM({config.trade_value_column}) AS w\r\n            FROM read_parquet('{fpath}')\r\n            GROUP BY exporter, importer\r\n            \"\"\"\r\n        ).df()\r\n        im_all = con.execute(\r\n            f\"\"\"\r\n            SELECT importer AS country, exporter AS partner, SUM({config.trade_value_column}) AS w\r\n            FROM read_parquet('{fpath}')\r\n            GROUP BY importer, exporter\r\n            \"\"\"\r\n        ).df()\r\n        ex_all[\"country\"] = ex_all[\"country\"].astype(str)\r\n        ex_all[\"partner\"] = ex_all[\"partner\"].astype(str)\r\n        im_all[\"country\"] = im_all[\"country\"].astype(str)\r\n        im_all[\"partner\"] = im_all[\"partner\"].astype(str)\r\n\r\n        ex_focus = ex_all[ex_all[\"country\"].isin(focus_codes)].copy()\r\n        im_focus = im_all[im_all[\"country\"].isin(focus_codes)].copy()\r\n\r\n        ex_focus = ex_focus.merge(centroids, left_on=\"partner\", right_on=\"code\", how=\"left\")\r\n        im_focus = im_focus.merge(centroids, left_on=\"partner\", right_on=\"code\", how=\"left\")\r\n\r\n        for code in focus_codes:\r\n            exg = ex_focus[ex_focus[\"country\"] == code].copy()\r\n            img = im_focus[im_focus[\"country\"] == code].copy()\r\n            ex_unmatched = exg[\"lat\"].isna() | exg[\"lon\"].isna()\r\n            im_unmatched = img[\"lat\"].isna() | img[\"lon\"].isna()\r\n            exm = exg.loc[~ex_unmatched].copy()\r\n            imm = img.loc[~im_unmatched].copy()\r\n            rows_by_country[code].append(\r\n                {\r\n                    \"year\": year,\r\n                    \"lat_exports\": weighted_mean(exm[\"lat\"], exm[\"w\"]) if len(exm) else np.nan,\r\n                    \"lon_exports\": weighted_mean(exm[\"lon\"], exm[\"w\"]) if len(exm) else np.nan,\r\n                    \"lat_imports\": weighted_mean(imm[\"lat\"], imm[\"w\"]) if len(imm) else np.nan,\r\n                    \"lon_imports\": weighted_mean(imm[\"lon\"], imm[\"w\"]) if len(imm) else np.nan,\r\n                    \"total_exports_value_final_matched\": float(exm[\"w\"].sum()) if len(exm) else 0.0,\r\n                    \"total_imports_value_final_matched\": float(imm[\"w\"].sum()) if len(imm) else 0.0,\r\n                    \"n_export_partners_total\": int(len(exg)),\r\n                    \"n_import_partners_total\": int(len(img)),\r\n                    \"n_export_partners_unmatched\": int(ex_unmatched.sum()),\r\n                    \"n_import_partners_unmatched\": int(im_unmatched.sum()),\r\n                    \"unmatched_export_partners_sample\": truncate_codes(\r\n                        exg.loc[ex_unmatched, \"partner\"].unique().tolist()\r\n                    ),\r\n                    \"unmatched_import_partners_sample\": truncate_codes(\r\n                        img.loc[im_unmatched, \"partner\"].unique().tolist()\r\n                    ),\r\n                }\r\n            )\r\n    con.close()\r\n    written = {}\r\n    for code, rows in rows_by_country.items():\r\n        out = pd.DataFrame(rows).sort_values(\"year\").reset_index(drop=True)\r\n        out_path = output_dir / f\"barycenter_{code}_{config.trade_year_start}_{config.trade_year_end}.csv\"\r\n        out.to_csv(out_path, index=False)\r\n        written[code] = out_path\r\n    return written\r\n\r\n\r\ndef write_legacy_special_country_trade_file(\r\n    source_path: Path,\r\n    output_path: Path,\r\n    country_prefix: str,\r\n) -> Path:\r\n    df = pd.read_csv(source_path)\r\n    prefix = country_prefix.lower()\r\n    rename_map = {\r\n        \"lat_exports\": f\"lat_{prefix}_exports\",\r\n        \"lon_exports\": f\"lon_{prefix}_exports\",\r\n        \"lat_imports\": f\"lat_{prefix}_imports\",\r\n        \"lon_imports\": f\"lon_{prefix}_imports\",\r\n        \"total_exports_value_final_matched\": f\"total_{prefix}_exports_value_final_matched\",\r\n        \"total_imports_value_final_matched\": f\"total_{prefix}_imports_value_final_matched\",\r\n    }\r\n    cols = []\r\n    for col in df.columns:\r\n        cols.append(rename_map.get(col, col))\r\n    df.columns = cols\r\n    df.to_csv(output_path, index=False)\r\n    return output_path\r\n\r\n\r\ndef detect_all_country_codes(config: ProjectConfig) -> list[str]:\r\n    con = _connect_duckdb()\r\n    s2_glob = str(config.dataset_trade_dir / \"S2_*.parquet\")\r\n    df = con.execute(\r\n        f\"\"\"\r\n        WITH all_s2 AS (\r\n            SELECT exporter::VARCHAR AS exporter_code, importer::VARCHAR AS importer_code\r\n            FROM read_parquet('{s2_glob}')\r\n        )\r\n        SELECT DISTINCT code\r\n        FROM (\r\n            SELECT exporter_code AS code FROM all_s2 WHERE exporter_code IS NOT NULL AND exporter_code <> ''\r\n            UNION\r\n            SELECT importer_code AS code FROM all_s2 WHERE importer_code IS NOT NULL AND importer_code <> ''\r\n        )\r\n        ORDER BY code\r\n        \"\"\"\r\n    ).df()\r\n    con.close()\r\n    return df[\"code\"].astype(str).tolist()\r\n\r\n\r\ndef render_global_maps(global_df: pd.DataFrame, shapefile_path: Path, fig_dir: Path) -> None:\r\n    world = gpd.read_file(shapefile_path)\r\n    if \"ISO_A3\" in world.columns:\r\n        world = world[world[\"ISO_A3\"] != \"ATA\"].copy()\r\n    if world.crs is None:\r\n        world = world.set_crs(epsg=4326)\r\n    exports = gpd.GeoDataFrame(\r\n        global_df[[\"year\", \"lat_exports\", \"lon_exports\"]].dropna().copy(),\r\n        geometry=[\r\n            Point(xy)\r\n            for xy in zip(\r\n                global_df.dropna(subset=[\"lat_exports\", \"lon_exports\"])[\"lon_exports\"],\r\n                global_df.dropna(subset=[\"lat_exports\", \"lon_exports\"])[\"lat_exports\"],\r\n            )\r\n        ],\r\n        crs=\"EPSG:4326\",\r\n    ).sort_values(\"year\")\r\n    imports = gpd.GeoDataFrame(\r\n        global_df[[\"year\", \"lat_imports\", \"lon_imports\"]].dropna().copy(),\r\n        geometry=[\r\n            Point(xy)\r\n            for xy in zip(\r\n                global_df.dropna(subset=[\"lat_imports\", \"lon_imports\"])[\"lon_imports\"],\r\n                global_df.dropna(subset=[\"lat_imports\", \"lon_imports\"])[\"lat_imports\"],\r\n            )\r\n        ],\r\n        crs=\"EPSG:4326\",\r\n    ).sort_values(\"year\")\r\n    if exports.empty or imports.empty:\r\n        return\r\n    exports_path = gpd.GeoDataFrame(geometry=[LineString(exports.geometry.tolist())], crs=\"EPSG:4326\")\r\n    imports_path = gpd.GeoDataFrame(geometry=[LineString(imports.geometry.tolist())], crs=\"EPSG:4326\")\r\n    crs_med = CRS.from_proj4(\"+proj=aeqd +lat_0=35 +lon_0=18 +datum=WGS84 +units=m +no_defs\")\r\n    world_p = world.to_crs(crs_med)\r\n    exports_p = exports.to_crs(crs_med)\r\n    imports_p = imports.to_crs(crs_med)\r\n    exports_path_p = exports_path.to_crs(crs_med)\r\n    imports_path_p = imports_path.to_crs(crs_med)\r\n    minx, miny, maxx, maxy = gpd.GeoSeries(\r\n        pd.concat([exports_p.geometry, imports_p.geometry], ignore_index=True),\r\n        crs=crs_med,\r\n    ).total_bounds\r\n    buffer = 1.2e6\r\n    xlim = (minx - buffer, maxx + buffer)\r\n    ylim = (miny - buffer, maxy + buffer)\r\n\r\n    def render(points_p, path_p, color, footer_label, basename):\r\n        fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(10, 6))\r\n        world_p.plot(ax=ax, linewidth=0.4, edgecolor=\"white\", color=\"lightgray\", zorder=1)\r\n        path_p.plot(ax=ax, linewidth=1.1, color=color, alpha=0.28, zorder=3)\r\n        points_p.plot(ax=ax, color=color, markersize=8, zorder=4)\r\n        ax.set_xlim(*xlim)\r\n        ax.set_ylim(*ylim)\r\n        ax.text(0.02, 0.02, footer_label, transform=ax.transAxes, fontsize=9, ha=\"left\", va=\"bottom\")\r\n        ax.set_axis_off()\r\n        plt.tight_layout()\r\n        fig.savefig(fig_dir / f\"{basename}.png\", dpi=300, bbox_inches=\"tight\")\r\n        fig.savefig(fig_dir / f\"{basename}.pdf\", bbox_inches=\"tight\")\r\n        plt.close(fig)\r\n\r\n    render(imports_p, imports_path_p, \"red\", \"Global imports barycenter\", \"barycenter_global_imports_mediterranean\")\r\n    render(\r\n        exports_p,\r\n        exports_path_p,\r\n        \"dodgerblue\",\r\n        \"Global exports barycenter\",\r\n        \"barycenter_global_exports_mediterranean\",\r\n    )\r\n\r\n\r\ndef _load_world_projected(shapefile_path: Path, crs_med: CRS) -> gpd.GeoDataFrame:\r\n    world = gpd.read_file(shapefile_path)\r\n    if \"ISO_A3\" in world.columns:\r\n        world = world[world[\"ISO_A3\"] != \"ATA\"].copy()\r\n    if world.crs is None:\r\n        world = world.set_crs(epsg=4326)\r\n    return world.to_crs(crs_med)\r\n\r\n\r\ndef _load_barycenter_points(\r\n    csv_path: Path,\r\n    lat_exports_col: str,\r\n    lon_exports_col: str,\r\n    lat_imports_col: str,\r\n    lon_imports_col: str,\r\n) -> tuple[gpd.GeoDataFrame, gpd.GeoDataFrame]:\r\n    df = pd.read_csv(csv_path).sort_values(\"year\")\r\n    df = df.dropna(subset=[lat_exports_col, lon_exports_col, lat_imports_col, lon_imports_col]).copy()\r\n    exports = gpd.GeoDataFrame(\r\n        df[[\"year\", lat_exports_col, lon_exports_col]].copy(),\r\n        geometry=[Point(xy) for xy in zip(df[lon_exports_col], df[lat_exports_col])],\r\n        crs=\"EPSG:4326\",\r\n    ).sort_values(\"year\")\r\n    imports = gpd.GeoDataFrame(\r\n        df[[\"year\", lat_imports_col, lon_imports_col]].copy(),\r\n        geometry=[Point(xy) for xy in zip(df[lon_imports_col], df[lat_imports_col])],\r\n        crs=\"EPSG:4326\",\r\n    ).sort_values(\"year\")\r\n    return exports, imports\r\n\r\n\r\ndef _build_projected_paths(\r\n    exports: gpd.GeoDataFrame,\r\n    imports: gpd.GeoDataFrame,\r\n    crs_med: CRS,\r\n) -> tuple[gpd.GeoDataFrame, gpd.GeoDataFrame, gpd.GeoDataFrame, gpd.GeoDataFrame]:\r\n    exports_path = gpd.GeoDataFrame(geometry=[LineString(exports.geometry.tolist())], crs=\"EPSG:4326\")\r\n    imports_path = gpd.GeoDataFrame(geometry=[LineString(imports.geometry.tolist())], crs=\"EPSG:4326\")\r\n    return (\r\n        exports.to_crs(crs_med),\r\n        imports.to_crs(crs_med),\r\n        exports_path.to_crs(crs_med),\r\n        imports_path.to_crs(crs_med),\r\n    )\r\n\r\n\r\ndef _global_extent_from_global_barycenters(global_csv_path: Path, crs_med: CRS, buffer: float = 1.2e6) -> tuple[tuple[float, float], tuple[float, float]]:\r\n    exports, imports = _load_barycenter_points(\r\n        global_csv_path,\r\n        \"lat_exports\",\r\n        \"lon_exports\",\r\n        \"lat_imports\",\r\n        \"lon_imports\",\r\n    )\r\n    exports_p = exports.to_crs(crs_med)\r\n    imports_p = imports.to_crs(crs_med)\r\n    minx, miny, maxx, maxy = gpd.GeoSeries(\r\n        pd.concat([exports_p.geometry, imports_p.geometry], ignore_index=True),\r\n        crs=crs_med,\r\n    ).total_bounds\r\n    return (minx - buffer, maxx + buffer), (miny - buffer, maxy + buffer)\r\n\r\n\r\ndef _selected_year_labels(ax, gdf_sorted: gpd.GeoDataFrame, years: set[int], dx: int, dy: int, fontsize: int) -> None:\r\n    for _, row in gdf_sorted.iterrows():\r\n        year = int(row[\"year\"])\r\n        if year in years:\r\n            ax.text(\r\n                row.geometry.x + dx,\r\n                row.geometry.y + dy,\r\n                str(year),\r\n                fontsize=fontsize,\r\n                color=\"black\",\r\n                alpha=0.8 if fontsize <= 5 else 0.9,\r\n                zorder=10,\r\n            )\r\n\r\n\r\ndef _render_single_footer_map(\r\n    world_p: gpd.GeoDataFrame,\r\n    points_p: gpd.GeoDataFrame,\r\n    path_p: gpd.GeoDataFrame,\r\n    color: str,\r\n    footer_label: str,\r\n    out_png: Path,\r\n    out_pdf: Path,\r\n    xlim: tuple[float, float],\r\n    ylim: tuple[float, float],\r\n    label_years: set[int],\r\n    dx: int,\r\n    dy: int,\r\n    fontsize: int,\r\n) -> None:\r\n    fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(10, 6))\r\n    world_p.plot(ax=ax, linewidth=0.4, edgecolor=\"white\", color=\"lightgray\", zorder=1)\r\n    path_p.plot(ax=ax, linewidth=1.1, color=color, alpha=0.28, zorder=3)\r\n    points_p.plot(ax=ax, color=color, markersize=8, zorder=4)\r\n    _selected_year_labels(ax, points_p.sort_values(\"year\"), label_years, dx, dy, fontsize)\r\n    ax.set_xlim(*xlim)\r\n    ax.set_ylim(*ylim)\r\n    ax.text(0.02, 0.02, footer_label, transform=ax.transAxes, fontsize=9, color=\"black\", ha=\"left\", va=\"bottom\")\r\n    ax.set_axis_off()\r\n    plt.tight_layout()\r\n    plt.savefig(out_png, dpi=300, bbox_inches=\"tight\")\r\n    plt.savefig(out_pdf, bbox_inches=\"tight\")\r\n    plt.close(fig)\r\n\r\n\r\ndef _render_combined_country_map(\r\n    world_p: gpd.GeoDataFrame,\r\n    exports_p: gpd.GeoDataFrame,\r\n    imports_p: gpd.GeoDataFrame,\r\n    exports_path_p: gpd.GeoDataFrame,\r\n    imports_path_p: gpd.GeoDataFrame,\r\n    xlim: tuple[float, float],\r\n    ylim: tuple[float, float],\r\n    title_prefix: str,\r\n    out_png: Path,\r\n    out_pdf: Path,\r\n) -> None:\r\n    fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(10, 8))\r\n    world_p.plot(ax=ax, linewidth=0.4, edgecolor=\"white\", color=\"lightgray\", zorder=1)\r\n    imports_path_p.plot(ax=ax, linewidth=1.0, color=\"red\", alpha=0.25, zorder=3)\r\n    exports_path_p.plot(ax=ax, linewidth=1.0, color=\"dodgerblue\", alpha=0.25, zorder=4)\r\n    imports_p.plot(ax=ax, color=\"red\", markersize=8, zorder=5)\r\n    exports_p.plot(ax=ax, color=\"dodgerblue\", markersize=8, zorder=6)\r\n    legend_elements = [\r\n        Line2D([0], [0], marker=\"o\", color=\"w\", label=f\"{title_prefix} imports barycenter (partners)\", markerfacecolor=\"red\", markersize=6),\r\n        Line2D([0], [0], marker=\"o\", color=\"w\", label=f\"{title_prefix} exports barycenter (partners)\", markerfacecolor=\"dodgerblue\", markersize=6),\r\n    ]\r\n    ax.legend(handles=legend_elements, loc=\"lower left\", frameon=False, fontsize=9)\r\n    years = sorted(set(exports_p[\"year\"].astype(int)).union(set(imports_p[\"year\"].astype(int))))\r\n    ymin, ymax = min(years), max(years)\r\n    label_years = set(range((ymin // 10) * 10, ymax + 1, 10))\r\n    label_years.update([ymin, ymax])\r\n    _selected_year_labels(ax, imports_p.sort_values(\"year\"), label_years, 35000, 35000, 8)\r\n    _selected_year_labels(ax, exports_p.sort_values(\"year\"), label_years, 35000, 35000, 8)\r\n    ax.set_xlim(*xlim)\r\n    ax.set_ylim(*ylim)\r\n    ax.set_axis_off()\r\n    plt.tight_layout()\r\n    plt.savefig(out_png, dpi=300, bbox_inches=\"tight\")\r\n    plt.savefig(out_pdf, bbox_inches=\"tight\")\r\n    plt.close(fig)\r\n\r\n\r\ndef render_legacy_special_maps(\r\n    global_csv_path: Path,\r\n    usa_csv_path: Path,\r\n    china_csv_path: Path,\r\n    shapefile_path: Path,\r\n    fig_dir: Path,\r\n) -> None:\r\n    crs_med = CRS.from_proj4(\"+proj=aeqd +lat_0=35 +lon_0=18 +datum=WGS84 +units=m +no_defs\")\r\n    world_p = _load_world_projected(shapefile_path, crs_med)\r\n    xlim, ylim = _global_extent_from_global_barycenters(global_csv_path, crs_med, buffer=1.2e6)\r\n\r\n    global_exports, global_imports = _load_barycenter_points(\r\n        global_csv_path,\r\n        \"lat_exports\",\r\n        \"lon_exports\",\r\n        \"lat_imports\",\r\n        \"lon_imports\",\r\n    )\r\n    global_exports_p, global_imports_p, global_exports_path_p, global_imports_path_p = _build_projected_paths(\r\n        global_exports,\r\n        global_imports,\r\n        crs_med,\r\n    )\r\n    global_label_years = {1977, 1980, 1985, 1994, 2001, 2009, 2018, 2023}\r\n    _render_single_footer_map(\r\n        world_p,\r\n        global_imports_p,\r\n        global_imports_path_p,\r\n        \"red\",\r\n        \"Global imports barycenter\",\r\n        fig_dir / \"barycenter_global_imports_mediterranean.png\",\r\n        fig_dir / \"barycenter_global_imports_mediterranean.pdf\",\r\n        xlim,\r\n        ylim,\r\n        global_label_years,\r\n        20000,\r\n        20000,\r\n        5,\r\n    )\r\n    _render_single_footer_map(\r\n        world_p,\r\n        global_exports_p,\r\n        global_exports_path_p,\r\n        \"dodgerblue\",\r\n        \"Global exports barycenter\",\r\n        fig_dir / \"barycenter_global_exports_mediterranean.png\",\r\n        fig_dir / \"barycenter_global_exports_mediterranean.pdf\",\r\n        xlim,\r\n        ylim,\r\n        global_label_years,\r\n        20000,\r\n        20000,\r\n        5,\r\n    )\r\n"}}

how=\"left\")\n+        im_all = im_all.merge(centroids_lookup, on=\"partner\", how=\"left\")\n+\n+        def summarize_flow(df: pd.DataFrame, flow: str) -> pd.DataFrame:\n+            unmatched = df[\"lat\"].isna() | df[\"lon\"].isna()\n+            work = df.assign(\n+                matched=(~unmatched).astype(int),\n+                unmatched=unmatched.astype(int),\n+                w_lat=np.where(unmatched, 0.0, df[\"w\"] * df[\"lat\"]),\n+                w_lon=np.where(unmatched, 0.0, df[\"w\"] * df[\"lon\"]),\n+            )\n+            summary = (\n+                work.groupby(\"country\", sort=True)\n+                .agg(\n+                    w_sum=(\"w\", \"sum\"),\n+                    w_lat_sum=(\"w_lat\", \"sum\"),\n+                    w_lon_sum=(\"w_lon\", \"sum\"),\n+                    n_total=(\"partner\", \"size\"),\n+                    n_unmatched=(\"unmatched\", \"sum\"),\n+                )\n+                .reset_index()\n+            )\n+            summary[f\"lat_{flow}\"] = np.where(\n+                summary[\"w_sum\"] > 0,\n+                summary[\"w_lat_sum\"] / summary[\"w_sum\"],\n+ .to_list()\n+            )\n+            keep_cols = [\n+                \"country\",\n+                f\"lat_{flow}\",\n+                f\"lon_{flow}\",\n+ 'import'}_partners_unmatched\",\n+                f\"unmatched_{'export' if flow == 'exports' else 'import'}_partners_sample\",\n+            ]\n+            return summary[keep_cols]\n+\n+        ex_summary = summarize_flow(ex_all, \"exports\")\n+        im_summary = summarize_flow(im_all, \"imports\")\n+        year_summary = ex_summary.merge(im_summary, on=\"country\", how=\"outer\")\n+\n+ Path(r'C:\\\\Python\\\\Geoanalisis\\\\runs\\\\trade_s2_v001\\\\2026-03-11_001_stage03-opt\\\\artifacts\\\\03_barycenters')\\nfiles = [\\n    'data/barycenter_imports_exports_1976_2023.csv',\\n    'data/barycenter_usa_trade_1976_2023.csv',\\n    'data/barycenter_china_trade_1976_2023.csv',\\n    'data/barycenter_USA_1976_2023.csv',\\n    'data/barycenter_CHN_1976_2023.csv',\\n    'data/barycenter_BRA_1976_2023.csv',\\n]\\nfor rel in files:\\n    old = base_old / rel\\n    new = base_new / rel\\n    print(f'FILE={rel}')\\n    if not old.exists() or not new.exists():\\n        print(f'  exists_old={old.exists()} exists_new={new.exists()}')\\n        continue\\n    dfo = pd.read_csv(old)\\n    dfn = pd.read_csv(new)\\n    print(f'  shape_old={dfo.shape} shape_new={dfn.shape}')\\n    print(f'  cols_equal={list(dfo.columns)==list(dfn.columns)}')\\n    if list(dfo.columns)==list(dfn.columns) and dfo.shape==dfn.shape:\\n        num_cols = [c for c in dfo.columns if pd.api.types.is_numeric_dtype(dfo[c])]\\n        maxdiff = 0.0\\n        for c in num_cols:\\n            a = pd.to_numeric(dfo[c], errors='coerce').to_numpy()\\n            b = pd.to_numeric(dfn[c], errors='coerce').to_numpy()\\n            diff = np.nanmax(np.abs(a-b)) if len(a) else 0.0\\n            if np.isnan(diff):\\n                diff = 0.0\\n            maxdiff = max(maxdiff, float(diff))\\n        nonnum_equal = True\\n        for c in [c for c in dfo.columns if c not in num_cols]:\\n            if not dfo[c].fillna('').equals(dfn[c].fillna('')):\\n                nonnum_equal = False\\n                print(f'  first_nonnum_diff_col={c}')\\n                break\\n        print(f'  max_numeric_diff={maxdiff}')\\n        print(f'  non_numeric_equal={nonnum_equal}')\\n    print()\\nold_fig = sorted(p.name for p in (base_old/'fig').glob('*.png'))\\nnew_fig = sorted(p.name for p in (base_new/'fig').glob('*.png'))\\nprint(f'FIG_PNG_EQUAL={old_fig==new_fig}')\\nprint(f'FIG_COUNT_OLD={len(old_fig)} FIG_COUNT_NEW={len(new_fig)}')\\nif old_fig != new_fig:\\n    print('ONLY_OLD', sorted(set(old_fig)-set(new_fig))[:20])\\n    print('ONLY_NEW', sorted(set(new_fig)-set(old_fig))[:20])\\ncountry_old = sorted(p.name for p in (base_old/'data').glob('barycenter_[A-Z][A-Z][A-Z]_1976_2023.csv'))\\ncountry_new = sorted(p.name for p in (base_new/'data').glob('barycenter_[A-Z][A-Z][A-Z]_1976_2023.csv'))\\nprint(f'COUNTRY_FILE_COUNT_OLD={len(country_old)} COUNTRY_FILE_COUNT_NEW={len(country_new)}')\\nprint(f'COUNTRY_FILE_SET_EQUAL={country_old==country_new}')\\n'@ | python {"timestamp":"2026-03-11T12:35:13.137Z","type":"event_msg","payload":{"type":"agent_message","message":"Implemented the safe `Stage 03` optimizations only.\n\n**What changed**\n- In [barycenters.py](C:/Python/Geoanalisis/src/geoanalisis/services/barycenters.py#L138), `compute_country_barycenters()` now computes per-country barycenters once for the full country universe and returns:\n  - discovered country codes\n  - written file paths\n  - in-memory per-country DataFrames\n- In the same file:\n  - grouped flow summaries now replace the old inner per-country subsetting loop, without changing weighting formulas\n  - `_load_barycenter_points_from_df()` was added at [barycenters.py](C:/Python/Geoanalisis/src/geoanalisis/services/barycenters.py#L364)\n  - `render_legacy_special_maps()` now accepts in-memory DataFrames at [barycenters.py](C:/Python/Geoanalisis/src/geoanalisis/services/barycenters.py#L517)\n  - the separate country-detection scan was removed\n- In [stage_03_barycenters.py](C:/Python/Geoanalisis/src/geoanalisis/pipelines/stage_03_barycenters.py#L37), the stage now:\n  - runs one all-country barycenter pass\n  - derives focus-country outputs from that result\n  - reuses in-memory `global_df`, USA, and China DataFrames for canonical map rendering\n\n**Expected performance impact**\n- Biggest gain: removed the duplicate focus-country + all-country computation pattern.\n- Secondary gain: removed the separate full country-code detection scan.\n- Additional gain: reduced repeated Python subsetting by using grouped aggregations.\n- Small gain: stopped rereading freshly written CSVs just to render canonical maps.\n\nObserved runtime improvement on a clean `Stage 03` rerun:\n- previous observed Stage 03 runtime: about `159.59s`\n- new clean Stage 03 rerun: `18.041s`\n- rough improvement: about `8.8x`\n\nClean rerun used:\n- run_id: `2026-03-11_001_stage03-opt`\n- Stage 01 prerequisite reused unchanged from an existing run\n- only `Stage 03` was executed\n\n**Parity check**\nCompared against baseline run `2026-03-10_005_full-pipeline`.\n\nCanonical outputs still match:\n- [barycenter_imports_exports_1976_2023.csv](C:/Python/Geoanalisis/runs/trade_s2_v001/2026-03-11_001_stage03-opt/artifacts/03_barycenters/data/barycenter_imports_exports_1976_2023.csv)\n- [barycenter_usa_trade_1976_2023.csv](C:/Python/Geoanalisis/runs/trade_s2_v001/2026-03-11_001_stage03-opt/artifacts/03_barycenters/data/barycenter_usa_trade_1976_2023.csv)\n- [barycenter_china_trade_1976_2023.csv](C:/Python/Geoanalisis/runs/trade_s2_v001/2026-03-11_001_stage03-opt/artifacts/03_barycenters/data/barycenter_china_trade_1976_2023.csv)\n- country barycenter file set: `252` files in both runs\n- canonical PNG figure set: identical `8` files in both runs\n\nDifferences are only tiny floating-point noise:\n- global CSV max numeric diff: `0.01171875`\n- USA / China / country files: around `0.0009765625` or smaller\n- non-numeric columns matched exactly\n\n**New Stage 03 runtime**\n- `18.041s` for `Stage 03` only\n- artifacts: [03_barycenters](C:/Python/Geoanalisis/runs/trade_s2_v001/2026-03-11_001_stage03-opt/artifacts/03_barycenters)\n\nIf you want, the next safe target is `Stage 05`, where the biggest win should come from reducing the three scans per yearly parquet to one.","phase":"final_answer"}}
