{"timestamp":"2026-03-11T17:55:57.071Z","type":"response_item","payload":{"type":"function_call_output","call_id":"call_qO3KyDa4SAcG2Rj2KW9zprQf","output":"Exit code: 0\nWall time: 0.5 seconds\nOutput:\n# ============================================================\r\n# Country barycenter trajectories (year-to-year great-circle paths)\r\n# - Two maps: exports and imports\r\n# - Mercator EPSG:3857, no Antarctica\r\n# - Trajectories are drawn as great-circle interpolations between yearly barycenters\r\n#\r\n# Inputs:\r\n#   - gc_state_long_demeaned.csv  (must contain raw x,y,z or demeaned)\r\n#   - Natural Earth shapefile (EPSG:4326)\r\n#\r\n# Outputs:\r\n#   - gc_trajectories_exports.png\r\n#   - gc_trajectories_imports.png\r\n# ============================================================\r\n\r\nimport os\r\nimport numpy as np\r\nimport pandas as pd\r\nimport geopandas as gpd\r\nimport matplotlib.pyplot as plt\r\nfrom shapely.geometry import LineString\r\n\r\n# ----------------------------\r\n# CONFIG\r\n# ----------------------------\r\nGC_BASE = str(GC_DATA)\r\nSTATE_FILE = os.path.join(GC_BASE, \"gc_state_long_demeaned.csv\")\r\nSHAPEFILE = str(NE_SHP)\r\n\r\nFLOWS = [\"exports\", \"imports\"]\r\n\r\n# Use raw geography vectors (recommended). Set True only if raw x,y,z missing.\r\nUSE_DEMEANED = False\r\n\r\n# Years to plot (inclusive). Use None to use full available range.\r\nYEAR_MIN = 1980\r\nYEAR_MAX = 2022\r\n\r\n# Trajectory detail: points per segment (year->year). 20 is smooth enough.\r\nPTS_PER_SEGMENT = 20\r\n\r\n# Plot control to avoid spaghetti:\r\n# - \"all\": draw all countries\r\n# - \"topN\": draw only top N countries by number of years present\r\n# - \"subset\": draw only ISO3 in SELECT_COUNTRIES\r\nMODE = \"all\"\r\nTOP_N = 60\r\nSELECT_COUNTRIES = [\"USA\", \"CHN\", \"DEU\", \"JPN\", \"MEX\", \"BRA\", \"IND\"]\r\n\r\n# Basemap styling\r\nBASEMAP_FACE = \"#f2f2f2\"\r\nBASEMAP_EDGE = \"#cfcfcf\"\r\nBASEMAP_EDGE_W = 0.35\r\n\r\n# Line styling (publish-friendly)\r\nEXPORT_LINE = \"#b2182b\"  # deep red\r\nIMPORT_LINE = \"#2166ac\"  # deep blue\r\nLINE_W = 0.55\r\nLINE_ALPHA = 0.20\r\n\r\n# Start/end markers (optional; keep subtle)\r\nDRAW_ENDPOINTS = True\r\nSTART_MARKER = \"o\"\r\nEND_MARKER = \"s\"\r\nMARKER_SIZE = 8\r\nMARKER_ALPHA = 0.35\r\nMARKER_EDGE = \"white\"\r\nMARKER_EDGE_W = 0.25\r\n\r\nDPI = 300\r\n\r\n# ----------------------------\r\n# Helpers (spherical interpolation)\r\n# ----------------------------\r\ndef assert_exists(path):\r\n    if not os.path.exists(path):\r\n        raise FileNotFoundError(f\"Missing file: {path}\")\r\n\r\ndef _normalize_rows(V):\r\n    n = np.linalg.norm(V, axis=1)\r\n    keep = np.isfinite(n) & (n > 0)\r\n    V2 = V[keep] / n[keep][:, None]\r\n    return V2, keep\r\n\r\ndef slerp(u, v, t):\r\n    \"\"\"\r\n    Spherical linear interpolation between unit vectors u and v.\r\n    u, v: (3,) unit vectors\r\n    t: float in [0,1]\r\n    returns: (3,) unit vector\r\n    \"\"\"\r\n    dot = float(np.clip(np.dot(u, v), -1.0, 1.0))\r\n    omega = np.arccos(dot)\r\n    if omega < 1e-12:\r\n        return u.copy()\r\n    so = np.sin(omega)\r\n    return (np.sin((1 - t) * omega) / so) * u + (np.sin(t * omega) / so) * v\r\n\r\ndef unitvec_to_lonlat_deg(p):\r\n    x, y, z = float(p[0]), float(p[1]), float(p[2])\r\n    lat = np.degrees(np.arcsin(np.clip(z, -1.0, 1.0)))\r\n    lon = np.degrees(np.arctan2(y, x))\r\n    # normalize lon to (-180, 180]\r\n    if lon <= -180:\r\n        lon += 360\r\n    elif lon > 180:\r\n        lon -= 360\r\n    return lon, lat\r\n\r\ndef build_country_lines(df_country, pts_per_segment=PTS_PER_SEGMENT):\r\n    \"\"\"\r\n    df_country: rows for a single country+flow with columns year,x,y,z (unit-ish)\r\n    returns: list of shapely LineString in lon/lat\r\n    \"\"\"\r\n    dfc = df_country.sort_values(\"year\")\r\n    V = dfc[[\"x\",\"y\",\"z\"]].to_numpy(float)\r\n    Vn, keep = _normalize_rows(V)\r\n    if Vn.shape[0] < 2:\r\n        return [], None, None\r\n\r\n    years = dfc[\"year\"].to_numpy(int)[keep]\r\n    # Build segments only where consecutive years exist (t -> t+1)\r\n    # If you want to connect across gaps, change this logic.\r\n    lines = []\r\n    start_ll = None\r\n    end_ll = None\r\n\r\n    for i in range(len(years) - 1):\r\n        y0, y1 = years[i], years[i+1]\r\n        if y1 != y0 + 1:\r\n            continue  # skip gaps; avoids long jumps across missing years\r\n        u = Vn[i]\r\n        v = Vn[i+1]\r\n        pts = []\r\n        for k in range(pts_per_segment + 1):\r\n            t = k / pts_per_segment\r\n            p = slerp(u, v, t)\r\n            lon, lat = unitvec_to_lonlat_deg(p)\r\n            pts.append((lon, lat))\r\n        if not start_ll:\r\n            start_ll = pts[0]\r\n        end_ll = pts[-1]\r\n        lines.append(LineString(pts))\r\n\r\n    return lines, start_ll, end_ll\r\n\r\n# ----------------------------\r\n# MAIN\r\n# ----------------------------\r\ndef main():\r\n    assert_exists(STATE_FILE)\r\n    assert_exists(SHAPEFILE)\r\n\r\n    state = pd.read_csv(STATE_FILE)\r\n    state[\"year\"] = state[\"year\"].astype(int)\r\n\r\n    if USE_DEMEANED:\r\n        needed = {\"country\",\"flow\",\"year\",\"x_demean\",\"y_demean\",\"z_demean\"}\r\n        miss = needed - set(state.columns)\r\n        if miss:\r\n            raise ValueError(f\"{STATE_FILE} missing columns: {sorted(miss)}\")\r\n        state = state.rename(columns={\"x_demean\":\"x\",\"y_demean\":\"y\",\"z_demean\":\"z\"})\r\n    else:\r\n        needed = {\"country\",\"flow\",\"year\",\"x\",\"y\",\"z\"}\r\n        miss = needed - set(state.columns)\r\n        if miss:\r\n            raise ValueError(\r\n                f\"{STATE_FILE} missing RAW columns: {sorted(miss)}.\\n\"\r\n                \"Set USE_DEMEANED=True only if you intentionally want demeaned trajectories.\"\r\n            )\r\n\r\n    state = state[[\"country\",\"flow\",\"year\",\"x\",\"y\",\"z\"]].copy()\r\n    state[\"country\"] = state[\"country\"].astype(str).str.upper()\r\n\r\n    # Filter years\r\n    if YEAR_MIN is not None:\r\n        state = state[state[\"year\"] >= YEAR_MIN].copy()\r\n    if YEAR_MAX is not None:\r\n        state = state[state[\"year\"] <= YEAR_MAX].copy()\r\n\r\n    # Basemap -> Mercator\r\n    world = gpd.read_file(SHAPEFILE)\r\n    if world.crs is None:\r\n        world = world.set_crs(\"EPSG:4326\")\r\n    else:\r\n        world = world.to_crs(\"EPSG:4326\")\r\n    world = world[world[\"ISO_A3\"].notna() & (world[\"ISO_A3\"] != \"-99\")].copy()\r\n    world = world[world[\"ISO_A3\"] != \"ATA\"].copy()\r\n    world_3857 = world.to_crs(\"EPSG:3857\")\r\n\r\n    for flow in FLOWS:\r\n        df = state[state[\"flow\"] == flow].copy()\r\n        if df.empty:\r\n            print(f\"[WARN] No data for flow={flow}\")\r\n            continue\r\n\r\n        # choose which countries to draw\r\n        if MODE == \"subset\":\r\n            use_countries = sorted(set([c.upper() for c in SELECT_COUNTRIES]))\r\n        else:\r\n            counts = df.groupby(\"country\")[\"year\"].nunique().sort_values(ascending=False)\r\n            if MODE == \"topN\":\r\n                use_countries = counts.head(TOP_N).index.tolist()\r\n            else:\r\n                use_countries = counts.index.tolist()\r\n\r\n        line_geoms = []\r\n        start_pts = []\r\n        end_pts = []\r\n\r\n        for c in use_countries:\r\n            dfc = df[df[\"country\"] == c]\r\n            if dfc.shape[0] < 2:\r\n                continue\r\n            lines, start_ll, end_ll = build_country_lines(dfc, pts_per_segment=PTS_PER_SEGMENT)\r\n            if not lines:\r\n                continue\r\n            line_geoms.extend(lines)\r\n            if DRAW_ENDPOINTS and start_ll and end_ll:\r\n                start_pts.append(start_ll)\r\n                end_pts.append(end_ll)\r\n\r\n        if not line_geoms:\r\n            print(f\"[WARN] No trajectories drawn for flow={flow} (check YEAR range / missing data).\")\r\n            continue\r\n\r\n        lines_gdf = gpd.GeoDataFrame(geometry=line_geoms, crs=\"EPSG:4326\").to_crs(\"EPSG:3857\")\r\n\r\n        # Endpoints (optional)\r\n        if DRAW_ENDPOINTS:\r\n            start_gdf = gpd.GeoDataFrame(\r\n                geometry=gpd.points_from_xy([p[0] for p in start_pts], [p[1] for p in start_pts]),\r\n                crs=\"EPSG:4326\"\r\n            ).to_crs(\"EPSG:3857\") if start_pts else None\r\n\r\n            end_gdf = gpd.GeoDataFrame(\r\n                geometry=gpd.points_from_xy([p[0] for p in end_pts], [p[1] for p in end_pts]),\r\n                crs=\"EPSG:4326\"\r\n            ).to_crs(\"EPSG:3857\") if end_pts else None\r\n        else:\r\n            start_gdf = end_gdf = None\r\n\r\n        # Plot\r\n        fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(14, 8))\r\n        ax.set_axis_off()\r\n\r\n        world_3857.plot(ax=ax, color=BASEMAP_FACE, edgecolor=BASEMAP_EDGE,\r\n                        linewidth=BASEMAP_EDGE_W, zorder=1)\r\n\r\n        line_color = EXPORT_LINE if flow == \"exports\" else IMPORT_LINE\r\n        lines_gdf.plot(ax=ax, color=line_color, linewidth=LINE_W, alpha=LINE_ALPHA, zorder=3)\r\n\r\n        if DRAW_ENDPOINTS and start_gdf is not None and end_gdf is not None:\r\n            start_gdf.plot(ax=ax, color=line_color, markersize=MARKER_SIZE, alpha=MARKER_ALPHA,\r\n                           edgecolor=MARKER_EDGE, linewidth=MARKER_EDGE_W, zorder=4, marker=START_MARKER)\r\n            end_gdf.plot(ax=ax, color=line_color, markersize=MARKER_SIZE, alpha=MARKER_ALPHA,\r\n                         edgecolor=MARKER_EDGE, linewidth=MARKER_EDGE_W, zorder=4, marker=END_MARKER)\r\n\r\n        ax.set_title(\r\n            f\"{flow.capitalize()} barycenter trajectories ({YEAR_MIN}â€“{YEAR_MAX}) â€” {MODE}\",\r\n            fontsize=14\r\n        )\r\n\r\n        out = os.path.join(GC_BASE, f\"gc_trajectories_{flow}.png\")\r\n        plt.tight_layout()\r\n        plt.savefig(out, dpi=DPI, bbox_inches=\"tight\")\r\n        plt.close(fig)\r\n\r\n        print(f\"âœ“ Saved: {out}  (countries drawn: {len(use_countries)}, segments: {len(line_geoms)})\")\r\n\r\n    print(\"\\nDone.\")\r\n\r\nif __name__ == \"__main__\":\r\n    main()\r\n"}}
{"timestamp":"2026-03-11T17:56:56.788Z","type":"event_msg","payload":{"type":"agent_message","message":"Ya tengo el mapa exacto:\n- `new_china.ipynb` aporta solo la lógica visual China con `AEQD` centrada en Asia Central y ventana fija `15E–115E`, `5N–60N`\n- `multicountry.ipynb` aporta 10 mapas país-específicos con proyección/extent custom por país\n- `trajectories.ipynb` aporta el estilo “hurricane” y la lógica de trayectorias; allí el default era `MODE=\"all\"`, pero para no sobrecargar `Stage 03` voy a migrarlo con una muestra determinística y explícita, como pediste\n\nAhora implemento esas tres capas dentro de `Stage 03` sin recalcular barycentros fuera del flujo normal del stage.","phase":"commentary"}}

49))},\n+        \"JPN\": {\"crs\": _aeqd(26, 30), \"extent\": _project_bbox(_aeqd(26, 30), (-16, 12, 75, 45))},\n+        \"GBR\": {\"crs\": crs_med, \"extent\": (eu_xmin, eu_ymin, eu_xmax, eu_ymax)},\n+        \"DEU\": {\"crs\": crs_med, \"extent\": (eu_xmin, eu_ymin, eu_xmax, eu_ymax)},\n+        \"FRA\": {\"crs\": crs_med, \"extent\": (eu_xmin, eu_ymin, eu_xmax, eu_ymax)},\n+    }\n+\n+\n+def render_country_specific_maps(\n+    global_df: pd.DataFrame,\n+    country_data: dict[str, pd.DataFrame],\n+    shapefile_path: Path,\n+    fig_dir: Path,\n+) -> None:\n+    world = gpd.read_file(shapefile_path)\n+    if \"ISO_A3\" in world.columns:\n+        world = world[world[\"ISO_A3\"] != \"ATA\"].copy()\n+    if world.crs is None:\n+        world = world.set_crs(epsg=4326)\n+    settings = _build_country_specific_settings(global_df)\n+    country_names = {\n+        \"BRA\": \"Brazil\",\n+        \"ARG\": \"Argentina\",\n+        \"CHL\": \"Chile\",\n+        \"COL\": \"Colombia\",\n+        \"MEX\": \"Mexico\",\n+        \"CAN\": \"Canada\",\n+        \"JPN\": \"Japan\",\n+        \"GBR\": \"United Kingdom\",\n+        \"DEU\": \"Germany\",\n+        \"FRA\": \"France\",\n+    }\n+    label_years = {1977, 1981, 1985, 1988, 1994, 1997, 2001, 2009, 2018, 2022}\n+    for iso3, cfg in settings.items():\n+        df = 4,\n+            )\n+\n+\n+def _latlon_to_xyz(lat: np.ndarray, lon: np.ndarray) -> np.ndarray:\n+    lat_r = np.deg2rad(np.asarray(lat, float))\n+    lon_r = np.deg2rad(np.asarray(lon, float))\n+    x = np.cos(lat_r) * np.cos(lon_r)\n+    y = np.cos(lat_r) * np.sin(lon_r)\n+    z = np.sin(lat_r)\n+    return np.column_stack([x, y, z])\n+\n+\n+def _normalize_rows(v: np.ndarray) -> tuple[np.ndarray, np.ndarray]:\n+    n = np.linalg.norm(v, axis=1)\n+    keep = np.isfinite(n) & (n > 0)\n+    return v[keep] / n[keep][:, None], keep\n+\n+\n+def _slerp(u: np.ndarray, v: np.ndarray, t: float) -> np.ndarray:\n+    dot = float(np.clip(np.dot(u, v), -1.0, 1.0))\n+    omega = np.arccos(dot)\n+    if omega < 1e-12:\n+        return u.copy()\n+    so = np.sin(omega)\n+    return (np.sin((1 - t) * omega) / so) * u + (np.sin(t * omega) / so) * v\n+\n+\n+def _unitvec_to_lonlat_deg(p: np.ndarray) -> tuple[float, float]:\n+    x, y, z = float(p[0]), float(p[1]), float(p[2])\n+    lat = np.degrees(np.arcsin(np.clip(z, -1.0, 1.0)))\n+    lon = np.degrees(np.arctan2(y, x))\n+    if lon <= -180:\n+        lon += 360\n+    elif lon > 180:\n+        lon -= 360\n+    return lon, lat\n+\n+\n+def _build_country_lines_from_barycenters(df_country: pd.DataFrame, flow: str, pts_per_segment: int = 12) -> tuple[list[LineString], list[tuple[float, float]], list[tuple[float, V = _latlon_to_xyz(dfc[\"lat\"].to_numpy(dtype=float), dfc[\"lon\"].to_numpy(dtype=float))\n+    Vn, keep = _normalize_rows(V)\n+    years = years[keep]\n+    if len(years) < 2:\n+        return [], [], []\n+    lines = []\n+    starts = []\n+    ends = []\n+    for i in range(len(years) - 1):\n+        if years[i + 1] != years[i] + 1:\n+            continue\n+        u = Vn[i]\n+        v = Vn[i + 1]\n+        pts = []\n+        for k in range(pts_per_segment + 1):\n+            p = _slerp(u, v, k / pts_per_segment)\n+            pts.append(_unitvec_to_lonlat_deg(p))\n+        lines.append(LineString(pts))\n+        starts.append(pts[0])\n+        ends.append(pts[-1])\n+    return lines, starts, ends\n+\n+\n+def render_hurricane_sample_maps(\n+    country_data: dict[str, pd.DataFrame],\n+    shapefile_path: Path,\n+    fig_dir: Path,\n+    sample_size: int = 24,\n+) -> dict[str, list[str]]:\n+    world = gpd.read_file(shapefile_path)\n+    if world.crs is None:\n+        world = world.set_crs(\"EPSG:4326\")\n+    else:\n+        world = world.to_crs(\"EPSG:4326\")\n+    if \"ISO_A3\" in world.columns:\n+        world = world[world[\"ISO_A3\"].notna() & (world[\"ISO_A3\"] != \"-99\") & (world[\"ISO_A3\"] != \"ATA\")].copy()\n+    world_3857 = world.to_crs(\"EPSG:3857\")\n+    selected: dict[str, list[str]] = {}\n+    for flow in [\"exports\", \"imports\"]:\n+        metric_col = f\"total_{flow}_value_final_matched\"\n+        ranked = []\n+        for country, df in country_data.items():\n+            if df.empty:\n+                continue\n+            sub = df[(df[\"year\"] >= 1980) & (df[\"year\"] <= 2022)].copy()\n+            lat_col = f\"lat_{flow}\"\n+            lon_col = f\"lon_{flow}\"\n+            sub = sub.dropna(subset=[lat_col, lon_col])\n+            if len(sub) < 10:\n+                continue\n+            ranked.append((country, float(sub[metric_col].mean())))\n+        ranked.sort(key=lambda x: (-x[1], x[0]))\n+        use_countries = [c for c, _ in ranked[:sample_size]]\n+        selected[flow] = use_countries\n+\n+        line_geoms = []\n+        start_pts = []\n+        end_pts = []\n+        for country in use_countries:\n+            lines, starts, ends = _build_country_lines_from_barycenters(\n+                country_data[country][(country_data[country][\"year\"] >= 1980) & (country_data[country][\"year\"] <= 2022)],\n+                flow,\n+                pts_per_segment=12,\n+            )\n+            line_geoms.extend(lines)\n+            start_pts.extend(starts)\n+            end_pts.extend(ends)\n+        if not line_geoms:\n+            continue\n+\n+        lines_gdf = gpd.GeoDataFrame(geometry=line_geoms, crs=\"EPSG:4326\").to_crs(\"EPSG:3857\")\n+        start_gdf = gpd.GeoDataFrame(\n+            geometry=gpd.points_from_xy([p[0] for p in start_pts], [p[1] for p in start_pts]),\n+            crs=\"EPSG:4326\",\n+        ).to_crs(\"EPSG:3857\") if start_pts else None\n+        end_gdf = gpd.GeoDataFrame(\n+            geometry=gpd.points_from_xy([p[0] for p in end_pts], [p[1] for p in end_pts]),\n+            crs=\"EPSG:4326\",\n+        ).to_crs(\"EPSG:3857\") if end_pts else None\n+\n+        color = \"#b2182b\" if flow == \"exports\" else \"#2166ac\"\n+        fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(14, 8))\n+        ax.set_axis_off()\n+        world_3857.plot(ax=ax, color=\"#f2f2f2\", edgecolor=\"#cfcfcf\", linewidth=0.35, zorder=1)\n+        lines_gdf.plot(ax=ax, color=color, linewidth=0.55, alpha=0.20, zorder=3)\n+        if start_gdf is not None and end_gdf is not None:\n+            start_gdf.plot(ax=ax, color=color, markersize=8, alpha=0.35, edgecolor=\"white\", linewidth=0.25, zorder=4, marker=\"o\")\n+            end_gdf.plot(ax=ax, color=color, markersize=8, alpha=0.35, edgecolor=\"white\", linewidth=0.25, zorder=4, marker=\"s\")\n+        ax.set_title(f\"{flow.capitalize()} barycenter trajectories (1980-2022) - sample top {len(use_countries)} by mean matched trade\", fontsize=14)\n+        out_png = fig_dir / {"timestamp":"2026-03-11T18:00:16.581Z","type":"response_item","payload":{"type":"function_call","name":"shell_command","arguments":"{\"command\":\"@'\\nfrom pathlib import Path\\nimport pandas as pd\\nimport numpy as np\\nbase_old = Path(r'C:\\\\Python\\\\Geoanalisis\\\\runs\\\\trade_s2_v001\\\\2026-03-11_001_stage03-opt\\\\artifacts\\\\03_barycenters\\\\data')\\nbase_new = Path(r'C:\\\\Python\\\\Geoanalisis\\\\runs\\\\trade_s2_v001\\\\2026-03-11_005_stage03-vis\\\\artifacts\\\\03_barycenters\\\\data')\\nfiles = [\\n    'barycenter_imports_exports_1976_2023.csv',\\n    'barycenter_usa_trade_1976_2023.csv',\\n    'barycenter_china_trade_1976_2023.csv',\\n]\\nfor rel in files:\\n    dfo = pd.read_csv(base_old / rel)\\n    dfn = pd.read_csv(base_new / rel)\\n    print(rel, dfo.equals(dfn), dfo.shape, dfn.shape)\\n    if not dfo.equals(dfn):\\n        num_cols = [c for c in dfo.columns if pd.api.types.is_numeric_dtype(dfo[c])]\\n        md = 0.0\\n        for c in num_cols:\\n            a = pd.to_numeric(dfo[c], errors='coerce').to_numpy()\\n            b = pd.to_numeric(dfn[c], errors='coerce').to_numpy()\\n            diff = np.nanmax(np.abs(a-b)) if len(a) else 0.0\\n            if np.isnan(diff): diff = 0.0\\n            md = max(md, float(diff))\\n        print('maxdiff', md)\\n'@ | python -\",\"workdir\":\"c:\\\\Python\\\\trade\",\"timeout_ms\":120000}","call_id":"call_UpegfSDQXatyKnS7FvMTDD1v"}}

{"timestamp":"2026-03-11T18:00:32.394Z","type":"response_item","payload":{"type":"function_call","name":"shell_command","arguments":"{\"command\":\"$env:PYTHONPATH='C:\\\\Python\\\\Geoanalisis\\\\src'; @'\\nfrom pathlib import Path\\nimport pandas as pd\\nbase = Path(r'C:\\\\Python\\\\Geoanalisis\\\\runs\\\\trade_s2_v001\\\\2026-03-11_005_stage03-vis\\\\artifacts\\\\03_barycenters\\\\data')\\ncountry_data = {}\\nfor fp in base.glob('barycenter_[A-Z][A-Z][A-Z]_1976_2023.csv'):\\n    country_data[fp.stem.split('_')[1]] = pd.read_csv(fp)\\nfor flow in ['exports','imports']:\\n    metric_col = f'total_{flow}_value_final_matched'\\n    ranked = []\\n    for country, df in country_data.items():\\n        sub = df[(df['year'] >= 1980) & (df['year'] <= 2022)].copy()\\n        lat_col = f'lat_{flow}'\\n        lon_col = f'lon_{flow}'\\n        sub = sub.dropna(subset=[lat_col, lon_col])\\n        if len(sub) < 10:\\n            continue\\n        ranked.append((country, float(sub[metric_col].mean())))\\n    ranked.sort(key=lambda x: (-x[1], x[0]))\\n    sel = [c for c,_ in ranked[:24]]\\n    print(flow.upper(), sel)\\n'@ | python -\",\"workdir\":\"c:\\\\Python\\\\trade\",\"timeout_ms\":120000}","call_id":"call_SC8JqgrgxfQhct0bvn6gx27p"}}

{"timestamp":"2026-03-11T18:22:34.575Z","type":"response_item","payload":{"type":"function_call","name":"shell_command","arguments":"{\"command\":\"@'\\nfrom pathlib import Path\\nimport pandas as pd\\nimport numpy as np\\nbase_old = Path(r'C:\\\\Python\\\\Geoanalisis\\\\runs\\\\trade_s2_v001\\\\2026-03-11_005_stage03-vis\\\\artifacts\\\\03_barycenters\\\\data')\\nbase_new = Path(r'C:\\\\Python\\\\Geoanalisis\\\\runs\\\\trade_s2_v001\\\\2026-03-11_006_stage03-names\\\\artifacts\\\\03_barycenters\\\\data')\\nfiles = [\\n    'barycenter_imports_exports_1976_2023.csv',\\n    'barycenter_usa_trade_1976_2023.csv',\\n    'barycenter_china_trade_1976_2023.csv',\\n    'barycenter_USA_1976_2023.csv',\\n    'barycenter_CHN_1976_2023.csv',\\n]\\nfor rel in files:\\n    dfo = pd.read_csv(base_old / rel)\\n    dfn = pd.read_csv(base_new / rel)\\n    print(rel, dfo.shape == dfn.shape, list(dfo.columns) == list(dfn.columns))\\n    num_cols = [c for c in dfo.columns if pd.api.types.is_numeric_dtype(dfo[c])]\\n    md = 0.0\\n    for c in num_cols:\\n        a = pd.to_numeric(dfo[c], errors='coerce').to_numpy()\\n        b = pd.to_numeric(dfn[c], errors='coerce').to_numpy()\\n        diff = np.nanmax(np.abs(a-b)) if len(a) else 0.0\\n        if np.isnan(diff): diff = 0.0\\n        md = max(md, float(diff))\\n    print('  maxdiff', md)\\n'@ | python -\",\"workdir\":\"c:\\\\Python\\\\trade\",\"timeout_ms\":120000}","call_id":"call_H1yRqKjitap4PUvvDoqD76jJ"}}

run(config: ProjectConfig, run_id: str, make_figures: bool = True) -> dict[str, str]:\r\n    logger = logging.getLogger(\"geoanalisis.stage_01_geo\")\r\n    initialize_project_tree(config)\r\n    run_dir = initialize_run_tree(config, run_id)\r\n    artifacts = build_geo_artifact_paths(run_dir / \"artifacts\" / \"01_geo\")\r\n\r\n    logger.info(\"Stage 01 started for dataset %s and run %s\", config.dataset_version, run_id)\r\n    codes = load_country_codes(config.dataset_reference_dir)\r\n    shapefile_path = ensure_natural_earth(config, logger)\r\n    _, centroids_modern = load_modern_centroids(shapefile_path)\r\n    write_geo_audits(codes, centroids_modern, artifacts, logger)\r\n    centroids = resolve_missing_centroids(codes, centroids_modern, artifacts, logger)\r\n    centroids.to_csv(artifacts.centroids_path, index=False)\r\n\r\n    od_matrix = build_od_matrix(centroids)\r\n    od_matrix.to_csv(artifacts.od_matrix_path, index=False)\r\n\r\n    if make_figures:\r\n        plot_centroid_diagnostics(centroids, shapefile_path, artifacts)\r\n\r\n    logger.info(\r\n        \"Stage 01 complete. centroids=%d od_rows=%d output=%s\",\r\n 3.3464),\r\n}\r\n\r\n\r\n@dataclass(frozen=True)\r\nclass GeoArtifacts:\r\n    stage_dir: Path\r\n    data_dir: Path\r\n    fig_dir: Path\r\n    centroids_path: Path\r\n    od_matrix_path: Path\r\n    natural_earth_present_path: Path\r\n    natural_earth_missing_path: Path\r\n    historic_mapping_report_path: Path\r\n    resolution_methods_path: Path\r\n    missing_codes_path: Path\r\n\r\n\r\ndef build_geo_artifact_paths(stage_dir: Path) -> GeoArtifacts:\r\n    data_dir = stage_dir / \"data\"\r\n    fig_dir = stage_dir / \"fig\"\r\n    return GeoArtifacts(\r\n        stage_dir=stage_dir,\r\n        data_dir=data_dir,\r\n        fig_dir=fig_dir,\r\n        centroids_path=data_dir / \"country_centroids_augmented.csv\",\r\n        od_matrix_path=data_dir / \"OD_Matrix.csv\",\r\n        natural_earth_present_path=data_dir / \"audit_natural_earth_codes_present.csv\",\r\n        natural_earth_missing_path=data_dir / \"audit_missing_codes_from_natural_earth.csv\",\r\n        historic_mapping_report_path=data_dir / \"audit_historic_mapping_report.csv\",\r\n        resolution_methods_path=data_dir / \"audit_resolution_methods.csv\",\r\n        missing_codes_path=data_dir / \"audit_missing_codes.csv\",\r\n    )\r\n\r\n\r\ndef load_country_codes(reference_dir: Path) -> list[str]:\r\n    codes_path = reference_dir / \"codes.csv\"\r\n    codes_df = pd.read_csv(codes_path)\r\n    if \"code\" not in codes_df.columns:\r\n        raise ValueError(\"codes.csv must have a column named 'code'\")\r\n    return sorted(\r\n        codes_df[\"code\"].dropna().astype(str).str.strip().loc[lambda s: s != \"\"].unique().tolist()\r\n    )\r\n\r\n\r\ndef ensure_natural_earth(config: ProjectConfig, logger) -> Path:\r\n    zip_path = config.natural_earth_zip_path\r\n    shp_path = config.natural_earth_shapefile_path\r\n    if shp_path.exists():\r\n        return shp_path\r\n\r\n    zip_path.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)\r\n    config.natural_earth_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)\r\n\r\n    if not zip_path.exists():\r\n        logger.info(\"Downloading Natural Earth boundaries to %s\", zip_path)\r\n        response = requests.get(config.natural_earth_url, stream=True, timeout=60)\r\n        response.raise_for_status()\r\n        with open(zip_path, \"wb\") as handle:\r\n            for chunk in response.iter_content(chunk_size=1024 * 1024):\r\n                if chunk:\r\n                    handle.write(chunk)\r\n\r\n    with zipfile.ZipFile(zip_path, \"r\") as zf:\r\n        zf.extractall(config.natural_earth_dir)\r\n\r\n    if not shp_path.exists():\r\n        raise FileNotFoundError(f\"Natural Earth shapefile not found at {shp_path}\")\r\n    return shp_path\r\n\r\n\r\ndef load_modern_centroids(shapefile_path: Path) -> tuple[gpd.GeoDataFrame, pd.DataFrame]:\r\n    world = gpd.read_file(shapefile_path).to_crs(epsg=4326)\r\n    iso = world[\"ISO_A3\"].astype(str).values\r\n    adm = world[\"ADM0_A3\"].astype(str).values\r\n    code = np.where(iso != \"-99\", iso, adm)\r\n    world = world.assign(code=code)\r\n    pts = world.geometry.representative_point()\r\n    centroids_modern = pd.DataFrame(\r\n        {\r\n            \"code\": world[\"code\"].astype(str).values,\r\n            \"lat\": pts.y.values,\r\n            \"lon\": pts.x.values,\r\n            \"name\": world.get(\"ADMIN\", \"\"),\r\n            \"assigned_from\": \"\",\r\n        }\r\n    )\r\n    centroids_modern = (\r\n        centroids_modern.dropna(subset=[\"code\", \"lat\", \"lon\"])\r\n        .drop_duplicates(subset=[\"code\"])\r\n        .reset_index(drop=True)\r\n    )\r\n    return world, centroids_modern\r\n\r\n\r\ndef write_geo_audits(\r\n    codes: list[str], centroids_modern: pd.DataFrame, artifacts: GeoArtifacts, logger\r\n) -> list[str]:\r\n    ne_codes = set(centroids_modern[\"code\"].astype(str))\r\n    your_codes = set(codes)\r\n    missing_ne = sorted(your_codes - ne_codes)\r\n\r\n    centroids_modern[[\"code\", \"name\"]].drop_duplicates().sort_values(\"code\").to_csv(\r\n        artifacts.natural_earth_present_path, index=False\r\n    )\r\n    pd.DataFrame(\r\n        {\r\n            \"code\": missing_ne,\r\n            \"is_historic\": [c in HISTORIC_TO_MODERN for c in missing_ne],\r\n            \"historic_maps_to\": [HISTORIC_TO_MODERN.get(c, \"\") for c in missing_ne],\r\n            \"historic_target_in_ne\": [\r\n                (HISTORIC_TO_MODERN.get(c, \"\") in ne_codes) if c in HISTORIC_TO_MODERN else \"\"\r\n                for c in missing_ne\r\n            ],\r\n            \"is_territory_fallback\": [c in TERRITORY_TO_SOVEREIGN for c in missing_ne],\r\n            \"territory_maps_to\": [TERRITORY_TO_SOVEREIGN.get(c, \"\") for c in missing_ne],\r\n            \"territory_target_in_ne\": [\r\n                (TERRITORY_TO_SOVEREIGN.get(c, \"\") in ne_codes)\r\n                if c in TERRITORY_TO_SOVEREIGN\r\n                else \"\"\r\n                for c in missing_ne\r\n            ],\r\n            \"has_manual_centroid\": [c in MANUAL_CENTROIDS for c in missing_ne],\r\n        }\r\n    ).to_csv(artifacts.natural_earth_missing_path, index=False)\r\n    pd.DataFrame(\r\n        {\r\n            \"code\": missing_ne,\r\n            \"is_historic\": [c in HISTORIC_TO_MODERN for c in missing_ne],\r\n            \"historic_maps_to\": [HISTORIC_TO_MODERN.get(c, \"\") for c in missing_ne],\r\n            \"mapped_target_in_natural_earth\": [\r\n                (HISTORIC_TO_MODERN.get(c, \"\") in ne_codes) if c in HISTORIC_TO_MODERN else \"\"\r\n                for c in missing_ne\r\n            ],\r\n        }\r\n    ).to_csv(artifacts.missing_codes_path, index=False)\r\n    hist_rows = []\r\n    for historic_code, target in HISTORIC_TO_MODERN.items():\r\n        hist_rows.append(\r\n            {\r\n                \"historic_code\": historic_code,\r\n                \"historic_in_your_codes\": historic_code in your_codes,\r\n                \"historic_in_ne\": historic_code in ne_codes,\r\n                \"maps_to\": target,\r\n                \"target_in_ne\": target in ne_codes,\r\n                \"target_has_manual\": target in MANUAL_CENTROIDS,\r\n                \"target_is_territory_fallback\": target in TERRITORY_TO_SOVEREIGN,\r\n            }\r\n        )\r\n    pd.DataFrame(hist_rows).to_csv(artifacts.historic_mapping_report_path, index=False)\r\n    logger.info(\"Stage 01 audits written to %s\", artifacts.data_dir)\r\n    return missing_ne\r\n\r\n\r\ndef resolve_missing_centroids(\r\n    codes: list[str], centroids_modern: pd.DataFrame, artifacts: GeoArtifacts, logger\r\n) -> pd.DataFrame:\r\n    ne_codes = set(centroids_modern[\"code\"].astype(str))\r\n    missing_ne = sorted(set(codes) - ne_codes)\r\n    c_index = centroids_modern.set_index(\"code\")[[\"lat\", \"lon\", \"name\"]]\r\n\r\n    def resolve_to_centroid(code: str, max_hops: int = 8):\r\n        seen = []\r\n        cur = code\r\n        used_hist = False\r\n        used_terr = False\r\n        for _ in range(max_hops):\r\n            seen.append(cur)\r\n            if cur in c_index.index:\r\n                method = \"NE\"\r\n                if used_hist and not used_terr:\r\n                    method = \"HIST_CHAIN\"\r\n                elif used_terr and not used_hist:\r\n                    method = \"TERR_CHAIN\"\r\n                elif used_hist and used_terr:\r\n                    method = \"HIST+TERR_CHAIN\"\r\n                return {\r\n                    \"lat\": float(c_index.loc[cur, \"lat\"]),\r\n                    \"lon\": float(c_index.loc[cur, \"lon\"]),\r\n                    \"source_code\": cur,\r\n                    \"method\": method,\r\n                    \"path\": \"->\".join(seen),\r\n                }\r\n            if cur in MANUAL_CENTROIDS:\r\n                lat, lon = MANUAL_CENTROIDS[cur]\r\n                method = \"MANUAL\"\r\n                if used_hist and not used_terr:\r\n                    method = \"HIST+MANUAL\"\r\n                elif used_terr and not used_hist:\r\n                    method = \"TERR+MANUAL\"\r\n                elif used_hist and used_terr:\r\n                    method = \"HIST+TERR+MANUAL\"\r\n                return {\r\n                    \"lat\": float(lat),\r\n                    \"lon\": float(lon),\r\n                    \"source_code\": \"MANUAL\",\r\n                    \"method\": method,\r\n                    \"path\": \"->\".join(seen),\r\n                }\r\n            if cur in HISTORIC_TO_MODERN:\r\n                used_hist = True\r\n                cur = HISTORIC_TO_MODERN[cur]\r\n                continue\r\n            if cur in TERRITORY_TO_SOVEREIGN:\r\n                used_terr = True\r\n                cur = TERRITORY_TO_SOVEREIGN[cur]\r\n                continue\r\n            break\r\n        return None\r\n\r\n    rows = []\r\n    resolution_rows = []\r\n    unresolved = []\r\n    for code in missing_ne:\r\n        resolved = resolve_to_centroid(code)\r\n        if resolved is None:\r\n            unresolved.append(code)\r\n            resolution_rows.append(\r\n                {\"code\": code, \"resolved\": False, \"method\": \"\", \"path\": \"\", \"source_code\": \"\"}\r\n            )\r\n            continue\r\n        rows.append(\r\n            {\r\n                \"code\": code,\r\n                \"lat\": resolved[\"lat\"],\r\n                \"lon\": resolved[\"lon\"],\r\n                \"name\": f\"{resolved['method']}:{resolved['path']}\",\r\n                \"assigned_from\": resolved[\"source_code\"],\r\n                \"resolution_path\": resolved[\"path\"],\r\n                \"resolution_method\": resolved[\"method\"],\r\n            }\r\n        )\r\n        resolution_rows.append(\r\n            {\r\n                \"code\": code,\r\n                \"resolved\": True,\r\n                \"method\": resolved[\"method\"],\r\n                \"path\": resolved[\"path\"],\r\n                \"source_code\": resolved[\"source_code\"],\r\n            }\r\n        )\r\n    pd.DataFrame(resolution_rows).sort_values([\"resolved\", \"code\"]).to_csv(\r\n        artifacts.resolution_methods_path, index=False\r\n    )\r\n    if unresolved:\r\n        unresolved_path = artifacts.data_dir / \"audit_unresolved_codes.csv\"\r\n        pd.DataFrame({\"code\": unresolved}).to_csv(unresolved_path, index=False)\r\n        raise ValueError(f\"Unresolved codes remain after resolver: {', '.join(unresolved)}\")\r\n\r\n    centroids = pd.concat([centroids_modern, pd.DataFrame(rows)], ignore_index=True)\r\n    centroids = (\r\n        centroids[centroids[\"code\"].isin(codes)]\r\n        .drop_duplicates(subset=[\"code\"])\r\n        .sort_values(\"code\")\r\n        .reset_index(drop=True)\r\n    )\r\n    if len(centroids) != len(set(codes)):\r\n        have = set(centroids[\"code\"].astype(str))\r\n        missing_final = sorted(set(codes) - have)\r\n        missing_final_path = artifacts.data_dir / \"audit_missing_from_final_centroids.csv\"\r\n        pd.DataFrame({\"code\": missing_final}).to_csv(missing_final_path, index=False)\r\n        raise ValueError(f\"Final centroid table incomplete. Missing {len(missing_final)} codes.\")\r\n    return centroids\r\n\r\n\r\ndef haversine_km(lat1, lon1, lat2, lon2):\r\n    radius = 6371.0088\r\n    lat1 = np.radians(lat1)\r\n    lon1 = np.radians(lon1)\r\n    lat2 = np.radians(lat2)\r\n    lon2 = np.radians(lon2)\r\n    dlat = lat2 - lat1\r\n    dlon = lon2 - lon1\r\n    a = np.sin(dlat / 2) ** 2 + np.cos(lat1) * np.cos(lat2) * np.sin(dlon / 2) ** 2\r\n    return radius * (2 * np.arcsin(np.sqrt(a)))\r\n\r\n\r\ndef build_od_matrix(centroids: pd.DataFrame) -> pd.DataFrame:\r\n    ids = centroids[\"code\"].values\r\n    lat = centroids[\"lat\"].values\r\n    lon = centroids[\"lon\"].values\r\n    dist = haversine_km(lat[:, None], lon[:, None], lat[None, :], lon[None, :])\r\n    od = pd.DataFrame(dist, index=ids, columns=ids)\r\n    od.index.name = \"origin\"\r\n    od_long = (\r\n        od.stack()\r\n        .rename(\"distance_km\")\r\n        .reset_index()\r\n        .rename(columns={\"level_0\": \"origin\", \"level_1\": \"destination\"})\r\n    )\r\n    return od_long[od_long[\"origin\"] != od_long[\"destination\"]].reset_index(drop=True)\r\n\r\n\r\ndef plot_centroid_diagnostics(\r\n    centroids: pd.DataFrame, shapefile_path: Path, artifacts: GeoArtifacts\r\n) -> None:\r\n    import matplotlib.pyplot as plt\r\n\r\n    world = gpd.read_file(shapefile_path)\r\n    world = world[world[\"ISO_A3\"] != \"ATA\"].copy()\r\n    if world.crs is None:\r\n        world = world.set_crs(\"EPSG:4326\")\r\n    else:\r\n        world = world.to_crs(\"EPSG:4326\")\r\n    g_cent = gpd.GeoDataFrame(\r\n        centroids, geometry=gpd.points_from_xy(centroids[\"lon\"], centroids[\"lat\"]), crs=\"EPSG:4326\"\r\n    )\r\n    world_m = world.to_crs(epsg=3857)\r\n    g_cent_m = g_cent.to_crs(epsg=3857)\r\n    iso = world[\"ISO_A3\"].astype(str)\r\n    adm = world[\"ADM0_A3\"].astype(str)\r\n    ne_code = iso.where(iso != \"-99\", adm)\r\n    ne_codes = set(ne_code.unique())\r\n    has_resolution_method = \"resolution_method\" in g_cent.columns\r\n    has_assigned_from = \"assigned_from\" in g_cent.columns\r\n\r\n    def is_problematic(row):\r\n        code = str(row[\"code\"])\r\n        if code not in ne_codes:\r\n            return True\r\n        if has_resolution_method and pd.notna(row.get(\"resolution_method\", None)):\r\n            return True\r\n        if has_assigned_from and str(row.get(\"assigned_from\", \"\")).upper() == \"MANUAL\":\r\n            return True\r\n        return False\r\n\r\n    g_cent_m[\"problematic\"] = g_cent_m.apply(is_problematic, axis=1)\r\n    groups = {\r\n        \"map_all_centroids.png\": g_cent_m,\r\n        \"map_no_problem_centroids.png\": g_cent_m[~g_cent_m[\"problematic\"]].copy(),\r\n        \"map_problematic_centroids.png\": g_cent_m[g_cent_m[\"problematic\"]].copy(),\r\n    }\r\n    titles = {\r\n        \"map_all_centroids.png\": \"All centroids (Mercator projection)\",\r\n        \"map_no_problem_centroids.png\": \"Centroids found directly in Natural Earth (no problem)\",\r\n        \"map_problematic_centroids.png\": \"Problematic centroids (manual / chained / not in NE)\",\r\n    }\r\n    for filename, points in groups.items():\r\n        fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(16, 9))\r\n        world_m.plot(ax=ax, linewidth=0.4)\r\n        points.plot(ax=ax, color=\"red\", markersize=12, alpha=0.8)\r\n        ax.set_title(titles[filename])\r\n        ax.set_axis_off()\r\n        fig.savefig(artifacts.fig_dir / filename, dpi=300, bbox_inches=\"tight\")\r\n        plt.close(fig)\r\n"}}

{"timestamp":"2026-03-11T18:39:20.313Z","type":"response_item","payload":{"type":"function_call","name":"shell_command","arguments":"{\"command\":\"@'\\nfrom pathlib import Path\\nimport pandas as pd\\nimport numpy as np\\nold = Path(r'C:\\\\Python\\\\Geoanalisis\\\\runs\\\\trade_s2_v001\\\\2026-03-10_005_full-pipeline\\\\artifacts\\\\01_geo\\\\data')\\nnew = Path(r'C:\\\\Python\\\\Geoanalisis\\\\runs\\\\trade_s2_v001\\\\2026-03-11_007_stage01-eqearth\\\\artifacts\\\\01_geo\\\\data')\\nfor rel in ['country_centroids_augmented.csv','OD_Matrix.csv']:\\n    dfo = pd.read_csv(old/rel)\\n    dfn = pd.read_csv(new/rel)\\n    print(rel, dfo.equals(dfn), dfo.shape, dfn.shape, list(dfo.columns)==list(dfn.columns))\\n    if not dfo.equals(dfn):\\n        num_cols = [c for c in dfo.columns if pd.api.types.is_numeric_dtype(dfo[c])]\\n        md = 0.0\\n        for c in num_cols:\\n            a = pd.to_numeric(dfo[c], errors='coerce').to_numpy()\\n            b = pd.to_numeric(dfn[c], errors='coerce').to_numpy()\\n            diff = np.nanmax(np.abs(a-b)) if len(a) else 0.0\\n            if np.isnan(diff): diff = 0.0\\n            md = max(md, float(diff))\\n        print('maxdiff', md)\\n'@ | python -\",\"workdir\":\"c:\\\\Python\\\\trade\",\"timeout_ms\":120000}","call_id":"call_Oe3qeYdFeK6L7R5H1YVWZfu8"}}

diagnostic maps from Web Mercator-style rendering (`EPSG:3857`) to **Equal Earth**:\n- projection used: `+proj=eqearth +datum=WGS84 +units=m +no_defs`\n\nWhy this projection:\n- it is a standard global map projection\n- it reduces high-latitude and polar distortion much better than Mercator\n- it is appropriate for plotting country centroids across the full world without overstating polar regions\n\n**What changed**\nOnly the map rendering in:\n- [centroids.py](C:/Python/Geoanalisis/src/geoanalisis/services/centroids.py)\n\nNo centroid coordinates, OD matrix logic, or other Stage 01 artifacts](C:/Python/Geoanalisis/runs/trade_s2_v001/2026-03-11_007_stage01-eqearth/artifacts/01_geo)\n\n**Figure files affected**\nThese Stage 01 figure files were regenerated under the new trade/output/05_gravitational_clustering/data/gc_star_members_rawgeo_PA1977-2000_PB2001-2022.csv\n- config.py: Geoanalisis/src/geoanalisis/config.py\n- run_structure.py: Geoanalisis/src/geoanalisis/utils/run_structure.py\n\n## My request for Codex:\nI want a conceptual and technical review of the current Stage 04 clustering analysis.\r\n\r\nThis is NOT a code-change request yet.\r\nDo NOT optimize, refactor, or rerun the pipeline.\r\nI want diagnosis, interpretation, and visualization design first.\r\n\r\nContext\r\n\r\nThe objective of this clustering exercise is not clustering for its own sake.\r\nThe objective is to understand whether the global trade system appears to have:\r\n\r\n- a single attractor\r\nor\r\n- multiple attractors\r\n\r\nThe intuition is gravitational:\r\nimagine country trade barycenters as planets moving through space over time.\r\nIf there is one dominant attractor, trajectories should tend to converge toward a common point or region.\r\nIf there are several attractors, trajectories should organize around multiple poles.\r\n\r\nBig current problem\r\n\r\nThe current Stage 04 produces a lot of intermediate outputs:\r\n- state tables\r\n- features\r\n- distance matrices\r\n- clusters\r\n- attractors\r\n- stability summaries\r\n\r\nBut it does not yet produce a clear scientific output that answers the main question:\r\nWhere is the attractor or where are the attractors, and how should we interpret them?\r\n\r\nIn the legacy notebook, my understanding is that we estimated the coordinates of the attractor point(s) toward which trajectories were moving, and then plotted those coordinates on a map.\r\nThat is the kind of result I want to recover conceptually and visually.\r\n\r\nYour task\r\n\r\nI want you to go through the Stage 04 logic carefully and assess whether the current implementation makes conceptual, mathematical, and visual sense for this objective.\r\n\r\nPlease do the following.\r\n\r\n1. Reconstruct the conceptual logic of Stage 04\r\nExplain in plain language:\r\n- what the clustering analysis is trying to measure\r\n- what a “trajectory” is in this context\r\n- what it means for two trajectories to be “close”\r\n- what the cluster structure means economically and geographically\r\n- what an “attractor” means in this framework\r\n\r\n2. Reconstruct the mathematical logic\r\nExplain more formally:\r\n- how trajectories are represented\r\n- how trajectory distance is defined\r\n- how the different distance components are combined\r\n- how clusters are selected\r\n- how attractors are estimated from the clustered trajectories\r\n- what stability is measuring\r\n\r\nI do not need full theorem-proof style, but I do want clear mathematical reasoning and notation where useful.\r\n\r\n3. Audit whether the current implementation really matches the intended gravitational interpretation\r\nI want you to evaluate:\r\n- whether the current algorithm truly captures the idea of trajectories moving toward one or several attractors\r\n- whether the current “attractors” are mathematically meaningful as geographic pull points\r\n- whether the clustering is identifying convergence basins, or something else\r\n- whether there are conceptual mismatches between the code and the intended interpretation\r\n\r\n4. Inspect the current Stage 04 outputs and identify what is missing\r\nI want you to state clearly:\r\n- which outputs are merely intermediate diagnostics\r\n- which outputs actually answer the single-vs-multiple-attractor question\r\n- what key output is currently missing\r\n- whether attractor coordinates are already being estimated in a usable way\r\n- whether they are being visualized appropriately or not\r\n\r\n5. Propose the best scientific interpretation framework\r\nI want you to propose how the analysis should be interpreted.\r\nFor example:\r\n- does one cluster imply one attractor?\r\n- should attractors be interpreted as cluster centroids in trajectory space, or as geographic coordinates in map space?\r\n- how should exports and imports be treated?\r\n- what would count as evidence of one dominant attractor vs multiple attractors?\r\n\r\n6. Propose the best visualization design\r\nThis is very important.\r\n\r\nI want you to design the best way to visualize results so that a reader can understand:\r\n- whether there is one attractor or several\r\n- where the attractor(s) are located geographically\r\n- how country barycenter trajectories move relative to those attractors\r\n\r\nPlease propose a compact but strong visualization package.\r\nFor each proposed figure, explain:\r\n- what it would show\r\n- why it matters\r\n- whether it should use all countries or a sample\r\n- how to avoid clutter\r\n- whether it should be canonical or exploratory\r\n\r\nI suspect we may need something like:\r\n- a map of representative trajectories with attractor points\r\n- a cleaner map showing only attractor locations and cluster regions\r\n- a convergence diagnostic over time\r\n\r\nBut I want your judgment, not just agreement.\r\n\r\n7. Address the “black hole” intuition directly\r\nI want you to discuss whether the “black hole” metaphor is mathematically appropriate here.\r\nDoes the current clustering framework really estimate a pull point?\r\nOr is it only grouping similar trajectories without estimating an actual force-like attractor?\r\nIf the metaphor is only partially valid, explain that clearly.\r\n\r\n8. Conclude with a practical recommendation\r\nEnd with a concise recommendation covering:\r\n- whether the current Stage 04 logic is conceptually sound\r\n- what the main conceptual gap is\r\n- what output(s) should be added\r\n- what visualization(s) should be created\r\n- and whether any methodological revision is needed before visualization\r\n\r\nImportant constraints\r\n\r\n- Do NOT modify code yet\r\n- Do NOT rerun the pipeline\r\n- Do NOT optimize performance yet\r\n- Do NOT give me generic theory detached from the actual script\r\n- Base your review on the actual current Stage 04 implementation and the legacy logic where relevant\r\n\r\nDeliverable\r\n\r\nReturn a structured conceptual review with these sections:\r\n\r\nA. Plain-language intuition\r\nB. Mathematical logic\r\nC. Audit of current implementation\r\nD. Missing output(s)\r\nE. Recommended interpretation\r\nF. Recommended visualization package\r\nG. Practical next step before coding\n"}]}}
