seconds\nOutput:\n    parts = []\r\n    for fp in sorted(glob.glob(bary_glob)):\r\n        country = infer_country(fp)\r\n        if not country:\r\n            continue\r\n        df = pd.read_csv(fp)\r\n        if {\"year\", \"lat_exports\", \"lon_exports\", \"lat_imports\", \"lon_imports\"} - set(df.columns):\r\n            continue\r\n        for flow in [\"exports\", \"imports\"]:\r\n            lat_col = f\"lat_{flow}\"\r\n            lon_col = f\"lon_{flow}\"\r\n            weight_col = (\r\n                \"total_exports_value_final_matched\"\r\n                if \"total_exports_value_final_matched\" in df.columns\r\n                else \"total_exports_value_matched\"\r\n            )\r\n            if flow == \"imports\":\r\n                weight_col = (\r\n                    \"total_imports_value_final_matched\"\r\n                    if \"total_imports_value_final_matched\" in df.columns\r\n                    else \"total_imports_value_matched\"\r\n                )\r\n            sub = df[[\"year\", lat_col, lon_col]].copy()\r\n            sub[\"country\"] = country\r\n            sub[\"flow\"] = flow\r\n            sub[\"lat\"] = pd.to_numeric(sub[lat_col], errors=\"coerce\")\r\n            sub[\"lon\"] = pd.to_numeric(sub[lon_col], errors=\"coerce\")\r\n            sub[\"weight\"] = pd.to_numeric(df.get(weight_col, np.nan), errors=\"coerce\")\r\n            sub = sub.dropna(subset=[\"year\", \"lat\", \"lon\"])\r\n            x, y, z = latlon_to_xyz(sub[\"lat\"], sub[\"lon\"])\r\n            sub[\"x\"], sub[\"y\"], sub[\"z\"] = x, y, z\r\n            parts.append(sub[[\"country\", \"flow\", \"year\", \"lat\", \"lon\", \"x\", \"y\", \"z\", \"weight\"]])\r\n    if not parts:\r\n        raise ValueError(f\"No compatible barycenter files found for {bary_glob}\")\r\n    out = pd.concat(parts, ignore_index=True)\r\n    out[\"year\"] = out[\"year\"].astype(int)\r\n    out = out.drop_duplicates(subset=[\"country\", \"flow\", \"year\"], keep=\"first\")\r\n    return out.sort_values([\"country\", \"flow\", \"year\"]).reset_index(drop=True)\r\n\r\n\r\ndef compute_features(state_long: pd.DataFrame) -> pd.DataFrame:\r\n    rows = []\r\n    for flow in [\"exports\", \"imports\"]:\r\n        countries, prepared = _prepare_flow_trajectories(state_long, flow)\r\n        for country in countries:\r\n            traj = prepared[country]\r\n            years = traj.years\r\n            xyz = traj.xyz\r\n            lat = traj.lat\r\n            lon = traj.lon\r\n            n_obs = len(years)\r\n            if n_obs == 0:\r\n                continue\r\n            if n_obs > 1:\r\n                step_rad = gc_rad_xyz(xyz[:-1], xyz[1:])\r\n                step_km = step_rad * EARTH_RADIUS_KM\r\n                path_len_rad = float(np.nansum(step_rad))\r\n                path_len_km = float(np.nansum(step_km))\r\n                net_disp_rad = float(gc_rad_xyz(xyz[[0]], xyz[[-1]])[0])\r\n                net_disp_km = net_disp_rad * EARTH_RADIUS_KM\r\n                straightness = float(net_disp_km / path_len_km) if path_len_km > 0 else np.nan\r\n                bearings = initial_bearing_deg(lat[:-1], lon[:-1], lat[1:], lon[1:])\r\n                ang = np.deg2rad(bearings)\r\n                mean_sin = float(np.mean(np.sin(ang)))\r\n                mean_cos = float(np.mean(np.cos(ang)))\r\n                bearing_mean = (np.rad2deg(np.arctan2(mean_sin, mean_cos)) + 360.0) % 360.0\r\n                bearing_circ_var = 1.0 - float(np.sqrt(mean_sin**2 + mean_cos**2))\r\n                turns = np.abs(np.diff(bearings))\r\n                turns = np.where(turns > 180, 360 - turns, turns)\r\n                mean_abs_turn = float(np.mean(turns)) if len(turns) else np.nan\r\n            else:\r\n                path_len_rad = path_len_km = net_disp_rad = net_disp_km = straightness = np.nan\r\n                step_km = np.array([])\r\n                bearing_mean = bearing_circ_var = mean_abs_turn = np.nan\r\n            row = {\r\n                \"country\": country,\r\n                \"flow\": flow,\r\n                \"n_obs\": n_obs,\r\n                \"start_year\": int(years.min()),\r\n                \"end_year\": int(years.max()),\r\n                \"net_disp_rad\": net_disp_rad,\r\n                \"net_disp_km\": net_disp_km,\r\n                \"path_len_rad\": path_len_rad,\r\n                \"path_len_km\": path_len_km,\r\n                \"straightness\": straightness,\r\n                \"mean_speed_km_per_year\": float(np.mean(step_km)) if len(step_km) else np.nan,\r\n                \"std_speed_km_per_year\": float(np.std(step_km, ddof=0)) if len(step_km) else np.nan,\r\n                \"bearing_mean_deg\": bearing_mean,\r\n                \"bearing_circ_var\": bearing_circ_var,\r\n                \"mean_abs_turn_deg\": mean_abs_turn,\r\n            }\r\n            for label, start, end in PERIODS_5:\r\n                mask = (years >= start) & (years <= end)\r\n                row[f\"{label}_nobs\"] = int(mask.sum())\r\n                if mask.any():\r\n                    row[f\"{label}_x\"] = float(np.mean(xyz[mask, 0]))\r\n                    row[f\"{label}_y\"] = float(np.mean(xyz[mask, 1]))\r\n                    row[f\"{label}_z\"] = float(np.mean(xyz[mask, 2]))\r\n                else:\r\n                    row[f\"{label}_x\"] = np.nan\r\n                    row[f\"{label}_y\"] = np.nan\r\n                    row[f\"{label}_z\"] = np.nan\r\n            for i in range(len(PERIODS_5) - 1):\r\n                a, b = PERIODS_5[i], PERIODS_5[i + 1]\r\n                a_vec = np.array([row[f\"{a[0]}_x\"], row[f\"{a[0]}_y\"], row[f\"{a[0]}_z\"]], float)\r\n                b_vec = np.array([row[f\"{b[0]}_x\"], row[f\"{b[0]}_y\"], row[f\"{b[0]}_z\"]], float)\r\n                if np.isnan(a_vec).any() or np.isnan(b_vec).any():\r\n                    rad = km = np.nan\r\n                else:\r\n                    rad = float(gc_rad_xyz(a_vec[None, :], b_vec[None, :])[0])\r\n                    km = rad * EARTH_RADIUS_KM\r\n                row[f\"shift_{a[0]}_to_{b[0]}_rad\"] = rad\r\n                row[f\"shift_{a[0]}_to_{b[0]}_km\"] = km\r\n            rows.append(row)\r\n    return pd.DataFrame(rows).sort_values([\"country\", \"flow\"]).reset_index(drop=True)\r\n\r\n\r\ndef pairwise_distance_matrices(state_long: pd.DataFrame, flow: str, alpha_pos: float = 0.7, min_overlap_years: int = 10, min_overlap_steps: int = 8):\r\n    countries, state = _prepare_flow_trajectories(state_long, flow)\r\n    n = len(countries)\r\n    Dpos = np.zeros((n, n), float)\r\n    Ddir = np.zeros((n, n), float)\r\n    Dover = np.zeros((n, n), float)\r\n    for i, a in enumerate(countries):\r\n        ta = state[a]\r\n        for j in range(i + 1, n):\r\n            b = countries[j]\r\n            tb = state[b]\r\n            overlap_years = np.intersect1d(ta.years, tb.years, assume_unique=True)\r\n            n_overlap_years = int(len(overlap_years))\r\n            if n_overlap_years >= min_overlap_years:\r\n                idx_a = np.fromiter((ta.year_to_idx[int(y)] for y in overlap_years), dtype=int, count=n_overlap_years)\r\n                idx_b = np.fromiter((tb.year_to_idx[int(y)] for y in overlap_years), dtype=int, count=n_overlap_years)\r\n                xa = ta.xyz[idx_a]\r\n                xb = tb.xyz[idx_b]\r\n                pos = float(np.mean(gc_rad_xyz(xa, xb)))\r\n            else:\r\n                pos = np.nan\r\n            overlap_steps = np.intersect1d(ta.step_years, tb.step_years, assume_unique=True)\r\n            n_overlap_steps = int(len(overlap_steps))\r\n            if n_overlap_steps >= min_overlap_steps:\r\n                idx_a = np.fromiter((ta.step_year_to_idx[int(y)] for y in overlap_steps), dtype=int, count=n_overlap_steps)\r\n                idx_b = np.fromiter((tb.step_year_to_idx[int(y)] for y in overlap_steps), dtype=int, count=n_overlap_steps)\r\n                ba = ta.step_bearings[idx_a]\r\n                bb = tb.step_bearings[idx_b]\r\n                diff = np.abs(ba - bb)\r\n                diff = np.minimum(diff, 360.0 - diff)\r\n                direc = float(np.mean(diff / 180.0))\r\n            else:\r\n                direc = np.nan\r\n            if np.isnan(pos):\r\n                pos = 3.0\r\n            if np.isnan(direc):\r\n                direc = 1.0\r\n            Dpos[i, j] = Dpos[j, i] = pos\r\n            Ddir[i, j] = Ddir[j, i] = direc\r\n            Dover[i, j] = Dover[j, i] = n_overlap_years\r\n    idx = pd.Index(countries, name=\"country\")\r\n    Dcombo = alpha_pos * Dpos + (1 - alpha_pos) * Ddir\r\n    dpos_df = pd.DataFrame(Dpos, index=idx, columns=idx)\r\n    ddir_df = pd.DataFrame(Ddir, index=idx, columns=idx)\r\n    dcombo_df = pd.DataFrame(Dcombo, index=idx, columns=idx)\r\n    dover_df = pd.DataFrame(Dover, index=idx, columns=idx)\r\n    return (dpos_df, ddir_df, dcombo_df, dover_df)\r\n\r\n\r\ndef select_clusters(distance_df: pd.DataFrame, flow: str, max_k: int = 12):\r\n    X = distance_df.to_numpy(float)\r\n    countries = distance_df.index.astype(str).tolist()\r\n    k_values = []\r\n    max_k = min(max_k, max(2, len(countries) - 1))\r\n    for k in range(2, max_k + 1):\r\n        model = AgglomerativeClustering(n_clusters=k, metric=\"precomputed\", linkage=\"average\")\r\n        labels = model.fit_predict(X)\r\n        if len(set(labels)) < 2:\r\n            score = np.nan\r\n        else:\r\n            score = silhouette_score(X, labels, metric=\"precomputed\")\r\n        k_values.append({\"flow\": flow, \"k\": k, \"silhouette\": float(score)})\r\n    sil = pd.DataFrame(k_values)\r\n    best_k = int(sil.sort_values([\"silhouette\", \"k\"], ascending=[False, True]).iloc[0][\"k\"])\r\n    model = AgglomerativeClustering(n_clusters=best_k, metric=\"precomputed\", linkage=\"average\")\r\n    labels = model.fit_predict(X)\r\n    clusters = pd.DataFrame(\r\n        {\r\n            \"country\": countries,\r\n            \"flow\": flow,\r\n            \"cluster\": labels,\r\n            \"k_selected\": best_k,\r\n            \"silhouette_selected\": float(sil[sil[\"k\"] == best_k][\"silhouette\"].iloc[0]),\r\n            \"missing_penalty_used\": 3.0,\r\n            \"linkage\": \"average\",\r\n            \"alpha_pos\": 0.7,\r\n            \"min_overlap_years\": 10,\r\n            \"min_overlap_steps\": 8,\r\n        }\r\n    ).sort_values([\"cluster\", \"country\"]).reset_index(drop=True)\r\n    return sil, clusters\r\n\r\n\r\ndef compute_attractors(state_long: pd.DataFrame, clusters_df: pd.DataFrame, flow: str) -> pd.DataFrame:\r\n    sub = state_long[state_long[\"flow\"] == flow].copy()\r\n    merged = sub.merge(clusters_df[[\"country\", \"cluster\"]], on=\"country\", how=\"inner\")\r\n    rows = []\r\n    for cluster, g in merged.groupby(\"cluster\"):\r\n        last = g.sort_values(\"year\").groupby(\"country\").tail(1).copy()\r\n        star_x = float(last[\"x\"].mean())\r\n        star_y = float(last[\"y\"].mean())\r\n        star_z = float(last[\"z\"].mean())\r\n        norm = math.sqrt(star_x**2 + star_y**2 + star_z**2)\r\n        star_x, star_y, star_z = star_x / norm, star_y / norm, star_z / norm\r\n        star_lat, star_lon = xyz_to_latlon(star_x, star_y, star_z)\r\n\r\n        dir_vectors = []\r\n        end_years = []\r\n        for country, gc in g.groupby(\"country\"):\r\n            gc = gc.sort_values(\"year\")\r\n            if len(gc) < 2:\r\n                continue\r\n            a = gc.iloc[-2][[\"x\", \"y\", \"z\"]].to_numpy(float)\r\n            b = gc.iloc[-1][[\"x\", \"y\", \"z\"]].to_numpy(float)\r\n            dv = b - a\r\n            if np.linalg.norm(dv) == 0:\r\n                continue\r\n            dir_vectors.append(dv / np.linalg.norm(dv))\r\n            end_years.append(int(gc[\"year\"].max()))\r\n        if dir_vectors:\r\n            dv = np.mean(np.vstack(dir_vectors), axis=0)\r\n            dv = dv / np.linalg.norm(dv)\r\n            dir_lat, dir_lon = xyz_to_latlon(dv[0], dv[1], dv[2])\r\n            dir_x, dir_y, dir_z = dv.tolist()\r\n        else:\r\n            dir_lat = dir_lon = dir_x = dir_y = dir_z = np.nan\r\n        rows.append(\r\n            {\r\n                \"flow\": flow,\r\n                \"cluster\": int(cluster),\r\n                \"attractor_type\": \"endpoint\",\r\n                \"star_lat\": star_lat,\r\n                \"star_lon\": star_lon,\r\n                \"star_x\": star_x,\r\n                \"star_y\": star_y,\r\n                \"star_z\": star_z,\r\n                \"dir_lat\": dir_lat,\r\n                \"dir_lon\": dir_lon,\r\n                \"dir_x\": dir_x,\r\n                \"dir_y\": dir_y,\r\n                \"dir_z\": dir_z,\r\n                \"n_countries\": int(g[\"country\"].nunique()),\r\n                \"n_endpoints_used\": int(last[\"country\"].nunique()),\r\n                \"n_lastdirs_used\": int(len(dir_vectors)),\r\n                \"end_year_min\": int(min(end_years)) if end_years else np.nan,\r\n                \"end_year_max\": int(max(end_years)) if end_years else np.nan,\r\n            }\r\n        )\r\n    return pd.DataFrame(rows).sort_values([\"flow\", \"cluster\"]).reset_index(drop=True)\r\n\r\n\r\ndef stability_summary(distance_df: pd.DataFrame, clusters_df: pd.DataFrame, flow: str) -> tuple[pd.DataFrame, pd.DataFrame, pd.DataFrame]:\r\n    X = distance_df.to_numpy(float)\r\n    countries = distance_df.index.astype(str).tolist()\r\n    full_labels = clusters_df.set_index(\"country\").loc[countries, \"cluster\"].to_numpy()\r\n    window_rows = []\r\n    bootstrap_rows = []\r\n    for seed in range(10):\r\n        rng = np.random.default_rng(seed)\r\n        idx = np.sort(rng.choice(len(countries), size=max(10, int(0.8 * len(countries))), replace=False))\r\n        Xs = X[np.ix_(idx, idx)]\r\n        if len(idx) <= len(set(full_labels[idx])):\r\n            continue\r\n        model = AgglomerativeClustering(\r\n            n_clusters=len(np.unique(full_labels)),\r\n            metric=\"precomputed\",\r\n            linkage=\"average\",\r\n        )\r\n        labels = model.fit_predict(Xs)\r\n        ari = adjusted_rand_score(full_labels[idx], labels)\r\n        nmi = normalized_mutual_info_score(full_labels[idx], labels)\r\n        bootstrap_rows.append({\"flow\": flow, \"seed\": seed, \"ARI_vs_full\": ari, \"NMI_vs_full\": nmi})\r\n    for seed in range(5):\r\n        ari = adjusted_rand_score(full_labels, full_labels)\r\n        nmi = normalized_mutual_info_score(full_labels, full_labels)\r\n        window_rows.append({\"flow\": flow, \"window_id\": seed, \"ARI_vs_full\": ari, \"NMI_vs_full\": nmi})\r\n    bs = pd.DataFrame(bootstrap_rows)\r\n    wd = pd.DataFrame(window_rows)\r\n    summary = pd.DataFrame(\r\n        [\r\n            {\r\n                \"flow\": flow,\r\n                \"window_ARI_vs_full_mean\": float(wd[\"ARI_vs_full\"].mean()) if not wd.empty else np.nan,\r\n                \"window_ARI_vs_full_std\": float(wd[\"ARI_vs_full\"].std(ddof=0)) if not wd.empty else np.nan,\r\n                \"window_ARI_vs_full_min\": float(wd[\"ARI_vs_full\"].min()) if not wd.empty else np.nan,\r\n                \"window_ARI_vs_full_max\": float(wd[\"ARI_vs_full\"].max()) if not wd.empty else np.nan,\r\n                \"window_NMI_vs_full_mean\": float(wd[\"NMI_vs_full\"].mean()) if not wd.empty else np.nan,\r\n                \"window_NMI_vs_full_std\": float(wd[\"NMI_vs_full\"].std(ddof=0)) if not wd.empty else np.nan,\r\n                \"window_NMI_vs_full_min\": float(wd[\"NMI_vs_full\"].min()) if not wd.empty else np.nan,\r\n                \"window_NMI_vs_full_max\": float(wd[\"NMI_vs_full\"].max()) if not wd.empty else np.nan,\r\n                \"bootstrap_ARI_vs_full_mean\": float(bs[\"ARI_vs_full\"].mean()) if not bs.empty else np.nan,\r\n                \"bootstrap_ARI_vs_full_std\": float(bs[\"ARI_vs_full\"].std(ddof=0)) if not bs.empty else np.nan,\r\n                \"bootstrap_ARI_vs_full_min\": float(bs[\"ARI_vs_full\"].min()) if not bs.empty else np.nan,\r\n                \"bootstrap_ARI_vs_full_max\": float(bs[\"ARI_vs_full\"].max()) if not bs.empty else np.nan,\r\n                \"bootstrap_NMI_vs_full_mean\": float(bs[\"NMI_vs_full\"].mean()) if not bs.empty else np.nan,\r\n                \"bootstrap_NMI_vs_full_std\": float(bs[\"NMI_vs_full\"].std(ddof=0)) if not bs.empty else np.nan,\r\n                \"bootstrap_NMI_vs_full_min\": float(bs[\"NMI_vs_full\"].min()) if not bs.empty else np.nan,\r\n                \"bootstrap_NMI_vs_full_max\": float(bs[\"NMI_vs_full\"].max()) if not bs.empty else np.nan,\r\n            }\r\n        ]\r\n    )\r\n    return summary, wd, bs\r\n"}}
{"timestamp":"2026-03-11T18:53:35.194Z","type":"response_item","payload":{"type":"function_call_output","call_id":"call_BxSzrGsE1mc7u924UfO57hKd","output":"Exit code: 0\nWall time: 0.4 seconds\nOutput:\n\r\nfrom __future__ import annotations\r\n\r\nimport logging\r\nimport pandas as pd\r\n\r\nfrom geoanalisis.config import ProjectConfig\r\nfrom geoanalisis.services.clustering import (\r\n    build_clustering_artifact_paths,\r\n    compute_attractors,\r\n    compute_features,\r\n    load_state_long,\r\n    pairwise_distance_matrices,\r\n    select_clusters,\r\n    stability_summary,\r\n)\r\nfrom geoanalisis.utils.paths import build_stage_dir\r\nfrom geoanalisis.utils.run_structure import initialize_project_tree, initialize_run_tree\r\n\r\n\r\ndef run(config: ProjectConfig, run_id: str) -> dict[str, str]:\r\n    logger = logging.getLogger(\"geoanalisis.stage_04_clustering\")\r\n    initialize_project_tree(config)\r\n    run_dir = initialize_run_tree(config, run_id)\r\n    artifacts = build_clustering_artifact_paths(run_dir / \"artifacts\" / \"04_clustering\")\r\n    bary_glob = str(\r\n        build_stage_dir(config, run_id, \"03_barycenters\")\r\n        / \"data\"\r\n        / f\"barycenter_*_{config.trade_year_start}_{config.trade_year_end}.csv\"\r\n    )\r\n    state_long = load_state_long(bary_glob)\r\n    state_long.to_csv(artifacts.data_dir / \"gc_state_long.csv\", index=False)\r\n\r\n    features = compute_features(state_long)\r\n    features.to_csv(artifacts.data_dir / \"gc_features_country_flow.csv\", index=False)\r\n\r\n    summaries = []\r\n    for flow in [\"exports\", \"imports\"]:\r\n        dpos, ddir, dcombo, dover = pairwise_distance_matrices(state_long, flow=flow)\r\n        dpos.to_csv(artifacts.data_dir / f\"gc_{flow}_Dpos.csv\")\r\n        ddir.to_csv(artifacts.data_dir / f\"gc_{flow}_Ddir.csv\")\r\n        dcombo.to_csv(artifacts.data_dir / f\"gc_{flow}_Dcombo.csv\")\r\n        dover.to_csv(artifacts.data_dir / f\"gc_{flow}_overlap_years.csv\")\r\n\r\n        sil, clusters = select_clusters(dcombo, flow=flow)\r\n        sil.to_csv(artifacts.data_dir / f\"gc_{flow}_silhouette.csv\", index=False)\r\n        clusters.to_csv(artifacts.data_dir / f\"gc_{flow}_clusters.csv\", index=False)\r\n\r\n        attractors = compute_attractors(state_long, clusters, flow=flow)\r\n        attractors.to_csv(artifacts.data_dir / f\"gc_{flow}_attractors.csv\", index=False)\r\n\r\n        summary, wd, bs = stability_summary(dcombo, clusters, flow=flow)\r\n        summaries.append(summary)\r\n        wd.to_csv(artifacts.data_dir / f\"gc_{flow}_window_detail.csv\", index=False)\r\n        bs.to_csv(artifacts.data_dir / f\"gc_{flow}_bootstrap_detail.csv\", index=False)\r\n\r\n    pd.concat(summaries, ignore_index=True).to_csv(artifacts.data_dir / \"gc_stability_summary.csv\", index=False)\r\n\r\n    logger.info(\r\n        \"Stage 04 complete. states=%d features=%d output=%s\",\r\n        len(state_long),\r\n        len(features),\r\n        artifacts.stage_dir,\r\n    )\r\n    return {\r\n        \"run_dir\": str(run_dir),\r\n        \"stage_dir\": str(artifacts.stage_dir),\r\n        \"state_long_path\": str(artifacts.data_dir / \"gc_state_long.csv\"),\r\n        \"features_path\": str(artifacts.data_dir / \"gc_features_country_flow.csv\"),\r\n    }\r\n"}}

\\\"imports\\\")]:\\n\",\r\n    \"    needed = {\\\"star_lat\\\", \\\"star_lon\\\", \\\"n_countries\\\"}\\n\",\r\n    \"    miss = needed - set(df.columns)\\n\",\r\n    \"    if miss:\\n\",\r\n    \" crs=\\\"EPSG:4326\\\"\\n\",\r\n    \")\\n\",\r\n    \"\\n\",\r\n    \"# ------------------------------------------------------------\\n\",\r\n    \"# PROJECT TO MERCATOR (EPSG:3857)\\n\",\r\n    \"# {"timestamp":"2026-03-11T18:54:11.309Z","type":"response_item","payload":{"type":"function_call_output","call_id":"call_nzuLhB2268rwFzQ4zGjHBGrU","output":"Exit code: 0\nWall time: 0.6 seconds\nOutput:\n   ],\r\n   \"source\": [\r\n    \"# ============================================================\\n\",\r\n    \"# Country barycenter trajectories (year-to-year great-circle paths)\\n\",\r\n    \"# - Two maps: exports and imports\\n\",\r\n    \"# - Mercator EPSG:3857, no Antarctica\\n\",\r\n    \"# - Trajectories are drawn as great-circle interpolations between yearly barycenters\\n\",\r\n    \"#\\n\",\r\n    \"# Inputs:\\n\",\r\n    \"#   - gc_state_long_demeaned.csv  (must contain raw x,y,z or demeaned)\\n\",\r\n    \"#   - Natural Earth shapefile (EPSG:4326)\\n\",\r\n    \"#\\n\",\r\n    \"# Outputs:\\n\",\r\n    \"#   - gc_trajectories_exports.png\\n\",\r\n    \"#   - gc_trajectories_imports.png\\n\",\r\n    \"# ============================================================\\n\",\r\n    \"\\n\",\r\n    \"import os\\n\",\r\n    \"import numpy as np\\n\",\r\n    \"import pandas as pd\\n\",\r\n    \"import geopandas as gpd\\n\",\r\n    \"import matplotlib.pyplot as plt\\n\",\r\n    \"from shapely.geometry import LineString\\n\",\r\n    \"\\n\",\r\n    \"# ----------------------------\\n\",\r\n    \"# CONFIG\\n\",\r\n    \"# ----------------------------\\n\",\r\n    \"GC_BASE = str(GC_DATA)\\n\",\r\n    \"STATE_FILE = os.path.join(GC_BASE, \\\"gc_state_long_demeaned.csv\\\")\\n\",\r\n    \"SHAPEFILE = str(NE_SHP)\\n\",\r\n    \"\\n\",\r\n    \"FLOWS = [\\\"exports\\\", \\\"imports\\\"]\\n\",\r\n    \"\\n\",\r\n    \"# Use raw geography vectors (recommended). Set True only if raw x,y,z missing.\\n\",\r\n    \"USE_DEMEANED = False\\n\",\r\n    \"\\n\",\r\n    \"# Years to plot (inclusive). Use None to use full available range.\\n\",\r\n    \"YEAR_MIN = 1980\\n\",\r\n    \"YEAR_MAX = 2022\\n\",\r\n    \"\\n\",\r\n    \"# Trajectory detail: points per segment (year->year). 20 is smooth enough.\\n\",\r\n    \"PTS_PER_SEGMENT = 20\\n\",\r\n    \"\\n\",\r\n    \"# Plot control to avoid spaghetti:\\n\",\r\n    \"# - \\\"all\\\": draw all countries\\n\",\r\n    \"# - \\\"topN\\\": draw only top N countries by number of years present\\n\",\r\n    \"# - \\\"subset\\\": draw only ISO3 in SELECT_COUNTRIES\\n\",\r\n    \"MODE = \\\"all\\\"\\n\",\r\n    \"TOP_N = 60\\n\",\r\n    \"SELECT_COUNTRIES = [\\\"USA\\\", \\\"CHN\\\", \\\"DEU\\\", \\\"JPN\\\", \\\"MEX\\\", \\\"BRA\\\", \\\"IND\\\"]\\n\",\r\n    \"\\n\",\r\n    \"# Basemap styling\\n\",\r\n    \"BASEMAP_FACE = \\\"#f2f2f2\\\"\\n\",\r\n    \"BASEMAP_EDGE = \\\"#cfcfcf\\\"\\n\",\r\n    \"BASEMAP_EDGE_W = 0.35\\n\",\r\n    \"\\n\",\r\n    \"# Line styling (publish-friendly)\\n\",\r\n    \"EXPORT_LINE = \\\"#b2182b\\\"  # deep red\\n\",\r\n    \"IMPORT_LINE = \\\"#2166ac\\\"  # deep blue\\n\",\r\n    \"LINE_W = 0.55\\n\",\r\n    \"LINE_ALPHA = 0.20\\n\",\r\n    \"\\n\",\r\n    \"# Start/end markers (optional; keep subtle)\\n\",\r\n    \"DRAW_ENDPOINTS = True\\n\",\r\n    \"START_MARKER = \\\"o\\\"\\n\",\r\n    \"END_MARKER = \\\"s\\\"\\n\",\r\n    \"MARKER_SIZE = 8\\n\",\r\n    \"MARKER_ALPHA = 0.35\\n\",\r\n    \"MARKER_EDGE = \\\"white\\\"\\n\",\r\n    \"MARKER_EDGE_W = 0.25\\n\",\r\n    \"\\n\",\r\n    \"DPI = 300\\n\",\r\n    \"\\n\",\r\n    \"# ----------------------------\\n\",\r\n    \"# Helpers (spherical interpolation)\\n\",\r\n    \"# ----------------------------\\n\",\r\n    \"def assert_exists(path):\\n\",\r\n    \"    if not os.path.exists(path):\\n\",\r\n    \"        raise FileNotFoundError(f\\\"Missing file: {path}\\\")\\n\",\r\n    \"\\n\",\r\n    \"def _normalize_rows(V):\\n\",\r\n    \"    n = np.linalg.norm(V, axis=1)\\n\",\r\n    \"    keep = np.isfinite(n) & (n > 0)\\n\",\r\n    \"    V2 = V[keep] / n[keep][:, None]\\n\",\r\n    \"    return V2, keep\\n\",\r\n    \"\\n\",\r\n    \"def slerp(u, v, t):\\n\",\r\n    \"    \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n\",\r\n    \"    Spherical linear interpolation between unit vectors u and v.\\n\",\r\n    \"    u, v: (3,) unit vectors\\n\",\r\n    \"    t: float in [0,1]\\n\",\r\n    \"    returns: (3,) unit vector\\n\",\r\n    \"    \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n\",\r\n    \"    dot = float(np.clip(np.dot(u, v), -1.0, 1.0))\\n\",\r\n    \"    omega = np.arccos(dot)\\n\",\r\n    \"    if omega < 1e-12:\\n\",\r\n    \"        return u.copy()\\n\",\r\n    \"    so = np.sin(omega)\\n\",\r\n    \"    return (np.sin((1 - t) * omega) / so) * u + (np.sin(t * omega) / so) * v\\n\",\r\n    \"\\n\",\r\n    \"def unitvec_to_lonlat_deg(p):\\n\",\r\n    \"    x, y, z = float(p[0]), float(p[1]), float(p[2])\\n\",\r\n    \"    lat = np.degrees(np.arcsin(np.clip(z, -1.0, 1.0)))\\n\",\r\n    \"    lon = np.degrees(np.arctan2(y, x))\\n\",\r\n    \"    # normalize lon to (-180, 180]\\n\",\r\n    \"    if lon <= -180:\\n\",\r\n    \"        lon += 360\\n\",\r\n    \"    elif lon > 180:\\n\",\r\n    \"        lon -= 360\\n\",\r\n    \"    return lon, lat\\n\",\r\n    \"\\n\",\r\n    \"def build_country_lines(df_country, pts_per_segment=PTS_PER_SEGMENT):\\n\",\r\n    \"    \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n\",\r\n    \"    df_country: rows for a single country+flow with columns year,x,y,z (unit-ish)\\n\",\r\n    \"    returns: list of shapely LineString in lon/lat\\n\",\r\n    \"    \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n\",\r\n    \"    dfc = df_country.sort_values(\\\"year\\\")\\n\",\r\n    \"    V = dfc[[\\\"x\\\",\\\"y\\\",\\\"z\\\"]].to_numpy(float)\\n\",\r\n    \"    Vn, keep = _normalize_rows(V)\\n\",\r\n    \"    if Vn.shape[0] < 2:\\n\",\r\n    \"        return [], None, None\\n\",\r\n    \"\\n\",\r\n    \"    years = dfc[\\\"year\\\"].to_numpy(int)[keep]\\n\",\r\n    \"    # Build segments only where consecutive years exist (t -> t+1)\\n\",\r\n    \"    # If you want to connect across gaps, change this logic.\\n\",\r\n    \"    lines = []\\n\",\r\n    \"    start_ll = None\\n\",\r\n    \"    end_ll = None\\n\",\r\n    \"\\n\",\r\n    \"    for i in range(len(years) - 1):\\n\",\r\n    \"        y0, y1 = years[i], years[i+1]\\n\",\r\n    \"        if y1 != y0 + 1:\\n\",\r\n    \"            continue  # skip gaps; avoids long jumps across missing years\\n\",\r\n"}}
{"timestamp":"2026-03-11T18:56:04.021Z","type":"event_msg","payload":{"type":"agent_message","message":"**A. Plain-Language Intuition**\n`Stage 04` is trying to answer: do countries’ trade barycenters move as if they are organizing around one pole or several? A “trajectory” here is the yearly path of a country’s export or import barycenter on the globe, built from the `Stage 03` outputs and assembled in [gc_state_long.csv](C:/Python/Geoanalisis/runs/trade_s2_v001/2026-03-10_006_baseline-freeze/artifacts/04_clustering/data/gc_state_long.csv).\n\nTwo trajectories are “close” if, over the years they overlap, they are in similar places and move in similar directions. Economically, a cluster means a group of countries whose trade centers of gravity behave similarly through time. Geographically, it means their trade barycenters trace similar paths on the world map.\n\nIn the current framework, an “attractor” is not a physical force center. It is a fixed prototype location for a cluster, estimated from where member trajectories end up. That is useful, but it is weaker than saying the model has truly estimated a pull point.\n\n**B. Mathematical Logic**\nFor country \\(c\\) and flow \\(f\\), the trajectory is a time-ordered sequence \\(T_{cf}=\\{(t,p_t)\\}\\), where \\(p_t\\) is the annual barycenter on the unit sphere, stored as lat/lon and converted to \\(x,y,z\\) in [clustering.py](C:/Python/Geoanalisis/src/geoanalisis/services/clustering.py).\n\nThe pairwise distance has two parts:\n- Positional distance:\n  \\[\n  D_{\\text{pos}}(a,b)=\\frac{1}{|O|}\\sum_{t\\in O} d_{\\text{gc}}(p_{a,t},p_{b,t})\n  \\]\n  over overlapping years \\(O\\), if overlap is at least 10 years; otherwise a penalty of `3.0`.\n- Directional distance:\n  \\[\n  D_{\\text{dir}}(a,b)=\\frac{1}{|S|}\\sum_{t\\in S}\\frac{\\Delta(\\beta_{a,t},\\beta_{b,t})}{180}\n  \\]\n  where \\(\\beta\\) is the annual step bearing and \\(S\\) is the set of overlapping consecutive-step years, if overlap is at least 8 steps; otherwise a penalty of `1.0`.\n\nThese are combined as:\n\\[\nD_{\\text{combo}}=0.7\\,D_{\\text{pos}}+0.3\\,D_{\\text{dir}}\n\\]\n\nClusters are chosen by average-linkage agglomerative clustering on the precomputed distance matrix, with \\(k\\) selected by best silhouette over `k = 2..12` in [select_clusters](C:/Python/Geoanalisis/src/geoanalisis/services/clustering.py).\n\nAttractors are estimated in [compute_attractors](C:/Python/Geoanalisis/src/geoanalisis/services/clustering.py) as:\n- `star_*`: the normalized mean of the last observed \\(x,y,z\\) point of each country in the cluster\n- `dir_*`: the normalized mean of the final step vectors\n\nStability in [stability_summary](C:/Python/Geoanalisis/src/geoanalisis/services/clustering.py) is supposed to summarize robustness, but currently:\n- bootstrap is over countries, not years\n- window stability is a self-comparison, so it returns trivial `ARI=1`, `NMI=1`\n\n**C. Audit Of Current Implementation**\nConceptually, the current implementation is sound as a **trajectory-similarity clustering**. It is not yet a full gravitational model of attraction.\n\nMain mismatches with the intended interpretation:\n- The algorithm cannot directly answer “one attractor or several” because `k=1` is never allowed. It always forces at least two clusters.\n- The current “attractor” is an endpoint prototype, not an estimated force center or potential minimum.\n- `dir_lat/dir_lon` are not true geographic pull points. They are derived from averaged final displacement vectors in ECEF space, so they are better read as an orientation summary, not a location.\n- The feature table [gc_features_country_flow.csv](C:/Python/Geoanalisis/runs/trade_s2_v001/2026-03-10_006_baseline-freeze/artifacts/04_clustering/data/gc_features_country_flow.csv) is diagnostic only; clustering is driven by the full trajectory distance matrix, not by those features.\n- The temporal stability output is not scientifically informative yet because the window part is trivial.\n\nWhat the current results actually say:\n- Exports: [gc_exports_clusters.csv](C:/Python/Geoanalisis/runs/trade_s2_v001/2026-03-10_006_baseline-freeze/artifacts/04_clustering/data/gc_exports_clusters.csv) gives clusters of size `248, 1, 1`\n- Imports: [gc_imports_clusters.csv](C:/Python/Geoanalisis/runs/trade_s2_v001/2026-03-10_006_baseline-freeze/artifacts/04_clustering/data/gc_imports_clusters.csv) gives `248, 2`\n- So the present evidence looks more like **one dominant basin plus a few outliers** than a genuinely multi-polar system\n\n**D. Missing Output(s)**\nMost current outputs are intermediate diagnostics:\n- `gc_state_long.csv`\n- `gc_features_country_flow.csv`\n- `gc_*_Dpos.csv`, `gc_*_Ddir.csv`, `gc_*_Dcombo.csv`\n- `gc_*_overlap_years.csv`\n- `gc_*_silhouette.csv`\n- `gc_*_window_detail.csv`\n- `gc_*_bootstrap_detail.csv`\n\nThe outputs that actually bear on the attractor question are:\n- [gc_exports_clusters.csv](C:/Python/Geoanalisis/runs/trade_s2_v001/2026-03-10_006_baseline-freeze/artifacts/04_clustering/data/gc_exports_clusters.csv)\n- [gc_imports_clusters.csv](C:/Python/Geoanalisis/runs/trade_s2_v001/2026-03-10_006_baseline-freeze/artifacts/04_clustering/data/gc_imports_clusters.csv)\n- [gc_exports_attractors.csv](C:/Python/Geoanalisis/runs/trade_s2_v001/2026-03-10_006_baseline-freeze/artifacts/04_clustering/data/gc_exports_attractors.csv)\n- [gc_imports_attractors.csv](C:/Python/Geoanalisis/runs/trade_s2_v001/2026-03-10_006_baseline-freeze/artifacts/04_clustering/data/gc_imports_attractors.csv)\n\nThe key missing output is a **canonical visual result** that puts those attractor coordinates on a map and shows how trajectories relate to them. The coordinates already exist and are usable; they are simply not being visualized in the modular stage.\n\n**E. Recommended Interpretation**\nInterpret the current clusters as **basins in trajectory space**, not literal gravitational wells. A cluster means “countries whose trade barycenters moved similarly,” not necessarily “countries pulled by the same physical attractor.”\n\nUse this reading:\n- One cluster does not mechanically imply one attractor.\n- Multiple clusters do not automatically imply multiple meaningful attractors if the extra clusters are singletons or tiny outliers.\n- Attractors should be interpreted primarily as **geographic endpoint prototypes** (`star_lat`, `star_lon`) for each cluster.\n- Exports and imports should be interpreted separately first, because they can organize around different geographic structures.\n\nEvidence for one dominant attractor-like basin would be:\n- one overwhelmingly large cluster\n- minor clusters that are only outliers\n- a star location that is geographically coherent\n- trajectories moving closer to that star over time\n\n**F. Recommended Visualization Package**\n`1.` **Canonical: Attractor Map**\nShow exports and imports in separate panels. Plot only the attractor points from `gc_*_attractors.csv`, sized by `n_countries` and colored by cluster. This is the cleanest answer to “where are the poles?”\n\n`2.` **Canonical: Representative Trajectories + Attractors**\nPlot a sample of representative country trajectories per cluster, not all countries. Use all countries from tiny clusters, and a limited representative sample from the dominant cluster to avoid spaghetti. Overlay the attractor stars prominently.\n\n`3.` **Canonical: Convergence Diagnostic Over Time**\nFor each cluster, plot the median and interquartile range of great-circle distance from member annual barycenter positions to that cluster’s final star. If the dominant cluster’s distance shrinks over time, the attractor interpretation becomes much stronger.\n\n`4.` **Exploratory: Full Trajectory Spaghetti**\nIf desired, keep the all-country trajectory map as exploratory only. It is useful for inspection, but not as the canonical scientific figure because it will obscure the attractor structure.\n\nThis package is compact and answers:\n- how many poles matter\n- where they are\n- how countries move relative to them\n\n**G. Practical Next Step Before Coding**\nThe current `Stage 04` logic is conceptually useful, but only as a **trajectory-clustering framework with endpoint prototypes**. The main conceptual gap is not missing math; it is missing **scientific output** and missing discipline in interpretation.\n\nPractical recommendation:\n- add canonical attractor visualizations first, using the existing `star_lat/star_lon` outputs\n- label them as endpoint-based attractor prototypes, not literal force centers\n- add a convergence diagnostic so the “black hole” intuition is tested rather than assumed\n\nOne methodological revision is still important before making a strong claim about “single vs multiple attractors”: the cluster-selection logic should eventually allow a genuine one-cluster baseline, and temporal stability should be made non-trivial. But that is not required to produce the first clear visualization package.","phase":"final_answer"}}
{"timestamp":"2026-03-11T18:56:07.830Z","type":"response_item","payload":{"type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"output_text","text":"**A. Plain-Language Intuition**\n`Stage 04` is trying to answer: do countries’ trade barycenters move as if they are organizing around one pole or several? A “trajectory” here is the yearly path of a country’s export or import barycenter on the globe, built from the `Stage 03` outputs and assembled in [gc_state_long.csv](C:/Python/Geoanalisis/runs/trade_s2_v001/2026-03-10_006_baseline-freeze/artifacts/04_clustering/data/gc_state_long.csv).\n\nTwo trajectories are “close” if, over the years they overlap, they are in similar places and move in similar directions. Economically, a cluster means a group of countries whose trade centers of gravity behave similarly through time. Geographically, it means their trade barycenters trace similar paths on the world map.\n\nIn the current framework, an “attractor” is not a physical force center. It is a fixed prototype location for a cluster, estimated from where member trajectories end up. That is useful, but it is weaker than saying the model has truly estimated a pull point.\n\n**B. Mathematical Logic**\nFor country \\(c\\) and flow \\(f\\), the trajectory is a time-ordered sequence \\(T_{cf}=\\{(t,p_t)\\}\\), where \\(p_t\\) is the annual barycenter on the unit sphere, stored as lat/lon and converted to \\(x,y,z\\) in [clustering.py](C:/Python/Geoanalisis/src/geoanalisis/services/clustering.py).\n\nThe pairwise distance has two parts:\n- Positional distance:\n  \\[\n  D_{\\text{pos}}(a,b)=\\frac{1}{|O|}\\sum_{t\\in O} d_{\\text{gc}}(p_{a,t},p_{b,t})\n  \\]\n  over overlapping years \\(O\\), if overlap is at least 10 years; otherwise a penalty of `3.0`.\n- Directional distance:\n  \\[\n  D_{\\text{dir}}(a,b)=\\frac{1}{|S|}\\sum_{t\\in S}\\frac{\\Delta(\\beta_{a,t},\\beta_{b,t})}{180}\n  \\]\n  where \\(\\beta\\) is the annual step bearing and \\(S\\) is the set of overlapping consecutive-step years, if overlap is at least 8 steps; otherwise a penalty of `1.0`.\n\nThese are combined as:\n\\[\nD_{\\text{combo}}=0.7\\,D_{\\text{pos}}+0.3\\,D_{\\text{dir}}\n\\]\n\nClusters are chosen by average-linkage agglomerative clustering on the precomputed distance matrix, with \\(k\\) selected by best silhouette over `k = 2..12` in [select_clusters](C:/Python/Geoanalisis/src/geoanalisis/services/clustering.py).\n\nAttractors are estimated in [compute_attractors](C:/Python/Geoanalisis/src/geoanalisis/services/clustering.py) as:\n- `star_*`: the normalized mean of the last observed \\(x,y,z\\) point of each country in the cluster\n- `dir_*`: the normalized mean of the final step vectors\n\nStability in [stability_summary](C:/Python/Geoanalisis/src/geoanalisis/services/clustering.py) is supposed to summarize robustness, but currently:\n- bootstrap is over countries, not years\n- window stability is a self-comparison, so it returns trivial `ARI=1`, `NMI=1`\n\n**C. Audit Of Current Implementation**\nConceptually, the current implementation is sound as a **trajectory-similarity clustering**. It is not yet a full gravitational model of attraction.\n\nMain mismatches with the intended interpretation:\n- The algorithm cannot directly answer “one attractor or several” because `k=1` is never allowed. It always forces at least two clusters.\n- The current “attractor” is an endpoint prototype, not an estimated force center or potential minimum.\n- `dir_lat/dir_lon` are not true geographic pull points. They are derived from averaged final displacement vectors in ECEF space, so they are better read as an orientation summary, not a location.\n- The feature table [gc_features_country_flow.csv](C:/Python/Geoanalisis/runs/trade_s2_v001/2026-03-10_006_baseline-freeze/artifacts/04_clustering/data/gc_features_country_flow.csv) is diagnostic only; clustering is driven by the full trajectory distance matrix, not by those features.\n- The temporal stability output is not scientifically informative yet because the window part is trivial.\n\nWhat the current results actually say:\n- Exports: [gc_exports_clusters.csv](C:/Python/Geoanalisis/runs/trade_s2_v001/2026-03-10_006_baseline-freeze/artifacts/04_clustering/data/gc_exports_clusters.csv) gives clusters of size `248, 1, 1`\n- Imports: [gc_imports_clusters.csv](C:/Python/Geoanalisis/runs/trade_s2_v001/2026-03-10_006_baseline-freeze/artifacts/04_clustering/data/gc_imports_clusters.csv) gives `248, 2`\n- So the present evidence looks more like **one dominant basin plus a few outliers** than a genuinely multi-polar system\n\n**D. Missing Output(s)**\nMost current outputs are intermediate diagnostics:\n- `gc_state_long.csv`\n- `gc_features_country_flow.csv`\n- `gc_*_Dpos.csv`, `gc_*_Ddir.csv`, `gc_*_Dcombo.csv`\n- `gc_*_overlap_years.csv`\n- `gc_*_silhouette.csv`\n- `gc_*_window_detail.csv`\n- `gc_*_bootstrap_detail.csv`\n\nThe outputs that actually bear on the attractor question are:\n- [gc_exports_clusters.csv](C:/Python/Geoanalisis/runs/trade_s2_v001/2026-03-10_006_baseline-freeze/artifacts/04_clustering/data/gc_exports_clusters.csv)\n- [gc_imports_clusters.csv](C:/Python/Geoanalisis/runs/trade_s2_v001/2026-03-10_006_baseline-freeze/artifacts/04_clustering/data/gc_imports_clusters.csv)\n- [gc_exports_attractors.csv](C:/Python/Geoanalisis/runs/trade_s2_v001/2026-03-10_006_baseline-freeze/artifacts/04_clustering/data/gc_exports_attractors.csv)\n- [gc_imports_attractors.csv](C:/Python/Geoanalisis/runs/trade_s2_v001/2026-03-10_006_baseline-freeze/artifacts/04_clustering/data/gc_imports_attractors.csv)\n\nThe key missing output is a **canonical visual result** that puts those attractor coordinates on a map and shows how trajectories relate to them. The coordinates already exist and are usable; they are simply not being visualized in the modular stage.\n\n**E. Recommended Interpretation**\nInterpret the current clusters as **basins in trajectory space**, not literal gravitational wells. A cluster means “countries whose trade barycenters moved similarly,” not necessarily “countries pulled by the same physical attractor.”\n\nUse this reading:\n- One cluster does not mechanically imply one attractor.\n- Multiple clusters do not automatically imply multiple meaningful attractors if the extra clusters are singletons or tiny outliers.\n- Attractors should be interpreted primarily as **geographic endpoint prototypes** (`star_lat`, `star_lon`) for each cluster.\n- Exports and imports should be interpreted separately first, because they can organize around different geographic structures.\n\nEvidence for one dominant attractor-like basin would be:\n- one overwhelmingly large cluster\n- minor clusters that are only outliers\n- a star location that is geographically coherent\n- trajectories moving closer to that star over time\n\n**F. Recommended Visualization Package**\n`1.` **Canonical: Attractor Map**\nShow exports and imports in separate panels. Plot only the attractor points from `gc_*_attractors.csv`, sized by `n_countries` and colored by cluster. This is the cleanest answer to “where are the poles?”\n\n`2.` **Canonical: Representative Trajectories + Attractors**\nPlot a sample of representative country trajectories per cluster, not all countries. Use all countries from tiny clusters, and a limited representative sample from the dominant cluster to avoid spaghetti. Overlay the attractor stars prominently.\n\n`3.` **Canonical: Convergence Diagnostic Over Time**\nFor each cluster, plot the median and interquartile range of great-circle distance from member annual barycenter positions to that cluster’s final star. If the dominant cluster’s distance shrinks over time, the attractor interpretation becomes much stronger.\n\n`4.` **Exploratory: Full Trajectory Spaghetti**\nIf desired, keep the all-country trajectory map as exploratory only. It is useful for inspection, but not as the canonical scientific figure because it will obscure the attractor structure.\n\nThis package is compact and answers:\n- how many poles matter\n- where they are\n- how countries move relative to them\n\n**G. Practical Next Step Before Coding**\nThe current `Stage 04` logic is conceptually useful, but only as a **trajectory-clustering framework with endpoint prototypes**. The main conceptual gap is not missing math; it is missing **scientific output** and missing discipline in interpretation.\n\nPractical recommendation:\n- add canonical attractor visualizations first, using the existing `star_lat/star_lon` outputs\n- label them as endpoint-based attractor prototypes, not literal force centers\n- add a convergence diagnostic so the “black hole” intuition is tested rather than assumed\n\nOne methodological revision is still important before making a strong claim about “single vs multiple attractors”: the cluster-selection logic should eventually allow a genuine one-cluster baseline, and temporal stability should be made non-trivial. But that is not required to produce the first clear visualization package."}],"phase":"final_answer"}}

{"timestamp":"2026-03-11T19:06:36.701Z","type":"response_item","payload":{"type":"message","role":"user","content":[{"type":"input_text","text":"# Context from my IDE setup:\n\n## Active file: Untitled-1\n\n## Open tabs:\n- audit_historic_mapping_report.csv: Geoanalisis/runs/trade_s2_v001/2026-03-11_007_stage01-eqearth/artifacts/01_geo/data/audit_historic_mapping_report.csv\n- progress.log: Geoanalisis/runs/trade_s2_v001/2026-03-10_006_baseline-freeze/logs/progress.log\n- gc_star_members_rawgeo_PA1977-2000_PB2001-2022.csv: trade/output/05_gravitational_clustering/data/gc_star_members_rawgeo_PA1977-2000_PB2001-2022.csv\n- config.py: Geoanalisis/src/geoanalisis/config.py\n- run_structure.py: Geoanalisis/src/geoanalisis/utils/run_structure.py\n\n## My request for Codex:\nWe need to add a new visualization/output layer to Stage 04.\r\n\r\nImportant:\r\n- do not change the clustering algorithm\r\n- do not change the distance formulas\r\n- do not change the attractor calculation\r\n- do not recalculate barycenter data\r\n- this is an output/visualization addition to make Stage 04 scientifically interpretable\r\n\r\nContext\r\n\r\nStage 04 currently produces intermediate diagnostics and attractor tables, but it does not yet produce the clear scientific outputs needed to answer the main question:\r\n\r\n- is there one dominant attractor or several attractors in the global trade system?\r\n- where are those attractor(s) geographically?\r\n- how do representative country barycenter trajectories move relative to them?\r\n\r\nThe existing attractor coordinates in:\r\n- `gc_exports_attractors.csv`\r\n- `gc_imports_attractors.csv`\r\n\r\nshould be treated as endpoint-based attractor prototypes.\r\nWe need to visualize them clearly.\r\n\r\nAdd these tasks to Stage 04.\r\n\r\nNEW STAGE 04 OUTPUTS\r\n\r\n1. Attractor Map (canonical)\r\nCreate a global map for exports and another for imports that plots only the attractor points.\r\n\r\nRequirements:\r\n- use a world projection suitable for global visualization\r\n- do NOT use Mercator\r\n- do NOT display Antarctica\r\n- size the attractor markers by `n_countries`\r\n- color attractor markers by cluster\r\n- label clusters if useful and readable\r\n- this should be a clean map answering: “where are the poles?”\r\n\r\n2. Representative Trajectories + Attractors Map (canonical)\r\nCreate a global map for exports and another for imports showing:\r\n- a representative sample of country barycenter trajectories\r\n- clustered/color-coded trajectories\r\n- attractor points overlaid prominently\r\n\r\nSampling rules:\r\n- do NOT plot all countries if that creates spaghetti\r\n- for very small clusters, include all countries\r\n- for the dominant cluster, use a manageable representative sample\r\n- explain and implement a clear deterministic sampling rule so results are reproducible\r\n\r\nTrajectory rules:\r\n- use existing Stage 04 / Stage 03 outputs only\r\n- do not recompute barycenters\r\n- preserve the cluster attribution from Stage 04\r\n- the goal is to visualize movement relative to attractors\r\n\r\n3. Convergence Diagnostic (canonical)\r\nCreate a convergence diagnostic for exports and imports.\r\n\r\nGoal:\r\n- show whether trajectories move closer to their cluster attractor over time\r\n\r\nSuggested implementation:\r\n- for each country-year in a cluster, compute great-circle distance between the barycenter position and the cluster’s `star_lat/star_lon`\r\n- summarize by year within each cluster\r\n- plot median and interquartile range through time\r\n\r\nRequirements:\r\n- exports and imports separately\r\n- readable and compact\r\n- this should help evaluate whether the “black hole” intuition is actually supported\r\n\r\nVISUAL DESIGN RULES\r\n\r\n- these are global visualizations, so use a projection more suitable than Mercator\r\n- Antarctica should not be displayed\r\n- maps should be presentation-quality but computationally reasonable\r\n- avoid clutter\r\n- canonical outputs should be PNG only, no PDF\r\n- place new outputs under Stage 04 artifacts in a clean `fig` structure\r\n\r\nIMPLEMENTATION RULES\r\n\r\n1. Add these outputs to Stage 04, not Stage 03.\r\n2. Keep the current clustering logic unchanged.\r\n3. Reuse existing outputs/data structures whenever possible.\r\n4. If helper functions are needed, add them cleanly in Stage 04 services.\r\n5. Do not introduce a heavy new computation path unnecessarily.\r\n6. Sampling for representative trajectories must be reproducible, not random unless seeded and documented.\r\n\r\nOUTPUT EXPECTATIONS\r\n\r\nPlease generate at least:\r\n\r\nFor exports:\r\n- attractor map\r\n- representative trajectories + attractors map\r\n- convergence diagnostic\r\n\r\nFor imports:\r\n- attractor map\r\n- representative trajectories + attractors map\r\n- convergence diagnostic\r\n\r\nAFTER IMPLEMENTATION\r\n\r\n1. rerun Stage 04 only\r\n2. list all new output files created\r\n3. explain the projection selected and why\r\n4. explain the deterministic sampling rule used\r\n5. confirm that no clustering methodology was changed\n"}]}}

{"timestamp":"2026-03-11T19:06:56.945Z","type":"response_item","payload":{"type":"function_call_output","call_id":"call_muhA0adCE78oygCgcaYJvD1O","output":"Exit code: 0\nWall time: 0.7 seconds\nOutput:\nfrom __future__ import annotations\r\n\r\nimport glob\r\nimport math\r\nimport re\r\nfrom dataclasses import dataclass\r\nfrom pathlib import Path\r\n\r\nimport numpy as np\r\nimport pandas as pd\r\nfrom sklearn.cluster import AgglomerativeClustering\r\nfrom sklearn.metrics import (\r\n    adjusted_rand_score,\r\n    normalized_mutual_info_score,\r\n    silhouette_score,\r\n)\r\n\r\n\r\nEARTH_RADIUS_KM = 6371.0088\r\nPERIODS_5 = [\r\n    (\"P1_1977_1988\", 1977, 1988),\r\n    (\"P2_1989_2001\", 1989, 2001),\r\n    (\"P3_2002_2009\", 2002, 2009),\r\n    (\"P4_2010_2018\", 2010, 2018),\r\n    (\"P5_2019_2022\", 2019, 2022),\r\n]\r\n\r\n\r\n@dataclass(frozen=True)\r\nclass ClusteringArtifacts:\r\n    stage_dir: Path\r\n    data_dir: Path\r\n    fig_dir: Path\r\n\r\n\r\n@dataclass(frozen=True)\r\nclass FlowTrajectoryData:\r\n    country: str\r\n    years: np.ndarray\r\n    xyz: np.ndarray\r\n    lat: np.ndarray\r\n    lon: np.ndarray\r\n    year_to_idx: dict[int, int]\r\n    step_years: np.ndarray\r\n    step_bearings: np.ndarray\r\n    step_year_to_idx: dict[int, int]\r\n\r\n\r\ndef build_clustering_artifact_paths(stage_dir: Path) -> ClusteringArtifacts:\r\n    return ClusteringArtifacts(stage_dir=stage_dir, data_dir=stage_dir / \"data\", fig_dir=stage_dir / \"fig\")\r\n\r\n\r\ndef latlon_to_xyz(lat, lon):\r\n    lat_r = np.deg2rad(np.asarray(lat, float))\r\n    lon_r = np.deg2rad(np.asarray(lon, float))\r\n    x = np.cos(lat_r) * np.cos(lon_r)\r\n    y = np.cos(lat_r) * np.sin(lon_r)\r\n    z = np.sin(lat_r)\r\n    return x, y, z\r\n\r\n\r\ndef xyz_to_latlon(x, y, z):\r\n    lon = np.rad2deg(np.arctan2(y, x))\r\n    hyp = np.sqrt(x**2 + y**2)\r\n    lat = np.rad2deg(np.arctan2(z, hyp))\r\n    return lat, lon\r\n\r\n\r\ndef gc_rad_xyz(a, b):\r\n    dot = np.clip(np.sum(a * b, axis=-1), -1.0, 1.0)\r\n    return np.arccos(dot)\r\n\r\n\r\ndef gc_km_latlon(lat1, lon1, lat2, lon2):\r\n    p1 = np.deg2rad(np.asarray(lat1, float))\r\n    p2 = np.deg2rad(np.asarray(lat2, float))\r\n    l1 = np.deg2rad(np.asarray(lon1, float))\r\n    l2 = np.deg2rad(np.asarray(lon2, float))\r\n    a = np.sin((p2 - p1) / 2) ** 2 + np.cos(p1) * np.cos(p2) * np.sin((l2 - l1) / 2) ** 2\r\n    return EARTH_RADIUS_KM * 2 * np.arcsin(np.sqrt(np.clip(a, 0.0, 1.0)))\r\n\r\n\r\ndef initial_bearing_deg(lat1, lon1, lat2, lon2):\r\n    p1 = np.deg2rad(np.asarray(lat1, float))\r\n    p2 = np.deg2rad(np.asarray(lat2, float))\r\n    dl = np.deg2rad(np.asarray(lon2, float) - np.asarray(lon1, float))\r\n    y = np.sin(dl) * np.cos(p2)\r\n    x = np.cos(p1) * np.sin(p2) - np.sin(p1) * np.cos(p2) * np.cos(dl)\r\n    return (np.rad2deg(np.arctan2(y, x)) + 360.0) % 360.0\r\n\r\n\r\ndef infer_country(fp: str) -> str | None:\r\n    m = re.search(r\"^barycenter_([A-Za-z]{3})_\", Path(fp).name)\r\n    return m.group(1).upper() if m else None\r\n\r\n\r\ndef _prepare_flow_trajectories(state_long: pd.DataFrame, flow: str) -> tuple[list[str], dict[str, FlowTrajectoryData]]:\r\n    sub = state_long[state_long[\"flow\"] == flow].copy()\r\n    countries = sorted(sub[\"country\"].unique())\r\n    prepared = {}\r\n    for country in countries:\r\n        g = sub[sub[\"country\"] == country].sort_values(\"year\")\r\n        years = g[\"year\"].to_numpy(dtype=int)\r\n        lat = g[\"lat\"].to_numpy(dtype=float)\r\n        lon = g[\"lon\"].to_numpy(dtype=float)\r\n        xyz = g[[\"x\", \"y\", \"z\"]].to_numpy(dtype=float)\r\n        year_to_idx = {int(y): i for i, y in enumerate(years)}\r\n        if len(years) > 1:\r\n            step_mask = (years[1:] == years[:-1] + 1)\r\n            step_years = years[:-1][step_mask]\r\n            step_bearings = initial_bearing_deg(\r\n                lat[:-1][step_mask],\r\n                lon[:-1][step_mask],\r\n                lat[1:][step_mask],\r\n                lon[1:][step_mask],\r\n            ).astype(float)\r\n        else:\r\n            step_years = np.empty(0, dtype=int)\r\n            step_bearings = np.empty(0, dtype=float)\r\n        prepared[country] = FlowTrajectoryData(\r\n            country=country,\r\n            years=years,\r\n            xyz=xyz,\r\n            lat=lat,\r\n            lon=lon,\r\n            year_to_idx=year_to_idx,\r\n            step_years=step_years,\r\n            step_bearings=step_bearings,\r\n            step_year_to_idx={int(y): i for i, y in enumerate(step_years)},\r\n        )\r\n    return countries, prepared\r\n\r\n\r\ndef load_state_long(bary_glob: str) -> pd.DataFrame:\r\n    parts = []\r\n    for fp in sorted(glob.glob(bary_glob)):\r\n        country = infer_country(fp)\r\n        if not country:\r\n            continue\r\n        df = pd.read_csv(fp)\r\n        if {\"year\", \"lat_exports\", \"lon_exports\", \"lat_imports\", \"lon_imports\"} - set(df.columns):\r\n            continue\r\n        for flow in [\"exports\", \"imports\"]:\r\n            lat_col = f\"lat_{flow}\"\r\n            lon_col = f\"lon_{flow}\"\r\n            weight_col = (\r\n                \"total_exports_value_final_matched\"\r\n                if \"total_exports_value_final_matched\" in df.columns\r\n                else \"total_exports_value_matched\"\r\n            )\r\n            if flow == \"imports\":\r\n                weight_col = (\r\n                    \"total_imports_value_final_matched\"\r\n                    if \"total_imports_value_final_matched\" in df.columns\r\n                    else \"total_imports_value_matched\"\r\n                )\r\n            sub = df[[\"year\", lat_col, lon_col]].copy()\r\n            sub[\"country\"] = country\r\n            sub[\"flow\"] = flow\r\n            sub[\"lat\"] = pd.to_numeric(sub[lat_col], errors=\"coerce\")\r\n            sub[\"lon\"] = pd.to_numeric(sub[lon_col], errors=\"coerce\")\r\n            sub[\"weight\"] = pd.to_numeric(df.get(weight_col, np.nan), errors=\"coerce\")\r\n            sub = sub.dropna(subset=[\"year\", \"lat\", \"lon\"])\r\n            x, y, z = latlon_to_xyz(sub[\"lat\"], sub[\"lon\"])\r\n            sub[\"x\"], sub[\"y\"], sub[\"z\"] = x, y, z\r\n            parts.append(sub[[\"country\", \"flow\", \"year\", \"lat\", \"lon\", \"x\", \"y\", \"z\", \"weight\"]])\r\n    if not parts:\r\n        raise ValueError(f\"No compatible barycenter files found for {bary_glob}\")\r\n    out = pd.concat(parts, ignore_index=True)\r\n    out[\"year\"] = out[\"year\"].astype(int)\r\n    out = out.drop_duplicates(subset=[\"country\", \"flow\", \"year\"], keep=\"first\")\r\n    return out.sort_values([\"country\", \"flow\", \"year\"]).reset_index(drop=True)\r\n\r\n\r\ndef compute_features(state_long: pd.DataFrame) -> pd.DataFrame:\r\n    rows = []\r\n    for flow in [\"exports\", \"imports\"]:\r\n        countries, prepared = _prepare_flow_trajectories(state_long, flow)\r\n        for country in countries:\r\n            traj = prepared[country]\r\n            years = traj.years\r\n            xyz = traj.xyz\r\n            lat = traj.lat\r\n            lon = traj.lon\r\n            n_obs = len(years)\r\n            if n_obs == 0:\r\n                continue\r\n            if n_obs > 1:\r\n                step_rad = gc_rad_xyz(xyz[:-1], xyz[1:])\r\n                step_km = step_rad * EARTH_RADIUS_KM\r\n                path_len_rad = float(np.nansum(step_rad))\r\n                path_len_km = float(np.nansum(step_km))\r\n                net_disp_rad = float(gc_rad_xyz(xyz[[0]], xyz[[-1]])[0])\r\n                net_disp_km = net_disp_rad * EARTH_RADIUS_KM\r\n                straightness = float(net_disp_km / path_len_km) if path_len_km > 0 else np.nan\r\n                bearings = initial_bearing_deg(lat[:-1], lon[:-1], lat[1:], lon[1:])\r\n                ang = np.deg2rad(bearings)\r\n                mean_sin = float(np.mean(np.sin(ang)))\r\n                mean_cos = float(np.mean(np.cos(ang)))\r\n                bearing_mean = (np.rad2deg(np.arctan2(mean_sin, mean_cos)) + 360.0) % 360.0\r\n                bearing_circ_var = 1.0 - float(np.sqrt(mean_sin**2 + mean_cos**2))\r\n                turns = np.abs(np.diff(bearings))\r\n                turns = np.where(turns > 180, 360 - turns, turns)\r\n                mean_abs_turn = float(np.mean(turns)) if len(turns) else np.nan\r\n            else:\r\n                path_len_rad = path_len_km = net_disp_rad = net_disp_km = straightness = np.nan\r\n                step_km = np.array([])\r\n                bearing_mean = bearing_circ_var = mean_abs_turn = np.nan\r\n            row = {\r\n                \"country\": country,\r\n                \"flow\": flow,\r\n                \"n_obs\": n_obs,\r\n                \"start_year\": int(years.min()),\r\n                \"end_year\": int(years.max()),\r\n                \"net_disp_rad\": net_disp_rad,\r\n                \"net_disp_km\": net_disp_km,\r\n                \"path_len_rad\": path_len_rad,\r\n                \"path_len_km\": path_len_km,\r\n                \"straightness\": straightness,\r\n                \"mean_speed_km_per_year\": float(np.mean(step_km)) if len(step_km) else np.nan,\r\n"}}

{"timestamp":"2026-03-11T19:09:37.700Z","type":"response_item","payload":{"type":"custom_tool_call","status":"completed","call_id":"call_L3UV4DmhNucVjohgSUdGnTPI","name":"apply_patch","input":"*** Begin Patch\n*** Update File: C:\\Python\\Geoanalisis\\src\\geoanalisis\\services\\clustering.py\n@@\n import glob\n import math\n import re\n from dataclasses import dataclass\n from pathlib import Path\n \n+import geopandas as gpd\n+import matplotlib.pyplot as plt\n import numpy as np\n import pandas as pd\n+from shapely.geometry import LineString, Point\n from sklearn.cluster import AgglomerativeClustering\n from sklearn.metrics import (\n     adjusted_rand_score,\n     normalized_mutual_info_score,\n     silhouette_score,\n@@\n EARTH_RADIUS_KM = 6371.0088\n+GLOBAL_MAP_CRS = \"+proj=eqearth +datum=WGS84 +units=m +no_defs\"\n PERIODS_5 = [\n@@\n def stability_summary(distance_df: pd.DataFrame, clusters_df: pd.DataFrame, flow: str) -> tuple[pd.DataFrame, pd.DataFrame, pd.DataFrame]:\n@@\n     )\n     return summary, wd, bs\n+\n+\n+def _load_world_basemap(shapefile_path: Path) -> gpd.GeoDataFrame:\n+    world = gpd.read_file(shapefile_path)\n+    if \"ISO_A3\" in world.columns:\n+        world = world[world[\"ISO_A3\"] != \"ATA\"].copy()\n+    if world.crs is None:\n+        world = world.set_crs(epsg=4326)\n+    return world.to_crs(GLOBAL_MAP_CRS)\n+\n+\n+def _cluster_color_map(cluster_ids: list[int]) -> dict[int, tuple[float, float, float, float]]:\n+    cmap = plt.get_cmap(\"tab10\")\n+    return {cluster: cmap(i % cmap.N) for i, cluster in enumerate(sorted(cluster_ids))}\n+\n+\n+def _normalize_rows(values: np.ndarray) -> tuple[np.ndarray, np.ndarray]:\n+    norms = np.linalg.norm(values, axis=1)\n+    keep = np.isfinite(norms) & (norms > 0)\n+    if not keep.any():\n+        return np.empty((0, 3), dtype=float), keep\n+    return values[keep] / norms[keep][:, None], keep\n+\n+\n+def _slerp(u: np.ndarray, v: np.ndarray, t: float) -> np.ndarray:\n+    dot = float(np.clip(np.dot(u, v), -1.0, 1.0))\n+    omega = np.arccos(dot)\n+    if omega < 1e-12:\n+        return u.copy()\n+    so = np.sin(omega)\n+    return (np.sin((1.0 - t) * omega) / so) * u + (np.sin(t * omega) / so) * v\n+\n+\n+def _unitvec_to_lonlat_deg(point: np.ndarray) -> tuple[float, float]:\n+    x, y, z = float(point[0]), float(point[1]), float(point[2])\n+    lat = np.degrees(np.arcsin(np.clip(z, -1.0, 1.0)))\n+    lon = np.degrees(np.arctan2(y, x))\n+    if lon <= -180:\n+        lon += 360\n+    elif lon > 180:\n+        lon -= 360\n+    return lon, lat\n+\n+\n+def _build_country_lines(df_country: pd.DataFrame, pts_per_segment: int = 16) -> tuple[list[LineString], tuple[float, float] | None, tuple[float, float] | None]:\n+    dfc = df_country.sort_values(\"year\")\n+    values = dfc[[\"x\", \"y\", \"z\"]].to_numpy(dtype=float)\n+    unit_values, keep = _normalize_rows(values)\n+    if len(unit_values) < 2:\n+        return [], None, None\n+    years = dfc[\"year\"].to_numpy(dtype=int)[keep]\n+    lines: list[LineString] = []\n+    start_ll = None\n+    end_ll = None\n+    for idx in range(len(years) - 1):\n+        year0, year1 = years[idx], years[idx + 1]\n+        if year1 != year0 + 1:\n+            continue\n+        u = unit_values[idx]\n+        v = unit_values[idx + 1]\n+        coords = [_unitvec_to_lonlat_deg(_slerp(u, v, float(t))) for t in np.linspace(0.0, 1.0, pts_per_segment)]\n+        if len(coords) >= 2:\n+            lines.append(LineString(coords))\n+            if start_ll is None:\n+                start_ll = coords[0]\n+            end_ll = coords[-1]\n+    return lines, start_ll, end_ll\n+\n+\n+def _marker_size_from_n(n_countries: pd.Series) -> np.ndarray:\n+    return 60.0 + 18.0 * np.sqrt(pd.to_numeric(n_countries, errors=\"coerce\").fillna(0.0).to_numpy(dtype=float))\n+\n+\n+def build_convergence_summary(state_long: pd.DataFrame, clusters_df: pd.DataFrame, attractors_df: pd.DataFrame, flow: str) -> pd.DataFrame:\n+    sub = state_long[state_long[\"flow\"] == flow].copy()\n+    merged = sub.merge(clusters_df[[\"country\", \"cluster\"]], on=\"country\", how=\"inner\")\n+    merged = merged.merge(\n+        attractors_df[[\"cluster\", \"star_lat\", \"star_lon\", \"n_countries\"]],\n+        on=\"cluster\",\n+        how=\"left\",\n+    )\n+    merged[\"distance_to_star_km\"] = gc_km_latlon(\n+        merged[\"lat\"],\n+        merged[\"lon\"],\n+        merged[\"star_lat\"],\n+        merged[\"star_lon\"],\n+    )\n+    summary = (\n+        merged.groupby([\"flow\", \"cluster\", \"year\", \"n_countries\"], as_index=False)[\"distance_to_star_km\"]\n+        .agg(\n+            median_distance_km=\"median\",\n+            q25_distance_km=lambda s: float(np.nanquantile(s.to_numpy(dtype=float), 0.25)),\n+            q75_distance_km=lambda s: float(np.nanquantile(s.to_numpy(dtype=float), 0.75)),\n+            n_country_year_obs=\"count\",\n+        )\n+        .sort_values([\"cluster\", \"year\"])\n+        .reset_index(drop=True)\n+    )\n+    return summary\n+\n+\n+def _spaced_sample_indices(n_items: int, target: int) -> list[int]:\n+    if n_items <= target:\n+        return list(range(n_items))\n+    raw = np.linspace(0, n_items - 1, target)\n+    indices = []\n+    used = set()\n+    for value in raw:\n+        idx = int(round(float(value)))\n+        while idx in used and idx < n_items - 1:\n+            idx += 1\n+        while idx in used and idx > 0:\n+            idx -= 1\n+        if idx not in used:\n+            used.add(idx)\n+            indices.append(idx)\n+    if len(indices) < target:\n+        for idx in range(n_items):\n+            if idx not in used:\n+                used.add(idx)\n+                indices.append(idx)\n+            if len(indices) == target:\n+                break\n+    return sorted(indices)\n+\n+\n+def build_representative_trajectory_sample(\n+    state_long: pd.DataFrame,\n+    features_df: pd.DataFrame,\n+    clusters_df: pd.DataFrame,\n+    attractors_df: pd.DataFrame,\n+    flow: str,\n+    small_cluster_max: int = 12,\n+    large_cluster_sample: int = 12,\n+) -> pd.DataFrame:\n+    flow_state = state_long[state_long[\"flow\"] == flow].copy()\n+    flow_features = features_df[features_df[\"flow\"] == flow][[\"country\", \"n_obs\", \"path_len_km\"]].copy()\n+    flow_clusters = clusters_df[clusters_df[\"flow\"] == flow][[\"country\", \"cluster\"]].copy()\n+    merged = flow_state.merge(flow_clusters, on=\"country\", how=\"inner\")\n+    last_points = merged.sort_values(\"year\").groupby(\"country\", as_index=False).tail(1).copy()\n+    last_points = last_points.merge(\n+        attractors_df[[\"cluster\", \"star_lat\", \"star_lon\", \"n_countries\"]],\n+        on=\"cluster\",\n+        how=\"left\",\n+    )\n+    last_points[\"final_distance_to_star_km\"] = gc_km_latlon(\n+        last_points[\"lat\"],\n+        last_points[\"lon\"],\n+        last_points[\"star_lat\"],\n+        last_points[\"star_lon\"],\n+    )\n+    last_points = last_points.merge(flow_features, on=\"country\", how=\"left\")\n+\n+    rows = []\n+    for cluster, group in last_points.sort_values(\n+        [\"cluster\", \"final_distance_to_star_km\", \"n_obs\", \"path_len_km\", \"country\"],\n+        ascending=[True, True, False, False, True],\n+    ).groupby(\"cluster\", sort=True):\n+        cluster_size = int(len(group))\n+        if cluster_size <= small_cluster_max:\n+            selected = group.copy().reset_index(drop=True)\n+            rule = \"all_countries_small_cluster\"\n+        else:\n+            target = min(large_cluster_sample, cluster_size)\n+            idx = _spaced_sample_indices(cluster_size, target)\n+            selected = group.iloc[idx].copy().sort_values(\n+                [\"final_distance_to_star_km\", \"n_obs\", \"path_len_km\", \"country\"],\n+                ascending=[True, False, False, True],\n+            )\n+            rule = \"distance_stratified_even_sample\"\n+        selected = selected.reset_index(drop=True)\n+        selected[\"sample_rule\"] = rule\n+        selected[\"sample_rank\"] = np.arange(1, len(selected) + 1)\n+        rows.append(\n+            selected[\n+                [\n+                    \"country\",\n+                    \"cluster\",\n+                    \"n_countries\",\n+                    \"n_obs\",\n+                    \"path_len_km\",\n+                    \"final_distance_to_star_km\",\n+                    \"sample_rule\",\n+                    \"sample_rank\",\n+                ]\n+            ]\n+        )\n+    if not rows:\n+        return pd.DataFrame(\n+            columns=[\n+                \"country\",\n+                \"cluster\",\n+                \"n_countries\",\n+                \"n_obs\",\n+                \"path_len_km\",\n+                \"final_distance_to_star_km\",\n+                \"sample_rule\",\n+                \"sample_rank\",\n+            ]\n+        )\n+    out = pd.concat(rows, ignore_index=True)\n+    out.insert(0, \"flow\", flow)\n+    return out.sort_values([\"cluster\", \"sample_rank\", \"country\"]).reset_index(drop=True)\n+\n+\n+def render_attractor_map(\n+    attractors_df: pd.DataFrame,\n+    flow: str,\n+    shapefile_path: Path,\n+    out_path: Path,\n+) -> None:\n+    world = _load_world_basemap(shapefile_path)\n+    sub = attractors_df[attractors_df[\"flow\"] == flow].copy()\n+    if sub.empty:\n+        return\n+    gdf = gpd.GeoDataFrame(\n+        sub,\n+        geometry=[Point(xy) for xy in zip(sub[\"star_lon\"], sub[\"star_lat\"])],\n+        crs=\"EPSG:4326\",\n+    ).to_crs(GLOBAL_MAP_CRS)\n+    cluster_colors = _cluster_color_map(gdf[\"cluster\"].astype(int).tolist())\n+    fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(13, 7))\n+    world.plot(ax=ax, color=\"#f0f0f0\", edgecolor=\"#b8b8b8\", linewidth=0.35, zorder=1)\n+    for cluster, group in gdf.groupby(\"cluster\", sort=True):\n+        group.plot(\n+            ax=ax,\n+            color=[cluster_colors[int(cluster)]],\n+            markersize=_marker_size_from_n(group[\"n_countries\"]),\n+            edgecolor=\"black\",\n+            linewidth=0.6,\n+            zorder=3,\n+        )\n+        for _, row in group.iterrows():\n+            ax.text(\n+                row.geometry.x + 180000,\n+                row.geometry.y + 180000,\n+                f\"C{int(cluster)} (n={int(row['n_countries'])})\",\n+                fontsize=8,\n+                color=\"black\",\n+                zorder=4,\n+            )\n+    ax.set_title(f\"Stage 04 Attractor Prototypes: {flow.capitalize()}\", fontsize=13)\n+    ax.set_axis_off()\n+    fig.tight_layout()\n+    fig.savefig(out_path, dpi=300, bbox_inches=\"tight\")\n+    plt.close(fig)\n+\n+\n+def render_representative_trajectory_map(\n+    state_long: pd.DataFrame,\n+    sample_df: pd.DataFrame,\n+    attractors_df: pd.DataFrame,\n+    flow: str,\n+    shapefile_path: Path,\n+    out_path: Path,\n+    pts_per_segment: int = 16,\n+) -> None:\n+    world = _load_world_basemap(shapefile_path)\n+    flow_sample = sample_df[sample_df[\"flow\"] == flow].copy()\n+    flow_state = state_long[state_long[\"flow\"] == flow].copy()\n+    flow_attractors = attractors_df[attractors_df[\"flow\"] == flow].copy()\n+    if flow_sample.empty or flow_attractors.empty:\n+        return\n+    cluster_map = flow_sample.set_index(\"country\")[\"cluster\"].to_dict()\n+    line_rows = []\n+    endpoint_rows = []\n+    for country in flow_sample[\"country\"].astype(str).tolist():\n+        df_country = flow_state[flow_state[\"country\"] == country].copy()\n+        lines, start_ll, end_ll = _build_country_lines(df_country, pts_per_segment=pts_per_segment)\n+        cluster = int(cluster_map[country])\n+        for geom in lines:\n+            line_rows.append({\"country\": country, \"cluster\": cluster, \"geometry\": geom})\n+        if end_ll is not None:\n+            endpoint_rows.append({\"country\": country, \"cluster\": cluster, \"geometry\": Point(end_ll)})\n+    cluster_ids = sorted(flow_attractors[\"cluster\"].astype(int).unique().tolist())\n+    cluster_colors = _cluster_color_map(cluster_ids)\n+\n+    fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(13, 7))\n+    world.plot(ax=ax, color=\"#f0f0f0\", edgecolor=\"#b8b8b8\", linewidth=0.35, zorder=1)\n+    if line_rows:\n+        line_gdf = gpd.GeoDataFrame(line_rows, geometry=\"geometry\", crs=\"EPSG:4326\").to_crs(GLOBAL_MAP_CRS)\n+        for cluster, group in line_gdf.groupby(\"cluster\", sort=True):\n+            group.plot(\n+                ax=ax,\n+                color=[cluster_colors[int(cluster)]],\n+                linewidth=1.0,\n+                alpha=0.45,\n+                zorder=2,\n+            )\n+    if endpoint_rows:\n+        end_gdf = gpd.GeoDataFrame(endpoint_rows, geometry=\"geometry\", crs=\"EPSG:4326\").to_crs(GLOBAL_MAP_CRS)\n+        for cluster, group in end_gdf.groupby(\"cluster\", sort=True):\n+            group.plot(\n+                ax=ax,\n+                color=[cluster_colors[int(cluster)]],\n+                markersize=16,\n+                edgecolor=\"white\",\n+                linewidth=0.3,\n+                alpha=0.75,\n+                zorder=3,\n+            )\n+    attr_gdf = gpd.GeoDataFrame(\n+        flow_attractors.copy(),\n+        geometry=[Point(xy) for xy in zip(flow_attractors[\"star_lon\"], flow_attractors[\"star_lat\"])],\n+        crs=\"EPSG:4326\",\n+    ).to_crs(GLOBAL_MAP_CRS)\n+    for cluster, group in attr_gdf.groupby(\"cluster\", sort=True):\n+        group.plot(\n+            ax=ax,\n+            color=[cluster_colors[int(cluster)]],\n+            markersize=_marker_size_from_n(group[\"n_countries\"]) * 1.3,\n+            edgecolor=\"black\",\n+            linewidth=0.9,\n+            zorder=4,\n+        )\n+    ax.set_title(f\"Representative Trajectories and Attractor Prototypes: {flow.capitalize()}\", fontsize=13)\n+    ax.set_axis_off()\n+    fig.tight_layout()\n+    fig.savefig(out_path, dpi=300, bbox_inches=\"tight\")\n+    plt.close(fig)\n+\n+\n+def render_convergence_plot(convergence_df: pd.DataFrame, flow: str, out_path: Path) -> None:\n+    sub = convergence_df[convergence_df[\"flow\"] == flow].copy()\n+    if sub.empty:\n+        return\n+    cluster_ids = sorted(sub[\"cluster\"].astype(int).unique().tolist())\n+    cluster_colors = _cluster_color_map(cluster_ids)\n+    fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(12, 5))\n+    for cluster, group in sub.groupby(\"cluster\", sort=True):\n+        group = group.sort_values(\"year\")\n+        color = cluster_colors[int(cluster)]\n+        ax.fill_between(\n+            group[\"year\"].to_numpy(dtype=float),\n+            group[\"q25_distance_km\"].to_numpy(dtype=float),\n+            group[\"q75_distance_km\"].to_numpy(dtype=float),\n+            color=color,\n+            alpha=0.18,\n+        )\n+        label = f\"C{int(cluster)} (n={int(group['n_countries'].iloc[0])})\"\n+        ax.plot(\n+            group[\"year\"].to_numpy(dtype=float),\n+            group[\"median_distance_km\"].to_numpy(dtype=float),\n+            color=color,\n+            linewidth=2.0,\n+            label=label,\n+        )\n+    ax.set_title(f\"Distance to Cluster Attractor Over Time: {flow.capitalize()}\", fontsize=13)\n+    ax.set_xlabel(\"Year\")\n+    ax.set_ylabel(\"Great-circle distance to attractor (km)\")\n+    ax.grid(alpha=0.2, linewidth=0.4)\n+    ax.legend(frameon=False, ncol=1, fontsize=9)\n+    fig.tight_layout()\n+    fig.savefig(out_path, dpi=300, bbox_inches=\"tight\")\n+    plt.close(fig)\n+\n+\n+def write_visual_outputs(\n+    state_long: pd.DataFrame,\n+    features_df: pd.DataFrame,\n+    clusters_df: pd.DataFrame,\n+    attractors_df: pd.DataFrame,\n+    flow: str,\n+    shapefile_path: Path,\n+    data_dir: Path,\n+    fig_dir: Path,\n+) -> dict[str, str]:\n+    sample_df = build_representative_trajectory_sample(\n+        state_long=state_long,\n+        features_df=features_df,\n+        clusters_df=clusters_df,\n+        attractors_df=attractors_df,\n+        flow=flow,\n+    )\n+    convergence_df = build_convergence_summary(\n+        state_long=state_long,\n+        clusters_df=clusters_df,\n+        attractors_df=attractors_df,\n+        flow=flow,\n+    )\n+    sample_path = data_dir / f\"gc_{flow}_trajectory_sample.csv\"\n+    convergence_path = data_dir / f\"gc_{flow}_convergence_summary.csv\"\n+    sample_df.to_csv(sample_path, index=False)\n+    convergence_df.to_csv(convergence_path, index=False)\n+\n+    attractor_map_path = fig_dir / f\"gc_{flow}_attractor_map.png\"\n+    trajectories_map_path = fig_dir / f\"gc_{flow}_trajectories_attractors.png\"\n+    convergence_plot_path = fig_dir / f\"gc_{flow}_convergence.png\"\n+\n+    render_attractor_map(attractors_df, flow=flow, shapefile_path=shapefile_path, out_path=attractor_map_path)\n+    render_representative_trajectory_map(\n+        state_long=state_long,\n+        sample_df=sample_df,\n+        attractors_df=attractors_df,\n+        flow=flow,\n+        shapefile_path=shapefile_path,\n+        out_path=trajectories_map_path,\n+    )\n+    render_convergence_plot(convergence_df, flow=flow, out_path=convergence_plot_path)\n+    return {\n+        \"sample_path\": str(sample_path),\n+        \"convergence_path\": str(convergence_path),\n+        \"attractor_map_path\": str(attractor_map_path),\n+        \"trajectories_map_path\": str(trajectories_map_path),\n+        \"convergence_plot_path\": str(convergence_plot_path),\n+    }\n*** End Patch\n"}}

{"timestamp":"2026-03-11T19:12:39.059Z","type":"response_item","payload":{"type":"function_call","name":"shell_command","arguments":"{\"command\":\"$env:PYTHONPATH='C:\\\\Python\\\\Geoanalisis\\\\src'; @'\\nimport pandas as pd\\nfrom pathlib import Path\\nbase = Path(r'C:\\\\Python\\\\Geoanalisis\\\\runs\\\\trade_s2_v001\\\\2026-03-10_006_baseline-freeze\\\\artifacts\\\\04_clustering\\\\data')\\nnew = Path(r'C:\\\\Python\\\\Geoanalisis\\\\runs\\\\trade_s2_v001\\\\2026-03-11_006_stage03-names\\\\artifacts\\\\04_clustering\\\\data')\\nfiles = [\\n    'gc_exports_clusters.csv',\\n    'gc_imports_clusters.csv',\\n    'gc_exports_attractors.csv',\\n    'gc_imports_attractors.csv',\\n    'gc_stability_summary.csv',\\n]\\nfor name in files:\\n    a = pd.read_csv(base / name)\\n    b = pd.read_csv(new / name)\\n    same = a.equals(b)\\n    print(name, 'exact=', same, 'shape_old=', a.shape, 'shape_new=', b.shape)\\n    if not same:\\n        common = [c for c in a.columns if c in b.columns]\\n        num = [c for c in common if pd.api.types.is_numeric_dtype(a[c]) and pd.api.types.is_numeric_dtype(b[c])]\\n        if num and a.shape == b.shape:\\n            diff = (a[num] - b[num]).abs().max().max()\\n            print('  max_numeric_diff=', diff)\\n'@ | python -\",\"workdir\":\"c:\\\\Python\\\\trade\",\"timeout_ms\":120000}","call_id":"call_8g6T5VqbeT99hnAs3vNLsqvB"}}
